813303846.Text: People are still trying to preserve flowers, despite the cold brought on by the Eternal Freeze. Like a good time, its beauty is fragile and temporary, but its fragrance will help you forget the tough times, even if only for a moment.

-752780095.Text: Why is there a hotel in a place virtually unvisited by outsiders for centuries?\n"Since everyone can't leave the city, why not rent a room in the most prosperous and beautiful part of the city? Relax and enjoy a delicious meal, like you would on any trip." Old Goethe said with a smile.

1976103260.Text: One of the few privately-owned in Belobog operated by a mechanic. They accept, but are not limited to, the following jobs, machine maintenance, troubleshooting, ancient relic repairs, Gepard's signature, and band performance events (rock and roll only)...

410019319.Text: The entrance intersecting the Fragmentum with reality. Do not enter unless absolutely necessary.

-1156064622.Text: A city can do without a town hall, clocktower, or church, but there must be shops. If you wish to purchase anything, check it out.

1572818733.Text: 【临时】险物诞生于绝境，后来者跨过碎刃，奔赴终点的嘉奖——英雄将被后世传颂。

6734792.Text: Why is there a hotel here? Are there even any guests? Many undergrounders don't know the answer, but the Goethe Hotel has stayed in operation through countless tax periods.

-1559349149.Text: In the Underground, miners who fake an illness are scolded and thrown out of the clinic—— not because the doctor lacks empathy, but because her time is too precious and must be reserved for people who really need it.

1169534206.Text: 【临时】贝洛伯格的城门外没有丝毫生命的迹象——有的只是无际的雪原与呼啸的寒风。

-1915579508.Text: 【临时】这条裂界通路复制了数百年前的时光一隅，将贝洛伯格的旧时景象固定在了碎裂的空间中。

813303847.Text: 【临时】大批的银鬃铁卫把守着这片军事禁区，任何企图窥探其秘密的渗透者都将受到惩罚。

-752780094.Text: 【临时】对于大部分闯入者来说，遍地污秽的裂界是他们挥之不去的噩梦。但无论围绕它发生的故事有多么毛骨悚然，仍然有小部分亡命之徒将其视为投机者的天堂。

1976103261.Text: 【临时】数百年间，地底人日夜无休地开采着地髓——他们中很少有人还记得，这些矿场本身也曾是扭曲裂界的一部分。

410019320.Text: 【临时】追随史瓦罗的喽啰们占据了这片古代采掘场遗迹，任何思想端正的地底居民都对这里敬而远之。

-996123329.Text: 【临时】对于行走在「开拓」命途上的人们来说，星穹列车永远是温暖的港湾。无论探索异世的旅途多么波折劳累，只要回到这里就能毫无顾虑地睡上一觉——当然，前提是帕姆不会在耳边唠叨。

671233823.Text: To ward off the Eternal Freeze, the Union city gates are closed off all year round, only opening to welcome returning Silvermane Guards from patrol. It wasn't until the arrival of the outsiders that this iron rule was broken.

1074518350.Text: The Union is proud of this central plaza lined with a variety of dazzling shops. If you encounter any outlaws or suspicious people, remember to contact the Silvermane Guards.

-491565591.Text: As the only high-end hotel in the Union, the exterior of the Goethe Hotel is magnificent. It's amazing how empty it is.

-1795527506.Text: Why is there a hotel here? Are there even any guests? Many undergrounders don't know the answer, but the Goethe Hotel has stayed in operation through countless tax periods.

2096155263.Text: In the Underground, miners who fake an illness are scolded and thrown out of the clinic—— not because the doctor lacks empathy, but because her time is too precious and must be reserved for people who really need it.

-602624914.Text: Enter Jarilo-IV, where trailblazers are greeted with endless white and unpredictable adventures.

-602624883.Text: The Fragmentum copies a moment of time from hundreds of years. It is unknown what stories the ancient residence with seals had at that time.

-1005909410.Text: A vacant area stacked with various building materials, everlasting in this moment frozen in the Fragmentum.

560174531.Text: There is an opening in the Fragmentum here. Where it leads remains a mystery.

-602624980.Text: The undergrounds call the Fragmentum, Devil's Stomach. Most people who venture inside in search of treasure do not ever return.

-1005909507.Text: The Fragmentum can crudely recreate scenes and objects from a moment in reality. If you're lucky, you might even pick up something of value there.

560174434.Text: Hidden deep within the Fragmentum, whoever built this mysterious pharmacy was either a genius or a madman - or maybe both.

1864136349.Text: Underground miners are already used to the stench of the mines. Common words cannot adequately describe the complex mixture of lime, mold and sweat.

-2027546420.Text: Miners would gather here after a long day's work. Ores and mining equipment that weren't transported away in time are stacked here. An unspoken rule among the miners is not to report the occasional slacker.

701336935.Text: Although undergrounders are used to it, mining Geomarrow is dangerous work. If you don't want to lose a finger or suffer serious burns, please memorize the safety protocols by heart.

-884949982.Text: A mixed group of prospectors gathered outside of Svarog's Base, where they are usually gossiping and boasting. Their exchange is surprisingly interesting, which goes beyond the undergrounder stereotypes.

681133959.Text: For a long time, Svarog has been stationed here, guarding the secrets behind him. However, Wildfire believes those secrets are the rightful property of the undergrounders and should be made public.

-602624949.Text: Many Silvermane Guards are guarding this restricted zone, even a snow rat wouldn't be able to enter without them knowing.

-1005909476.Text: Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone is powered all day by an energy hub that is guarded 24-hours. Evidently, if anything were to happen, the commander in charge would suffer serious consequences.

560174465.Text: Many Union soldiers have dedicated their lives to protecting the secrets inside the Fragmentum. In the end, they do not even know the true nature of those secrets.

1864136380.Text: Its stagnant air, fragmented space, and pervasive pollution warn all intruders that they are not welcomed here in this maze.

-2027546389.Text: Hundreds of years ago, Plamya led her most trusted people deep to explore the unknown and dangerous Fragmentum. They courageously ventured here to lay a solid foundation for the Union.

701336966.Text: While standing here, you can look up to see the grand fortress looming in the distance of the Fragmentum. Perhaps, that's where the heart of the Union lies.

1104621493.Text: Over the centuries, the depths of the corridor have been severely eroded by contamination. What's surprising is the ancient devices have remained intact, as if being miraculously protected.

674619982.Text: Underground Mines: Mining Team Resting Area

-790707379.Text: Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone: Entrance Outpost

775376562.Text: Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone: Energy Hub

-1953506793.Text: Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone: Exclusive Zone

-142176641.Text: [Blue Lamp Umbrella] "What exactly is an umbrella?" Blue Lamp Umbrella is a type of fungi that is commonly found growing near Geomarrow. Small-sized fruiting body, flat cap with a dimple and gills, 0.5-2cm in diameter, smooth, dry, color fades from cyan to light-blue, color becomes lighter towards the center. The stipe is 3-5cm, 1mm or thicker, smooth, curved, and hollow. The cap is covered in shining dust, making the mushroom give off a blue glow in the dark. This fungi is edible, but not recommended because the flesh is hard to chew. It's used primarily by some clinics for pharmaceutical purposes. Fungi of the Blue Lamp family have analgesic effects, which have proven effective for trigeminal neuralgia, migraines, fracture pains, and rheumatoid arthritis. The discovery of this fungi has done wonders for miners who commonly suffer from these problems. Its name "umbrella" was borrowed from a common object to describe its shape, but over time, Underground people forgot what an umbrella was. [Corrosive Ore Moss] "Random thoughts creep up in an abandoned mine lit up by an eerie green light." Corrosive Ore Moss is a small plant with subspherical reflex cells on top, usually found in cave entrances or abandoned Geomarrow mines. The plant absorbs the moisture and organic matter in the air through its compact structure, secreting acidic substances constantly during the growth process. In Geomarrow mines, the acidic substances dissolves Geomarrow powder, creating a chemical reaction that will release weak light and energy. Its strong protonema is usually round and can reflect or refract light into a greenish glow. In the depths of a cave where there is almost no light, this moss is still capable of using photosynthesis to survive. There is an ecological phenomenon, known as the "Moss Corridor" where the mossy overgrowth expands to unimaginable proportions inside a Geomarrow mine. [Zombie General] "This really is a zombie..." The Zombie General is not an actual zombie, nor is it a real general, it's just the name of a dead giant banyan tree. The giant banyan tree existed long before the Eternal Freeze. This single tree extended to form an entire forest and a famous eco-attraction. To extend their respect, the people at the time named this tree "General Sherba." When the Eternal Freeze came about, the exposed parts of "General Sherba" died in the frost and snow. However, in its slow process of agony and death, the roots of "General Sherba" kept growing and took over underground water sources to absorb even more energy. The expansion of its underground roots system continued even after the main tree had died. The elder members of the Geomarrow Development Group were shocked the first time they ran into the roots because Belobog was more than a hundred kilometers away from the General. From this point on, the ever-growing roots were referred to by the people as the "Zombie General." Even to this day, the "Zombie General" still acted as a very important source of materials for the Underworld and its people. As for the overall cultivation of the roots, it would be safe to say that less than 1% of it has been tapped into.

-1708260582.Text: [Azure Ghost Umbrella]   "Its name is not meant to sound cool, but serve as a warning." Azure Ghost Umbrella, also known as Deadly Blue Umbrella, is very similar to the Blue Lamp Umbrella in terms of shape. It is umbilical shaped in the middle and has grooves. The color is a pure indigo, with no gradient. The only difference is the presence of subtle white spots, which is harder to detect in darker environments. 50 grams of this highly-toxic poison is enough to kill an adult weighing 50 kilograms. Its toxins are mainly amatoxins and toxic peptides, both of which are extremely high in fresh Azure Ghost Umbrella. When ingested, these toxins cause serious damage to the liver, kidney, blood vessel linings, and the central nervous system, resulting in organ failure and eventually death. Its resemblance to the Blue Lamp Umbrella had resulted in several poisoning incidents within the Geomarrow Development Group. Their response was the massive search and destruction of these fungi. It would be hard to find them now. In addition, the Underground people began to cultivate Blue Lamp Umbrella farms and no longer needed to search for them in the wild. This also drastically reduced the number of poisoning incidents. [Aroma Ether Grass] "'Fairy Tale King'? Don't be fooled by such a nickname!" Aroma Ether Grass is commonly found in caves with high concentrations of radiation. Its thick short stems store rich aroma volatiles. At low temperatures, the plant leaves quickly produce a scent that attracts many underground creatures. The scent also has a hypnotic effect and creatures eventually huddle around the plant to sleep. The creatures gathered around to sleep look like a group of children listening to a story quietly, which is why this plant has been nicknamed "Fairy Tale King." However, sleeping in a place with high concentrations of radiation isn't as sweet as a fairy tale. The volatiles have an effect on humans, but is less effective on healthy adults. The Underground markets have made use of the volatiles by diluting it to create aromatherapy that induces sleepiness. [Soothing Mushroom] "This is way better than ordinary painkillers!" Soothing Mushroom, medium-sized soft body, thick cap, 4-8cm wide, convex cap, black and green with ciliiform scales and curled edges. Soothing Mushroom contains psilocybin, a special blocker ingredient that severs neural connections when serotonin becomes lacking, removing partial sensory input of the person. Due to its special effect of blocking nervous transmissions, it was developed into a type of pain-killer. However, the actual effects do not only block out the feeling of pain. During the test phase, the Soothing Mushroom brought on tremendous stress on the organs of the test subjects. It wasn't until an outside medical expert modified the formula by lowering the dosage and using regional injections that the pain suppressant ability of the mushroom was realized.

1020622773.Text: Mine Poison Antidote Inhalant Manual Please read the instructions carefully and use according to the instructions or under the guidance of your physician. [Drug Name] Mine Poison Antidote Inhalant Manual [Ingredients] This product is a compound. Every gram contains 400 mg of Crystalline Lizard extract and 110 mg of Blue Lamp Umbrella extract. Other ingredients include the animal fat of various underground organisms, which is not fixed. [Properties] This product is a fiber tubing, which is to be inserted in the wooden sleeve for nasal inhalation. It has a pungent yet stimulating scent. [Category] This product is an over-the-counter medicine in otorhinolaryngology. [Purposes] Used for mild dyspnea caused by the inhalation of Geomarrow dust. [Specifications] Each tube weights 0.675 grams, containing 0.27 grams of Crystalline Lizard extract and 0.075 grams of Blue Lamp Umbrella extract. [Pharmacological Effects] This product is a blood vessel irritant composed of Crystalline Lizard extract, Blue Light Umbrella extract, and animal fats. It enters through the nasal blood vessels and decomposes Geomarrow dust, easing the effects of crystallization. [Usage] Please do not directly use the drug in an environment exposed to Geomarrow dust. When using, unfasten the covering and place the wooden sleeve to one side of the nostril. Press the other side with your other finger and take two or three deep breaths. If the wing of your nose crystallizes, close the other nostril with a sanitary plug for similar effects. [Side Effects] Very rare cases showed a rejection and allergic reaction, causing swelling inside the nasal cavity resulting in suffocation. No other side effects being reported. [Precautions] Not known. Please follow the usage instructions and dosages. Do not overdose.

-901691528.Text: To all parents who are miners: Hello, I am Doctor Natasha. I know many of you are exhausted from work, so you entrust your children in the care of the orphanage. I understand how difficult it must be for your family as the tax period approaches. However, some of you haven't seen your children for so long that I can't help but wonder about your motives. I have enclosed a list of the children who haven't seen their parents in over 100 days. I hope these parents will visit within 3 days after the tax period ends. If not, I will be adding your names to my notebook. Those who are in my notebook had better pray you don't get sick or hurt, or I'll be sure to take care of them using my family's secret "compassionate" recipe. Isabella: 11 years old, 133 days without visits Little Bella is the most well-tempered and sensible child at the orphanage. She often volunteers to help with the chores and takes care of the other children while I'm away. If you're Bella's parents, I suggest you learn about childcare from her, because I'm pretty sure you wouldn't even be able to care for a mad mole that eats anything. Big Charlie: 9 years old, 164 days without visits Big Charlie is a child with quite the temper, probably something he learned from his parents. He doesn't know his own strength and frequently gets into fights with the other children, for which I've scolded him on numerous occasions. He has shown an interest in medicine and is capable of making basic potions that he uses on his own bruises. If you're Charlie's parents, please visit him soon, or I'll teach him how to make "special" potions for you. Little Charlie: 7 years old, 115 days without visits It's been almost a year since Little Charlie joined us at the orphanage, yet he hasn't said a single word. Little Charlie's parents, I know what you look like because he always keeps a family photo in his pocket. Believe me when I say that I'll personally come to find the two of you if you don't come to visit. I can only assume you're suffering from amnesia, and I have the right remedy for it. Alina: 9 years old, 140 days without visits Alina is a pretty girl, adored by many boys at the orphanage. They shower her with small gifts and often fight with each other to win her favor. If you're Alina's parents, I hope you'll visit soon, or I'll have to tell the boys' parents about all this. Timmy: 8 years old, 153 days without visits Little Timmy, oh little Timmy, what a headache. I don't know where he read about the existence of "pigeons." Ever since then, he's been obsessed with studying "birds". I told him that there were no birds in the Underground, but he won't listen. He constantly daydreams about them. If you're Timmy's parents, I suggest you visit Timmy before you have to capture two of these "birds" to make him happy.

261107886.Text:
(I)

I heard you can make more money finding ancient relics than mining, so I asked some of my dad's acquaintances for help. 

Half a month later, the only relic I found was something similar to a Rand Counter. The difference may only be its smaller size. I don't know anything about ancient relics, so I had to spare a little fortune to hire Balaway the Miner's Lamp for an appraisal.

Mr. Balaway said the relic is called a virtual pet. People from the Old World would feed images of their pets into it. Through the device, the pet could live on forever with its owner.

I didn't know what a pet is, so I asked him. According to the Miner's Lamp, a pet is something of a luxury, something you had to feed and take care of. I had no interest in it whatsoever.

Balaway mentioned that the parts inside could be exchanged for some money, but told me not to get my hopes up. He also told me to find Svarog.

What the hell. I thought this was supposed to make more money than mining.


(II)

Thankfully, everything is virtual. I fed it a ton of food and drinks.

Being that I can barely keep myself alive, it would be impossible for me to have a pet. Thanks to this... "virtual pet" thing that I can be a real pet owner.

Daddy's friend says that he's looking for a buyer for me. For some reason, I kinda feel like keeping it now. Oh well, I guess I'll just help the next owner take care of it for now... I think today is "Sesame's" bath day.

By the way, I named it "Sesame". Cute, isn't it?


(III)

Never thought virtual pets would get sick too.

I went and found Natasha to have her look at Sesame, but she said that there was nothing she could do and sent me off to look for the Miner's Lamp. I heard that she was a great doctor, how couldn't help Sesame?

Don't be scared Sesame, daddy's right here, hang in there.


(IV)

Sesame has recovered!

Oh man, I was so worried these couple of days that I couldn't even eat! I feel so relieved right now! I have to tell everyone, they need to know that Sesame has recovered!

It's almost Sesame's birthday, I'm gonna go get some Rock Crabs for the celebration! ... Though it can't really eat anything...


(V)

Oh no! Sesame has been stolen by a bunch of bandits! Those thieves! Those scavengers! Trash!

I recognize their faces, they're Svarog's men, good at flipping ancient relics. They were also at the doctor's last time and heard about Sesame.

Dammit! I'm gonna get Sesame back even if it costs me my life!


(VI)

Sesame! Oh, my sweet Sesame! How could you already be salvaged...

By the time I found them, they said they've already sold Sesame to Svarog and that it's probably been salvaged by now.

Waaaah Sesame! It'd be pointless for me to throw my life at them now that you're already gone... How could this be... I might as well take my own life...

After making Sesame's grave, maybe... I'm just saying... maybe I should get a Crystalline Lizard.


(VII)

Lizards are troublesome, I'll give up.

-1304976055.Text: [Crystalline Lizard] "In old legends, Crystalline Lizards were thought to be soldiers of Mine Mama." Crystalline Lizard is a cold-blooded reptile found in the underground mines. As it matures into adulthood, their leathery scales will produce Krystalloid, which will vary in color depending on the region, temperature, and health of the lizard. Experts have pointed out that Crystalline Lizards expel Geomarrow and other minerals from their salt glands through the osmotic pressure in the body. The Krystalloid is a byproduct of regulating the osmotic balance of body fluids. The randomness of the Krystalloid color has sparked a wave of collectors, who are willing to pay exorbitant prices for wild Crystalline Lizards with a rare color. This has caused an ecological imbalance in the mines. In the past, many pest outbreaks have been tied to the decline in the Crystalline Lizard population. In response, the Union has prohibited the hunting of wild Crystalline Lizards. The alkaloids in the body fluids of the Crystalline Lizard can promote the release of minerals such as Geomarrow, which is often used in pharmaceutics. Thus it is common for Underworld clinics to breed Crystalline Lizards in captivity, but they don't have any value to the collectors. [Geomarrow Olm] "How can there be a reptile that feeds on pure energy?!" Geomarrow Olm is an amphibian that burrows near underground water sources. With white and pink feathery scales, its highly-developed hearing and smell make up for its degraded vision. It's able to survive both on land and in the water. According to the first Termite team, populations of Geomarrow Olm were always found near large mines, for reasons unknown. People assumed that these olms fed on Geomarrow, hence the name. Biologists have pointed out that the Geomarrow Olms live in an environment lacking in food. This severe environment has caused them to evolve into organisms with superior metabolism and antioxidant capacity. They are capable of surviving without food for up to 10 years, due to their slow metabolism and limited movement. However, it is scientifically reasonable to assume that they could survive more than 15 years without feeding. So, experts believe that it is more likely that their feeding behaviors are hard to observe, rather than draw the conclusion that they feed on Geomarrow. The Geomarrow Olms by nature are afraid of and reside away from the humans in darker and more moist areas. The Geomarrow Olms do not pose a threat to humans. [Funeral Worm] "Only these bugs are not disgusted by a crystallized corpse, never missing out on a funeral." Funeral Worms are a subspecies of coleoptera commonly found in underground caves. They range in lengths of up to 3.5 centimeters, with an average length of 1.2 centimeters. They are usually black and orange in appearance. Their flat soft bodies allow them to crawl under animal carcasses. After discovering a potential animal carcass, they gather in swarms around the body. They spend a great deal of time confirming that the animal is dead before they dig the ground beneath it to decompose it. Funeral worms observing a dead animal is similar to attending a funeral, hence the name. Some biologists have suggested their unique habits may be related to creatures who barely move, like the Geomarrow Olm. If they misjudge prey, they might become prey themselves. Funeral worms are one of the most important parts of Underground ecology, breaking down carcasses and feces of a wide range of animals. They are also commonly found in cemeteries and bat caves.

1067676940.Text: [Crystal Scorpion] "Crystal Scorpions don't have crystals. Isn't it common knowledge?" Adult Crystal Scorpions are almost entirely covered with highly transparent carapaces. Growing up to five or six centimeters long, the body is clearly segmented into two: head and chest, and the abdomen. There are six pairs of appendages on the head and chest. The first pair is used for feeding. The second pair is threatening crab-like claws for attacking and defending, and for touching things. The remaining appendages are all used for movement. The abdomen is the scorpion tail, which is bent forward and poisonous. The venom is not lethal to humans, but it will cause a burning sensation. The transparent carapace for which the Crystal Scorpion is famous turns out to be a mimicry of Geomarrow, and not some expensive crystal. While Geomarrow dust is processed from the body, it assimilates with the hard shell to form this unique transparent shell. With its help, Crystal Scorpions can feed on Crystalline Lizards who live near Geomarrow. During the period in which Crystalline Lizards were hunted by collectors, Crystal Scorpions were also hunted. As a result, their population was reduced significantly to the point where they're now considered a rare and endangered species. They are traded at high prices in Underground markets. [Mad Mole] "I can't help but wonder what would happen if I had the misfortune of stumbling into their burrow while exploring the dark depths of a cave?" The Mad Moles have stout powerful limbs and well-developed claws, which are good for digging. However, they would much prefer to use their head and teeth. Their eyes are completely defunct, with little or no vision. They have no outer ear, and the ear shell is a small fold of skin around the ear hole. With short tails that are slightly longer than their hind feet, the moles are usually bare or a little hairy. People once thought they fed primarily only plant roots, but it is now common knowledge that they are omnivores. Ecologists believe that the Mad Mole is an organism susceptible to radiation. Affected by Geomarrow, the moles have developed interesting characteristics that are detrimental to the survival of the species. The first is their extremely aggressive behavior. They won't hesitate to swarm and quickly devour animals with an advantage in numbers and size. Then, it is the never-ending hell of survival. The mad moles rarely leave their burrows, but keep expanding them. If an unfortunate creature were to fall into their burrow, they would eat it. If there were no such creatures, they would eat each other. There will always be a massive pile of bones in the mad moles' burrows. Finally, they reproduce like crazy. Even if the population were to be nearly wiped out by humans, its population will quickly return to normal, which means enormous, after a few months. There was once an unfortunate miner who heard the moles scurrying behind the wall every night as he slept. He would experience chronic hallucinations and nightmares. Even when clear-headed, he would mumble in a language that no one could understand. Fortunately, the large burrow was connected to the stove in the kitchen. One night, soot, which hadn't been completed burned out, caught on fire and exterminated the mad moles. That miner was saved from a gruesome fate. [Rock Crab] "You're joking when you say this thing almost destroyed the Union, right?" The rock crab, a mix between a shrimp and a crab, likes to live inside rocks, and many of them use hard rocks as a shell for protection. According to research, the rock crab originally lived along the coastal regions. They were oceanic creatures that would dig through rocks to lay their eggs, then returned to the sea. After the Ice Age, the rock crabs bred in large numbers and moved underground to the warmer Belobog, causing massive tunnel collapses as they migrated. During this period, they were known as tunnel rock crabs. However, for reasons unknown, their population declined significantly, almost to the point of extinction. Nowadays, rock crabs are considered a gourmet meal in the Underworld. Although the crab is rare and difficult to obtain, the delicious texture of its meat makes it a star ingredient in the Underground. People usually steam the crab, but there are many other variations are loved by residents: stir-fried crab meat with hammer peppers, salt-baked rock crab, and much more. Rock crab powder can also be combined with a variety of other ingredients for rich flavors. With the dwindling crab population nowadays, many people doubt the old rumor that every family eats crab.

-498407001.Text: Semicircular Electrode Collar Danger:★★☆☆☆ Rarity:★★★★☆ Trading Value: 400g-600g [Appearance] It is an incomplete arc, a semicircle with two metal synapses on the interior. There are also several buttons, which have been tested but don't work. Their functions are unknown. [Description] This object was discovered by our regular customer, Conrad. It was found in a place with some other small ancient relics, which I presume were mostly Old World households or personal objects. In terms of value, they won't make you rich overnight, but they're worth a bit of money. Conrad pointed out that its shape was strange, even the great Miner's Lamp had trouble identifying it. The Old World is full of strange objects, but this object doesn't even come close to being the strangest. With a little thought, I can try analyzing it. As usual, we'll start by analyzing its structure. With how neat the arc is, I'm inclined to believe that it was designed that way, rather than being forced into this shape by "an external force." The bulk of the weight is concentrated at the center, so the sides appear to supportive. The arc interior is a concave surface... Don't think my words are too profound. When I identify ancient relics, I must use professional and precise words. If it was like your wife's chit-chat, then I might as well take down the "Miner's Lamp" sign, close my business, and make you pay for my storytelling. I'm only kidding. Let's continue the appraisal. The most striking thing about the exterior is the two curved pieces of metal. I've seen this before. This is similar to something that I had appraised before. When the switch was flipped, two metal pieces made a crackling sound. I quickly shut it down for fear of what would happen. It smelled like something was burning, You wonder what it was? The man who brought the object to me put his hands directly on it. I'll just say, bless Ol' Man Copper that my hands aren't fast. You see, the key to identifying ancient relics is better safe than sorry. This philosophy has gotten me out of trouble many times. So, I assume the metal parts are an electrode, which is the same as that thing. Wasn't it easy to deduce the function of this strangely shaped object? The key to identifying ancient relics is knowledge. That will come naturally with experience. Wasn't the key is better safe than sorry? Well, there's many. Why else would I be doing this line of business, and not you? I went through manuscripts and magazines from the Old World. I found some similarly shaped handicrafts made from cloth. They were worn from the back of the neck. No wonder it was shaped like an arc! However, I was afraid of what would happen, so I called over Conrad and put the thing around his neck. And what do you know? It was a perfect fit. If someone from the Old World saw it, he would have said it's worn around the neck as well. Conrad was overjoyed at how well it fit. He said the two pieces of metal fit comfortably on the nape of his neck and wondered what they were for. When I told him it was a torture device, he was so frightened that he took it off immediately. He lifted my chair and beat me hard. I was wronged! First of all, it wasn't powered. Secondly, I didn't even press the switch. Honestly! Since I have not seen another ancient relic like it so far, its rarity is temporarily set at four stars. Although it is a torture device, its effect is mild compared to the relics that can heat up and explode. Its danger level has been temporarily set at two stars. Appraiser: Balaway, the Miner's Lamp

1423907299.Text: Old World Building Blocks Danger: Unknown Rarity:★★★★★ Trading Value: 2g [Appearance] Several metal blocks of varying shapes with tongue and groove joints, which can be used to connect the blocks. These appear to be ancient relics, even by Old World standards. [Description] This object is really worthless, based solely on the price. Those who use the manual for the Miner's Lamp to identify ancient relics are looking to make a quick buck. But again, this isn't worth much. There is a price difference between different ancient relics. The Bluecoats millitaris are only interested in relics that contain special technology or rare materials. Do you understand what I mean? They aren't collectors. They only want what's useful. These metal building blocks were ancient relics even in the Old World. In this day and age, they are useless junk with an interesting shape. Mr. Gaim brought the metal blocks to me. I told him at that time it wouldn't sell for anything. It didn't glow or heat up, had no buttons or wires, nor did he have enough of it. It was worthless junk metal with strange patterns. He didn't believe me, so he took it to the Bluecoats. And what happened? They laughed at him for wasting their time. Do you get it now? If you do want to sell, don't sell it as an ancient relic. Afterward, he sold it to me for cheap and I inquired about where he found them. It took me half a month to dig out the rest of the metal blocks. How troublesome... It's probably at this point that someone will say I was tricking him. Why would I go through the trouble of digging it up if it's worthless? "None of your damn business!" That's what I was going to say. But just to explain, you can't survive in my line of business without a little OCD. You tell me, "here are some blocks, but there's a few missing." Could you bear it? Absolutely not! I had to dig it out. Since then, I would find the time to piece together a few pieces in between appraisals. These strange metals block had to be one of a kind! I had never seen anything quite like it, at least in my line of work. In the beginning, I had no clue how to piece them together. Gradually, a shape began to take form, but it was taking up too much of my time. You can call me stupid, but it felt like some mysterious force was holding me back. Eight years had passed and I still hadn't completed it. But that mysterious voodoo-like quality made it all the more interesting. I read many more manuscripts from the Old World in those eight years. There was an article about "cursed blocks." According to legends, there was a king who suffered a coup. The king's most loyal wizard sealed the king's soul into blocks and split them to protect the king from being killed. However, the vengeful king will possess the person who pieced the blocks back together and reclaim his throne. Based on the description, it should be the same thing. Why are the people from the Old World so unreasonable? Don't invent these weird objects in the first place. Just invent powerful weapons so the king can suppress the coup. Then, I'd be able to make some money by selling those weapons, right? For safety concerns, I stopped trying to piece the metal blocks back together. It's not that I can't, but I don't like the thought of being possessed by an evil spirit from the Old World. Why would I want to? There's nothing left to reclaim. It's all just useless junk. Give up. But here's the twist. Someone finished the puzzle when I wasn't looking! The person didn't even say anything. I don't even know if the person was possessed by an evil spirit! And worst of all, the metal blocks were just left there. I admire the person for not stealing, but why would you just leave it there? What if something happened? So, I sold it immediately. I heard it's being used as a stone press for kimchi by a dama. The marked price is the price I sold it for. Appraiser: Balaway, the "Miner's Lamp"

-142176642.Text: Reference Number: XC100451 Medical Certificate [Name]: Alger [Gender]: Male [Infection History]: A member of the third mining team. Long-term exposure to Geomarrow crystals, crystalline dust, and noise. [Symptoms]: 1. Middle stages of crystallization on the chest, lower neck, and wing of the nose. The right hand shows symptoms of late-stage crystallization, with the ring finger having crystallized and broken off. The radial and ulnar arteries of the right hand are prominent, much more so than the left hand. 2. Dyspnea, accompanied by nasal obstruction, hemoptysis, chest pains, and pulmonary dysfunction. Difficulty lying down and edema of lower limbs. 3. Hearing loss, severe tinnitus, occasional auditory hallucinations, and severe insomnia. 4. The emotional regulation function is damaged. Hard to stabilize the mental state. Frequent episodes of anger, impulsion, and depression. [Doctor's Advice]: The patient has been mining Geomarrow for over five consecutive years and has symptoms of gradual skin crystallization. The patient isn't immune to Geomarrow contamination. It is suspected that he failed to strictly follow safety protocols. His right hand and respiratory tract were exposed to infection. His physical condition will make it impossible for him to continue working in the mines. [Doctor] (Signature): Natasha [Appendix]: Suggest thoroughly inspecting the safety and quality of the protective equipment.

-1708260583.Text:
Lyrics:

Maddon Tunnel is collapsing,
collapsing, collapsing.
Maiden Tunnel is collapsing,
crystalline buddy.

Build it up with wooden stakes,
wooden stakes, wooden stakes.
Build it up with wooden stakes,
crystalline buddy.

Wooden stakes will bend and break,
bend and break, bend and break.
Wooden stakes will bend and break,
crystalline buddy.

Mine all the Geomarrow,
Geomarrow, Geomarrow.
Mine all the Geomarrow,
crystalline buddy.

Geomarrow is not with me,
not with me, not with me.
Geomarrow's not with me,
crystalline buddy.

Meaning:

The meaning of the song may simply be related to the difficulties in the construction of the Maddon Tunnel. However, the exact location is unknown. Only the song survives. There also exist other theories of its origin.

Results of the suppression towards the attack:

There's another theory. The rebels were crushed by the Silvermane Guards and escaped to the Maddon Tunnel. They wanted to utilize its complex trenches to defend themselves, but the Silvermane Guards bombarded the tunnel continuously. In the nursery rhyme, "crystalline buddy" was a term for the rebels. But because the existence of the Maddon Tunnel couldn't be verified, many people don't believe it happened.

The Old World's primitive idea of sacrifice:

Other theories relate to primitive cultures from the Old World that sacrifice can protect tunnels from collapsing. However, that theory doesn't work in the Underground because mine tunnels will collapse, no matter how many people are sacrificed.

1020622772.Text:
To all the lucky miners who unearthed ancient relics:

Regrettably, I will be stopping my appraisal services at the end of this tax period. The consulting location at the market will also be closed.

In other words, from this day forward, if you stumble over something in the mines, or if your mining team happens upon an ancient weapon, don't think it's a gift from the gods. And don't bother carrying all that junk over to me.

Like it's said above, it's done. I'm done.

I'm sure some of you will wonder the reason behind quitting when work is going well. I suppose I can go out of my way to give everyone a proper response.

First of all, it's a thankless job.

When I started this business, I wanted to use the manuals left behind from the Old World to analyze and appraise ancient relics. It allowed all of you to bargain with the Bluecoats for more rations and mining equipment.

It's pretty clear for those who know. These days, no one understands how people from the Old World built these machines, much less the knowledge they had in their heads.

Some ancient relics explode when exposed to the air, making it more dangerous than a mine filled with gas. Some were as plain as a metal rod, not unlike the one your wife uses to poke the stove. But push the button somewhere on it, it'll be as scary as one of your wife's temper tantrums.

Let me give you an example. During the last tax period, I received an egg-shaped object. (Yes, many of you haven't eaten eggs, but you've seen canned poultry, right? That's where it comes from.) Anyway, I went through all the manuscripts, including Old World magazines, and finally concluded that it was some kind of jewel, known as the "Ramlea Egg."

Well, that begs the question: what's a jewel? After asking the old Uther, we learned that jewelry is a decoration that people would wear. Honestly, the Old World people had nothing better to do.

The old man banged the egg a few times on the table, then removed the pin on top, which might have been used to hang something on it. Suddenly, the egg started heating up and glowing. It produced a light stronger than hundreds of mine lamps stacked together (may be exaggerated by Uther). The old man could barely see anything, and now he definitely can't. You can say that I was lucky. When the incident happened, I was in the other room making tea. My eyes were fine but the old man's son threatened to take out my eyes until I offered him 60 canned goods.

Do you understand now? I'm putting my life on the line for you guys, but the money you're paying me won't even cover my medical fees.

Here's the second point.

Ever since Boss Svarog and his prospectors entered the market, they've been taking ancient relics as compensation for settling disputes and solving problems.

I know people don't like hearing this, but you'd rather believe that buffoon than in my wisdom. Good! That means this world is one step closer to ending.

I have offered my loyal services to all of you lucky dogs, without once coveting any of your ancient relics. I can't say the same for Svarog! That monster will take whatever ancient relics he can from you. Keep that in mind the next time you ask him for an appraisal... Think if your treasure would be snatched by him. Think of what supply the Bluecoats can give you.

Here's the final and most important point.

While doing business with Boss Svarog, there were some unpleasant disputes, which resulted in an accident that fractured my radius. I'm currently recovering, but I won't be taking any more work related to the identification of ancient relics.

In short. Goodbye, my friends!

-901691529.Text: "Stars" I swing a rusted pick, At the stubborn veins, Ding! Ding! Golden sparks fly, It is the only starlight I know. Sky - The sky I long for, Why won't you embrace me, Even set the cold "Earth" as separation, Deny me the light of the stars. Ding! Ding! I swing a pick with all my might, At the veins more generous than the sky, Give it sweat to trade for star light. "The Sun" A boy with a backpack, Embarks on an ambitious path. The boy feels a wave of emotions, Decides to search for the sun. He asks the mole wandering by the road, If it knows where the sun is. The mole shakes its head, "I heard it's in the sky, But I've never left the ground." The boy is not discouraged, And asks the sand martin where the sun is. The sand martin shakes its head. "The sun hides in the clouds, That cannot be reached without wings." The boy begins to waver. Is the sun really out of reach? An old miner sees him depressed, Pats him on the shoulder and hands him a mining hoe. "Dig down, little boy, There's another sun hidden in the Earth."

1827191826.Text: Margarita Margarita, Margarita, Your dark skin, Soaked with the sweat, Your thick lips, Smiling with delight, Margarita, Margarita, Your soft voice, Singing for the stupid Earth, Your beautiful eyes, Are only focused on the gravel. Margarita, Margarita, How I want to win your heart, Will you put down your pick for me, If only for a moment? Will you do your hair for me, And blush at me? You don't know, you don't know I'm captivated by the view of your back. A love so deep, Turns to torture and pain. Margarita, Margarita, Why is your gentleness so cruel? Why is your passion so cold? If I become scrap metal, If I cannot catch your attention, Then let me be one with the Earth, To hear you sing, forever in silence. "Boxer" In a dark and enclosed corner, The man sits alone, Feasting on fish set on the stone table, Guzzling down the fine wines from the cellar. The man is a boxer, The celebration is not his reward, But to help him gain strength The "grace" of his sponsor. He may not be the tallest one, But his fists never tire, He rids all those with prideful eyes, And fill his sponsor's pockets full of silver. But the boxer is aged, The fire in his heart grows dim, He found a conspirator, To trade his honor for a better life. Defeat is a terrible feeling, People curse and shout in his face, But as he feels his plump pockets, No shame is too hard to swallow. The boxer's stomach is filled with meat and wine, He starts to estimate the fight's result, Is it greed? Is it corruption? No, it is but the way to survive in the underground.

261107885.Text: Life The banging of iron, Woke me from slumber. Mother served a bowl of hot soup, We cracked those old jokes The smell of soup and meat filled the house, It's a warm peaceful life. The endless work, It numbs me. The workers are chattering, Cannot tell it is elegant or crude. The hot and dry air presses against the naked eye, What a boring and stale life this is. The aging mine mule, Sadden me. What difference is there between us and it? These repetitive questions are pointless. The bell ring is muffled and painful to the ears, This is a life of never-ending turmoil. Lying on the ice-cold sheets, I fell deep in thought. A slave who never sees the master, Robbed of the right to even daydream about revolting. The fool binds himself silently, We were born, but we know not what life is. "Defiance" Pierce the dagger through the heart of the beast, Listen to its painful roar, Look straight into its furious eyes. The blood of defiance runs in the beast's veins, Oh, foolish and subordinate one, How long till your tolerance runs to an end? Have you ever thought of being defiant yourself, Leave this waterless land without light, Take a look at the starry night sky, Does that not sound romantic, Would the hailing blizzard, Be unbearable torment. Oh, child of the Underground, look around you, Your skinny brothers are starving, Your saddened sisters need new cloth, Let go, put down the mining pick in your hand, Toss it into the burning furnace, Turn it into guns and blades, Follow me out of this blackened cave. Remember the fury before the beast passed, Rather die free, Than live in shame.

-1304976056.Text: To the shady businessman in toad glasses: You're a dead-man, you know that? You didn't think you'd be able to wander around in the Underground after you've cheated Mr. Scott out of his money, did you? Someone stuck numerous receipts beneath the line of words and wrote with scribbled words: It's all on contract, it was a fair and honest trade, I cheated no one. Someone drew a beaten-up face with a mustached bald head under the words and an arrow pointing towards it, on the other side of which reads "Mr. Scott, triggered." I had Balaway do an appraisal. The ancient relics you sold me are nothing but a pile of worthless scrap metal. I can't believe I fell for something as ridiculous as paying for a mere 5% premium, it's absurd! You listen and you listen good, you have one week to return every dime you swindled from me. Otherwise, you will wait to be tossed into the mines to be fed to Svarog along with your "ancient" scrap metal! If you're not that fraud, you're just here for the show: If you can capture that blue-hair shady businessman with toad glasses on his face, Mr. Scott will reward you handsomely! Remember, bring him to me alive, I have a lot of unfinished business with him - bring me no dead! Someone crossed out the line of words above and added: Selling large amounts of ancient relics, with a reliable source and only charging a 5% premium. Real prices, honest business!

1067676939.Text: "This is an interview published on the 'Crystal Daily - Weekend Edition' before the Solwarm Festival." Among the Crystal Peaks - Do You Really Know About Ice Storms? Last week, a strong cold front appeared in the suburbs and downtown area, forcing the Union Weather Center to issue an orange ice storm warning. The short-term Freezing Rain caused Glaze to form over surfaces and long icicles formed on many structures and trees. Thanks to Plamya's sacred protection, the Union remains warm all year long, but there are rare occasions of Freezing Rain. We have invited a Union meteorologist, Professor Yegard, to give us a brief introduction on Ice Storms. Reporter Millie (hereinafter referred to as M): Hello, Professor Yegard. Professor Yegard (hereinafter referred to as Y): Hello. M: Last week, the Union experienced an ice storm in the suburbs and downtown area. Can you give a brief explanation of the causes? Y: Here at the weather station, we usually call it Freezing Rain. They refer to the same weather condition. Before we talk about that, I must first mention the abnormally low temperatures that occur in the Union before the Solwarm Festival. M: I believe it is the Union's winter. The newspaper has made announcements in every region. Y: The dropping temperatures combined with the sudden strong winds from outside the Union greenhouse area, which rushed to the suburban, formed Freezing Rains. The urban heat island lied in the city center, so the temperature there was higher. The temperature only formed raining. M: It's more like sleet where I live. What makes Freezing Rain special is the Glaze. How is Glaze formed? Y: It is formed when warm and cold air collides. Below the surface, the air and ground temperatures drop below freezing. Above the surface, it is relatively wet and temperatures are above freezing. As water transitions to the surface below, it forms supercooled water drops. M: So supercooled water drops are water drops below freezing temperature? Y: Yes, since the temperature near the ground is lower, the supercooled water drops form a thin layer of crystal clear ice when they touch buildings, trees, vegetation, or roads. The formations on trees are usually called "icicles." M: So that's how it is. Based on your description just now, is "Freezing Rains" a rare occurrence? Y: That's right. There's only a short time before and after the Solwarm Festival in which this natural phenomenon can occur. Citizens are free to enjoy this beautiful scenery in the suburb, with proper attire and safety conditions. M: Thank you Professor Yegard for taking the time to teach us about Freezing Rains. Y: You're welcome.

-498407002.Text: Jarilo: God of Spring and War Perun, the God of thunder and lightning and the one ranked the highest in the Pantheon, is fashioned with a beard made of bronze and armed with a shield and an ax which he uses to slay sinners and evil spirits. He hurls his ax at his enemies and summons bolts of lightning before it returns to his hand. Perun exterminates demons that scourge across the skies with fiery arrows from his war chariot, what later became known as thunder and lightning to the world. Veles, a sworn enemy of Perun and previously also a God of war, now oversees the land, water, forest, and the Hades. Veles takes the form of half-human and half-snake with great bull horns on his head and a thick coat of fur on his back. He herds the cattle in the Land of Death while controlling wealth, rewards, music, and the night. He is an expert in magic and trickery. Pursuing Veles across the Earth, the enraged Perun cares not for the things that Veles shapeshifts to hide behind and strikes them down with indifference, even the humans. The mischievous Veles climbs from the damp underground to steal Perun's cattle, wife, and children. On the last night of February, Perun's 10th son Jarilo was born. It was also that night that he was stolen and brought to the Land of Death where Veles raises him. Land of Death is a lush prairie of eternal spring across the sea where Jarilo helps his foster father guard the cattle. Birds migrate to that land every winter. Jarilo didn't like to spend his life underground. He is the God of youth and life with the powers of reproduction and plant growth. At the end of winter, Jarilo would return to Earth and use the full extent of his powers as the god of springtime and war to drive the cold and darkness away. This process was difficult but necessary to bring about spring to the world. Jarilo was first noticed and welcomed by the Goddess of nature and death, Morana, who is Perun's daughter and Jarilo's sister. The two quickly fell in love and celebrated their wedding in the summer. The sacred unification of Jarilo and Morana symbolized a promise of fertility and lush vegetation. It also meant a temporary peace between Perun and Veles, and the late summer harvest would be successful and bountiful. However, Jarilo was not a faithful husband, so Morana killed him and used his corpse to build a new home. Without her husband, Morana gradually became a depressed old witch. The frightening and dangerous goddess of death would bring about a cold bitter winter. Morana would die at the end of the year, being reborn as Vesna. At the same time, Jarilo would also be reborn and the myth would start all over again. The endless cycle of life and death continued for a long time. The unfaithful Jarilo got killed after the autumn harvest and the lonely Morana died at the end of the year. The two gods were reborn and fell in love again, ensuring the bountiful harvest every day. It wasn't until one year when Morana killed Jarilo as usual, but the house she built was weak and collapses. With no shelter in the frigid winter, Morana's anger could not be contained any longer and she no longer accepted her fate of reconciling with Jarilo every year. As the goddess of nature and death, she placed a cruel curse on herself, preventing her from being reborn as Vesna, the goddess of youth and springtime. The curse worked. In her solitude, Morana became a dangerous witch that didn't die at the end of the year. The cold and darkness raged on, far greater than it ever had. Jarilo was reborn into this desolate place, just as Morana died, never to be revived. Faced with the ruthless winter, Jarilo realized that he had made a mistake. Spring would not come and Jarilo could not face the cold nights alone. He found Plamya, the fire of mercy, and prayed to her for forgiveness. Plamya sympathized with the witch but didn't agree with what she did. Plamya believed that only faithful love and devotion can calm Morana's anger. By autumn, the cold had not subsided. This time, out of regret, Jarilo decided to sacrifice himself. With the help of Plamya, Jarilo's body was transformed into a warm spring, forever to remain in Belobog, so that people could have a place to settle in this desolate world.

1423907302.Text: "Jarilo: God of Spring and War" Explanation Folklore Expert Chico One of the earliest historical references to the God is in the biography of a diplomat from the Saya kingdom, about 1800 years before the Eternal Freeze. On his expedition, he came across a tribe who happened to be celebrating the God of war, Jarroway. Experts from before the Eternal Freeze believe Jarroway to be the predecessor of Jarilo. Around 1200 years ago, in the northern region of Nycatha, a festival known as "Jarilo" was celebrated in late spring or early summer. Groups of villagers would walk along the countryside, symbolizing the return of the god back into this world. His return meant fertility and vegetation. Experts from before the Eternal Freeze found traces of religious rituals in honor of this God. The ritual sacrifice of a God's death carries heavy symbolic meaning. In other mythologies, the bodies of dead Gods create the world, with blood turning into rivers, bones turning into mountains, and hair turning into forests. With the energy crisis before the Eternal Freeze, the world created by the gods gradually weakens, until the house built from Jarilo's corpse could no longer support human life and collapses, just like it did in the myth. It is clear the myth's ending was influenced by the Eternal Freeze and the new religions. Before the Eternal Freeze, Jarilo was the typical "birth-death-rebirth" God of nature, whose mythological cycle was the same as that of crops in the northern region of Nycatha, from sowing to growing to harvesting (viewed as crop destruction). However, after the Eternal Freeze, the seasonal cycle was broken, replaced by the ice age and Belobog's unique climate. In the myth, this climate change is explained by the household affairs between the Gods. It's worth mentioning that Jarilo is described as a devout believer, seeking redemption from the great sage Plamya and paying a heavy price for his indiscretions. His mischievous nature is thought to be linked to the moon's changing phases. After the Eternal Freeze, people lack knowledge of astronomical phenomena and are more inclined to draw a moral conclusion to the ending. So, the humane ending for this god is that he paid the price for his love.

-142176639.Text:
By now, you should have received the "Heating Maintenance Safety Operation Manual." Make sure you read that first.

As you are well aware, my wife is about to give birth, so I will be heading home earlier today. I'll be gone for half a month, which means I won't be here for the big maintenance before the Solwarm Festival. But relax, Bel will walk you through everything. Just don't forget what I taught you and you'll be fine. I know you pick up on things fast.

However, the maintenance of heating pipes is the most important maintenance job in the Union. I've made some notes on here, in case you forgot. While you read this, open your "Heating Maintenance Safety Operation Manual" and follow along. Remember to ask Bel if you have any questions.

I won't go over the first and second points because there's nothing to say about getting the certificate and wearing protection. Those are too basic. For the third point, check the lighting for the heating maintenance area. You should pay special attention, especially to the emergency lighting. There was an accident in the past with my master's master where the furnace wasn't lit. No lighting is a recipe for more accidents.

The fourth point talks about the protective coating for the pipes. In the manual, it says to sprinkle the holy water from the church onto the pipes. If the people from church aren't there, just sprinkle it on your feet. The temperature of the pipes is too high. Spraying water on it will make the room filled with steam, which isn't safe. But you know the deal with church rituals. If you have to do it, just make sure you do it carefully and you'll be fine.

The eighth point goes over the most important maintenance of the Geomarrow reactor. Halfway through the maintenance, be sure to check your maintenance clothes and protective goggles. This is the largest reactor in the world and you can't be too careful. For the tenth point, the alarms and monitoring devices we use now are different from the ones in the manual. Just go with what I taught you.

That's about it. Once the heating maintenance is complete, you'll be a full-fledged maintenance worker responsible for your own part.

Come by during the Solwarm Festival and I'll treat you to a special suncake. It's more delicious than anything you'll find in the stores. Also, my wife's relatives are coming over, so dress up a bit.

-1708260580.Text:
Marlet,

I haven't seen you in a long time. I told my father I missed you, so he said I could write to you.

As I wrote this letter, my father was reading over my shoulders. I didn't want him to, but he'd probably read it when he mails it for me. So, whatever.

It's all your fault that I had a fever lately. I cried for the longest time after you moved away because I thought you'd forgotten me. You could imagine my surprise when I received the cookies you sent me a few days ago. I couldn't stop thinking about you, and I did poorly on a test. My father said it's just an excuse, but I really believed that it was your fault. Same with this fever.

My father said I could go visit you over the weekend, but we lived too far apart. He thought after some time, maybe half a year, or the next Solwarm Festival, we'd make new friends and we'd gradually forget about each other. That's what happened to him when he was younger. When I asked him about it, he looked away and it was only then that I noticed he was blushing.

My mother was reading my letter just now, but now they're gone.

I understand why my father is worried. You'd better send cookies to me every week and I'll also write to you. If you think it is unfair, then you can write to me and I'll bake you some cookies. My parents are back now. My mother said it would be nice if we'd work in the same factory. That's where my parents met.

Adults think they know everything. They think we want to get married, but our relationship isn't like that at all. They don't know anything about us. Right now, they're giggling to themselves. If I didn't have a fever, I'd write this letter in secret and mail it myself, so they wouldn't be able to read it.

I want to become a scientist, capable of creating the largest furnace this world has ever seen. That way, when we go out, we won't have to wear so many layers of clothing. Do you remember? When we went out to play, I wore so many clothes that I couldn't even put my hand down. We ended up not doing anything fun. When I create that furnace, we'll play there again.

So, I probably won't go to work at the factory. I'm more interested in going to the research institute. If you want to come with me, you can be a scientist. I'll understand if you want to do something else though. That's what makes me different from those adults.

I'm a little sleepy as I'm still on medication today. That's all for now. My dad said he'll mail the letter for me later.

Arna

1020622775.Text: "This is a survey published in the 'Crystal Daily - Weekend Edition'" The upcoming Solwarm Festival has everyone busy with festival preparations. My dear friends, have you made your suncakes? The reporter visited a simple household in the Union. They talked about what preparations they had done and what the Solwarm Festival meant to them. The interviewee is a young member of the Silvermane Guards and his family. The interview was scheduled for the morning before the Solwarm Festival. The husband was preparing to go out and purchase food for the festival while the wife stayed home to watch the child. Reporter: Hello, sorry to intrude. I'm Abel, the one who contacted you before. Ethan: Ah... it's no problem. It's my first interview so I'm a bit nervous. Reporter: Can you please introduce yourselves? Hannah: Hello, I'm Hannah and I work in the library. Beck: ... Boock. Reporter: Beck is so cute. How old is he? Hannah: He just turned one. Beck: Ge... Tad. Reporter: It seems like he's trying to say something. Are you going to the grocery? Why are you bringing so many things with you? Ethan: I asked for a leave of absence. Today, I'm going to take some comfort foods to my good friends at the Garrison Headquarters. Then, I'll head to the market. Beck: Ge Tad. Hannah: Shh... Reporter: Your friends must be happy. By the way, is Beck talking already? Hannah: No no, he can only say a few simple words. Beck: Ge! Tad! Reporter: Hahaha, that's still impressive for his age. What's he trying to say... Hannah: Gepard bastard. Ethan: I... don't agree with him. I always respect my superiors. Reporter: Well, I won't keep you. While you're out shopping, I'll interview your wife. Ethan: Wait... I'll go out later then. I'm afraid of what you will talk about during the interview. Ethan and Hannah have been married for two years. Based on Solwarm Festival customs, Ethan should have made suncakes for his mother-in-law. However, the nature of his job required him to be on duty for two consecutive years. This year, he has a break from work, which means he finally has the opportunity to showcase his cooking skills. Ethan: To be honest, I still don't know how to make it... Hannah: It's because you don't care. If the reporter didn't bring it up, you'd probably have forgotten. What would you have my mother eat? Ethan: Ah... Eh... Reporter: Please stop fighting, Beck just fell asleep. It's my understanding that Ethan doesn't usually cook? Ethan: Yeah, my wife usually cooks, since my job... Hannah: There you go again with your excuses. I'm busy with work too, but I make the time to cook. It all depends on whether you want to or not. Reporter: How about this? Why don't we learn how to make the suncake now? With an apron on and a kitchen knife in one hand, Ethan looked like a chef, even though it was his first time cooking. Hannah didn't seem like she was not going to help him in any way. She whispered to the reporter, saying she was going to use this opportunity to teach her husband a lesson. So, Ethan looked up the recipe for suncakes and followed it step-by-step. Add sugar and salt to three eggs and beat quickly. Add in Moo Milk and flour and mix until thick. While mixing, Ethan also removed the lumps in the mixture, which is good for a beginner. Brush the pan with a light layer of oil and put it in the egg mixture. And the suncakes are done! Ethan spiced up the recipe by adding some frosted salmon. The reporter was honored to be invited for a taste test. Reporter: Even though it was your first time, it seemed like you knew what you were doing... Ethan: I'm a little nervous. Hannah: ... Then let's dig in. P.S. The suncake was so delicious the reporter took 50 suncakes to go.

-901691526.Text: Suburbia Union Year 694, Richard and Dyanna founded the brand "Suburbia" together. The two designers both worked as lead designers at fashion brands "In the Stars" and "Industrial Threadwork." "Suburbia" embodies the meaning of being outside the system. The name is the representation and understanding of Union life and working style of the two designers. "The Union is a place overloaded with orders. In this city, no matter who you are, if you express your thoughts, dress the way you like, or lead a life with individuality, you will be deemed as someone outside the system. We sensed this conflict and wanted to use our design to fix it." Suburbia has been focused on creating unique outfits that also suit the taste of the young people: Whether it be an eye-catching leather jacket, colorful synthetic leather, phenomenal vest set, goth long coat, or the famous Union-wide modified military uniform... Though all these styles incorporate the current mainstream street fashion, it still has a lot of original Suburbia's elements in it. Though it's a very young brand, it has caught the core element of traditional culture and used it as if it were their own. Other than that, we've also seen their greater ambitions: "We're trying to create a relationship of space and time with this world. There needs to be not only the Union but also everything in the past. To be honest, we prefer the period in history when the Eternal Freeze has not yet occurred. The world was separated into different regions, each with its own style. "Suburbia" is not here just to serve the Union, but to also give support to help those in areas buried in wind and snow," Richard and Dyanna said. Recently, the young brand cooperated with the Edelweiss Workshop and began a line of designs including leather boots, outfits, and accessories. The two used blue, the color of the Union, as the design base, and retro patterns in combination with mainstream and industrial styles for their designs. Edelweiss Workshop has also brought back both classical and new-age fashion concepts to young people.

1827191829.Text: (I) The Devil's Mirror and Its Fragments A long time ago, there was someone called the Devil, the evilest person living in the Underground. When others were hard at work mining Geomarrow, he would be lazing about. When others were resting, he would get working to cause trouble. One day, when everyone was resting, he created a mirror that would reverse everything. Everything beautiful would turn ugly in the mirror. Good men would grin like criminals. Heroes would become the vilest criminals... The devil declared to the people in the Underground, "This mirror was dug up from the ground and used by the last civilization to see the truth of the world and humankind." Life in the Underworld was thrown into chaos: loving couples suspected each other of cheating; close families fell apart; true friends became enemies. The devil went around the Underworld with the mirror, destroying everything good. But some didn't believe in the devil, who neither listened to his words nor looked into the mirror. "It is only a twisted illusion that deceives the eye, it cannot fool the mind." Gradually, more people started to not believe in the Devil, which made him furious. He needed the mirror to twist one's heart, so that he could be happy and not be in pain and torment all the time. So he carried the mirror on his back, struggled through the blizzard to the Overworld, and went for the Snow Queen, thinking she might help him. But the Snow Queen was the coldest and the most heartless person. She wouldn't help anyone unless it benefited her. Unfortunately, the snow was too much for the Devil to bear, and he was frozen into an ice sculpture before he could even reach the Snow Queen. Later, the Snow Queen happened upon the Devil and his mirror. "Maybe I can use this mirror to freeze all the Underworld." The Snow Queen's blizzard had shattered the mirror into countless pieces. Many of the pieces, smaller than gravel, were blown into the Underworld by the howling wind. Every small piece possessed the same power as the whole mirror, causing more misfortunes than ever before. A small fragment in your eye would make you only see the foolish and ominous. A small fragment in your heart would turn you evil. The mirror was more dangerous as countless pieces than as a whole. In the world above, the devil, who had already become an ice sculpture, was happy with laughter. His ominous laughter spread quickly down into the Underworld under the roar of the wind.

261107888.Text: (II) A Boy and A Girl Kyle and Elena were neighbors living in the Underworld. Their parents were great friends, who grew even closer after they were born. Kyle was a gentle and frank boy who liked Elena. Elena was a lovely and kind girl who liked Kyle. The two of them loved each other and were inseparable. They even planted white and red roses together. There exist flowers in the Underworld, but they were a rare occurrence, for there is a lack of sunlight. Flowers that managed to bloom were small and quick to wither. However, the roses bloomed splendidly under the care of the two children. They remembered a hymn they had heard growing up: Roses grow rich and fragrant in the valley, where we met the great sage Plamya. "Look, Elena, this is all thanks to the great sage Plamya!" Kyle exclaimed. Elena nodded in agreement. Besides planting roses, the two children looked forward to hearing stories from Elena's grandmother about the Overworld. Her grandmother told them that the Overworld was covered in a blizzard, only Belobog still had sunlight. Elena asked, "What's a blizzard?" Her grandmother replied: "The white bees are gathering." Kyle was smart and knew that every bee swarm had its queen. He asked, "Do they also have a queen?" Her grandmother told them: "Yes, the Snow Queen lived in the deepest part of the blizzard. She used to fly through the streets on cold nights, peek into windows and listen in on people's conversations. Places she flew past would form strange pieces of ice, like flowers in bloom." "Will the Snow Queen come here?" Elena asked with concern. "We are protected by Plamya's sacred flame. The Snow Queen has not yet been able to freeze the Underworld." Her grandmother said as she pushed up her reading glasses. "If she dared enter," Kyle exclaimed, "I'll make her sit on the warm furnace, and she'll melt." Kyle started laughing. At that moment, a strong gust of wind blew open the window and Kyle got up to close it. Standing in front of the window, Kyle noticed a glimmering fragment in the air, which flew straight at him. Scared, Kyle quickly closed the window. "Ah! Something got in my eye!" Kyle cried in pain as he closed his eye. Elena rushed over to Kyle, who blinked a few times. "I think it's gone!" But it wasn't gone. What fell was a fragment of the mirror that we are familiar with. It was the magic mirror, created by the devil. It brought out the worst in humans, turning all good into evil. Another fragment was stuck in poor Kyle's heart. The Snow Queen is petty, and she probably overheard Kyle talking bad about her. He looked at the crying Elena with disgust and pushed her aside. "Why are you crying? I don't like how ugly you look." Elena was hurt and taken back by what Kyle said. Tears welled up in her eyes, but she still liked Kyle. "No, I wasn't crying." This upset Kyle even more. Kyle kicked over the red and white roses that they had planted together and stormed out. Elena, carefully protecting the shattered flower pot on the ground and yelled in Kyle's direction: "Kyle, what are you doing?! The roses are innocent!" The little girl couldn't contain her feelings anymore and burst into tears. Her grandmother held Elena in her arms and looked anxiously out the window. Strange pieces of ice formed on the window, the grandmother couldn't see anything. Even though there was no sunlight in the Underground, he was still in the warm Belobog. Standing out in the open, Kyle felt colder than he had ever felt before as if his heart was pierced with a dagger made of ice. It was at that moment that he noticed a white snowflake, something that shouldn't have appeared in the Underground. The snowflake grew bigger until it became a woman. A white veil made of countless star-like snowflakes enveloped her. Beneath the veil, Kyle could see a slim and beautiful woman, but her body was made of pure ice that was full of life. Her eyes were brilliant and her face showed no emotions as she slowly walked towards Kyle. Kyle knew at once that she was the Snow Queen. He was terrified but could not move a step because of the cold. "Are you shaking from the cold? Come into my veil." The snowflakes behind the Snow Queen formed a sleigh. She covered Kyle in her veil and placed him on the sleigh. The Snow Queen kissed Kyle lightly on the forehead. Ah! Her kiss was colder than ice and pierced his already half-frozen heart. Kyle felt he was going to freeze to death, but that feeling didn't last for very long. Soon, he didn't feel cold anymore, for the mirror fragment had embedded itself deeper into his heart. "We'll go to a magical kingdom! There, you'll forget about everything! Your heart will turn into ice and you won't feel any happiness or sadness, only indifference and cold! What joy that is! Soon, everyone here will have their hearts turned into ice, just like you. When that time comes, I will have frozen the entire Underground." With that said, she gently kissed Kyle on the forehead again. He has now completely forgotten about Elena, her grandmother, and everyone else. All that's left is the blizzard before him and the song of the howling wind. Kyle slowly drifted off and fell asleep.

-1304976053.Text: (III) The Girl Who Knew Magic and the Garden Nobody knew where Kyle went. Some said he turned into a mushroom in the abandoned mine. Some said he drowned in the underground water. Many shed tears for Kyle, but none cried as hard as Elena. The days were long and hard until everyone assumed Kyle was dead. The sun in the Overworld heard the cries of Elena and asked, "Elena, why are you crying so sad?" "Kyle's gone, he died!" Elena said. "I won't believe it!" The sun replied. "He's gone, he died," Elena said to the sand martin. "We won't believe it," They replied. In the end, Elena wouldn't believe it either. The next morning, she puts on her prettiest clothes and a pair of mining boots Kyle has never seen. She kissed her grandmother goodbye before heading to the underground water alone to find Kyle. "Did you really take my dear playmate away? If you return him to me, I'll give you my mining boots. The water flowed quietly, but when Elena dropped her favorite mining boots into the water, the calm water gently pushed the boots back to shore. It seemed the water was reluctant to accept for gift, for it did not take her dear Kyle. But Elena thought she didn't throw it far enough. She got into a small raft by the underground river that miners used so that she would be closer to the center of the water. She threw her mining boots once again, but they remained afloat on the surface. It still wasn't far enough. The raft was slowly drifting away from shore without notice. By the time Elena had realized what was happening, it was already too late. The small raft was following the water current towards the dark depths of the underground river with her boots following close behind. She had no paddles or miner's lamp, she had no choice but to cling onto the raft. As the underground river guided her through one cave after another, Elena was terrified and began to cry. The small raft was too far from shore for anyone to hear her cries. The olms emerged from the cave and followed her closely on the cave ceiling. They couldn't help her back to shore, but they stopped and swayed their bodies as if they were trying to comfort her. Gradually, Elena was not as terrified anymore. In slightly wider spaces, she would sit up to watch the ceiling full of glowing moss as she passed through the corridors. Maybe this is what a starry night looked like, she thought to herself. She had never seen it before but could imagine it would look a hundred times more beautiful. Soon, she reached a large open cavern with various kinds of flowers and Moss Corridors formed by Corrosive Ore Moss. Elena's roses had to be placed under the sunlight to grow, but the flowers here were much healthier than the ones she cared for. The garden was divided by the roots of giant trees, forming small spaces for the flower to grow. There were two lizard soldiers with long spears guarding the doorway. Elena greeted them, they were real soldiers and naturally didn't respond. It was at this moment that the small raft drifted closer to shore. As Elena drifted closer to the soldiers, they grew cautious of her and drew their long spears. The soldiers closing in on her scared her, and she screamed out. The woman noticed Elena and quickly told the soldiers to sheath their weapons. When Elena reached the shore, she couldn't help but stare at the woman, who was more beautiful than anyone she'd ever seen. "You poor baby, it's not free to stare at my beauty, you know? It'll cost you. Come with me, tell me why you've drifted here." Elena followed the emerald-like woman through the garden doorway. She looked back to find that the mighty lizard soldiers had become gems. Elena told the woman everything, to which she replied that Kyle had not passed through here. As Elena walked through the garden, she saw flowers more beautiful than the ones outside. When the two of them reached a root-dwelling hut, all kinds of creatures emerged from the garden, curious about the stranger. But the little girl just looked sad and worried.
