"I've wanted a cute little sister like you for a long time," said the woman, as she brushed Elena's hair. "Forget about the boy. When you fall asleep, you won't feel sad anymore." And Elena did just that. As drowsiness began to set in, her eyelids began to droop, and she gradually forgot about Kyle. The woman wasn't an evil witch. All she did was use a little magic so that Elena would remain by her side. Elena slept like she never did before: it was like falling asleep in a sack full of goose feathers. It turned out the woman was the true leader of the Underground. People from the northern region of Nycatha called her Mine Mama. When Elena fell asleep, Mine Mama revealed the green lizard tail from her back. She gently kissed Elena on the forehead, sincerely hoping that she would forget her troubles and live with her forever. Elena woke up in the middle of the night, and Mine Mama was deep in slumber. Not knowing how many nights had passed, Elena could no longer remember why she was here. She got up and went into the garden, where she noticed a pair of red and white roses. "What beautiful flowers! How can you bloom in a place with little sunlight?" "Don't you know? There is a sun in the Overworld that can heal everything." "That's where we're from. We already absorbed enough sunlight, so we don't need it anymore." "In the Overworld, the sunlight is warm and cozy. You wouldn't ever want to come back here once you've experienced it." "It's nice here though. It's quiet and safe. Nobody can find us. You're the first..." Elena looked at the white and red roses. Suddenly, a memory came rushing back to her, like water bubbling out from a dry well. She remembered the roses that she and Kyle planted together, a memory she should not have forgotten. She could not stay here any longer. Determined to leave the garden, she went barefoot to the garden door where it was locked. With a gentle twist, she unlocked the rusty lock. She saw that the flowers outside the garden had withered, but everything inside was still luscious green. She ran away quickly until her legs were sore and tired. After making sure she wasn't followed, she stopped to catch her breath. "Ah, I've wasted so much time!"

1067676942.Text: (IV) The Bandit Girl After leaving the garden, Elena encountered a Crystalline Lizard with pitch-black scales, reflecting the image of her face. For some reason, the lizard didn't run away from Elena but started talking to her. Elena asked if it knows the whereabouts of her dear playmate. "I might have seen him... might have seen him. I remember a boy taken away by bandits. They were singing a happy song, the echoes of which can still be heard in the cave." "That must be Kyle." "That must be Kyle." "But those are bandits. They're too much for you to handle." But Elena was determined, and nothing could stop her. She begged the Crystalline Lizard to help her save Kyle. The lizard, impressed by her fervor of determination, stood up and gave her a gentleman's salute. Not knowing much about etiquette, Elena mimicked the lizard and saluted back. "Please call me Sir Raven. I will take you to him. My lover is with the bandits, so she knows all about them." "In the Underworld, there was a very clever bandit leader who read all the newspaper in the world, then forgot about them. But he never said a word. The truly clever were like that because they could never convey their thoughts at the speed that they were being processed in their mind," Sir Raven explained. "So, he needed someone to convey his orders, and that someone was the bandit girl." "The bandit girl?" Sir Raven led Elena outside the bandit's camp, where they were still singing joyous songs. Just as she was about to enter, Sir Raven stopped her, "You must wait, Elena. Wait until they have drunk. Wait until they are hoarse from singing. Wait until they stop dancing. Wait until they are asleep. Then we will go in and rescue your companion, then flip away." As the songs died down, another dark Crystalline Lizard appeared. She was Sir Raven's lover and definitely a charming beauty. They were a cute couple as they hugged and kissed. After hearing Elena's story, she also decided to help Elena. The bandits in the Underground hardly kept to a strict schedule. As instructed by their leader, it was a simple life that they led: eating, sleeping, singing, and robbing whenever they felt like it. As time passed, the sound of singing was replaced by snoring. It was time for Elena and the others to set out. They sneaked past the gatekeepers and patrols and reached the place where the bandits kept their loot. Their loot consisted of more than just goods. There were various underground organisms: Crystalline Lizards, Moles, and even a Ground Dragon, which was responsible for carrying the loot back to the bandit's camp. The organisms here were either kept in cages or bounded by robes, but they weren't what Elena was looking for. Deeper inside, she spotted a boy with blond hair sleeping on the floor. She rushed over and shook his shoulders, "Kyle! Wake up, Kyle!" The boy woke up from his slumber and turned to look at Elena. On closer inspection, Elena noticed he was plumper than Kyle. The boy screamed out in terror, which woke up the sleeping bandits, including the short and thin bandit girl. "Who are you guys? How dare you try and rob us?" The boy stood up and quickly stood to the side of the bandits. The girl called out to Elena, "This boy has decided not to return to the city. He'd rather live here with us and be a happy bandit. Give up." "Don't be angry with me. He's not the one I'm searching for." "Who are you and why are you here? Explain yourself." Elena explained everything that had happened and how much she liked Kyle. The bandit girl's gaze shifted from the bandits, then to the couple. She walked over to Elena, gently wiped her tears away, and said seriously: "Even if I was angry, they couldn't kill you, because I would have done so already. We're all tired today, so why don't you come sleep with me and my animals." After the other bandits went to rest, the bandit girl showed off the animals that she had imprisoned. These animals are an evil bunch. They'd betray you the first chance they'd get if they weren't locked up. She took out her dazzling dagger again and tickled the Ground Dragon's neck with the tip. "He's afraid of me tickling him with the dagger. This is what you have to do to make sure it doesn't escape." The pitiful dragon stomped in place, but dare not act rashly. The bandit girl laughed and dragged Elena to bed. "Do you always sleep with your dagger beside you?" "Of course," The bandit girl replied, "I always sleep with my dagger because you never know what will happen. Don't worry about that. Tell me again about Kyle and how you embarked on this journey alone." Elena told her story again. After hearing the story, the bandit girl was deep in thought. Ready to sleep, she put her arm around Elena while still holding onto her dagger. Elena whispered, "You don't have to lock up the animals. Although they won't leave you while locked, they won't love you anyway." After a while, a mole in a cage nearby called out to Elena, "Elena! Elena! I saw the boy you mentioned in the Overworld..." "Ah! Where?" "No, there were no signs of happiness or sadness in his eyes. It's as if his heart had turned to ice. I don't think he can feel the cold." "My poor Kyle, I will go find him in the Overworld..." Elena said as tears fell down her face. "Those fragments come from the devil's mirror." The bandit girl that Elena thought was asleep sat up. She added, "The devil's mirror was shattered into countless pieces. If a fragment gets into a person's eye, they will turn evil and their hearts will turn to ice." Elena seemed to remember what had happened. "The fragments have thrown the Underworld into chaos. The fragments would use the human body as a breeding ground and spread to the rest of the body. As a result, people lost their happiness and sadness, turning into crazy beasts and criminals that'd wander the streets. We didn't want to turn into that, so we left. I advise you not to go back either. Why not stay here with me and become a happy bandit? "I'm sorry. I can't turn my back on Kyle, knowing his heart has been turned to ice. I have to find him." The bandit girl fell silent. After a while, she got up and went over to the mole's cage. "You know how to get to the Snow Queen's palace?" The mole nodded nervously. "I'm going to open this cage and let you out. You will dig your way hard enough to the Overworld, but you have to make sure this little girl can follow you in your tunnel. Take her to the Snow Queen's Palace, where her companion is." The mole happily leaves its cage and hugs Elena, who is crying from tears of joy. The bandit girl pointed at Elena, "I don't like how you're crying when you should be happy. Take this bread and leave with the mole, do it while everyone is still asleep. You're such a great girl that I'm curious to see what the boy you've been searching for is like. Don't ever come back again, live a good life in the Overworld!" The bandit girl avoided Elena's gaze and handed Elena her dagger. She turned to the mole and said, "Hurry and go, you must take good care of this girl." Elena accepted the dagger and hugged her, "Goodbye!" With that, she followed the mole quickly into the tunnel towards the Overworld. After making sure Elena had left, the bandit girl turned to her animals, "I'm going to set all of you free now, so you won't think me unfair. You better leave quickly, before I change my mind." The bandit girl released all the animals and started crying. The animals didn't leave, because they liked her. Watching from the shadows, the bandit leader walked over and the bandit girl threw herself into his arms and cried even harder.

-498406999.Text: (V) Snow Queen's Palace It got colder as they got to the surface. The soil became harder to dig through, so the mole had no choice but to stop sometimes. Elena couldn't bear to see the mole in pain, so she tried to warm up its claws with her warm breath. But even so, they were still far away from the surface, and the mole couldn't dig anymore. The frigid cold made the soil as hard as metal. There was no way of knowing how much longer it would take to reach the surface. Elena took out the bread the bandit girl gave to her and offered it to the mole, pleading for its help. The mole said to her: "My lovely Elena, you already have the power to overcome everything. You walked barefoot across this world and moved many people and animals you don't know to help you. Your power lies in the heart of yours, which is full of naive enthusiasm and love. If you still cannot reach the Snow Queen and remove the fragment from Kyle, then none of us can." Elena said goodbye to the mole, who dug a small burrow and fell asleep. Elena had to dig the rest of the way herself. She tried to rub her hands and use that warmth to thaw frozen soil, then chip it away with her dagger. The process was slow, but little by little, she made her way to the surface. Again and again, blood flowed from her fingers, froze, and fell off. Only Elena knew how painful that was. As time passed, Elena felt the chill in her bones and hunger so bad she could eat soil. At last, a clean translucent block of ice appeared in front of her. It was different from the soil she had been digging away. Looking through the ice, Elena could make out the domed shape of the ice and a snowflake pattern. "This must be the palace of the Snow Queen. Kyle must be in there." After finally reaching the palace, Elena discovered that the wall is made of ice. Strong auroras illuminated the countless houses covered in white. Some towered into the clouds, while others stretched for miles. Never had Elena seen anything like it in the Underground. But there was not a single sign of life. The Snow Queen's Palace was empty and vast, sitting atop a frozen lake, which had been split into thousands of pieces, each of which is the same size and shape. The Snow Queen called it the "Mirror of Reason" and believed it to be the best mirror in the world. And Kyle was out there, doing something with the ice in vain. His body was not as much blue as black with cold, but with his ice solid heart and a kiss from the Snow Queen, he could no longer shiver. All he cared about was creating a pattern -- the most complicated pattern in the world. In the game of ice blocks called reason. For Kyle, the ice blocks were beautiful and important. The fact that he couldn't create the pattern "Eternity" that he wanted was probably because of the fragment in his eyes. The Snow Queen never forgot what Kyle said about her. "If she dared enter, I'll make her sit on the warm furnace and she'll melt." She didn't take Kyle away to let him live a happy life. She said to him, "If you can create this pattern, then you'll be your own master. I will give you the world and a pair of ice boots as a present." But he couldn't do it. There were still some volcanoes in the world that had not yet been completely covered by snow. "I must go and see if I can make those pale jars a little... darker," said the Snow Queen. So the Snow Queen was absent when Elena finally reached the palace. It was only Kyle continuing with his game of ice blocks called reason. He was unmoved even when Elena came to him in tears. "Kyle, my beloved Kyle! I've finally found you!" However, Kyle remained indifferent to her presence. Instead, he continued playing with his ice blocks, unable to complete the pattern of "Eternity." Elena threw herself on top of him and held him down, but he was motionless as if he were unaware of her presence. Elena's burning tears fell onto Kyle's chest and seeped into his cold heart, melting the fragment. He slowly turned around and looked at Elena. "He finally reacted to me, but it's not enough!" Elena thought of what the flowers in the garden told her: the sunlight of the Overworld could heal everything. But the sunlight could not reach this place, so she started singing a hymn praising the sunlight: Roses grow rich and fragrant in the valley, Where we met the great sage Plamya. Tears were overflowing from Kyle's eyes. He cried so hard that even the broken fragment's powder in his eyes flew out. It was only then that he recognized his beloved Elena. He cried out happily: "Elena, my beloved Elena! Where have you been all this time? Look at your feet, naked and blue with cold! Look at your hands full of wounds! Ah! Where am I?" Kyle looks around and exclaimed, "It's so cold and empty here!" He hugged Elena tightly. At that moment, Elena was the happiest she had ever been. She laughed and cried tears of joy while dancing in the Snow Queen's Palace. Even the ice blocks around them joined in on the joyous occasion. They laughed and danced until they fell exhausted to the ground. While lying on the ground, it was by coincidence that the two of them formed the pattern "Eternity." As Snow Queen once promised, Kyle became his own master and owned the whole world and a pair of ice boots. Elena kissed Kyle and life returned to his face. She kissed his eyes and the brilliance in his eyes shined once again. She kissed his hands and feet, and they were dexterous once again. The Snow Queen could come back at any moment, but the proof of Kyle's freedom was gleaming on the ice. At that moment, the mole that helped Elena earlier appeared from the ground. It brought a message from the bandit girl: "Don't come back to the Underground. The fragments are still searching for pure souls. The Underground is full of crazy beasts and criminals. Your grandmother has long passed away, so you have nothing to return to! Go to the Overworld and live under the sunlight! The Snow Queen won't dare visit there, for it is illuminated by Plamya's light." Kyle and Elena were devastated by the news of her grandmother's death, but they had to leave for Belobog. As they headed for Belobog in the blizzard, snowflakes followed them close to the ground, growing larger and more terrifying as they came closer. They were sentries for the Snow Queen, turning into indescribable monsters. The two started singing hymns. The temperature was so low that Elena saw her own breath wrapping around her neck before dispersing. It made her feel better. By the time they finished singing, the two were enveloped in fog, as if protected by angels. When they arrived at the gates of Belobog, the Consul personally welcomed the two children. Behind the Consul, the church choir was singing, "Those that seek to be sheltered by the sunlight must be pure like children." Their mission is to provide the best for the children. As they entered Belobog, they could hear the clocks ticking. They found themselves turning into adults and began their happy life in the Overworld. The adventure from before seemed like a dream. They had completely forgotten about the Snow Queen and the cold empty palace, and the Underworld that was then the Devil's Den. They sat on the bench in front of their new home and looked at each other. It was then that they understood the meaning of the hymn: Roses grow rich and fragrant in the valley, Where we met the great sage Plamya. They were already adults, but they remained children - at heart. It was at the moment when the sun shined the brightest in Belobog.

1423907301.Text: February 28, 699 UE Do the people at the Intellectual Property Administration of Technology understand anything? Can't they hire someone competent for the patent review? I've applied for a patent 16 times already. What a waste of time and energy! How will they reimburse me if someone registers my invention first? Time is life. They're my money, ruining my life. I've been working on this device for quite some time. But two months ago, when the museum opened an exhibit showcasing newly discovered Old World relics, I was 100% sure that time travel might come true. There was a mysterious device called the "600-Kilowatt Diesel Generator," which was exactly the machine that appeared in my dreams. With that machine, my device would surely be able to warp time, deformation, massive breaks, and create time tunnels. The prophetic dream perfectly confirmed my theory. In my dream, there was a Geomarrow resonance tunnel hidden in the Underworld. With the completion of my device, I could achieve time travel! Rather than waiting for those fools to review my patent, why don't I raise the money myself to build the device? Right now, I only have the house to mortgage, but it belongs to my father. I don't know if he'll agree on the mortgage, but I'm a little reluctant to do so since we've lived there for many years... No no, what am I saying? If I can create a time tunnel, I'd transform the era! I'd be the modern-day Plamya! How would I shed tears on a house? Let's do it. I'll mortgage the house next week and get my device built. Oh my, I could revolutionize the Union... No, the entire universe!

-142176640.Text: March 4, 699 UE Even my father doesn't understand me. Why did I have to be born into this family? Can't bear it. It's fine if he didn't want to sell the house, but why did he have to kick me out? I've devoted my life to science... I have no idea how to take care of myself! It's a waste of my abilities to learn those menial tasks! I read in the newspaper that there would be a salon next week for civilian scientists. There may be investors there, so I must go to secure funding. But will the investors understand the main principles behind time tunnels in such a short amount of time? They'd need to do some research beforehand, at least understand the Big Universe Time Tunnel Theory. I'll ask Daniel about the possible investors. If I can get their address, I'll visit them in advance.

-1708260581.Text: March 12, 699 UE\n\nI won't go to those salons anymore. Now all I have to do is wait for investors to contact me. Hopefully, some of them wise up and recognize my talents. I am the only one there who understands the science. But if this doesn't work out, I'll just become a normal undergrounder.

1020622774.Text: March 21, 699 UE Everything, just perishes. Nobody really respects science. I thought Daniel from the "Crystal Daily" had a bit of talent, but oh how I was wrong. When I first went to see him, he was able to have a serious discussion with me. He pointed out some flaws with my theory that were reasonable. It's normal for minor flaws to occur, but what's more important was that I found someone who shared my passion for science. Every month, I'd go to see him to discuss the latest ideas. Where else would he find such a good friend? Hmm. How interesting. Now he's finding excuses to avoid me. What? He can't keep up with me anymore? I don't really mind that he can't. Last time, I asked him to get me the list of investors. Something so simple, and yet he still hasn't gotten back to me yet. Isn't his job to know these things? It's not like I was asking him about physics. I camped outside the newspaper office for two days and finally found him. But when he saw me, it was as if he had seen an undergrounder. He scolded me. Untalented liar without morals! I thought he was my friend! I'll never come to you for anything else. Just wait, you'll regret your decision. Even now, no investors have contacted me. I'm beginning to suspect the academic salon was just a cover. It seems I need to secure the funding myself somehow. Is there any hope left for the academic world?

-901691527.Text: "This is the content of a letter that was pieced back together." Miss Lila, I hope you will keep this letter safe. Maybe it'll lift curtains of a nice story on everybody's lips. In the month that we've been working together, I'd just say it was too short. The first thing that I noticed about you was your beauty. You are more beautiful than most other women, but not quite as beautiful as my first love. But that's not important. Your real charm is your down-to-earth work attitude, which is the quality a woman in the Union should have in this new era. The earnest way in which you prepared for interview articles is charming. I was captivated by your clear organization and thoughtful questions. If I were conducting the interview, I would have been more thorough, especially with the improvisation and humor. In the future, feel free to communicate with me and I'd be more than happy to give you constructive feedback, making your work easier. I heard your magazine, Voyager, was a young media company that was only recently established. Plenty of challenges await in the future. An unwed woman like yourself will surely need an outstanding partner to face those challenges together. Allow me to re-introduce myself. For three consecutive years, I've been voted as the backbone of the magazine, "Crystal Daily." Many colleagues call me "the one who is most daring to speak out." Of course, in our line of work, that is a good quality to have. In my spare time, I participate in various sports. I'm exceptionally good at long-distance running and have won many rewards for the suburban snow cross-country. I think you'll find that there aren't many men like me. However, if you do like me, please don't become too attached. Plamya guides our love, and we must do our utmost for the Union. What kind of love would require us to sacrifice our job? What kind of love would have us be filled with guilt and shame? Please respond within 7 days of reading this later. Otherwise, I'll be too busy to (the rest of the torn letter is missing)

1827191828.Text: Law Enforcement Records Time of Event:16:22, March 9, 699 UE Report Method:Discovered by the patrol on duty Time of Arrival:16:22, March 9, 699 UE Person: Gender:/ Age:/ Contact Info:/ Report Contents: (Brief) While on duty, the patrol discovered a man in ragged clothes had broken into a home. Judging by his appearance, the man is around 50 years old. He was digging downward the ground with tools and claimed to be building a safe cellar. The man's mental state seemed unstable with bleeding hands and protruding joint bones. The man didn't stop digging even after serious warnings by the patrol. Processing: Two patrols subdued the man and brought him to the garrison. He was questioned once his mental state was stable. The man called himself Eugene. There are no records of this man in neither the resident registration forms nor the Statistics' record, ruling out the possibility he's a Union resident. The man's statements were confusing and contradictory with madness. Preliminary investigation determined the person was from the Underground. While mining, their crew of three members discovered a "fissure" that appeared out of thin air. The crew entered, only to be attacked by monsters. This man was somehow the only survivor and escaped to Belobog. He believed that monsters would continue to attack from the "fissure," so he broke into a residence for shelter. However, he still felt unsafe and began digging underground with his miner tools to build a safe cellar. Damages (dangers): The resident's door and ground were damaged. No valuables were taken. Processing result: 1. Criminal Case ( ) 2. Public Security Case ( ) 3. Civil Dispute Case ( ) 4. Rescue Services ( ) 5. Other (√) Guard on Patrol (Signature): Ethan Gepard Notes: 1. This "Lost One" must be kept under close supervision at the Garrison Headquarters. 2. Everyone related to this incident must keep strict confidentiality. 3. Reinforce the guards and keep a close eye on the "fissures" in Belobog. I don't want a repeat of this incident.

261107887.Text: "This is an advertisement flyer from a diner that closed down." The Legends of the "Snapper Jam Appetizer" The Eternal Freeze was imminent and the Union had not yet been formally established. Plamya summoned workers from across the country for the largest insulation project ever in human history. This enormous layer of insulation would prepare Belobog for the endless blizzard. At that time, a young worker by the name of Nicholas was good at cooking. Not only had he mastered all kinds of traditional dishes, but also had developed many new ones. Every weekend, workers would bring over food to his home, so that he could prepare a big meal for everyone. Every worker would leave his home satisfied with a full stomach. However, it wasn't long until they wanted to taste his delicious food during the work days. "Is there any way to eat a delicious meal on a cold construction site?" Food would quickly turn cold and hard due to the weather conditions on a construction site. It didn't taste as good as dishes fresh out of the pot. The heating technology at the time also wasn't as developed. The smart Nicholas believed this to be a major problem, one that almost all workers would encounter. Solving it meant that he could help everyone. Thus, he began working on the ultimate dish that could overcome the cold. Nicholas spent countless hours trying different dishes at home. He tried various combinations, but none of the results satisfied him. One day, he was mentally exhausted and hadn't realized the fried fish was already cold. On top of that, he accidentally knocked the fried fish into the pot, cooking it together with jam. By the time he realized, it was already too late. He put the dish aside, ready to throw it out the next day. However, when he woke up the next day, he decided to give it a taste. To his surprise, it was delicious! He had come up with an ultimate cold dish, one that workers could eat in the cold weather. Thanks to Nicholas' promotion, Belobog workers can enjoy a delicious lunch, motivating them to work harder. The news spread to Plamya, who tasted his unique dish and praised his innovation. Plamya named the dish "Snapper Jam Appetizer." Afterward, Nicholas made further improvements to the dish. It is now the most famous dish for Belobog gatherings. Many doodles on the page: Making up stories Write a novel? Consul's secret visit It was around 100 UE when they cultivated the Redsunset fruit for jam that could withstand the cold So that's more useless knowledge Nice

-1304976054.Text: 26 The Traveler Who Survived the Blizzard ① Thomas Reading Tips This is a poem about the beginning of the Eternal Freeze. With the coming of the Ice Age, winds grew stronger and the temperature dropped even further. People would traverse through the blizzard from all over the world, so they could reach the final light of human civilization, Belobog. The poem likens Belobog to a warm harbor at the end of a journey, inspiring migrants to keep going. Read the poem carefully to see how the author sees through the eye of migrants to show a positive attitude and confidence towards the future, despite fears of uncertainty. Push through the wall of fog A faint light in the distance, In the drifting snow, It seemed to disappear. The migrant walks alone in the cold, On the dead frozen snow path, The weary bells, Ring helplessly. A song in the distance, Can be heard echoing from the past: It was the vibration of strings, It was the reverberation of drums... No more faint lights, No more black walls, Everything was in ruins... All that I encountered was, The stone pillars that marked my path... Sorrow, anxiety... Tomorrow, my family, I will go to the warm harbor, Celebrate the end of my journey, Rest my stiff bones in bed. We walk to the distant lights Reflect the outline of Belobog, Shake off the weariness, For we will not despair. Oh my family, our journey has been long, The strings and drums fall silent, Only the weary bell rings in the wind, In the direction where the light once appeared. ① From "Selected Poems of Thomas." Katana Thomas (66 - 12 PUE) was a famous poet. At the end of 47 PUE, Thomas was part of a refugee migration from Novgofia that traversed the snow to reach Belobog. Their arrival was warmly received by Plamya, who described it as the "Great Voyage", bringing with it culture, art, and technology that are still influential in the Union today. Thomas' poem, known as the "poem of battle," has inspired other refugees who migrated with him.

1067676941.Text: "This is a page cut out from the 'Voyager' magazine." Keeping up with fishing parties How about some juicy Frosted Salmon Bread Reporter: Lila The lake in the Belobog suburbs may be frozen solid, but hidden beneath the surface is a powerful undercurrent. That's right! It's the undercurrent caused by a large amount of Frosted Salmon in search of a spawning ground! It is also the time of year that they are the fattest, because they stock up the energy and nutrients to prepare for the swim upstream, during which they are unable to feed. The Union fishing teams head out to prepare for the seasonal hunts. Through efficient and large-scale hunting, the country's Frosted Salmon reserves are plentiful. Of course, the proximity of the hunting ground to Belobog means the extreme temperature and climate have their risks. Such a grand scale of fishing can only be read through the articles. But, starting this year, you can also be a part of the fishing parties! Three years ago, the Union approved the "Fishing and Hunting Co-op Program." After a two-year trial run, they're finally ready to open up registrations. As one of the members who participated in the trial run, I will be starting a new column, "Fishing and Hunting Co-op", in the "Voyager" magazine. The column will cover travel preparation, delicious foods, and various activities, as well as aspects of the Co-op Program that may require special attention. As the saying goes, "the way to a man's heart is through his stomach." For the first issue, we will be focusing on the unique delicacy, Frosted Salmon Bread. Everyone knows that the Frosted Salmon we get in the city has been processed to prolong preservation. After the head and guts are removed, the salmon is salted and dried. This process brings out the flavor that we are so familiar with. However, the salmon at the reserves taste even better. The frosted salmon will freeze quickly out of the water, but you can't get that freshness anywhere else. The most traditional frosted salmon dish, which is highly recommended by the head chef of the fishing parties, is the Frosted Salmon Bread. It is simple to make, so here are the steps: You will need to have sliced your bread beforehand. It will take a lot of effort to cut it in this cold climate. You will also need to prepare the Redsunset jam beforehand. Cut the Redsunset into pieces along with various berries, then stir with seasoning. Be sure to store it in a thermal container, or you will have to melt the jam with hot water, which will be difficult. Cut the Frosted Salmon into slices. You can ask the chefs from the fishing team to help with this step. Next, you will need to peel off the skin, then place the fish meat and jam onto the bread. And there! You'll have an authentic Frosted Salmon Bread. As you may have noticed, the hardest part of the recipe is the fresh Frosted Salmon, which may be hard to come by in Belobog. You'll find plenty of it at the fishing reserves though. The salmon's freshness and the jam's acidity produce a smooth texture that will have you salivating within seconds. The tenderness of the fish, with a lighter fish stench and more fatty meat, is different from the dry salmon that we're used to. It is truly unforgettable and definitely worth a trip to the reserves. Of course, the fishing parties have a minimum amount of salmon that they must catch before tourism activities can begin. Rest assured, there are plenty of Frosted Salmon. The co-op program will only require a one-time fee, which means tourists participated are free to eat as much as they want within the fishing reserves. Next time, we'll be taking a look at how to stay warm in the frigid cold. Stay tuned, avid travelers. This is Lila and I'll see you in the next episode.

-498407000.Text: Touched by God's Grace: "Plamya" Premiere Spectacle and Behind-the-Scene Published in the "Crystal Daily" on January 20, 700 UE. Reporter: Anna On January 13, 700 UE, Mr. George, in response to a charity fundraiser organized by the Union government, premiered the once-rehearsed "Plamya" in the new concert hall in Central Belobog. The poster for the show read, "For the benefit of poor criminals and the interest of Saint Grace Hospital." In an interview with the "Daily Crystal," he said, "All proceeds from the show will go to charity. It will be split equally between the welfare society for criminals and Saint Grace Hospital." The performance was conducted by George himself. The entire church choir was in attendance. It was an unprecedented event, evident by the tickets being sold out the day it was available. The new concert hall, with 1,500 seats fully booked for the premiere, was unable to meet the demand. On the day of the premiere, the concert hall asked ladies "better not to wear bustle dresses" and gentlemen "better not to carry swords," so that there would be more room for the audience. 1,800 guests crammed into the concert hall, with many of them sitting on the steps of the walkway, to watch the premiere of "Plamya." The performance was a sensation, and famous music critic Borovsky praised, "Words cannot express the shock and joy that this amazing performance brought to the full house." On the day after a premiere, Crystal Daily devoted a full page to "Plamya" and its performance. It praised, "Exquisite, grand and gentle. We had to search through the dictionary for the most elegant and vivid words to describe the joys our ears and heart felt." As promised, after deducting the expenses for the orchestra and the concert hall, George donated all the remaining proceeds to the welfare society and Saint Grace Hospital. On January 19, the Union residents' demand for a second show intensified. At the request of the Union government and Consul Cocolia, an encore of "Plamya" was quickly arranged and performed at the Golden Theater (the new concert hall had other shows arranged). The tickets were available on January 18 and sold out in half a day. At that time, many conservatives thought an oratorio was inappropriate for the theater. Consul Cocolia silenced the outmoded by attending the show in person. On the evening of the 19th, when the orchestra played "Plamya," the Consul couldn't contain her excitement and stood up to pay her tribute. When she did so, the whole audience followed suit. Some critics think this will become an unspoken rule that the audience will have to stand for this part of the show in the future. After the show, Consul Cocolia personally took to the stage to thank George. It is reported that the proceeds of the second show, after deducting expenses, were donated to the orphanage. According to the "Crystal Daily," Mr. George imparts a truly sublime and pure meaning to the work he conducts.

1423907296.Text: Rye Bread Soda Iceberg At the beginning of the Eternal Freeze, many migrants thought there wouldn't be a need for cold snacks in Belobog. But soon, they couldn't resist the frozen delight. Belobog's central heating warmed every room of every household, and the heat made the air dry. Who wouldn't want to eat a frozen delight after a hearty steaming meal? Eating that frozen delight indoors will hit all the right spots. Eating it outdoors? Well, that's a whole other sensation! One of the classic Union frozen delights is the Rye Bread Soda Iceberg. Rye Bread Soda is almost as old as the Union. At the time, resources were limited and the Union wanted to make full use of everything without wasting anything. In 73 UE, a Union food committee, not wanting the surplus bread to go to waste, suggested an idea to several bakeries to create another product using the bread. This was surely a Union's model for making the most of resources. The result was the Rye Bread Soda, created by processing the bread in various ways. It's been proclaimed as an iconic drink that even children can enjoy. In recent years, there is a new soda on the market, called Rye Bread Soda Iceberg, based on the Rye Bread Soda formula. This new soda is quite popular among young people. It is common to see young couples outside a dessert shop eating an iceberg with small spoons. Rye Bread Soda Iceberg is easy to make and the ingredients are simple. Let's learn how to make it at home! Rye Bread Soda Iceberg Ingredients Rye Bread Soda: 800 ml Moo Yogurt: 330 ml (one bottle) Sugar: 10g Steps to Make Rye bread Soda Iceberg 1. Pour the Rye Bread Soda into an airtight bag and leave it outside overnight to freeze. 2. Use special tools to shave the iced soda onto the plate and form an iceberg. 3. Pour the Moo Yogurt into a bowl and stir with sugar. Add jam of your liking to the mixture. 4. Pour the mixture from the top of your dark iceberg to form a sweet and sour layer and you're done! Tips 1. Feel free to replace the Rye Bread Soda with any other sweet beverage. It will taste the best with Rye Bread Soda though! 2. Please finish the iceberg as soon as possible if you are indoor. If you're worried about it melting too fast, you can add small amounts of well-cooked animal collagen to the mixture. You won't have this problem if you eat it outdoors!

-142176645.Text: Bellerain Petroleum Jelly for extra protection. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly has always been your best partner when you march ahead through the blizzard. Lock in moisture and warmth with this jelly, which is usable by anyone anywhere. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly is your guardian of skin moisture. Apply a thin layer of Bellerain Petroleum Jelly to lock in moisture. The jelly is made with non-irritants, making it usable by almost everyone, even those sensitive to alcohol and perfume. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly is your protector of warmth. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly is effective at preventing the loss of body temperature, even bare skin exposed to the air. Our jelly will provide a thin layer of insulation for your journey into the cold. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly is your doctor of small wounds. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly does not provide anti-inflammatory relief, but it can still help heal small wounds by insulating the wound from the air and germs. This will allow the skin to heal faster, not leaving behind any scars. The dry air can cause nasal capillaries to rupture, but a thin layer of our jelly can be applied to prevent further bleeding. Bellerain Petroleum Jelly is your friend of the blizzard. In 376 UE, a Union worker discovered the by-product of burning Geomarrow had a small analgesic effect on burns and cuts. Twelve years later, Head Apothecary Professor Robert refined the Geomarrow remnants into the Bellerain Petroleum Jelly we know today and applied it to the fields of medicine and cosmetics, and began the "Petroleum Jelly Age". The Bellerain brand was founded in 390 UE and has been protecting the skin of the Union people for over 300 years. We are confident that it will continue to protect you and your family for the years to come.

-1708260586.Text:
This is an excerpt from 'Winterlands Evening News'

Recently, the "Crystal Daily" has completed their Union residents satisfaction survey for this year. Based on the results, we've analyzed various factors that influence life in the Union.

(I) Work Satisfaction - Relatively High

In our work satisfaction survey, 75.6% seemed to be satisfied.

Most of the positions in the Union are allocated collectively, which guarantees stable employment for everyone. The work environment is safe and healthy, with almost no overtime and peer pressure.

The apprenticeship training method has provided professionals craftsmen for the Union, ensuring its smooth operations. However, due to the closed-off skillset training, talent crossovers and migrations are extremely rare.

In an interview, someone said, "When I was a student, I didn't know anything about career planning. I started working at the factory right after graduation. People there taught me everything I needed like I was their son. I wouldn't say that I like this job. Rather, it's the only thing I'm good at, so I'll probably end up doing it for the rest of my life."

(I) Family Satisfaction - Relatively Low

In our family satisfaction survey, only 49.2% seemed to be satisfied.

The dissatisfaction comes mostly from marriages. Many people stated that they had been dating their partners for only a brief amount of time before they got married. After the marriage, they realized there was a conflict in personality and values. This problem is quite common among young couples, which might be related to the little social interactions young people have with those of the opposite gender.

Response from the interviewee: "After I got married, there were many things that I found great about him, but there were things that I couldn't stand as well. It's not to say that I can't stand him, but I can't say that I'm satisfied."

(III) Physical and Mental Satisfaction - Relatively High

In our physical and mental satisfaction survey, physical satisfaction was the highest, reaching 87.7%, while mental satisfaction reached 80.1%.

Physical satisfaction comes from the abundant sporting events available throughout the year. Over a hundred sporting events are hosted by companies, and people of all ages are encouraged to participate. Spiritual satisfaction comes from the efforts of Plamya Church. Many people attribute their spiritual health to faith.

Response from the interviewee: "I can feel the sage's charm as if a great spirit has been passed down. I feel like I have to contribute to it."

1020622769.Text:
Recently, the psychiatric department has received a lot of patients suffering from poor sleep quality. The common causes were related to stress from work and life.

Normally, I'd ask patients to record their dreams, which I would then analyze. Through the Dreamland, patients could learn from their mental experience, something they wouldn't have done otherwise. I've found this method helpful for treating the illness.

Some would believe that dreams are meaningless, but I don't agree. Dreams can stir up people's feelings and emotions, which, at the very least, indicates a connection with the person's feelings or experiences.

But what exactly are these dream metaphors? The Old World literature is incomplete, and I don't have a definitive answer. What's interesting is that there is a link between the Dreamland and reality. In the past, people have used the Dreamland to predict the future.

I remain skeptical about the saying that dreams can predict.

A "deja vu" is when a person feels like something that is currently happening has already happened before. I believe that this is not an exact dream, but a dreamlike feeling which is just as mysterious as dreams.

But "Dreamland inheritance" is a possibility.

I previously had a patient who recorded some sort of ritual she dreamed. As far as I know, this ritual only existed in the past. Nobody around her knew anything about the ritual, so how did she know it? Thus, I came concluded that Dreamland might be genetic.

I want to gather more Dreamland records from patients to prove my hypothesis.

Madam Consul asked me before if there was any truth to the Dreamland. I couldn't give her an exact answer with my intuition and memories mixed.

We can only admit that we know too little about the Dreamland.

-901691532.Text: Time Change Notice For Oratorio Rehearsal To all members of the Union choir: The oratorio rehearsal for "Plamya" next Tuesday has been rescheduled due to the heating device maintenance in the church. Please read the following: 1. The chorus rehearsal, originally scheduled to start at 9:30, has been changed to 13:30 due to maintenance. The church will not provide lunch for this rehearsal. 2. The orchestra will not participate in this rehearsal because all instruments have been moved for maintenance. Please wait for further notices. 3. The tenor, Frank, has been hospitalized due to an accident. The first part of the Oratorio, "Warm the People" and "Devil, Go Back to Hades!" will not be rehearsed. 4. As the date for the official performance draws near, rehearsal time will be extended by three to four hours. The Union has arranged for transportation to ensure all members reach home safely. For those that live far from the church, you may apply for accommodations at the Goethe Hotel. Inform each other and make sure everyone has read the notice. Please make the proper arrangements. May the light of Plamya shine on us. Union Choir Committee December 22, 699 UE

1827191823.Text: For foodies! Belobog sausages: which type is your favorite? 100 years have passed since the Belobog Meat Factory first introduced sausages. Since then, it has become a familiar taste in every Belobog household. When it comes to eating the Belobog sausages, everyone has their own opinion. We've compiled a list of your favorite ways to eat it. What's your favorite? The first way is to eat it directly. The sausage itself is already cooked and has been processed in many ways. It can be eaten as a snack or as a side dish. The second way is to cook it with hammer peppers. The sausage can be stir-fried with many vegetables, but hammer peppers can add on a strong spiciness that enhances the flavor. The third way is to grill it. Cut across the sausage before putting it on the grill and adding spices. It's great as street food. The fourth way is to wrap it in suncakes. Make a suncake wrap by adding thin slices of the sausage. The two flavors will balance out, which is worth the taste! Over the years, upgrades to the process and machinery have greatly improved its production capacity. The fat but not greasy characteristic is unique to this sausage. Its original delicious taste has been retained, and various flavors are available for consumers to choose from. To celebrate the Union centenary, Belobog Meat Factory is offering discounts on all products to all customers. Welcome to the store and try the products!

261107882.Text: [Extract from "Crystal Daily - Weekend Edition"] Many friends believe that church literature mostly consists of scripture and dogma. However, that's not the case. There are also many works of literature with high practicality and readability. Here are five must-read church classics, all of which are sold in major bookstores and churches in the Union. 1. "Banner of the Slums" The prominent church activist Abel wrote about his missionary efforts in the slums. It is a testament to the positivity that faith can bring to people. In the book, he describes how many times he's helped teenagers improve themselves. He taught them to control their minds and focus on faith, instead of poverty and strife. On subsequent visits, he found that most of the teenagers had been lifted from poverty, and 30% of them had started working in the church. Abel was not surprised by the result and hoped that he could use the word of faith to further help the lost souls. 2. "Taking Control of Your Life" This book is well-known in the church. Although it was highly recommended, very few people actually read it. These fables were written by Kuzma over 300 years ago. However, the book in modern times can still inspire readers. It is worth mentioning that Kuzma was a firm non-believer. The book was also a journey through his life to find a purpose. He was influenced by Plamya's teachings and became a devout person. The content of the book is quite raw, but you'll quickly realize that it is a masterpiece that should be preserved forever. 3. "City of Plamya" This book has a unique perspective that showcases Plamya's spirit throughout the city. The author, Berg, extracted from literature twelve places that Plamya frequented to talk about the changes of time. Time has moved on, but the spirit of Plamya remains in those places. By reading this book, it feels like she is still living amongst us. 4. "Repentance Memo" First published nearly 500 years ago, the autobiography of the former priest Brady has always been a bestseller. The book is a poignant account of his turbulent childhood and his misdeeds before becoming a priest. In this autobiography, Brady sincerely expresses the meticulous details of his life. As a literary book of the times, it has received high praises in the literary world. Instead of drawing criticism, his sketchy past became a distinctive part of his priesthood. It is widely praised as an inspirational book, giving many people the courage to change from now. 5. "Enlightening" A collection of the most acclaimed speeches by "Mouthy" Dominic, taken from his weekly Union Church speeches. Much like the title of the book, each of Dominic's speech were powerful and inspiring. This power comes from the passion of his writings and his perfect interpretation of Plamya's spirit. He had a knack for throwing in a bit of humor in his otherwise lively case study, which always had a sudden profound insight. It's no wonder why he always had a packed house! Dominic may be retired now, but he still gives church lectures in his spare time. If you are interested in his speeches after reading this book, visit the Union Church to experience the wisdom of enlightenment.

-1304976059.Text:
Between October 9 and 12, Zeleni Basin Forest Development Retrospective Meeting was successfully held in Novgofia. The meeting was hosted by World Energy Development Association and organized by the Northern Nycatha Union University. It focused on the review and reflection of the disappearing Zeleni Basin Forest. Its participants included over 500 experts from 69 different countries. 121 expert shared reports on the conference.

Conference host Professor Paul began with a review of the positive aspects of developing the Zeleni Basin Rainforest. It was a difficult decision, but one that had to be made for the survival of humanity. The available resources were used to the full extent possible without any waste.

A century ago, the Zeleni Basin Rainforest was known as the "Green Eye." They discovered a small amount of oil beneath the basin, but the costs of ecological destruction outweighed the energy yield. At the time, experts and scholars from all over the world agreed to not develop the rainforest, and they collected and stored genes of the rainforest' various species. Half a century later, the energy crisis worsened. The world had not yet completed the transition to green energy and was still heavily reliant on oil. In the face of a greater crisis, the world government had no choice but to carry out the irreversible energy development of the last tropical rainforest. Drilling operations were established and the land was used to grow biofuel, which eased the oil demand.

Professor Marconi pointed out that such development only accelerated the energy crisis. The irreversible damage to the rainforest had a profound impact on the global climate. For half a century, the world used more energy to fight climate change than what the oil reserves in the rainforest yielded. Countries around the world failed to anticipate the effects of ecological destruction, let alone come up with countermeasures. The current technology was incapable of restoring the ecological function of the rainforest, which has resulted in a massive nutrient loss in the soil and plummeting biofuel production. The decline in oxygen has also demanded countries consume large amounts of energy to produce oxygen.

Professor Goodman, an energy expert, added that biofuels, such as corn and sugar cane, were too inefficient to replace oil and other energy sources. In the long term, biofuels should be abandoned to avoid soil erosion, at least until more efficient conversion technologies are available.

Other issues, such as ecology, species, climate, were also discussed during the meeting. 56 papers were selected by reviewers, among which 12 were recommended to be included in the journal, "Energy and Future."

1067676936.Text:
To my dear butler Bellis:

I have already settled all of Lady Bronya's work arrangements for this week and will send you the itinerary for the next day every night. Please follow the time arrangements and remind Lady Bronya to complete the tasks.
I understand that Lady Bronya is still angry with me and probably does not wish to see me, but I'm sure she will come around soon enough. The time of her inheritance of the Consul's position is near, she must get used to a life of discipline and planning. That is the only way she'll be able to become a successful ruler like Madam Cocolia. It is necessary for me, Lady Bronya's Sensei Assistant, to be strict with her sometimes. This is the only way to reign in her temper and to make her understand the seriousness of things.

There's no need to worry about me, I can take care of most of my work even from home. Besides, it's a good thing to be able to rest for a few days. Please see to it that you take good care of Lady Bronya, she's a stubborn young lady, but she's kind and gifted. You should know that better than anyone. One day, she will be grateful for what we've done for her, which I can assure you.

Your truest friend,
Sonya
January 16, 700 UE

Attachment: Lady Bronya's itinerary, January 17, 700 UE

06:00 Wake up
06:10-06:20  Quick shower
06:20-06:45  Breakfast; the butler announces arrangements of the day
06:45-07:45  Morning combat training (See Lady Bronya's morning lesson curriculum and instructor schedule for details)
07:45-08:30  Bath, image management, make up, wardrobe
08:30-09:00  Head to the Plamya Military Academy
09:00-10:00  Lesson One: Military Theory and The Study of Rulership
10:00-11:00  Lesson Two: Political Theory
11:00-12:00  Lesson Three: General Science
12:00-12:30  Lunch, read up on major headlines in the Union Morning Post of the day
12:30-14:00  Surveillance patrol with troops (see Lady Bronya's Surveillance patrol route arrangement for more details)
14:00-15:00  Lesson Four: Writing and Speech
15:00-16:00  Lesson Five: Art Appreciation
16:00-17:00  Lesson Six: Union History
17:00-18:00  Edit speech for "Plamya Church Book Club Union 700 Year Anniversary Celebration" (Overview by the Writing and Political Theory senseis)
18:00-18:30  Dinner and listen to Plamya News Radio's "Union in 30 Minutes" news report
18:30-18:45  Head to Plamya Garrison Headquarters
18:45-19:30  Tour and inspect garrison work
19:30-19:45  Return to Consul Residence
19:45-20:00  Prepare report of what is learned today
20:00-20:30  Meet Madam Cocolia and report academic learning and patrol progress
20:30-21:00  Make up and do hair to prepare for the Silvermane Officer's Ball at the residence
21:00-21:45  Attend the Silvermane Officer's Ball (Please make sure Miss Bronya stays till the end as she will try to sneak back to her room)
21:45-22:10  Remove make up, bath
22:10-23:00  Reading time (See Miss Bronya's must read list for details)
23:00-23:30  Free time inside the residence
23:30  Bedtime

-498407005.Text:
1. Attention before opening

Before opening the interior packaging for Snow Burning Cloud Home Geomarrow Energy Cell, please carefully inspect the following to confirm the legitimacy of the item you've purchased. This will ensure your safety, the stability of the product, and the rights to the after-sales services as promised:

Please inspect the packaging and make sure there are no tears, holes, or signs of being already opened. If the mentioned examples are found, please put on your anti-contamination equipment immediately and help others in the room to do the same.

Please make sure the interior packaging is not showing any signs of pre-heating. If the interior package is showing signs of pre-heating, please follow the same protective steps as the previous entry and contact a City Security Personnel.

2. Opening and user's instructions

If you've confirmed the legitimacy of the origin of the product and that the interior packaging is not showing signs of pre-heating, then you may follow the next steps in opening and using the product:

1. Put on the heat/contamination-resistant eyewear and gloves provided in the exterior packaging.

2. Place the interior packaging and the product on a cool, sturdy, and non-flammable surface.

3. Slowly cut along the indicated line on the interior packaging with an edge made from any material that is not Geomarrow.

4. Use the safety clamp included with the product (you should hear a crisp "click" when the clamp is fully engaged) to lift the cylindrical Geomarrow Energy Cell. Place the cell carefully into the cooker. (Please make sure the specifications of the energy cell match the cooker you are using.)

5. If your primary purpose for using this product is to heat your home, please make sure that the Geomarrow furnace is not accessible to children or pets. If your primary purpose for using this product is cooking, please make sure there are no open flames or flammables near the furnace. It is strictly prohibited to use the Geomarrow furnace to assist with Geomarrow mining. For the safety of you and others, please do not attempt to use it in this manner.

6. If you need to turn on the Geomarrow furnace, please avoid touching the switch with your bare fingers, unless you have no other use for that finger.

3. Other

We solemnly promise that customers who have purchased the genuine Burning Cloud products can enjoy the following after-sales service:

Warranty discount within 6 months (based on the level of your Burning Cloud membership).

Discounts for booked door-to-door cleaning and maintenance services within 1 year

If our company product was confirmed by professional evaluation to be the cause of an accident, the customer can enjoy 40% or more of the accident compensation (including home repair, medical fees, funeral costs, and other expenses).

1423907295.Text:
<i>The first chapter mainly talks about Artem, a member of the Union "Supernova" Expedition Team. He was accidentally dragged into a blizzard outside of the Union. After falling off a cliff, he began his struggle to survive...<i>

When Artem first joined the Ancient Ruin Expedition Team, he was known as "the most promising young man of the expedition team in history."

He graduated top of his class from the best medical institute in the Union, also having degrees in archaeology and geology. His physical capabilities rival that of a professional athlete and have claimed multiple national rock-climbing championships as the head of the medical institute's rock-climbing association.

However, beneath the glamour and praise hides an extremely stubborn person who's obsessed with the relics of the Old World. When he first joined the expedition team, he often became "overly devoted" to the studies of the ancient relics. One day, he locked himself in the research center for days, until the staff found him passed out on the floor of the lab.

"What? Did I even forget to eat?!"

"Yes, Artem, you even forgot to sleep!"

"That won't do, I'm gonna fill my stomach and get back to my research."

It was not until recently that he learned to keep some canned food in his drawer in the lab. This only made him stayed even longer in the lab than before.

In the beginning, there were many young ladies with their eyes on Artem, for "a little while" at least. But everyone soon realized that the humanoid organism named Artem seems to be unable to develop emotional connections with others. What a pity.

In his own words, "For now, I just want to explore."

The ancient relic exploration team belongs to the Union Museum. They are a team of explorers responsible for the search of Old World relics in the areas surrounding Belobog. Relics brought back will be studied extensively by archaeologists and historians. However, archaeological work must be done in a harsh blizzard environment. Risks and accidents are frequent in this line of work, which requires both wisdom and courage. They are the true explorers of the Union. It shouldn't come as a surprise that the explorers may die at any moment.

This moment was no different.

Artem painstakingly dragged the lower half of his body near the ridge. The wind was much weaker here, providing some temporary relief before his inevitable death. His vision was blurry, but he couldn't tell whether it was due to the blizzard or his eyes had been wounded. He tried looking up the ridge where he probably fell from.

Did the team member he pulled up find safety? He must have, right? It was only a matter of time before he met up with the other team members. That's a relief...

...

As the blizzard grew more violent, Artem would soon become a corpse that he had passed by on previous adventures. It gave him some relief to think he wouldn't be alone.

...

He was just three years ago when he climbed the drainpipe to the rooftop, much to the horror of his parents... My life's already flashing before my eyes? Ah, how many memories are in a lifetime...

Artem had drifted off to sleep under the ridge wall.

The flame of his life flickered in the blizzard. It was about to die out.

-142176646.Text: Continuation: Artem was rescued by an unknown man and his daughter, and later woke up in a small medical facility. What he saw was a world strange to that of the Union. Everything here was made from "Rain Water," and the residents here lived in harmony with the wind and the snow. Artem was amazed by the unimaginable sights built from special materials... As the flow of time continued, Artem found himself lying in a medical facility. However, this medical facility had nothing in common with the Union's Saint Grace Hospital. No hospital would have beds made of crystals. It wasn't until Artem noticed the IV drip attached to his arm that he realized he was being treated. There was drumming in the ears and his eyes were only able to make out the blurred and burning lights around him. His motor skills haven't returned, but what's for sure is that he had almost crossed over to the other side. The person to receive him after being discharged from the hospital was the girl that helped him in his dreams. "Congratulations on your recovery." "Thank you. Where... who are you... I should probably introduce myself first... No, wait..." Artem was filled with questions, his words became jumbled, and were unable to form a complete sentence. "My name is Anna." "I'm A-Artem..." Anna didn't tie her hair as they met at the first time. The light shined through her long flaxen hair with translucent edges. Her long eyelashes and calming eyes were perfect for her thin face. In the past, Artem tried to avoid observing a woman's facial features for too long. With Anna, however, he couldn't stop until both their faces were flush with embarrassment. Artem felt something he had never felt before, it's probably because his life had been saved. Just as Artem had predicted, he was not in the Union. It was a city that had been built in the blizzard. People called it the Frozen City. Different from the Union, the ancestors of Frozen City used Old World technology so that the fire of civilization could survive in the blizzard. Artem was in the happiest phase of a culture shock. Everything was brand new to him as if the past technologies in the museum were waking up, the same as what happened in his countless dreams. Accompanied by Anna, this city gradually unfolded before Artem. "During your surgery, they should have also injected the Edelweiss in your body." "Edelweiss... What is Edelweiss?" "Ah, how should I put it? It's like a small... machine?" "Machine? I thought machines were big things with chains, gears, valves, and steam? It... can be injected into the body? "I don't understand the principle very well. I just know all residents of Frozen City are injected with Edelweiss at birth. We do not fear the cold because of it. When I was young, the explorers told me that outsiders wear heavy clothes to stay warm. It wasn't until I saw you that I realized it was true." "I wonder if I am an exotic outsider in your eyes... Right, speaking of which, are there other cities in this world besides Frozen City?" "But of course, the world is a big place." Anna suddenly stopped moving forward, and Artem stopped as well. In the clearing in front of them, a group of uniformed men gathered around a cannon, which was pointed at the sky, as if ready to fire. "Are they planning on shooting the cannon at the building across from here?" "Hahahaha..." Anna couldn't help but laugh at his genuine question. She wasn't used to guiding outsiders. "You're not wrong, but they're planning on constructing a new building here." "Constructing a new building?" "Yes, but I won't know what it looks like until it is complete." The cannon fired into the sky and thick clouds began to form above the clearing. Moments later, the water Anna called "rain" fell from the sky. "Rain Water" was a rare term that Artem only read about in the museum archives. He had never witnessed it before. It was a common weather condition in the Old World. Only snow would fall from the skies in Belobog. It wasn't until this moment that Artem understood that the cold snowflakes at home could fall in such a gentle way. The Rain Water didn't disperse as it fell on the open ground. Instead, it quickly solidified into a concrete shape, as if invisible hands were sculpting it. As the rain continued to fall, the outline of the building became clearer. The style was much different from those in the Union. A building tens of meters high was completed before Artem's eyes in a matter of minutes, like a miracle. Anna was no longer surprised by the shock in Artem's eyes. She explained that all buildings in Frozen City were created from the miracle "Rain Water." If the weather monitor "Cloudship" detected an incoming blizzard, these buildings would be reconverted into water on command, then transformed into new buildings as the people of Frozen City migrated to a more hospitable terrain. Unique blueprints were traded among residents. Strange buildings rose every day. People were used to it by then. …………

-1708260587.Text: Continuation: Following Anna, Artem witnessed the technological miracles of the Frozen City, including the "Rain Water," frozen tunnel network, and many others. During the tour, Anna delivered the Queen's invitation to Artem. He was being summoned as a foreign guest of Frozen City, where he met Dalmir of the Lava Kingdom... Responding to the summon, Artem arrived at the Frozen City's Crystal Palace. He had no issues finding the place, as the palace could be seen from anywhere in the streets. Under the guidance of the soldier in charge of reception, Artem passed through the main hall and entered the most prized part of the Crystal Palace, the hall of history. The glimmering light of the dawning sun cast a delicate outline upon the corridor walls. Following Artem's footsteps, the sculptures of the ancient heroes and kings began to move. The spears in their hand extend forward towards the evil monsters and their cloaks sway in the air. However, all motions came to an end as Artem stops moving. Artem spent his fair share of time in the museums and is knowledgeable of the histories that lie within the silent relics. He is familiar with the techniques used by ancient artists and gotten used to ignoring the details and effects, so he could get right to the history itself. He couldn't help but slow down his footsteps to gaze upon the magnificent works in the corridor: Appearing from the shattered sky, with golden blood running through veins, the God looks down upon the mortal word with cold and condescending eyes... Warriors of the Frozen City were in combat with the monster army. They wielded weapons emitting flames of death and wore armors that Artem has never seen before... A tank the size of a mountain soared through the air as a snowgull. At the apex of this tidal wave of an army sat a woman with a diamond crown on her head. She was gripping onto her spear, pointing it towards the merciless God in the sky... Artem was almost overwhelmed by the power emitting from the sculpture. He let out a long breath and noticed that the soldier leading him was looking at him with a smile. Artem gave a gesture of apology and quickly paced over to the soldier. At the reception, Artem was told by the soldiers that he could move freely here while waiting for the Queen's summons. Not long after he sat down, a ragged outsider came in and sat next to him. Before Artem could speak, the man introduced himself. "Hello, I'm Dalmir of the Lava Kingdom. I'd like to know..." "I'm Artem of the Union. I'm not a guest, just an adventurer that ran into trouble." "The Union? I've never heard of it, but I'm glad to meet someone in the same line of work!" "Same line of work? What an expected joy! Judging from your clothes, you must have experienced many thrilling adventures." "I guess thrilling would be an accurate description. I came here on the cannon. It was eye-opening! I've never seen snow before..." "What did you say? You came here... on a cannon?" "Yeah, yeah! You put a man inside the cannon, then BOOM! The man's flying through the air, get it?" Dalmir excitedly drew a parabola with his fingers. "No, could a man survive that?" "Here we go! This is my favorite moment. That incredible look on your face. Now I get to slowly explain my hometown to you. Haha, I'm used to it by now." Dalmir smiled proudly with his hands on his hips. "There are no snowflakes in the Lava Kingdom where I'm from. The temperature is night and day compared to the Frozen City. There are many volcanoes where I live. Do you know about volcanoes? It's the complete opposite of snow mountains. Volcanoes have smoke coming out the top, and sometimes, it even spews fire." Artem could not imagine a mountain so hot that it could breathe fire. All he could do was nod absently. "... And so, we used the power of the volcano to create a volcano cannon. We use it to launch adventurers, such as myself, to explore other countries. But it was only in the air that I realized the only way back was by walking. I guess that's how the previous adventurers returned home, hahaha!" Dalmir was naturally talkative, and his words were full of passion that made Artem feel a sense of kinship. As they talked, Artem learned for the first time what a volcano was, what the movement of Earth's crust was, and what a volcano cannon was... The Lava Kingdom adventurers are dispatched to explore the ends of the earth, but they can always find their way back with a compass that always points home. However, the trip back may be more dangerous than flying on the cannon, and only a handful of adventurers make it. The ones who do make it always bring home reliable outsiders. The outsiders bring knowledge and technology that the Lava Kingdom would not normally have access to. "Artem, I need your help! Please come back with me to the Lava Kingdom. We must get there before the biggest volcanic event in history!" "The biggest volcanic event in history..." "That's right! We're preparing our biggest volcano cannon yet. We're going to fly to the Satelleden of the Old World!" (To Be Continued)

1020622768.Text: Continuation: After walking through the hall of history, Artem arrived at the reception room where he was greeted by Dalmir as he waited for the Queen's summon. The two hit it off and Dalmir shared his true intentions of being here. Dalmir fulfilled his wish, as he was searching for a companion to return to the Lava Kingdom with him to soar into the skies... Artem was once again blown away. His mind was as a drum being heavily beaten. "Leave the surface of the ground... and head for the skies?" Each day, the world that Artem knows of is repeatedly challenged by never seen before technologies, unknown histories, and distant and mysterious nations. Just as he thought nothing else would be able to surprise him, the man in front of him invites him to leave the surface using a cannon powered by a volcano. He didn't have a choice—— "Sorry, I refuse!" "What? I thought you'd be more adventurous than that. I was certain you would agree." "That's two completely different issues. Going to a far and unfamiliar nation, or climbing to heights never reached before, may sound crazy to some people, but they're all within human logic and understanding. Using a volcano to launch yourself into the sky... that's just suicide!" After short chats, he said his farewell to the volcano adventurer. "Maybe it's not that ruthless of an idea, young man." A commanding and soothing voice came from the side. Artem noticed a woman in expensive clothing staring at him from afar. Artem immediately realized who it was and bowed. The woman smiled back. "May I ask you a question? Adventurer from the Union, why did you embark on this perilous journey?" "Perhaps... the restless blood runs through me?" Artem pondered for a moment, then shook his head. "Perhaps life was too peaceful in the Union. We hide in our greenhouse and listen to the preaching of Plamya Church day after day. Soldiers guard our borders, stopping monsters from entering our city and preventing our people from leaving. If you work hard, you don't have to worry about food or shelter. But every day is the same. Life is peaceful without any changes. People are afraid of an unstable future and afraid of losing the life to which they've grown accustomed. The Union has a history of over a hundred years, but nobody has truly ever left there." "I don't want to read the same newspaper every day. I don't want to cover my eyes and ears and pretend the world is the size of our city. I want to know if I can survive a life that's not peaceful." The woman smiled. "It seems you were born in the wrong place. You are different from the other Union people I know." "There have been other Union people who arrived here?" "That was over a hundred years ago, and it ended regrettably. Fortunately, the Frozen City stays not in one place, and the disaster of war was avoided." Artem was shocked at the words coming out of the woman's mouth. "You mean to say, the Union and Frozen City almost went to war?" The woman raised her hand to stop his questioning. "From then on, we were cautious of all visitors from that country. As you can see, the Frozen City is not built with unchanging ice. It is built with water, free of prejudice." "A man must be judged like a gem, by examining him from all sides. I have heard Anna's description of you. We have read about your adventures. And now, I have seen you. By the standards of the Frozen City, you are a remarkable man." "I thank you for your kind judgment, Your Majesty." Artem lowers his head. "Let's jump back to our topic. I asked you before why you embarked on this perilous journey. Your response proved your wish to be free of constraints and to live a life without repetition. Frozen City, Lava Kingdom... You have heard and seen many wondrous things that many people can only dream of. But this city is bound to the skies, just as the Union is bound to the land. Do you understand what I mean?" "I... understand, Your Majesty. I just have a hard time believing that people can fly through the air?" Artem murmured, "I thought only birds could do that. I can't tell if Dalmir's invitation is suicidal madness or an adventurous gamble." "Is there any difference between the two? The spirit of adventure is a precious quality against the nature of human survival, yet worthless. But it is this spirit that allowed the people of the Old World to reach unimaginable heights. Strictly speaking, adventures and explorations are part of the heritage of the Old World." "You couldn't have imagined what the Frozen City looked like, if you decided to live peacefully behind the Union walls. Dalmir was not lying when he mentioned the Satelleden. It is a miracle left behind by the people from the Old World. People have long forgotten what it was like nowadays," the Queen sighed regrettably. "You've walked through the Snow Plains in search of ancient relics, all so that you could inherit the Old World heritage. The way back has already been covered in snow, Artem. The only way is forward, for you... and for the Frozen City, as it always has been." "I will take that into consideration." Artem is deep in thought and lets out a long sigh. "I will go talk to Dalmir." Suddenly, there was a stern look on the Queen's face. "I don't mean to persuade you, young Artem. Impulse will take you on adventures, but that alone will not take you to the ends of the earth. However you choose, I have a gift for you." The Queen extended her hands from beneath her cloak. She held out a one-cubit long wand to Artem. The wand was forked on one end, and there was a double-headed eagle on the other. "Young Artem, we do not bow here in the Frozen City. But, as a token of respect to the wisdom of the Old World, go down on one knee. Bow, not to me, but the Old World." The Queen took hold of the wand. Suddenly, liquid began to flow over the surface, forming a blueish ice blade. She turned the blade with incredible speed and touched Artem once on each shoulder and once on the top of his head. Artem felt the ice breathing and jumping on his shoulders. "May your path be clear, your luck persists, and your courage prevail." "The technology for this blade comes from the Old World. I present it to you, knight of the Frozen City. To the sky, it is but a small needle. To the earth, it is only a tool to cut through obstacles. However, to you, it is the courage to move forward in the face of danger." "Go now, Artem."

-901691533.Text: Continuation: This chapter talked about Artem and his two companions arriving at the warm grasslands after leaving the snowy mountains. Though the soil is rich and the plantations ample, the danger is still lurking in every corner of this pleasant place. Dangers such as never before seen gigantic insects...
