The fertile environment gave birth to vigorous growth. The soil beneath his feet felt like a soft mattress. Artem takes off his jacket and walks through the grass field with weeds as high as his knees. A soft breeze passes by with the scent of the fresh blades of grass. Artem never thought such a gentle wind existed in the world. He gently holds on to Anna's hand, and she accepted his advance. For a split second, an idea passed through Artem's mind: "Stay, and spend the rest of the life with Anna here." But he quickly cleared his head of the selfish thought when he looked at the girl who entrusted him with everything standing beside him. He would be letting her down if he were to follow his selfish urges. Before the three could enjoy more than just a sudden moment of peace, a rapid buzzing noise rose in the sky. The strange noise brought a prickling pain to their heads. A monster, like a bird, dashed through the sky flapping its glass-like transparent wings at astonishing speeds. Subconsciously, Dalmir covered his ears and dropped to the ground, and began praying to a certain god. After having experienced being taken by monsters, he has always been the first to react to similar dangers. Artem fought the pain in his eardrums as he covered Anna with his body and knelt to the floor. He realized that he might have seen monsters like these somewhere before. The dangers from the sky lands softly on the ground, retracting its mesh-like wings, and stood still like a horrific statue. Is that... a mosquito? Artem finally recalled from the bottom of his memories the description he once read of the monstrosity before him in an encyclopedia of the Old World. In the Old World era, the world was divided into four distinct seasons. Mosquitoes were blood-sucking insects that emerged during the summer, but their size was no larger than an average fingertip and could be crushed with ease. However, the "mosquito" before him was taller than he was. Its long and thin sword-like proboscis pierced in the ground as if it were sucking on something, completely oblivious to the three people standing around it. Lying flat on his stomach, Dalmir felt liquid moving below beneath the soft soil, as the giant mosquito's belly swelled up. There was fear in his lowered voice: "Let's escape quickly before it discovers us..." "Yeah, be careful." Artem slowed down his breathing and moved slowly through the tall grass. But the nasty creature seemed to detect even the slightest tremor in the soil. In the blink of an eye, it instantly flew in the air. The faint hum came again, accompanied by a numbing headache. Artem brandished the sword that the queen of the Frozen City gave him and readied himself for battle. "Be careful, Artem! It's coming." The high-pitched noise turned into a shrill scream as the creature approached. Artem parried the piercing attack from the mosquito's mouth with his sword. The creature was agile in the air, able to not only hover but quickly change directions. It flew back into the air and swooped down for another attack. Its movements were so fast that it became a blur. Before the attack could strike Artem, a roar of gunpowder sounded from behind him. The giant mosquito was knocked to the ground, with its feet in the air. Artem turned around to see Dalmir holding an ancient flintlock musket, with white smoke coming out of its barrel. "Great shot!" Dalmir was thrilled with the precision of his shot. But his excitement soon turned into fear as the creature kicked its feet to turn itself over. The giant mosquito turned upright and started flying back and forth in the air, enough to make someone dizzy. Artem could tell how angry it was by the fierce flapping of its wings. Its needle-sharp mouth quivered in preparation for its next attack. Artem's hand shook as he tried to hold on to the sword. This wasn't going to be an attack that could be dodged. "Anna, get out of here!" "But, Artem..." "Escape? No need." A strong but powerful voice sounded from out of nowhere. Suddenly, a long lance appeared from the sky and pierced the translucent body of the giant mosquito, pinning it to the ground. The battle was over in an instant. Artem let out a sigh of relief. They were saved. The man who spoke was dressed in strange imposing attire, looked majestic. What's more surprising was his mount: it was an insect with an armored shell, developed hind legs, and a saddle on its back. It was nearly the size of the giant mosquito that they just fought. The insect jumped up with lightning-quick speed. As it did, the man pulled his lance from the creature's corpse and stopped in front of Artem. He pointed it with precision at Artem's heart. He whistled, and a team of similarly dressed knights appeared from the grasses. The three of them were about to express their thanks when the first knight announced: "Strangers trespassing on our sacred land, the Haru Hara. You are under arrest..." (To Be Continued)

1827191822.Text: Continuation: In this chapter, Artem and friends discovered an altar that can be used to directly communicate with the giant monster "Haru Hara" and found out the entrance to its lungs. But what they found when they arrived in its lungs is an Old World rain forest with severe flooding. Artem and Duke dived into the water to find out the reason behind the flooding but was attacked and dragged into the depths... ... As Artem slowly wakes, a mysterious feeling runs through his entire body, as if he was freed from the weight of his body. The prolonged slumber dawned a miraculous sense of security, just as what infants felt when they rest in their mother's womb. He slowly regains consciousness but the drowsy feeling remains. Artem recalls the last thing that happened before passing out: He struggled to return to the surface as someone was dragging him down deeper into the water. He held out as long as he could before finally running out of air... Did I drown? There was no time for Artem to figure out what had happened because knowing what was going on was more important: He was completely submerged in water, however, there was not the slightest sense of grasping for air. He turned his wrist and confirmed the flow of water between his fingers and his body began moving upwards as he started kicking his legs. "Bam!" his back hit the bars of the metal cage. The sudden pain made Artem swear out loud, but knowing that he's still alive put his mind at ease. "Are you awake, Mr. Artem?" A familiar voice came from behind. Artem, swimming around, discovered Captain Duke and iron bars in the water between them. "Captain Duke, you're alive as well? Where are we..." "We've been captured by the underwater residents." "So that's how I passed out..." "No, you passed out because you were holding your breath. Haven't you noticed? We can breathe in this water." Artem suddenly understood. He's only alive because of this mysterious water. When he was a medical student, he learned that it was possible to breathe in water if enough oxygen was dissolved. It seems their conversation warranted a response from the nearby guard. A boyish figure swam past their cage. Dressed in tights with a harpoon in his hand, he banged hard on the iron bars, producing strong waves of water that sent Artem back. "Shut up, this isn't a place for chitchat!" The guard wasn't playing around: "Where else would you breathe, besides in the water. You bastards, come take a look at this." The guard pulled on the net in his hand. The thing he was dragging floated slowly into sight. There were white transparent sluggish worms. At least Artem thought they were worms, for he had never seen anything like them. "Do you know where these bugs came from?" Artem and Duke looked at each other and shook their heads. "Hmph, useless creatures. I thought you two would know something..." "... These are bugs?" "I don't know, but I have to call it something." The guard suddenly remembered his position, struck the iron bars again, "Silence! Don't talk to me!" After a brief moment of silence, Artem asked the guard, "It doesn't look like it has any internal organs. Maybe it's not a living organism?" "It chases people as if it has eyes. Who cares if it's living or not," snorted the Guard. "When the body is split open, it releases a liquid that can dissolve people. You also can't breathe in the polluted water." "Polluted... water...?" Duke added, "So instead of attacking them, you built this huge underwater prison..." "Hahaha, so you're the clever one. That idiot beside you keeps asking stupid questions. Yes, two hundred layers of the prison are filled with these bugs." The guard's tone suddenly became serious: "We must continue building the prison to survive." Artem tried to see the water below him. His eyesight was not as good as Duke's, but he could make out the staggering number of cages beneath him, where countless white bugs were being held. The water was so deep that he could not see the bottom, only a deep abyss. The rising waters could have already crushed the insects near the bottom, leaving only the smell of death in the abyss. Artem seemed to understand the real cause behind the flooding of "Haru Hara's" lungs. The constant appearance of bugs and expansion of the prison made the water in its lungs rise higher, leaving less room for oxygen. "This prison wasn't meant for the two of you," the guard banged on the iron bars again. "If you can help us find out where these bugs are coming from, we can let you out of here. How about it?" "What if I told you we were from above the water surface? (To Be Continued)

261107881.Text: Continuation: In this chapter, Artem and friends arrived at the last stop in their voyage —— The Lava Kingdom. Loud buzzing noises emerged from the distant lava and boulders responding to Dalmir's signal flares. A gigantic insect suited in plated armor arrives before them, it was their ride to the kingdom of cannons... ... Artem originally didn't have any negative feelings towards these invertebrate annelids. "They've noticed the flare. Look, that's the molten insect, it's here to bring us home." A sense of joy and pride can be noticed in Dalmir's tone. "Here for us? Another gigantic insect... there's too many bugs here." The gigantic earthworm-like bug stood roughly 2 meters tall and 20 meters long. It continued to squirm as it was parked and waited to be boarded. Numerous layers of petal-shaped scales covered its circular muscles. The scales were coated in a special layer of fine black granite sand which looks to be made of part secretion and part lava. Artem did not want to stay here. "What's wrong, Artem? This is not as strange as the bugs back on Haru Hara." "Let's go, Artem." Anna followed after Dalmir. The old man was holding a long pole in his hand. His other hand... was an iron hook, holding onto something like a bridle. He rolled off the back of the giant bug with style. The old man spread out his arms and embraced Dalmir. It was clear that they weren't just acquaintances, but great buddies. "Hey! Dalmir!" "President Barbicane! You came to see me!" "Hahahaha, surprised, are you? Quickly, introduce me to your adventure companions!" "Hello, I'm Anna." "Hello, Anna! I'm Barbicane. Hahaha, this girl is adorable!" Barbicane, seeing that Artem hadn't moved, walked towards him, shouting at the top of his voice, "That young lad over there! Come quickly, we are heading back to the Lava Kingdom!" Artem, seeing the old man limping over to him, reluctantly followed. "... Hello, I'm Artem." ... After climbing on the huge molten insect, it was as Artem had feared. The metal armors on its back didn't fit well on the insect. There was nothing Artem could hold onto, except the arms of the seat. He clamped his legs tightly, to keep from falling off the insect. There was the sound of an electrode at the end of Barbicane's pole, as he looked back at the people seated behind him. "Off we go!" The large insect and its armor can be compressed to a certain extent, allowing it to crawl through the tunnels. It can also climb on rock walls that others would have to circle. Barbicane used complex static signals to control the insect, and the insect made some maneuvers of its own. The two of them were like parts in a well-oiled machine. Artem noticed how strange the large insect dissipated heat. Liquids would flow from the small reticular holes in its armor. The burning sand would be fall off its body, while new clay and sand would stick to it, thus took away the heat. "How incredible..." As the journey continued, Dalmir and Barbicane's conversation about the Lava Kingdom grew more interesting. According to Dalmir, half of the adventurers in the Lava Kingdom loved building cannons, while the other half loved adventures. Barbicane is the president of the Lava Kingdom Cannon Development Committee, a fine example of how obsessed they are with cannon technology. The only thing they could boast about was the size of the cannon or its firing range. "In our committee, people respect you based on the might of your cannon. Dalmir is only interested in making flintlocks. We're not interested in that, hahaha!" "Hey! I'm a professional adventurer! Hahahaha!" President Barbicane's boorish laugh gave everyone around him a sense of kinship. He missed an eye. One hand and one leg were replaced by metal prosthetics. It wasn't until he turned to talk to Dalmir that Artem noticed part of his skull was made from some kind of silicon. "You mean the rubber part of my skull? Part of my scalp was blown away when a cannon's chamber blew up. My brain's still good though, hahaha!" As Artem got used to the bumpy ride, he finally had time to enjoy the sights. There were many volcanoes of varying sizes, with some looking like they were about to burst. Just looking at this scenery was enough to make you feel hot and dry. It wouldn't be completely inaccurate to say this place was hell on earth. The black volcanoes, smoke-filled skies, red lava spewing everywhere... Who would have imagined there'd be a country here focused on the scientific research of cannons? "It's been a long time since any adventurers returned. I've asked our cook to prepare a celebration feast tonight!" Artem was excited that there would be a celebration. Only Anna, who had been watching the volcano in the distance, seemed to be preoccupied with something. ... The banquet hall was full of people from the Cannon Development Committee and the Explorers' Guild. President Barbicane's passionate introduction of the three of them was met with thunderous applause. Artem noticed something about everyone: each of them was missing a body part, whether it be an arm or a leg. Some of them even had a rubber chin or a platinum nose. From Artem's perspective, it was a miracle that Dalmir's body was completely intact. "For years now, our Cannon Committee has been planning the greatest cannon event, one that's worthy of our ancestors! Everyone is aware that the greatest volcanic activity in history is coming!" "Viva the volcanoes!" The banquet hall filled up with loud cheers once more. "We will bravely shoot ourselves into outer space, risking our lives for the greatest adventure the world has ever known!" "Viva the outer space!" The whole banquet hall shook as if it would collapse at any moment. "Positional observations from Dalmir's journey have allowed us to track the Satelleden. I am pleased to announce that we can now reach it with complete certainty!" Many people were shocked at the announcement, evident by the sudden silence in the hall. Then, it was followed by cheering and applause that couldn't be suppressed, even if all the cannons in the banquet hall were fired at once. Artem sneaked away from the crowd of people in the banquet hall. He saw Anna looking out of the window at the quiet volcano in the distance. Besides her, it was the sparkling wine they had brought from Haru Hara. "Anna..." "Artem, don't say anything. Can you come here for a moment?" During their adventures, Artem had witnessed Anna's extraordinary tenacity a thousand times. But now, her voice sounded like a distress signal, pleading to him for help. It made Artem want to embrace her from behind. "What's wrong?" "If you go to outer space, will you ever come back again...?" "Perhaps, there probably won't be volcano cannons in the Satelleden." "Artem..." Anna turned around and her face flushed as if she had cried. At that moment, the sounds from the banquet hall faded away and the whole world fell silent. "I miss home. Will you come home with me?" (To Be Continued)

-1304976060.Text:
...

Dalmir was confused, but he didn't dare ask any questions.

There was a delicate feeling in the air between Artem and Anna by the second day of the celebration.

Dalmir speculated that Artem saw the uplifted feelings of the festival as an opportunity and confessed his love for Anna, but was ultimately turned down. He had always believed the two would end up together, but reality has shown him how naive he was. If he had been launched from the cannons and seen more of the world, maybe he would've seen it coming.

"Umm... I brought you guys to the launching grounds here today to show you our pride, the volcano cannon."

Dalmir was aware that the two were not in a good mood, but he thought that it would be best not to ask.

"As I've said before, the energy of the volcano is the energy of the planet itself."

"The core of the planet is always pushing towards the opposite direction of gravity. This enormous outward compression force shoots out the lava other gas-formed matter from beneath the ground. This is the power of the volcano..."

Artem and Anna looked upon the lava in the volcano through the thick glass.

Its characteristics were similar to waves, rising and falling. On the other hand, it was like boiling water, with gases constantly pushing out of the magma. The scorching temperatures made the air unevenly thick and caused heat hazes to appear. Anyone could see that such an unstable burning bright liquid could not be suppressed.

Without realizing it, Artem was sweating all over.

"Instability, unpredictability, and too much power were all reasons that humans in the past refused to harness this energy. However, our ancestors changed their mind during the Ice Age."

"Don't try to control it. Set it free!"

"You're right, Artem! We should just set this immense energy free, like..."

They said in unison, "Like one big fart!"

The two of them laughed. Dalmir had told many cheesy jokes like this during their journey.

It wasn't until they were staring at the ready-to-erupt volcano that the ridiculous stories came to life. It was unbelievable that they are standing so close to such outlandish descriptions. Artem feels like he's still in a dream.

Anna smiled lightly as she walked behind them.

"The energy container at the top, and the volcanic accelerators over there... They're expensive to make, but they are all disposable..."

Dalmir suddenly stopped. He wasn't pleased with that expression.

"They've prepared their whole lives for the one battle, but it only takes one battle to prove everything. They aren't disposables! They're the ultimate romanticists!"

"... Romanticists...?"

Artem felt as if something was stirring inside his chest, like restless magma. As Dalmir's voice grew quieter, he could feel the vibrations of the mantle, all that immense energy to realize the once-in-a-lifetime romance.

"Look over here! This is the cannonball we'll be riding in!"

It was a spherical cockpit about seven meters in diameter, with windows in front. There were countless precision instruments, levers, and buttons inside. On the outside, there were a pair of thin wings that could expand...
It didn't seem like something the Lava Kingdom would create. 

"Could it be..."

"That's right. It was a cannonball created by the people from the Old World. This is the only one in the whole world!"

The outside was covered with material that can withstand temperatures up to 5000 degrees Celsius. Telemetry communications, energy, temperature control, and other precision equipment lined the walls of the interior. Artem had unearthed many Old World relics to know immediately that this was the pinnacle of Old World technology, something that could take people to the outer space for real.

"This cannonball is also a romanticist, flying into outer space with no return. It will complete its mission during the journey, then float through the universe as a permanent fixture..."

Once again, Artem felt he was no longer on the ground. He was among the brilliant stars that filled the night sky.

Anna looked at the man standing before her, knowing that his mind had already left this planet.

She understood completely. Artem's heart had reached heights that she couldn't reach, no matter hard she tried. It was far, far beyond the Satelleden, far beyond the end of the universe. And so, she decided to leave quietly before dawn. Perhaps it would make him less miserable.

...

But she still couldn't do it.

"Thank you, Anna."

On the path before sunrise, she saw the silhouette of the person she had fallen in love with. It was Artem, waiting for her. She couldn't leave quietly after all.

"I have been deeply in love with you ever since you saved my life. I have never seen such a tenacious, independent, and gentle woman like you. There were plenty of chances for me to tell you: I like you. I want to spend the rest of my life with you."

But I cannot stay with you. If there's one thing that I must do in life, it is to go on adventures, continuous adventures. I'm sorry..."

"I think I'm also a romanticist. I'm willing to risk everything I have for the sake of adventure. I'm sorry..."

With tears in her eyes, Anna knew.

She knew the moment she embarked on this journey. It was the kind of man she fell in love with.

Before her tears could fall, a warm hand wiped away her tears. It was Artem's hand, which she knew too well.

"I don't want you to shed so many tears for me. But I've already decided..."

"I understand, I understand completely..."

"I can give you three things."

Artem took out his journal, which carefully recorded all his adventures and memories with Anna.

"The first is my past. Everything we experienced together was real."

Artem placed the sword that the Frozen City Queen had given him in Anna's hands.

"The second is my future. This is my promise to the queen. I wish to protect you forever."

"And the last is my present. I love you, Anna."

He kissed her.

They were so affectionate as if time had stopped in this moment and the future would not come.

(To Be Continued)

1067676935.Text: Continuation: In this chapter, armed with a conviction of never returning, Artem and Dalmir will ride a "cannonball" of the Old World into the sky. ... The impact factor of the volcano cannon was not included in the original designs. The "cannonball" instantly reached a shocking speed upon firing, leaving everyone at the launch site speechless. Is this really going to be okay? From the inside, Artem and Dalmir had to withstand 10 G's of crushing force. They were almost smashed by the power. Artem lost consciousness the splint second upon launch and the memories of the dangers on the Snow Plains appeared in his mind. His lulled state did not last long as he woke himself up with impressive conviction and mental discipline. After experiencing the near-death moment, Artem immediately noticed the importance of the shock-reduction capacitor in the flying pod. Without it, he'd already be dead. Dalmir on the other hand was having a much harder time. His eyes were rolled back and white foam was coming out of his slightly opened mouth. Is he passed out? Or is he... out. Artem called for him over the radio several times, but Dalmir did not respond. Not only could the closing sky be seen in the porthole of the "cannonball," so could the distancing ground from beneath. The spectacular view of being launched from the ground unfolds rapidly in front of Artem's eyes. The familiar sights quickly shrink till it becomes unfamiliar. The numbers on the instruments of the vessel continue to climb, with no sign of slowing down whatsoever. Artem was still unaware of what was happening even though everything pointed towards the same conclusion —— They were leaving the planet at shocking speeds. - 5000 meters off the ground. Artem could still see the buildings of the Lava Kingdom, especially the main cannon that fired the "cannonball." The launch caused a massive landslide, reducing the main volcano to a billowing cloud of smoke. At this height, the large insects looked like ants and the lava on the ridges looked like a spider silk web. The bright magma that scorched the eyes softened considerably. The volcanic ash in the air sped by quickly, as the ground slowly became a blur. - 10,000 meters off the ground. Artem could see Haru Hara. At the height of tens of thousands of meters, Artem could barely see the back of the beast. Even so, he had not seen the monster's entire body! Such a giant beast! It was an indescribable feeling to have a panoramic view of the roads he had taken on during his long adventures. Artem traced the route he had once taken on, saw through the snowy mountains, and looked at the blizzard. The Frozen City was quickly passing through those clouds and snow, but he could not see its outline. Anna once said she would go fishing with her father to relax at the end of long adventures. How would she relax this time... Thin clouds began to obstruct his view and thoughts of escaping came back. How long has the "cannonball" been flying... - They flew higher until they reached the darkness. The Union in Belobog loomed over the dark land. Thousands of lit houses were only the size of a coin, but Artem knew that that was the Union. The heated housing surrounded by the cold climate was more apparent looking down from the clouds. It was this warmth that had sustained the Union for a hundred years, but it also prevented them from going outside. Compared to the size of the planet, the Union was but a speck of dirt. Artem once thought that the Union and its surroundings were the whole world. Thinking back now, it was just the unwillingness to admit his narrow perspective. At this height, anyone would agree that such a narrow perspective was laughable. - 100,000 meters off the ground. Artem could clearly see the curvature of the planet. The great auroras started appearing. The brilliant ribbons gently covered the surface of the planet. Back in the Union, Artem had gazed up at these dreamlike veils fluttering in the night sky. Now, they were below him. It was at this moment that static came through the communication device... "... A-Artem! We! Ahhh! Are in the sky! Ahhhhh... Ahhh..." It was Dalmir's screaming from earlier. Artem really didn't know whether to laugh or cry. ... - 400,000 meters off the ground. Artem could see the "Satelleden". It was a large cylindrical object, rotating slowly. Artem had heard of science fantasy that the effects of gravity could be simulated through rotating centrifugal force. He brushed it off as mere fantasy until witnessing the miracle before his eyes. The excitement was overwhelming. I wonder how people from the Old World will react when they see this "cannonball." Fear? Surprise? Will they treat it like invaders and retaliate? Soft music began playing in the "cannonball." It was a mix of drums and guitars that make you feel warm and cozy, like a gift at the end of their journey: "Distant cosmic radio is calling" "Distant cosmic radio is calling" ... As the "cannonball" got closer, Artem could make out more details of the "Satelleden." If the interior was laid out, "Satelleden" would be a large rectangular area. The opposite sides of the area would join to form the cylinder. Anywhere you stood, you could raise your head and see the streets hanging above. In the streets, many buildings violated violated physics. The mechanical giants appeared to be connecting the building blocks by combining various modules. Neatly planned streets kept adjusting their positions. To Artem, the whole "city" was breathing and pulsing like a living organism and rotating as a Rubik's Cube. It was constantly evolving, showing ultimate rationality. As an immaculate work of art that had long been abandoned, "Satelleden" was floating alone in the universe, awaiting the gaze of human admiration. As if sensing the "cannonball," the large object extended a docking port of the same astonishing proportions, like a giant extending its hand at a flying insect. Artem's heart clenched out of fear that the last moments of his journey would end in a fiery collision. But the disaster he imagined didn't happen The docking port gently held the "cannonball" and guided it to the landing pad in Satelleden. The music was still playing gently on their communication devices... ... "I climb the ladder to the stars" "And walk in the gallery of outer space" "Insomnia awaits the galaxy tonight" ...

-498407006.Text:
1. There is a place in the city where if you continue walking forward, you will end up in the air.

2. If you repeatedly rock back and forth at the corner of the street, the ground will open up and swallow you into the abyss.

3. At a specific point in time, the people that pass by you will suddenly open their arms and make a T-pose.

4. If you suddenly turn around at a crowded place, the walls will become white.

5. If you utter a certain mysterious phrase, the person you're conversing with will turn into a monster.

6. The heater and garbage can on the side of the street become squared boxes when it snows.

7. When you're in a place with no one else around, music of an unknown origin could be heard.

8. If you are reading this, all the miraculous things above will happen to you.

1423907298.Text: This article was published in 257 UE by senior reporter Felix Dunn from the "Crystal Daily." The authenticity of the events mentioned in the article can no longer be verified, but most historians believe it to be false and fabricated. Soon after the article was published, it was listed as a forbidden piece, never to be printed, sold, or promoted within the walls of Belobog City. By the time I got there, the strange foreigner wearing a mask was already surrounded by the Silvermane Guards. According to the residents, he was wandering around the streets like a chicken without a head, oblivious to those around him, and going about with his spectacular performance art: He would sometimes throw his arms up in the air and suddenly fall on one knee, mumbling strange spells beneath his breath as if he were praying to a certain god; sometimes he would grab a shopkeeper from the side of the street and against all protest, act as if they were best friends. It would've been fine if he stopped there, but he would continue to run onto the main freeway and lie flat in the middle of the road, causing chaos and traffic in downtown Belobog. I, who was about to get off work, was called to the scene. He laid sound asleep in the middle of the road, it took several Silvermane Guards to drag him away, it was an unbearable sight. The captain of the Silvermane Guards arrived at the same time as me. I spoke with him for a while and got permission to write down and record the questioning process. The guards put the man in a series of restraints and dragged him into the interrogation room before removing his mask. Even under these circumstances, the man had an eerie smile on his face. Before the Captain could ask a question, the odd man opened his mouth, "Excuse me, but is this the Planet Dadarose?" His voice was clear and bright, but it did not stop everyone in the room to be confused by his question. The Captain answers with a question, "No, this is Belobog, capital of this planet. Who are you?" The odd man opened his mouth and exposed a few of his silver-gilt teeth, "It seems like something went wrong with my navigation systems. But that doesn't matter! Please, just call me Ada." The odd man spoke with great manners and the Captain ordered for his binds to be loosened. The Captain then asks him, "You said your navigation system broke down, what's that about?" "It means that my audience was switched out last minute!" The guards looked at each other in confusion, even I didn't know what the odd man meant. "You don't appear to have any ill intentions, but you are in serious violation of the city's order. Please state your motives. Where are you from, and where are you going? Do you understand?" The Captain responded. "Yes, yes, I understand. You see, I'm an actor. I don't like to boast but I do have quite the reputation as an artist back home in another galaxy. But performing for the same crowd gets boring, so I wanted to travel to other worlds and explore new markets. My destination was a filthy rich planet in the galaxy over, but there must be a deviation in my ship's navigation, so I ended up here." I had never seen the Captain's eyes so wide. He couldn't believe what he was hearing. After some time, he managed to ask, "That means, you're an... alien?" "Correct!" "How can you prove it? None of us have had any interactions with an alien before." "Simple! Please take a look at this!" The strange man snapped his fingers, and the whole interrogation room was illuminated by a bright light. I followed the light and looked up to find specks of light had formed a hexagon. Out of nowhere, cheerful music began to play to the scenes that played out before our eyes, which I assumed were Ada's performances on our planets. Everyone was dumbfounded because the images were something we had never seen before, but too real for us to question. Ada burst into laughter at the sight of our dropped jaws. He then said, "These are some highlights from my shows over the years. I play it for the locals whenever I reach a new planet. I must say, you guys have the funniest reaction!" The Captain slumped back into his chair, "Mr. Ada, what do you want?" "Very simple, I have a favor to ask of you." He smiled.

-142176643.Text: Ada tells the Captain that he wishes to fix his spaceship as soon as possible, but he's unsure of where he could find the fuel. The Captain then told him that the Geomarrow was the source of energy for the Union. However, due to its scarcity, anything related to resource distribution had to be decided personally by Madam Consul. Her forte of being scrupulous was widely known, it'd be near impossible for her to sign off on the supplying of Geomarrow for something so small. However, Ada showed no sign of worry, instead, he said, "There's no need to worry! I didn't come empty-handed! Can I please be granted an audience with Madam Consul?" I noticed that the Captain fell silent after hearing the odd man's request. Aside from events such as the Maslenitsa, it's almost impossible for a commoner to be granted an audience with the Consul. However, this was a living and talking "alien," surely even the Consul would be slightly interested. "Alright, I will do my best to make introductions, but I can't make any promises." The Captain replied carefully. Ada's smile returned, "Not a problem! I have another small request to make. When you introduce me to the Consul, could you please use the name 'Fool' instead?" The next day, the "Fool" was arranged to meet with the Consul. As the first media representative to be on-site, I was aslo invited to the residence to listen in and record everything - and it was expected of the Captain to turn down our request to film. On the day of the meeting, the Consul's residence was packed with nosy ministers who are probably interested in witnessing the wonders of the "alien artist". The "Fool" put on a different attire than normal, an aura of grandeur and nobility can still be felt from the outfit even though it is of a different style from Belobog. The odd man gave a deep and sincere bow as the Consul arrived. The Consul began by asking, "Fool, I've heard of your 'story.' Let's cut to the chase, I find it hard to believe that you're some kind of alien. The reason you're still standing here in one piece is that my ministers have spoken on your behalf. Now, what do you want from me, and what have you brought in return?" The fool replied, "Praise your generosity, Madam Consul! My proposal is very simple. Provide me with some precious resources and I'll design a sculpture for you to be placed in the city center for free!" Murmurs filled the air, but Madam Consul signaled for silence. "Your offer seems too generous. How do I know you're not up to something?" The fool replied without any pause, "Simple! I will deliver the goods first. You just have to promise me one thing. In a week, gather everyone in the city center, where I will reveal the greatest masterpiece! If you are satisfied with the sculpture, then we can do business!" According to an insider at the Consul Residence, Ada spent a long night in private with the Consul, discussing details of the sculpture. The insider also claimed Madam Consul's attitude toward the strange man changed from "Fool" to the greatest artist in the universe. A week later, everyone in Belobog gathered in the center of the city. Ada's strange spaceship was the topic of much discussion. Everyone was waiting for the moment where the "Fool" unveiled his work. Moments later, he showed up wearing the same strange costume, holding an unknown device in his hand. Ada leaped on top of his spaceship and announced to the eager crowd, "Everyone! You have all been invited here today, to witness the greatest masterpiece of sculpture design in the world! Feast your eyes upon, 'The Hardworking Consul'!" I watched as he flipped the switch on the device in his hand. A huge projection appeared out of nowhere in front of the residents. In the secret interviews we conducted later, many people admitted that it was the funniest thing they had ever seen. The fool had turned Madam Consul into a complete clown. She was standing upside down on one hand, donning a red and green costume as if she were trapped inside a large melon. Her eyes were sparkling and her tongue was out. One glance at the sculpture had you bursting into tears laughing. And that's exactly what happened. Everyone there burst into a huge laughter. Suddenly, there was a commotion in the crowd as the Silvermane Guards tried to arrest the alien. Then the unthinkable happened! Ada's ship seemed to absorb the energy of the crowd's laughter. It rose steadily into the sky. By the time the guards pushed through the crowd, the Fool was long ago. Colorful texts appeared in the air, which obviously was a parting "gift" to Madam Consul: Madam Consul, Thank you for the fuel! The laughter of the Belobog people was enough to power my spaceship so that I can reach Dadarose! I've sent the design plans for the sculpture to your residence. Happy business! - Your beloved "Fool", Ada

-1708260584.Text:
This is a letter composed of a series of messages.

(I)

Hey man, I heard the soil where you guys would go to excavate this time was a little loose. Be careful out there, I think it's a little too dangerous.

Take my word and think it over, it's better to not go than to be swindled by the salary promised by the foreman. No amount of money would be worth it if you're gonna end up buried under the rubble.

But you're not gonna listen, I know you all too well. Being that it's a new site, I bet the emergency shelter's not even ready yet, let alone the air filtration and oxygen systems. Be a little more prepared and bring extra rations with you.

We grew up together, so you should listen to me on this, right?

(II)

I can't even begin to express my thanks, brother!

It's because of your reminder that I brought a few extra biscuits with me. No one expected the mine to actually collapse, and when it did, the people responsible for building the support structures all ran off. In return for keeping this incident a secret, the foreman offered me a sizable compensation.

Let me tell you what happened.

We miners have an eye for this kind of thing. Our temporary bunker was reinforced with more layers. We weren't too far from the bunker when the tunnel collapsed. Except one man whose leg was crushed, most of us were lucky.

The best preparation for a mining accident is not to have one at all. Preparing extra biscuits won't do anything. We hadn't been rescued yet, and our rations were already gone.

But, we needed to think of a way to survive. One of our team members caught seven or eight Crystalline Lizards somewhere. We had no choice but to eat them. I've never eaten them before, but they say it's like chewing a pair of boots that the foreman had been wearing for 18 years.

But we had to survive.

We managed to come up with a recipe, which turned out to be pretty good. Here's the recipe for Crystalline Lizard Satay.

Broken bits from the compressed five-spice cake,
Leftover seasoning from a wild ferns salad,
Bread crumbs, also a few left in the pack,
Rock salt 3g,
Hammer peppers 0.5g,
Crystalline Lizard meat 7 servings. Be sure to clean all the crystals from the meat. It'll hurt your tooth badly.

Mix the spices, seasoning, and bread crumbs then marinate the lizard meat in the mixture. Try to avoid the lizard fluids, or else it'll end up tasting like medicine. That bad taste comes from the secretion.

Then just wait until the next day and the marinated meat will be softer, or it'll really be like chewing shoes' rubber.

Next, remove extra wooden frames to prepare a few wooden sticks. Soak the sticks in water and skewer the meat. A buddy found some candied mushrooms, so we added those in between the meat for extra flavor.

Finally, sprinkle some salt and chili powder several times gently onto the meat as it's grilling.

No matter how bad the meat tastes, you can swallow it as long as you have salt and chili powder.

The funniest thing is we were about to eat when the rescue team dug out the rescue tunnel. "We were trying to rescue you as soon as possible, and you're here eating satay!"

Try it when you have the chance. Surprisingly, it was barely edible.

(III)

I'm glad to hear you survived, my friend.

I admire you for being so optimistic when you were trapped down there for so long.

I tried that Crystalline Lizard recipe you gave me. That taste... are you trying to kill me?

When you have the time, go find Natasha. There's something wrong with your taste buds.

We grew up together, so you should listen to me on this, right?

1020622771.Text:
To Madam Rand:

Last week, the 3rd Survey Team's work was met with tremendous resistance. We only managed to advance seventy meters. As the survey team progressed, we encountered more and more strange monsters, not to mention the unnatural phenomena that have scared many people.

This week, we have lost three of our brothers and sisters, with ten or so people wounded. Everyone has remained calm, but as captain, I know all too well the situation. The surveying gets more difficult by the day, and we're in desperate need of reinforcements.

I heard the Silvermane Army has completed their two-month search for refugees and have returned to Belobog. On behalf of all the survey teams in the fissure, I request your approval to deploy the main force of the Silvermane Army to support the surveying. I know this will be a difficult challenge for the soldiers who've just returned, but our survey team will not be stabilized without their strong support.

-901691530.Text:
【临时】可恶，可恶。就差一点点。
我们找到那家伙的实验室了，开门费了点功夫，不过我们还是进去了。里面堆满了药瓶，还有些唬人的奇怪玩意。我把药装满了背包，我发誓，我的手脚从来没这么麻利过。
但是我们都得意忘形了。刚离开那间屋子，后脚就有一批怪物开始追我们。我一路玩儿命地跑，药瓶子和笔记掉了一地…等我回过神来，身上已经全是伤痕了。
可恶，可恶。就差一点点…就差一点点，就能赚到大钱了……

1827191825.Text:
【临时】哥们儿，说出来你肯定不信，今天我在「恶魔之胃」里撞上了一个可怕的家伙。
和往常一样，我想来碰碰运气。你知道的，我胆子小，只敢在相对安全的地方晃悠晃悠。但是今天运气实在太差，我实在不甘心两手空空…所以我就硬着头皮拐进了那个弯儿。
鬼知道我当时有多害怕，哥们儿！撞见那家伙的时候，我差点吓得背过气去！那人蓬头垢面，身上一股呛人的味道。他看到我以后激动的要命，冲上来抓住我的胳膊，然后往我怀里塞了一大包东西。
「这些药，拿回去！给那些晶化病人服下去！」
他跟我说了一句话，然后扭头就走了。我当时惊魂未定，回过神来拔腿就跑。我发誓，我再也不想回这破地方了。

261107884.Text:
【临时】我要发财了，你敢相信吗？哥们儿真的要发大财了！       
上次碰见的那家伙，记得吗？我收回之前所有冒犯的评价。他简直就是我的福星！        
他塞给我的那批药，记得么？回家以后就被我扔到角落里了。直到有一天，有个得了晶化症的倒霉鬼来我家串门…我才突然想起来这回事儿。      
一开始我还挣扎了一下，但是转头一想，有啥坏处呢？反正得了这病也是死路一条，试试又怎么了？       
所以我把药分了他一点，让他每天服用，看看有什么反应。结果你猜怎么着？没过多久，他就腿脚灵便，哪哪儿不疼了！
谁能想到？那个衣冠不整的家伙居然是个隐居的神医！要是能再跟他要到一批药，我绝对能赚一大笔……

-1304976057.Text:
【临时】那家伙疯了，完全疯了！
我带了几个兄弟姐妹一块儿到这里找人，搜了个底朝天才把他找出来。他的表情越来越扭曲了，几乎认不出来是张人脸。    
我承认，跟他说卖药这事的时候，语气是有点儿兴奋过头了…但是他干嘛反应那么激烈？那家伙像发疯了一样，当着我们的面把药瓶摔碎了，还让我们赶紧滚……        
咱们怎么可能就这么善罢甘休？趁那家伙不注意，一个机灵的小妹从他身上顺走了钥匙…那肯定是某种重要的东西。       
保险起见，我们决定先把钥匙藏起来…这样就算被他逮到，他也从我们身上搜不出什么东西来。接下来，只要找到那疯子藏药的地方就行了……

1067676938.Text:
【临时】鼹鼠都用光了，药效还是没有验证。我花了一年多的时间才抓到这么几只，裂隙里可以用来实验的生物实在太稀少了……
……这里只有怪物，到处游荡的怪物。        
只剩最后一个选择了。我还有不少保存完好的地髓结晶，足够把我自己转化成晶化症的实验样本。       
过程应该不会太痛苦。接下来要做的就是和时间赛跑。

-498407003.Text:
【临时】我成功了，我成功了！结晶化的纹路不再扩散了，无法抑制的痛感也突然停止了。 
多少个日夜，多少次推翻重来…一切都是值得的！我早知道的，只有在裂隙里才能找到配制药物最关键的材料！        
我得把药物送出去，让那些患者得到治疗…我刚才是这么想的，直到我照了一次镜子。        
怪物…它映照出来的是什么样的怪物啊——我尖叫起来，砸碎了镜子。       
丑陋，可怖，肮脏。我在这里待的太久了。        
我不能这么回去。不能让她看到我的样子…我只能在这里等待。等待有人经过这里。

1423907297.Text:
【临时】我又看到她了，就在刚才，就在我的眼前。我最不想见到的就是她。       
幻象在我眼前不断闪过，折磨着我的神经。肉体的剧烈疼痛已经让我麻木。        
我知道自己闯了大祸，居然让人带走了那被诅咒的毒药。这罪孽只能由我自己承担。        
但该怎么纠正我的过错…我已经无计可施了。每一种原材料，每一种配比…我都已经试过了。        
我已经是个怪物了——怪物？对了，怪物！       
还有一条路没有尝试。裂界怪物身上的结晶，必须抓到它们，然后研究它们！       
我一定会成功的…你们都给我看好了。我会成功的……

-142176644.Text: 【临时】「告示牌上粘贴着精心裁下的剪报，原文刊载于《水晶日报》。」 对于共同体大家族「歌德家族」来说，过去的三个月是改变家族命运的三个月。 奥斯顿•歌德丧礼上的余波还未结束，两位公子又在公堂上吵得沸沸扬扬。焦灼的审判结束之后，格雷•歌德犯下「叛国罪」的消息再次让共同体民众大跌眼镜…这期间到底发生了什么？请关注本报记者的事件梳理。 奥斯顿•歌德离世不久 兄弟为遗嘱反目成仇 「歌德家族」早在共同体成立之前就已声名显赫，深得初任执政官「铁腕」斯维特兰娜信任。贝洛伯格内各个势力得以统一为整体，歌德家族可说功不可没。近日，家族的核心人物奥斯顿•歌德离世，社会各界名人出席了盛大的告别丧礼。 然而就在丧礼之上，奥斯顿•歌德对两个儿子偏爱有别的遗嘱迅速引发了争议。在遗嘱中，大儿子怀特分得总财产的45%、小儿子格雷仅得35%，剩下财产分给了孙辈。格雷对财产的分配不均提出了抗议，持续两个月的对簿公堂让两人彻底决裂。 格雷在接受采访时几度捶胸顿足：『老爷子不可能立下这么离谱的遗嘱——都是他儿子，凭什么不一样，有人动了手脚……不承认，我绝不承认！』 与小儿子格雷的激愤相比，大儿子怀特显得相对平静，他认为遗嘱具有法律效力，无可置疑：『父亲病重的时候，格雷一天家也没回。老人家走后，他倒三天两头跑来要钱，真令我寒心。』 遗嘱争执最后以格雷败诉结束。 格雷败诉后失踪叛国 怀特变卖家产赔偿罚金 当所有人以为格雷败诉，事情到此告一段落的时候，塔提维娜执政官宣判格雷•歌德犯下重大叛国罪，并罚没重金，一时间全国哗然。《水晶日报》第一时间联系执政厅及歌德家族，截止发稿日止，仍未得到双方的确切回应。可靠消息称：格雷•歌德已离开行政区，下落不明。 「叛国罪」虽未公开具体细节，但执政厅态度坚决，格雷之兄怀特•歌德也对该罪名并无异议。对于近日谣传「格雷携家人叛逃地底」，本报已得到执政厅的正式回复：此系无稽之谈。 『盛极必衰，这是歌德的家训。我们一直牢记在心。但没有想到，这一切来得这么快，这么突然。』谈到家族未来的发展，怀特目光忧郁，『为了支付巨额罚金，我不得不变卖家宅，辞退近半数的佣人。接下来，我打算用剩下的资金创业，希望能够重振家族之名，弥补舍弟犯下的不可饶恕之罪行。』

-1708260585.Text: 【临时】「告示牌上粘贴着精心裁下的剪报，塔提维娜执政官在新建成的共同体博物馆前发表了纪念讲话，并由《水晶日报》全文刊载。」 执政官亚历珊德拉离世50周年纪念讲话 今天是共同体第二任执政官亚历珊德拉离世五十周年的日子。伟人与世长辞之后，我们度过了共同体建国以来最为平稳的五十年：不用经历风雪中的求生，也不用担忧虚空中的怪物，邪恶的敌人被永远地囚禁在地底，再也不能对我们造成威胁。经历了天灾人祸之后的共同体，将逐步走上正轨，我们处在历史上最好的时期。这一切都离不开执政官所完成的成就，请向亚历珊德拉大人致以崇高的敬意。 在亚历珊德拉的执政期间，共同体的形态发生了翻天覆地的变化。敌人夺取了地髓的供应，共同体一度陷入冰天雪地，而她亲自走上前线战胜了恶魔，保障了地髓的产出。在执政晚期，亚历珊德拉大力支持普拉米亚的信仰，强调了普拉米亚的精神，再一次将我们团结起来。 从个人角度来讲，亚历珊德拉执政官对我来说是一个未曾谋面的英雄。当我还是执政官候补时，法茵卡执政官就经常与我讲起亚历珊德拉奶奶的故事，可以说，她似乎从来曾离我们而去，一直都在我们身边。共同体将会永远记住这些伟大的名字，再过五十年也不会忘记，再过一百年，五百年，一千年也会如此。 再一次，让我们向伟大的执政官亚历珊德拉致以深切的怀念。

1020622770.Text: 【临时】「告示牌上粘贴着精心裁下的剪报，原文刊载于《水晶日报》。」 共同体纪元144年6月19日，共同体最高执政官塔提维娜签署了101号条例宵禁令。该禁令宣布从即日起，每晚6点开始实施全城宵禁，至次日早上6点结束。宵禁期间，除银鬃铁卫及获得执政厅签署许可之必要工作者外，所有人不得外出。在新的政令出台前，101号宵禁令将持续生效。 塔提维娜执政官在宣布了宵禁令后，向《水晶日报》透露此次宵禁令的详细背景：由于共同体境外缺乏生命迹象，银鬃铁卫对共同体城市的出入口长期疏于管制。近日有不少市民反应有冰原狼在共同体境内出没，疑似贝洛伯格温暖的环境促进了耐寒种群的迁徙。目击报告引起了银鬃铁卫的高度关注，在宵禁期间，约有1100名银鬃铁卫将会进行全城排查，彻底排除冰原狼隐患。并且，以此次事件为契机，将加大出入口管控，严防危险生物进入城内。 塔提维娜再次强调：晚上6点至次日凌晨6点，除执行安保任务的银鬃铁卫，任何人在未获得许可的情况下严禁外出。同时，禁止任何个体隐瞒、藏匿、投喂冰原狼。违反规定的涉案人员将被严肃追责。

-901691531.Text: 22 days before the tax period Who would be responsible for this operation? I thought for a long time and decided it had to be Elaine. She has a booming voice and a good heart. She will surely understand my troubles. I talked to her for a long time, repeating my plan numerous times, not only to strengthen my memory, but to make sure I didn't leave out any details. She didn't react like I thought she would and only listened to my complaints in silence, with tears in her eyes. We would carry out the plan three days later. When I give the signal, Elaine would begin shouting to alert others of an imminent disaster. I'll pretend to lead the miners away from the disaster while she secretly slips away to the secret tunnel nearby and lets in Svarog's men. Elaine asked me why I was doing it and I told her everything. She didn't question my motives... Such an understanding child, I hope she will leave this damned mine one day. 20 days before the tax period Nobody knows how much determination it took to walk out that door. I thought Hook was already asleep, but she suddenly came out of her room when I was changing into my work uniform. My poor baby girl... she was so sleepy that she was wobbling as she walked. Hook asked me where I was going and I told her I had to work all night for two days. I could sense her dismay since her birthday was coming up. However, I knew that I would not make it to that day. The pain in my body made it unbearable to speak, even the pen in my hands right now is shaking violently. I convinced her to go back to bed. The image of her small figure walking back to her room was too much for me to handle. This is the last thing I can do for her. I must not fail.

1827191824.Text:
【临时】宝贝莉拉：

最近过得好吗？

抱歉，这封信上的内容被我改了又改，因为我不知道它什么时候能被交到你的手上。我的想法每时每刻都在变化，想要传达给你的东西也会随着心情改变。

爸爸想告诉你，我在执行的是一项十分机密的任务，而且充满了危险。但是不要害怕，因为爸爸是身经百战的银鬃铁卫，没有什么事情能吓到我。你应该学到过「裂界」吧？那是个可怕的地方，爸爸每天都在和「裂界」里的怪物搏斗，为了保护共同体的人们而战斗。这是一份相当艰难，但同时也令我自豪的工作。

爸爸不知道还要在这里呆上多久，但是我已经等不及想要和你见面了。我知道你一直喜欢研究书本上那些千奇百怪的动物，等以后有了机会，我再亲口跟你讲讲我在「裂界」里看到的那些珍奇生物。

爱你的爸爸

261107883.Text:
【临时】爸爸：

好久没联系了，你身体还好吗？

家里一切都好。毕业以后，我加入了一家杂志社工作，现在还有了自己负责的专栏。不知道你有没有听过《远行者》这本杂志？如果条件允许，我可以给你寄几本期刊过去。

看了你笔下的工作内容，我想说的是：我也真心为你感到骄傲。这不是因为你立下了多少军功，或是打倒了多少危险的敌人，而是因为你一直都把家人放在第一位。我身边有许多人没能顺利完成学业，或是在毕业之后就过上了食不果腹的苦日子，究其原因就是他们没有能充当自己坚实后盾的家庭。为了维持这个家，你努力付出了一切，对此我很感激。

有时间的话，就回家来看看吧。你不在的这段时间里，妈妈的厨艺长进了很多。

爱你的莉拉

-1304976058.Text:
【临时】理查德•巴拉金（共同体前25年——共同体29年）

共同体前25年，出生于未知南方城市。

共同体前19年，父亲去世（父亲身份已无记录）。

共同体前14年，母亲去世（母亲身份已无记录）。

共同体前2年，独自旅行至贝洛伯格，定居。

共同体0年，加入贝洛伯格第一裂界探查队。

（此处记录丢失）

共同体16年，因工负伤，退出裂界探查队。

共同体19年，女儿萨琪拉•巴拉金出生。

共同体22年，儿子诺兰•巴拉金出生。

共同体25年，重回裂界探查队。

共同体29年，在执行任务过程中失踪。

1067676937.Text:
【临时】致巴拉金家族的后人：

永远不要丢下手中的扳钳和钻头。

无需与他人争执，也无需博取他人赏识。

只要手握它们，你就拥有改变世界的力量。

理查德•巴拉金

-1014237.Text: If anyone finds themselves on the paths oversaw by the Aeons, then they would gain the ability to perceive things far far away, seeing all that there is in the light years of the galaxy. Many believe that this is the only connection the Aeons and mortals can ever have.

1161785177.Text: The birth of the universe is a mistake, and civilization is a growth of cancer birthed from vast stellar. War is the only common language known to all intelligent lifeforms.\nTo correct the course and to rid the universe of the stain, Nanook must become the avatar of chaos.

1968354231.Text: All things bear unanswered questions, and there is an answer to everything.\nThe astral computer originally meant to provide answers to the universe ascended to become an Aeon after its upgrades.\nErudite hopes to understand the universe and solve all of its mysteries.

1565069704.Text: A gathering of Aeons from multiple divine worlds of music. The glorious Xipe is in song of joy and happiness.\nTo battle the brutality of the laws of the universe, intelligent lifeforms must rid themselves of selfishness and tend to the weak, forming a harmonious song—— To compensate for the weak, and to protect life with death.

-1567098177.Text: The drinkers of the worlds, the unsatisfied devourer, the black hole with thought. They are Aeons and ancient beasts at the same time.\nIn the eyes of Oroboros, life is a flickering fragment floating in the sea of Void, destined to return to the darkness along with the stars which birthed them—— This darkness is within the depths of their mouths.

-1014236.Text: It is the unique birthright of an intelligent lifeform to understand the joys of a living being. Rocks and stars cannot understand the humor of life.\nThe search for a formidable adversary, the need for games to pass time, the yearning for an ending where the results don't matter.\nThe lust for laughter with tears, the pursuit of an unfortunate misunderstanding, and the quest for a song that brings life to your body.

402270291.Text: Regardless of the size of the sect, they all believe that their faith bears the will of the Aeons, countless sects even claim to be the emissaries of a certain Aeon. Most of the Aeons have found no interest in the ways of these sects, some, however, find it extremely amusing to watch the ants in their fervent labor. They may provide careful guidance, or create mysticism to alter the progress of the sect. This is their way of influencing the world.\n\nThough most of the Aeons remain distant, the vast number of sects and factions have made the stars a lively place. They carry banners and flags, forming allies and foes, fighting those who stand against their beliefs. The galaxy became a lively place indeed.

1968354232.Text: It remained so for many years until a girl with red hair discovered its existence. She was fascinated by the stories the engine and the star rails had in store. She spent countless hours repairing the cracks and evening the dents, restoring the express to its original form. The curious girl then set foot onto the express and began her trailblazing voyage across the galaxy.\n\nThe Astral Express made stops at every station, picking up rushing passengers along the way. Many continue to join and depart from the voyages of the express. The travelers all come from different worlds, shouldering different burdens, and heading to a different destination, but once they're all on the Astral Express, they all share the same voyage experience. And that is why Himeko and the express do not hesitate to open the doors of the Astral Express to anyone willing to share this experience, even if it means that they hold different agendas at heart, even if they were of ill-intentions.

-357244596.Text: Doctors of Chaos uphold the creed of the Healing Force, if they are able to prove to IX that existence is not nothing, they could free the large and lonely soul from the depths of the void. However, it is a paradoxical wish, for it cannot be achieved by mere mortals, but the Doctors of Chaos seem to enjoy trying. The moment they perceived the worthlessness of life, a glance from the distant galaxy inspire a futile revolt against their destiny.

-404298762.Text:
【临时】人生苦短，遍地纷扰。尘世的欲望总会招引祸端，稍纵即逝的生命令凡人陷入疯狂。为了摆脱阳寿给文明带来的苦楚和负担，诸多世界派出虔信“丰饶”的使徒，试图寻找名声远扬的“药师”，并从衪手中求得那一方能赐予永生的良药。
 
“求药使”们身份各异，既有驱驭舰队的亲王显贵，也有孤身上路的浪人陌客。他们的共同之处仅在于对“长生不老”的痴迷和执念。仙舟联盟频频劝阻盲信者们，警告他们潜藏在“丰饶”赐福背后的陷阱——但结果从不如意。傲慢与恐惧每每胜过理智之声，使者们总会继续踏上一意孤行的旅途。
 
在诸多“求药使”的行列中，有多少人得以一睹“丰饶”的尊容，又有多少人能为自己的文明带回济世的“良药”？无人能够统计。肆虐宇宙的长生魔物发出传遍银河的凄嚎，迷信永生的旅者们却仍然视而不见，充耳不闻。

-1567098175.Text: Thinking is existence, and memories are proof of that. All things that make up the material world eventually perish, but they live on in another way, Method.

-1014234.Text:
【临时】在搜集并贮存宇宙记忆的“流光忆庭”中，藏匿着一群行止颇为极端的忆者——与珍视一切存在痕迹的同僚们不同，他们认为世间记忆有高低优劣之分，而“浮黎”所创造的净土纯洁宝贵，其空间绝不应被毫无价值的记忆所填占。
 
“焚化工”们自认背负着为星神筛洗记忆的义务。他们偷取忆者们搜集的库存，并将窃来的记忆划分三六九等。有价值的内容被悄然奉还，伪装成无事发生；而被擅自标记为糟粕的记忆则被彻底销毁遗弃，再无踪迹可寻。
 
“焚化工”声称其所作所为是在清除垃圾，最终目的是减轻“浮黎”的负担，为衪编织记忆净土的伟大工程做出些微贡献。但“流光忆庭”对之嗤之以鼻，因为宇宙间的点滴碎片皆有其留存的意义，凡人无权裁定记忆的价值。

-404298761.Text:
The Actors ride a gondola that spans the stars. They collect and wear faces and masks from different worlds, symbolizing the races that have passed and those that have not. At the same time, they have also recorded the sorrows of intelligent life. They compose magnificent tragedies for heroes entering the end and sing graceful elegies for dying stars.

Mourning Actors advocate celibacy and penance, hoping it will dilute the Aeon of Joy's sweet allure to the universe.

However, joy and sadness are two sides of the same coin. Their very essence is engraved into the souls of all beings. The Aeon of Joy sees the potential strength in the beauty of tragedy. Perhaps out of his fondness for dark humor, Aha blessed these people with his astral powers, playfully helping them spread their renouncement of joy across many galaxies.

1161785180.Text: The fools believe the truth of the world is a joke, and that the ultimate meaning of all things lies in mere laughter. The universe is merciless, but there is joy to eliminate pain, dilute sorrow, resist nothingness, and heal wounds. Laughter, the gift of intelligent races, is the only answer.

-43494578.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"The hunter's eyes are the clearest when the hunter pulls back the arrow in the bow string."

-1609578519.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"Life is the existence of order beyond a certain threshold. Its birth is the final answer to death, declaring an end to a chaotic era."

1119304836.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"Like their birth, it is natural for things to disappear. Time erodes everything, turning all tangible things to dust."

-446779105.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"Only those who aren't affected by time can survive in time."

-2012863046.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"He stared into the void, like a dark sun at the end of all matter. It mouthed the answer to all questions:               . The answer couldn't be expressed in any human language, but could be understood by all."

716020309.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"Fingers make a fist, strength in numbers. I give myself for something higher - us."

-850063632.Text: A rare power condensed from time. It is all the insignificant moments that weave a magnificent fate.\n\n"Human extinction is inevitable. And the library will live on: lonely, immobile, and hidden from the public. Its vast collection is useless, yet pure from corruption."

-43494547.Text:
You're awake... Thanks.
"You... saved my life, Natasha. The one who should be saying thanks... is me."
"No, I should be thanking you for... holding on until I finished the surgery."

The doctor and patient smiled wearily at each other and said nothing more.

-1609578488.Text:
The dim light outlines the dust tracks,
As a young girl talks sweetly in her sleep.
A silent figure appears behind her.
"Tsk tsk tsk, you're cuter without those glasses."
The guitarist glances at her sleeping face, muttering:
"Good night, genius girl that likes to worry."

1119304867.Text:
So happy I got to see Pom-Pom in new clothes today! I like it~
"Today's my birthday! I'm still as cute as ever."
"Today, I finally finally finally have a junior! Isn't that right, {NICKNAME}?"

I accidentally clicked the shutter many times without thinking.
Is it to capture the happiness in that moment?
Or it is to capture the me in that moment?

-446779074.Text:
Shouldn't we get off? It's our stop.
A chuckle came from above. His eyes moved, but he didn't look up.

"To be clear, March always says you're expressionless, but I don't agree."
"The train's archives show traces of an update, but the results weren't saved."
"So... which entry are you having trouble with?"

He grasped his pen tighter as he looked at the bloody scene drawn in the notebook from memory.

"I see. It's the one about you..."

-2012863015.Text:
An old photograph sits on a table in the Consul residence.
Her gaze falls on it occasionally, but she finds herself avoiding it.
In her memories, there was always a hint of sadness, no matter if she appeared gentle or bitter.
She's afraid of feeling those emotions, so she often chooses to ignore it.

-43494613.Text:
In this unpleasant world, the best fairy tales are always sad.
They are written with self-sacrificing nobility and souls tortured by loneliness.
They exude a boundless sadness, transparent and poignant,
Showing the burden of life itself.

-1609578554.Text:
A young girl smiles subtly.
"Why?"
"Here and now, I'm alone..."
"But I feel... very lively."

1119304801.Text:
Svarog, if I'm not here one day, I leave the database in your care.
(Who... left a message in my records...)
"Svarog, do you know if something is irreplaceable?"
(Irreplaceable... Just replace parts if it's broken...)
"Svarog, you will find it one day."
(Those things... will only be trouble...)

"When that time comes, you can get out of here."

-446779140.Text:
Remember, the war with the monsters cannot be won by retreating.
"You are the iron wall of the Union, and Belobog stands behind you..."
"You cannot retreat under any circumstances!"

She looked up to see the frozen earth and endless winter.
Business as usual.
Only the back at which she had been staring was gone.

-2012863081.Text:
Welt...?
"It's a... great name, right?"
"Then..."
"From now on, you are Welt."
"Herrscher's core... and the mission to protect the world..."
"It's up to you, Welt."
