1462041775.Text: I see my brother has decided to dump guest-welcoming on your plate too. Say, why don't you become one of the family, Pela?

-1266841588.Text: Aww, you're just too cute! I wouldn't want to share you with him anyway~ I wrote a new song, wanna try it out later?

1462041866.Text: These are orders from the Consul herself.

1462041869.Text: To pass unnoticed among the people, devils conceal themselves with a human visage. Just as the teachings foretold... the Devil's Children come to us in our own image.

1462041868.Text: Devil's Children!? We came from outside! ...How many times do we have to say it!?

1462041871.Text: Outside the Union, there are no civilizations left in the world. This is the truth stated in Plamya's Admonishments.

-1266841492.Text: Plamya's truth is irrefutable and incontestable.

-1266841493.Text: But we really did come from outside.

-1266841490.Text: Ice and snow have engulfed this world. Plamya's providence is the only reason why the Union still exists.

-1266841488.Text: The tool of the devils' deceit is their honeyed tongues, with which they utter sweet-sounding blasphemies.

-1266841486.Text: I shall follow my decree. Bring them before the supreme Consul for judgment.

1462041833.Text: Who's the new girl? She's scary!

1462041830.Text: We seem to have gotten off on the wrong foot...

1462041831.Text: Devil's Children? What's that supposed to mean?

1462041836.Text: She appears to hold a higher rank than Gepard, and has some... innate animosity toward us.

1462041837.Text: How's that fair? We haven't done anything bad yet!

1462041835.Text: Well, I do kinda feel like we tend to bring trouble to every world we visit.

1462041840.Text: If they really want to condemn us, they'll trump up a charge.

1462041841.Text: They may skip straight to the punishment.

-1266841523.Text: She must be in a position of power.

-1266841522.Text: She has some... innate animosity toward us.

-1266841525.Text: How's that fair? We haven't done anything bad yet!

-1266841519.Text: Well, I do kinda feel like we tend to bring trouble to every world we visit.

-1266841518.Text: If they really want to condemn us, they'll trump up a charge.

-1266841521.Text: They may skip straight to the punishment.

-1266841520.Text: I've got a bad feeling about this...

-1266841514.Text: *sigh* It didn't take much for us to go from guests to criminals.

-1670126050.Text: The only difference is the kind of hotel you get to stay in.

-1670126049.Text: Is this really a good time for witty remarks!?

-1670126052.Text: You win some you lose some.

-1670126048.Text: And just how are you planning to do that?

-1670126047.Text: I'm not sure yet... We should make a move once an opportunity arises.

-1670126041.Text: The girl's hostility troubles me. My instincts tell me we're in great danger... and I don't plan to hand ourselves to them on a silver platter.

-104042109.Text: Whoa, seems like 7th has had a bad influence on you.

-104042108.Text: ...Yeah, you feeling okay Danheng? I'm usually the one talking about escape plans.

-104042110.Text: Hey! Danheng, look! There's a fissure over there! We can use the Fragmentum to escape the guards!

-104042104.Text: But there's no time to lose! You were the one talking about escape — this might be our only chance!

1462042164.Text: The infernal shall one day return to hell... They really are devils.

1462042158.Text: Do you understand now, soldiers? This is why Madam Consul instructed me to apprehend them.

1462042159.Text: Lady Bronya... what are you planning to do?

1462042160.Text: Bring them to me, Bronya. Do this for me.

1462042161.Text: I will bring them before the Consul.

1462042258.Text: 【临时】请各位不要离我太远，否则，我不能保证你们的人身安全。

1462042261.Text: 【临时】还是乖乖跟着这个小姑娘吧……

-971219995.Text: You opened the letter and it read: Dear Mr. Carl Eyre, your work files of the month are in my hands. Don't worry, they are safe right now.

-971219996.Text: But if you want them back home safely as well, put $100 in the shaft of the fishing rod in the chest locked by your wife on top of your closet before 3 PM this Saturday. Bring this fishing rod out and I'll contact you again.

-971219997.Text: Listen, you can tell your wife about this but dare not to call the police, or your work of the entire month will become a pail of garbage. What would your superior think of you? I bet your wife wouldn't let this happen either.

-971219990.Text: This mailbox looks like it had been through a lot. You faintly saw someone who mistakenly put a sausage bread into the mailbox——or else, the mailbox was mistaken for a rubbish bin.

-971219991.Text: This is a phone booth. People use it to contact friends from afar.

-971219992.Text: This is a phone booth. You find it novel because you were born in the mobile era.

-971219993.Text: You stood next to the booth and began to think of the fantasy drama you used to watch. You couldn't help wondering about how it feels like to rip off a shirt in a phone booth.

-971219986.Text: Looking at this phone booth, you felt like you could transform into a super hero.

-971219987.Text: Since you've only been to the district so far, you began to wonder: how big is the Union exactly? How many people rely on phones to contact each other?

1757663361.Text: You approached this small device, feeling its warmth coming out.

1757663360.Text: The heater distributes warmth. It’s inconspicuous, but the people of the Union can't live without it.

1757663359.Text: Geomarrow ores excavated from the underground are converted into heat in here.

1757663358.Text: As you approached it, you felt the warmth gently rubbing your leg like a lovable pet.

1757663365.Text: The designer must have spent so much effort on building this heat supply network in this eternal city.

1757663364.Text: The little heater stands in there, resisting nature's cold wind with its weak power.

1757663362.Text: The street lamps are not on yet.

1757663369.Text: It reminds you of a penalty from the ancient world where misconducted people would be hung on the street lamps in a black cloak.

1757663368.Text: In the other worlds, lamps make lovable nests of the birds, but in Belobog, lamps stand all alone.

191579420.Text: When the night comes, street lamps lighten up people's way home.

191579419.Text: You're curious to know whether street lamps are also supplied by Geomarrow as well.

191579418.Text: The metal rod of this lamp already began to crack.

191579417.Text: The stain on the lamp tells all about its history.

191579424.Text: 【临时】书随便挑，《水晶日报》不带一份走么？

-971219759.Text: You're wrong! Never put animal's fur in the wet garbage bin!

-971219765.Text: Knowledge of garbage sorting swarms into your head.

-971219764.Text: You're immersed in the joy of sorting garbage...

-971219763.Text: Paper fragments are all over the black garbage bags. They're shredded so evenly that almost make you think that this world has unlocked the technology of making a paper shredder.

-971219769.Text: Hello... Are you going to fix it all up?

-971219768.Text: Without realizing how long it took, you finally completed this great work: thousands of paper fragments have become a wrinkled complete piece of paper. It was a letter between two citizens, but the content was pretty unacceptable.

1757663594.Text: The content was way too scary that the letter crumbled into flying ashes as you finished reading it.

1757663596.Text: Right, no point to waste time on it.

1757663597.Text: An overwhelming fishy smell came out of the garbage bin as you opened it. But there was no fish in there but a fish-related advertising flier. Was that the so-called synaesthesia?

1757663590.Text: There're two garbage bins. One stands glamorously, one lies down in rust.

1757663591.Text: You wanted to lift it up but ended up not doing it, because a garbage bin has its own dignity as well.

1757663592.Text: A pretty postcard was lying inside the bin and it read, "Stop here. Only these people have the right to ruffle Belobog's garbage bin, Tatalov, garbage king and me."

1757663593.Text: Then it went, "And the 45 cleaners." There's nothing written on the back of the postcard.

1757663587.Text: Even garbage bin in the city sounds pretty serious.

191579653.Text: You found another wrinkled hand-written flier in the bin. On top of it, something was written in beautiful handwriting, "The Rightful Rights Society."

191579654.Text: It basically tried to convince people who have given up their hobbies because of family and their sense of responsibility to join this weird organization.

191579655.Text: Members of this organization cover up and help each other to get away from family or work on the weekend (even on weekdays) and enjoy a day of their own. They call it a move "to uphold the rightful rights."

191579649.Text: Actually, I'm a little tempted... Sometimes I wish I could spend a day doing whatever I want!

191579650.Text: What kind of charity it is!

191579651.Text: Yes, they're way too considerate...Sometimes I wish I could spend a day doing whatever I want!

191579652.Text: Then you'll spend a day sleeping in your cabin.

191579645.Text: A car in Belobog. An old-fashioned (in your eyes) vehicle.

191579646.Text: The body of the car is cold. Seems like no one has been driving it for quite long.

-1374504288.Text: There's a thick layer of dirt on the car window. The more you look into it, the more you feel like you're looking into the abyss.

-1374504287.Text: The dark car window looks you back just like the gaze of IX from the deep black hole.

-1374504286.Text: The car body slightly steams. Seems like its owner has just left.

-1374504285.Text: These are cars shared by the residents of the Union. They could be parked anywhere. You began to envy this world without parking fees.

-1374504292.Text: Peculiar marks are drawn on the license plate which makes you believe that traffic restriction here must be pretty loose.

-1374504291.Text: Although you have already been on exotic vehicles like the Astral Express, cars in Belobog still amazed you.

-1374504290.Text: In some worlds, minors are not allowed to drive. It must be the same here for safety reasons, you think.

-1374504289.Text: This iron gate is ice cold, you have no idea what's behind it.

-1374504296.Text: This iron gate looks like it's concealing the biggest secret of the entire universe.

-1374504295.Text: You wanted to sit down but you failed. But you were sure that you'd make it next time.

1354379068.Text: This flower has eight petals with white dots at the end of each. They look like melting drops of the first snow.

1354379069.Text: You saw a long and think plant. The stem of which was pillar-shaped and had holes inside.

1354379070.Text: A flower in red and yellow color. The shape of it looks like the sun.

1354379063.Text: You saw an indigo sphere. Thick petals covered its pistil. Nothing special about it.

1354379064.Text: A colorful flower. Seems cheap just from the look of it.

1354379065.Text: You wondered which flower was the so-called Marquise, but apparently it was not in here.

1354379066.Text: You saw a sign that said, "Marquise, the Specialty of the Store. Never on-sale!"

1354379059.Text: You saw a sign that said, "First Snow, Easy to preserve, Best home decoration!"

1354379060.Text: You saw a sign that said, "Fuxia Bamboo - propitious and cheap!"

-211704874.Text: You saw a sign that said, "Sunflower - flourishing goods!"

-211704873.Text: You saw a sign that said: "Ball Peony - right at its best bloom of the year!"

-211704872.Text: You saw a sign that said, "Rainbow - buy 1 and get 6 for free!"

-211704871.Text: A silvermane guard defends the road downward.

-211704878.Text: The silvermane guard coldly stares at you to drive you away.

-211704877.Text: The guard seems impatient of your arrival. Apparently he didn't want you to approach him.

-211704876.Text: For no reason, a cute and innocent female voice popped up in your head: Explore the areas below next time!

-211704875.Text: The silvermane guard defends the road. This guy has to stay here all day long, he must be bored! Plus, it does no good to his knees.

-211704882.Text: The silvermane guard stands still like a statue. You couldn't help wondering: Maybe he was excluded and suppressed to be allocated here.

-211704881.Text: You've pictured the entire life of the silvermane guard in your head, about how he graduated, get allocated, achieved at a young age, offended someone evil and eventually get banished.

-1777788815.Text: Wait, is he a real person? Don't tell me it's a statue.

-1777788814.Text: Hmm... seems like it is a statue.

-1777788813.Text: You noticed that as you approached, the expression of the guard's eyes began to get fiery.

-1777788819.Text: A hut that sells food. But no one is inside.

-1777788818.Text: This is a shop in the plaza. Unexpectedly, there's another shop in the opposite side of the plaza.

-1777788817.Text: There are 2 huts in the plaza, one is a shop, the other one is also a shop.

-1777788816.Text: The food in the shop attracted your attention, but apparently no capitalist had promoted self-service yet in this world.

-1777788823.Text: You saw the menu read, "Moo milk, the daily fresh milk! $2 per bottle."

-1777788822.Text: ... How did they manage to deliver fresh milk without having a person on duty?

951094540.Text: You saw the menu read, "Belobog's instant soup tin! Delicious and nutritious in all sorts of flavors! Grape flavor: $4 per can."

951094542.Text: You saw the menu read: "Parterre honey drink! Best honey drink of the world! Sold out."

951094536.Text: You saw the menu read: "Roger's drink, the price of water offers the taste of bread! $5 per bottle."

951094537.Text: ... Water in this world is so expensive.

951094538.Text: You saw the menu read: "Tasty Skewer! You can't live without it! The owner is back to the hometown, come again another time."

951094539.Text: ... I feel bad for those who already can't live without it.

951094532.Text: You saw the menu read: "Fire cream. Legendary taste of the summer. $1 each. Alert: Don't buy it if you're allergic to chili."

951094533.Text: ... Fire cream, sounds like a mythic creature Danheng once described.

-1327450121.Text: A red mailbox with an edge of a white letter coming out. This letter is unfortunately stuck.

-1327450119.Text: You carefully nipped the letter out with your fingers.

-1327450125.Text: You touched the letter lightly, it soon lost its balance and disappeared in the dark inside of the mailbox. You felt like your mark of judgment (if something like this ever exists in this world) just elevated.

-1327450123.Text: You shrugged and left. Every moment, someone in this world was either born, sick or dead. There are billions of mails that aren't properly delivered in this universe. So what's so special about this one?

-1327450122.Text: Someone will come and deal with it. Actually, you're sure that you won't see this letter again next time when you come to this mailbox.

-1327450129.Text: The letter read: Could someone with a good soul please buy me a stamp and post it out for me? Thanks so much!

-1327450128.Text: You opened the letter, a message was written on the edge of a newspaper, Sister, send me $50 ASAP! From your loved brother.

1401433234.Text: This is a red mailbox. Seems like the postman has just cleared it up. It stands on the street all alone.

1401433235.Text: Nothing seems special about this mailbox, but somehow you feel like something is wrong. You looked at it carefully again and there was a white letter hidden in the shadow of the mailbox.

1401433237.Text: You picked up the letter and put it into the mailbox. Deep inside your heart, a sublime existence smiled and winked and gave you a big thumb up, as if it was estimating the explosion distance using the jump eye method.

1401433233.Text: You shrugged and left. Every moment, someone in this world was either born, sick or dead. There are billions of mails that aren't properly delivered in this universe. So what's so special about this one?

1401433226.Text: Someone will come and deal with it. Actually, you're sure that you won't see this letter again next time when you come to this mailbox.

1401433227.Text: The letter read, To Carl Eyre in room B03 building 35, 6th street, district.

-971219665.Text: A garbage bin. Don't get close without courage.

-971219662.Text: A garbage bin in Belobog. Great place to hide something.

-971219663.Text: A garbage bin. A good match of yours during your downfalls.

-971219669.Text: You haven't missed anything. Nothing worth noticing in this bin.

-971219666.Text: No point to investigate a garbage bin.

1757663691.Text: These are two trash cans, not remarkable in any way.

1757663690.Text: You've been staring at them for a while, but they're just normal trash cans.

1757663693.Text: Ugh... What do you want to do?

1757663692.Text: You try to hold back the urge to open them.

1757663687.Text: As you continue staring at the trash cans, they seem to turn before your very eyes. The edges were no longer rusty and the dents were smoother.

1757663686.Text: From under the lid came a faint golden glow - sweet and alluring.

1757663689.Text: For a moment, the trash cans turned into treasure chests.

1757663683.Text: You take a deep breath and open the lid: it's empty...

1757663682.Text: Wait! You reach deeper into the trash can. There's a piece of iron scrap on the bottom. Your hard work paid off! You finally found the treasure!

191579750.Text: You look back at your companions and see their complicated expressions.

191579749.Text: ... You don't need to explain. I get it. That urge is too great for you to resist.

191579752.Text: There's no turning back once you've walked down this path.

191579751.Text: There is a leisurely elegance to the trash cans, which exudes a commanding confidence. It may have something to do with the letter fragment in its possession.

191579746.Text: You walk around the mailbox inspecting it, but don't find any more letters.

191579747.Text: An unopened bottle of Jim Roger bread soda.

191579742.Text: A copy of the "Crystal Daily" with Consul Cocolia on the front page, surrounding by Silvermane Guards. Crumpled paper ball.

191579741.Text: Some withered flowers whose thermal dome is broken.

-1374504191.Text: To understand a city, go through its trash can.

-1374504192.Text: Two garbage bins. One for dry garbage, one for wet garbage.

-1374504189.Text: Well then, which one does a coconut shell belong to?

-1374504195.Text: Correct. Coconut shell doesn't rot and it's not toxic, you can't recycle it either, so it belongs to the dry garbage!

-1374504193.Text: You're wrong! Although the coconut shell is a fruit waste, it's hard and it doesn't crumble easily, so it doesn't go to the wet garbage!

-1374504199.Text: Knowledge of garbage sorting swarms into your head.

-1374504200.Text: You're immersed in the joy of sorting garbage...

1354379164.Text: The inside of this garbage bin was bright and clean. You've never seen a garbage bin this clean before...

1354379163.Text: Rather, you've never seen anything this clean before. This garbage bin was like the incarnation of purity in this universe, which is so absurd.

1354379165.Text: You stretched your hand out, and your fingers gently stroked the body of the bin. A soft, tender, smooth and unconstrained sense of touch came right from your fingertip...

1354379160.Text: You were greatly satisfied. You felt like the princess who slept on top of 9 layers of mattress. At this moment, all concerns and exhaustion and sense of duties flew away...

1354379159.Text: No doubt, this was the most perfect garbage bin in this world.

1354379161.Text: You took a good look at it. The inside of this bin was dust-free and perfectly black, like a dark material cloud cluster surrounding IX.

1354379156.Text: It grabbed your attention, and your eyes were fixed on it... You then saw yourself, you saw March 7th and Danheng, you saw the fate of the Astral Express and the ending of the future...

1354379155.Text: You force yourself to stop staring and everything disappeared. It feels like a dream of fantasy.

-211704778.Text: Only you know how much courage it took to move your gaze away from the most perfect garbage bin of this world.

-211704775.Text: What's up, what's in it? Your face looks weird.

-211704776.Text: Nothing. You've decided not to tell her.

-211704781.Text: A huge paper cluster which contains some wrinkled paper fragments if you unfold it.

-211704782.Text: 1. "This will is going to be the footnote of my life. In the last manute of my life, I"

-211704779.Text: 2. "This will is going to be the footnote of my life. In the last minute of my life, I want to be honest. Generally speaking, I have nothing to done"

-211704780.Text: 3. "This will is going to be the footnote of my life. In the last minute of my life, I want to be honest. Generally speaking, I have nothing to do and there's no point to lave"

-1777788718.Text: A wrinkled hand-written flier was lying inside the garbage bin, it faintly read,

-1777788719.Text: Lowest price of love flowers today! Time to send your partner some flowers!

-1777788716.Text: This is not bad among all the other cliche advertising writings.

-1777788717.Text: Wow, I'd be so mad if someone ever gave me this.

-1777788723.Text: Oh wow, nice sense of humor {NICKNAME}.

-1777788720.Text: What do you like to give as present?

-1777788726.Text: ... But don't tell me it's flowers for sale or in clearance or from the street.

-1777788727.Text: You could smell a strong stink only by approaching the garbage bin...

951094637.Text: As if there was a black and twisted aura around it that clearly stated "get away."

951094636.Text: You want to open it even so?

951094638.Text: You held your breath and proceeded to the bin in the surprised eyes of your companions, you felt like a soldier charging in the rain of bullets.

951094633.Text: Speaking of which, why are you so interested in flipping garbage bins everywhere? What for?

951094635.Text: You decided to open the lid of the bin with a determined mind, at your first glance, you saw that strange stinky thing.

951094634.Text: For a moment, you felt like a monk with no feelings or desire at all as you quickly stretched your hands out and caught that thing.

951094629.Text: But you didn't feel delighted at all, because your companions just left you with their noses pinched.

-1327450024.Text: Wise choice. There must be something better out there in the world than what's in front of you. Although you don't know what that is yet, it stops you from crossing that line at this moment, which is good enough.

-1327450022.Text: Wise choice. You backed off and took a deep breath to let fresh air refill your lungs. Yes, this is the real life, this is what's called being alive. Knowing how to give up is the lesson of life.

-1327450023.Text: A nice-looking garbage bin, but it's just another garbage bin.

-1327450028.Text: A silver foil parchment letter. If it appeared next to a mailbox, you would have all the reasons to believe that it was just a wrongly delivered love letter, but it was a flier coupon instead... and it was lying inside a garbage bin.

-1327450029.Text: It read, "Today is Plamya's birthday, the person who receives this letter must forward it to three other people, or else bad luck will be stuck with you for the rest of the year."

-1327450026.Text: You wonder what happened to the person who threw it in here.

-1327450027.Text: Someone threw a book into the garbage bin. You picked it up. It was a long serial novel called Tale of the Winterlands. You have the 4th volume in your hands.

-1327450032.Text: You smelt a burnt smell as you approached the bin.

-1327450033.Text: This garbage bin looks like it has been burnt. The entire bin is twisted and some tanned materials covered the bottom of the bin. When the light comes in, they float lightly in the air as if they were dancing.

1401433331.Text: A pretty postcard was lying in the garbage bin and read, "Friend, you've crossed the line. It's not something you should touch. Signed, the garbage king."

1401433330.Text: A coin was glued to the back of the postcard. Guess this king understood the rules very well.

1401433333.Text: You've got a real competitor in terms of flipping garbage bins.

1401433332.Text: Why would someone call himself the garbage king? Is he not embarrassed?

1401433327.Text: You might have noticed, yes, there're so many garbage bins in this city, so much beyond your imagination. Someone proposed to reduce the amount of garbage bins before, or to initiate a garbage tax policy.

1401433326.Text: But it only increased the amount of garbage on the street. Now people in this city have come to the consensus that garbage bin is the other city-card of Belobog.

1401433329.Text: Two garbage bins. One for dry garbage, one for wet garbage.

1401433328.Text: Which one does Pom-pom's fur belong to?

1401433322.Text: Correct! Pom-pom's fur is hard to rot and it's not toxic, so it belongs to the dry garbage!

163865938.Text: —This planet really is one big snowball.

163865935.Text: This planet really is one big snowball.

163865934.Text: That's because the Trailblaze is granting us some power.

163865931.Text: In simpler terms, it allows us to adjust to harsh climates relatively easily.

163865932.Text: Ugh, you don't have to explain you know, it's not like knowing will make me feel any warmer.

163865945.Text: So, Danheng? Where should we go?

163865946.Text: ...Based on the data, the target is up ahead.

-1402218004.Text: Then what are we waiting for? Let's go.

-1402218003.Text: Slow down, let's have a quick meeting first—

-1402218008.Text: It's a Danheng thing. He likes to lay things out very, very, very, very clearly.

-1402218007.Text: Even the word "meeting" makes me feel tired.

-1402218010.Text: You are still young and chipper. Where did all these worries come from?

-1402218009.Text: Is this part of the train's company culture or something?

-1402217996.Text: No, it's just Danheng's company culture.

-1402217995.Text: ...At the bare minimum, there are some things that we should clarify.

1326665351.Text: Fine, I'll give you a chance. What were you going to say.

1326665352.Text: First, let's note the coordinates of where we arrived. We may need to come back to the Teleport Waypoint here if we want to return to the train.

1326665349.Text: Eh? Why!? Don't we have a tickets?

1326665350.Text: That brings me to my second point: Tickets aren't "get out of planet free cards."

1326665347.Text: We may encounter all manner of unforeseen circumstances during our mission. We need to make sure we have a way back.

1326665348.Text: Argh, you're such a scaredy pants. We can look out for ourselves, y'know.

1326665346.Text: Calm down, {NICKNAME}. There's nothing that can stop the three of us.

1326665359.Text: I mean c'mon — you've a Stellaron in your body, I have my special Six-Phased Ice powers, and Danheng... Uhh, he's got that mysterious past thing going for him.

1326665360.Text: I feel sorry for anyone who gets in our way!

-239418589.Text: Right? I don't want to be treated like a kid by Mr. Yang and Ms. Murata.

-239418592.Text: We'll show them. We'll ace this expedition!

-239418593.Text: Even you, Danheng. You don't want Himeko to always just see you as my caretaker, right?

-239418595.Text: I guess everybody's on the same page then? Let's go!

-239418581.Text: This is the spirit of trailblazing!

163866169.Text: This blizzard is strong. Don't get separated.

163866202.Text: Which direction are we headed in?

163866200.Text: Isn't it normally colder up north?

163866197.Text: Quite the opposite. On this planet, north is warmer.

1185502816.Text: There seems to be something over there... I saw it move!

1185502815.Text: ...It might be a wild beast native to this planet. I'd advise caution.

1185502848.Text: This isn't the same kind of ice that you were sealed in.

1185502849.Text: How did you know I was thinking about that!?

1185502755.Text: There used to be people living here.

1185502785.Text: Did you guys hear something? It sounded like someone's talking...

1185502786.Text: Don't be in such a hurry! The voice was coming from behind us! Why don't we check it out?

1185502787.Text: Ugh, the blizzard is raging. It's so cold...

1185502780.Text: Let's return to the main road.

1185502685.Text: Holding your breath won't help y'know...

1185502691.Text: The best way to deal with someone who's playing the ostrich.

1185502690.Text: Is to drag their head out of the sand... well, snow in this case.

1185502719.Text: My friends, I think that was a bit much, eh? There's nothing wrong with me crawling into some snow. No need to poke me with a spear, right?

1185502716.Text: Uh, but, I can't really blame you! Haha, I appeared too abruptly, so I deserved it. Of course, it had to happen! Otherwise, I would've never had the chance to become acquainted with such a jolly bunch as yourselves? Haha...

1185502717.Text: ...Is Captain Landau here yet? I know him quite well...

1185502723.Text: Wait, you aren't Bluecoats! My friends, you should've told me that earlier. There's no point in brothers fighting each other.

1185502720.Text: Pleasure to meet you. I'm Sampo Koski.

-1543380637.Text: Excellent. Well now, it's not every day you bump into living souls in this frozen wasteland. Where's your hideout? Can you hide me there? The Silvermane Guards are still after me.

-1543380639.Text: Huh... Is this your first time doing business? You haven't even prepared a safe house?... Uhh, you know it's a crime if you leave the Overworld without a permit, right?

-1543380629.Text: Oh, newcomers huh? Do you want to get your hands on some ancient relics? Want to make it big, eh? Hehe, that's fine, you can open your heart to me. I, Sampo Koski, will never let down a brother in need. There are tons of treasure out there, so let's get rich together, hahaha.

22703304.Text: But of course! I'm always glad to help out a newcomer — but make it quick or the Guards will catch us.

22703302.Text: Well, that depends on your definition of nearby. I wouldn't say it's far, but it's not really that near either. Regardless, if you continue north for a stretch, you'll arrive at the Union.

22703303.Text: Wait, you don't know the way? Wow, did you manage to gain control over a fissure and use it to travel out here or something? Very impressive, friends!

22703309.Text: You know, those sealed off, distorted-looking openings in space that seem as if a huge hole was dug into the air itself. Those things can take you anywhere once you're inside. They're dangerous, but pretty handy if you can find your way through them.

22703306.Text: He must be talking about the Fragmentum.

22703307.Text: Fragmentum? Is that what you call those things? That's fine with me. I mean there's no official name for them.

22703312.Text: What do you mean by the Union?

22703313.Text: Humanity's final fortress to ward off the Eternal Freeze? The city that received Plamya's providence? Well, at least that's what the people from the Overworld always say.

1588787245.Text: You haven't heard of the Union before? My dear friends, did you escape from the Underworld or something? Which route did you take? ...I—I mean, which fissure did you take?

1588787246.Text: Well, you don't have to tell me. I understand, it's a trade secret.

1588787243.Text: You keep talking about the Overworld and the Underworld. What exactly are you talking about?

1588787244.Text: There are two regions you need to know about. The one above ground is called the Overworld, and the one below ground is called the Underworld. The Overworld is full of happiness, and the Underworld is full of suffering. Residents of the Overworld are saints, and residents of the Underworld are devils.

1588787249.Text: That's what they say at least. There's actually not a lot of difference. Life's difficult wherever you live.

1588787247.Text: Ahh, the Bluecoats. The military power of the Union. Enforcers, police, soldiers...

1588787248.Text: To put it simply, it's the enemy that people in our line of business dread about.

1588787254.Text: You know, just lookin' around here and there... If there's a relic or something, then that's some quick cash... In general, things that the Bluecoats don't want us to do.

-783865750.Text: But as you know, a man will die for his wealth. There are troves of treasure scattered around the Union, and they don't even bother digging them up. It'd be a shame for it to all go to waste.

-783865749.Text: I had some bad luck this time. When I was sneaking out of the city, a bunch of Bluecoats spotted me. Even worse — that Gepard Landau was leading the squad... I had to hide in the snow to get them off my trail.

-783865751.Text: Uh, sure! Let's get out of here, who knows if the Guards are still on my case...

-783865745.Text: Uh, sure! Let's get out of here, who knows if the Guards are still on my case...

-783865748.Text: Can you show us the way? We aren't too familiar with things around here...

-783865747.Text: Well missy, showing you the way is easy enough, but—

-783865742.Text: B—But then I'm a naturally helpful person, haha! I, Sampo Koski, am the paragon of kindness. Follow me, friends, and keep quiet — don't want to be spotted by the Bluecoats.

1185502620.Text: Let's be open, friends. Where exactly are you from? Maybe you can indulge my curiosity. I mean, you guys could have ran me through back there, so you could say we've been through life and death together, right?

1185502622.Text: He won't understand. He'll just suspect we are lying to him.

1185502625.Text: That stings. I, Sampo Koski, may not be the brightest bulb in the room, but I can still understand what you're saying!

1185502653.Text: Are there colorful ice crystals where you're from, Sampo?

1185502654.Text: *sigh* ...Where I'm from, you wouldn't find snow, let alone ice.

1185502655.Text: That's a question for an Overworlder.

1185503082.Text: You folks don't seem to be from the Union... But you also don't seem to be from the Underworld.

1185503081.Text: Uh, better if you don't ask.

1185503080.Text: We came from outside. That's all you need to know.

1185503115.Text: The snow's thinning out, we're heading in the right direction. This is the blessed city, the only place impervious to the wind and snow... all thanks to Plamya. You guys at least know who Plamya is, right...?

1185503113.Text: Uh, you remember the Silvermane Guards I mentioned? That's them...

1185503110.Text: Help me, old friends! I don't want to be captured!

-118459097.Text: There are more of them now. He's got accomplices!

-118459099.Text: Uh... W—Well, let's find out together, accomplice!

-118459128.Text: I've apprehended his accomplices. Maybe he'll try to save them.

-118459134.Text: I hope he tries, I'll help you beat the heck out of him.

-118459133.Text: I get the feeling we'll be waiting a lifetime.

-118459132.Text: Even then he probably wouldn't show up.

-118459122.Text: Yeah, we'd never team up with someone like that!

-1684543072.Text: I'm not trying to create trouble, but... we're not friends with that scoundrel. Did you see how fast he ditched us?

-1684543071.Text: We rescued him from the snow out of the kindness of our hearts — we had no idea he might be using us to get past you! Are you really dumb enough to fall for his—

-1684543070.Text: Silence! How dare you offend Captain Landau!

-1684543069.Text: That'll do, Kyle. She was only defending herself — a Plamya-given right. But if she happens to be lying...

-1684543076.Text: Then the Consul will naturally mete out the punishment she deserves.

-1684543075.Text: You said you rescued that convict from the snow? You are aware of course, that citizens are forbidden from leaving the Union? That alone is already a grave offense.

-1684543074.Text: *sigh* You probably won't believe this... we're not from here.

-1281258544.Text: We came from somewhere far, far away.

-1281258543.Text: We heard there were humans here, so we decided to check it out!

-1281258542.Text: ...Far away? You mean, beyond the ruins?

-1281258536.Text: Utter nonsense! Captain, permission to silence them! This is blasphemy!

1447624810.Text: We're telling the truth — look at our clothes! We come from a place where fashion exists!

1447624811.Text: No, Kyle. We cannot judge the truthfulness of their statements just yet.

1447624812.Text: Look at their attire. It is very different to that of the Union. Not to mention their ancient-looking relics... We've never excavated anything like it before, and I haven't seen anything similar in our museums.

1447624806.Text: Note to mention, and call it intuition if you like...

1447624807.Text: ...but I sense something special about them... something that reminds me of the Madam Consul...

1447624808.Text: At the very least, Kyle, their judgment is not ours to pass.

1447624809.Text: If they truly are outsiders, then they're the first evidence of humanity outside the Union — the only evidence we've received in thousands of years. We must report to the Consul immediately.

1447624818.Text: They must not be treated like criminals before the Consul passes her judgment.

-2087827599.Text: Send a squad to patrol the city walls. He's got nowhere to run.

-2087827598.Text: We have a more urgent task at hand now.

-2087827597.Text: Well would you listen to that! I caused a bit of trouble and saved everyone! Thank you, March 7th!

-2087827603.Text: Quite right! I'm not gonna hold back this time: I'm a genius!

-2087827601.Text: I was scared for a moment, he's pretty fearsome.

-2087827590.Text: Don't get too comfortable. We aren't out of danger just yet.

641055756.Text: Please, come with me. I will take you to Belobog. If what you have said is true, then I believe the Supreme Consul will want to see you personally.

641055757.Text: I welcome you to the Union.

-2019278767.Text: 【临时】我…即将迎来时间的审判。

-2019278768.Text: 【临时】最后一次，踏足这个支离破碎的空间……

-2019278769.Text: 【临时】最后一次，登上我亲手筑垒的高塔……

-2019278770.Text: 【临时】最后一次…触碰这无常的命运。

-2019278772.Text: 【临时】我的一切…都将由你背负……

-2019278734.Text: Hah! See, they aren't brave enough to follow us! Freedom at last!

-2019278737.Text: Serves them right! Better luck next time, slowpokes, haha!

-2019278738.Text: Hee-hee, it's the result that counts~ Gotta take risks sometimes if you wanna succeed~

-2019278740.Text: I didn't say we should jump into the Fragmentum.

-2019278727.Text: *sigh* Blame me then! You take the credit, I'll bear the responsibility~

709604620.Text: Well, you can get good at being lucky~

709604621.Text: We should make haste. There's no sign of the soldiers, but we don't know how big this could become.

709604618.Text: Pursuit? They wouldn't dare follow us. The Fragmentum is a distorted alternate dimension. If they messed up, they'd never make it out!

709604619.Text: Oh, so you were aware of the dangers.

709604616.Text: Uhh... What choice did we have? Were you guys looking forward to a prison vacation on planet fridge?

709604617.Text: Speaking of which, let's hope we don't meet the same fate.

709604614.Text: The Fragmentum is essentially how you described it. There should be many exits.

709604615.Text: ...But paths to such exits will be dangerous and difficult to find.

709604629.Text: You guys have a point. Let's try and find a way out of here... as quick as we can.

-2019278833.Text: Trying to find the exit of an unfamiliar Fragmentum is akin to looking for a needle in a haystack.

-2019278830.Text: But the entrance must be heavily guarded now.

-2019278831.Text: So... we'll need to have a plan, we can't wander around aimlessly.

-2019278836.Text: Okay, got it. It's not like I don't know what getting stuck in the Fragmentum is like...

-2019278470.Text: 【临时】 我找到一座属于您的老宅。

-2019278473.Text: 【临时】属于我？…呵呵，您大概是弄错了。我的名下只有这座歌德宾馆，别的什么都没有——呃，你不是税务局的人吧？

-2019278472.Text: 【临时】它在裂界里，有些年头了。

-2019278475.Text: 【临时】喔…我听说过：在裂隙里会重现古老的街巷，出现已死的故人。要不是银鬃铁卫拦着，不知多少人闯进去冒险了。

-2019278474.Text: 【临时】听我一句劝，年轻人，那地方危险得很。过去重新拜访你，从来不是什么好事。

-2019278479.Text: 【临时】嗯…但确实有那么个地方。好久以前，歌德家族也是声名赫赫。不是吹，我祖先以前可是和那个「朗道」齐名哩！

-2019278478.Text: 【临时】不过，我祖上…好像是第十三代，出了一对兄弟。两兄弟不知为什么事吵了一架，闹上法庭要求分家。

709604884.Text: 【临时】弟弟败诉后失踪了，执政官宣布他犯了叛国罪，我的祖先变卖家产偿付罚金，用剩下的钱开了这所宾馆。歌德家道中落就是那时……

709604885.Text: 【临时】…您真的见过我家的祖宅吗？如果是真的，也许您能找到什么东西，给我当作纪念？家族过去的荣光已经不剩什么了……

709604879.Text: 【临时】噢，我理解…如果危险的话，呃…那就算了。您判断就好。

709604876.Text: 【临时】我回去找一找，记得祖宅还有一把没转交的钥匙在箱子里……

-856479057.Text: 【临时】【获得物品：歌德家的旧钥匙】

-856479056.Text: 【临时】你撕下封条，把钥匙插入铁门，轻轻一拧，铁门就打开了。

-856479059.Text: 【临时】保存记录旧日的时光——裂界也有好的一面呢。

-856479058.Text: 【临时】裂界只会给你被污染的时光。

-856479061.Text: 【临时】你们在裂界创造出的歌德宅邸里寻宝，不时丢弃一些被污染到外形扭曲，或安全性难以保证的东西。

-856479060.Text: 【临时】【获得物品：古老的钱币x5，古老的肖像画，古老的吊坠】

-856479062.Text: 【临时】啊，您还真的去了。您们没事吧？要是害几位受伤了，老朽可过意不去。

-856479064.Text: 【临时】呵呵，您没事就好。虽说是祖宅呵…但毕竟真正的宅子早就没了，裂隙里的仿品，也没什么值得在乎的。

1872404297.Text: 【临时】啊，这是尼古莱•歌德！银鬃铁卫历代队长里，就数他最默默无闻了！我爷爷常跟我说，尼古莱才是真正的歌德：闷声发大财！呵呵……

1872404295.Text: 【临时】噢，这是谁的，居然还有执政官的签名…法茵卡，她是第三任执政官，也是最勤勉的一任。我猜这吊坠的主人很可能是雅思缇娜夫人，她是法茵卡执政官的好朋友。她们在同一年去世。

1872404290.Text: 【临时】啊，这是尼古莱•歌德！银鬃铁卫历代队长里，就数他最默默无闻了！我爷爷常跟我说，尼古莱才是真正的歌德：闷声发大财！呵呵……

306320357.Text: 【临时】噢，这是谁的，居然还有执政官的签名…法茵卡，她是第三任执政官，也是最勤勉的一任。我猜这吊坠的主人很可能是雅思缇娜夫人，她是法茵卡执政官的好朋友。她们在同一年去世。

306320358.Text: 【临时】<color=#F2D79D>画像和吊坠，还有一些钱币…</color>

306320353.Text: 【临时】啊，这是尼古莱•歌德！银鬃铁卫历代队长里，就数他最默默无闻了！我爷爷常跟我说，尼古莱才是真正的歌德：闷声发大财！呵呵……

306320351.Text: 【临时】噢，这是谁的，居然还有执政官的签名…法茵卡，她是第三任执政官，也是最勤勉的一任。我猜这吊坠的主人很可能是雅思缇娜夫人，她是法茵卡执政官的好朋友。她们在同一年去世。

306320352.Text: 【临时】谢谢您，年轻人，您给了我非常珍贵的宝物…言语无法表达这对我的家族有多重要，怎样的赠予都不足以回报您的慷慨！

306320349.Text: 【临时】给，我知道这远远不够，但请您一定要收下！

306320350.Text: 【临时】【根据玩家提交了多少东西，给予相应的报酬；古老的钱币一定会保留下来，带着它可以和华劳斯对话获得额外选项。】

-1259763584.Text: Hmm? Is there anything worth checking out?

-1259763586.Text: 【临时】古钱币？有意思，让我看看……

-1259763588.Text: 【临时】噢，孩子，这些钱币不错，保存得挺完好，应该值点钱。不过嘛，这东西我那儿有一整箱呢，说多宝贵吧也谈不上，至少我是看腻了。

-1259763587.Text: 【临时】喏，我兜里就有一枚，拿去吧，我留着也没用，给你凑个整。

-1259763589.Text: 【临时】这扇封锁的门似乎和那个“歌德家族”有关。也许在上层区我们能找到这个家族的后人。

689401876.Text: The Union has invested countless people over hundreds of years into researching this place. Certainly we would understand it as well as you, if not better.

689401875.Text: You thought that Union guards wouldn't follow you in here because it is dangerous? You thought that our conviction would waver when faced with the fear of death?

689401873.Text: 【临时】喂喂，要开打吗？那孩子的话还没说完……

689401872.Text: 【临时】她不是普通的女孩子，忘了吗？她是——

689401871.Text: I, Bronya Rand, the divinely appointed, hereby declare you be brought before the supreme Consul for judgment.

689401909.Text: I know this fight is unavoidable, but Danheng... this girl won't like you for this.

689401907.Text: Ah forget it. Who needs her to like us anyway!?

1993363535.Text: Hmm... I'll be handling these evildoers myself!

1993363560.Text: This girl... she's pretty strong. Hey, Danheng, show us all that secret strength you've been hiding.

1993363555.Text: I will admit, you are quite strong.

1993363556.Text: But Gepard is being led here by one of my guards as we speak. I know I can at least hold you here until he arrives. You can't escape.

1993363553.Text: Surrender. This senseless struggle has no need to—

1993363554.Text: Ugh, sorry, I didn't mean to ruin the intensity of the moment~

1993363470.Text: I just want to say that Sampo would never disappoint his friends who helped him. I say what I mean and mean what I say!

1993363494.Text: It's impossible to go back the way we came.

1993363395.Text: There's a chest behind the iron gates... Ugh, fine, I can't open it.

1993363394.Text: We'll have to take a detour.

1993363428.Text: Wow, what a spectacle! Some things are terrifyingly large and some fly in the sky. It's hard to find the right words to describe the Fragmentum.

1993363425.Text: ... And the roaming strange monsters. Speaking of which, here they come.

1993363329.Text: Danheng, what are the monsters in the Fragmentum?

1993363332.Text: Herta has written a thesis on Fragmentum's organisms. Want to hear about it?

1993363331.Text: Nah, I probably wouldn't understand it anyway.

1993363362.Text: The gate is locked. You see the seal on the lock

1993363791.Text: Several carefully cut newspaper clippings were pinned to the bulletin board.

1993363790.Text: Consul Tatiana delivers an important speech - The newspaper clipping contains the full speech that Consul Tatiana gave in June, 144 UE.

1993363789.Text: Direwolves sightings, Consul enforces curfew - The newspaper clipping contains a curfew signed by Consul Tatiana in June, 144 UE.

1993363788.Text: Brothers in court, Goethe mansion sealed temporarily. The newspaper clipping contains a family dispute between the Goethe brothers over succession to the family business in June of Union Year 144.

1993363787.Text: Ugh... these news don't seem to be meaningful.

1993363785.Text: Come on, if we wait any longer, the guards will catch up.

1993363783.Text: ... What did you find? Let's hear it.

427279850.Text: The date on the newspaper are all from June, 144 UE. What's so special about it?

427279849.Text: ... Gepard mentioned that the Union was established 700 years ago. If the date is Union Year 144, that means the objects shown in the Fragmentum are not recent, but from Belobog a long time, right?

427279847.Text: Tatiana... the Consul from 144 UE? What's so special about her?

427279846.Text: I've heard the name Goethe somewhere.

427279844.Text: The Consul's speech... Wow, I can tell how powerful of a speech it was, based on the words alone. Direwolves sightings... So there were tenacious creatures survived the frigid winter.

427279843.Text: It mentions that the Goethe mansion was seized. Perhaps that's what behind the locked gate?

830564378.Text: Come on, if we wait any longer, the guards will catch up.

830564376.Text: 【临时】《塔提雅娜执政官发表重要讲话》

830564375.Text: 【临时】《冰原狼出没，执政官宣布宵禁》

830564374.Text: 【临时】《兄弟对簿公堂，歌德家宅暂封》

830564373.Text: 【临时】歌德家宅……就是旁边这栋建筑吧？

1993363824.Text: The gate is locked. You peer through the gate and see the name "Goethe" carved into the building door.

407076610.Text: Some wooden crates are scattered along the wall.

407076643.Text: There's a device next to the crates. This may be the mechanism to open the gate.

407076547.Text: It'll be a fierce battle. Let's not open the gate until we're ready.

407076578.Text: The switch is on the side.

407076478.Text: ... So much building materials piled up here.

407076511.Text: The phone booth was a mere shell. Although the Fragmentum replicated it, it cannot assign an operator for it.

407076412.Text: The Fragmentum faithfully recreated the data from the past: the materials were old yet sturdy.

407076415.Text: Hey guys, I just came up with a great idea. Weren't the people believe in the Preservation purchasing building materials across the universe at high prices? We can sell them these materials.

407076414.Text: The Fragmentum will continue to digitize whenever it touches then print them out. We'll have a limitless supply of goods! Great interests with zero-cost.

407076417.Text: Someone already came up with that idea when the Stellaron first appeared, but it didn't work. The Interastral Peace Corporation was smart enough to avoid trading things related to other Aeons.

407076445.Text: Is it just me or are there more and more monsters...

407076874.Text: I don't see a switch on the door.

407076907.Text: There's a monster wandering around the switch. We need to deal with it first.

1711038525.Text: There's a familiar coffee fragrance coming from behind the locked iron gate.

1711038555.Text: Yes, that's a fissure, an exit from this Fragmentum. But who knows where it will take us to...

1711038556.Text: Well, we'll find out soon enough~ Let's go, the sooner we get out of here the better!

1711038459.Text: You're here again? I told you I was busy, like very busy. The public needs to be served in so many ways...

1711038461.Text: 【临时】嗯，是有这么回事。你是建筑行会的？这事我不负责，你去市政厅三楼……

1711038455.Text: 【临时】你怎么不早说呢，特事特办，你直接跟我说吧，省得跑一趟了。

1711038458.Text: 【临时】等一下，{NICKNAME}，你打算让他们用裂界复制的建材做工程吗？

1711038457.Text: 【临时】这有安全隐患吧？再说，裂界做出来的东西，在裂界外面究竟会发生什么变化， 黑塔也没办法保证啊。

1711038468.Text: 【临时】喂喂，几位公民，你们在嘟嘟嚷嚷什么呢？什么能比贝洛伯格的建设更重要？现在正是需要你们的时刻，拿出觉悟来，贡献一份力量！

1711038467.Text: 【临时】你们的贡献会永远被共同体铭记，执政官会亲自接见你们，为你们戴上勋章，我保证！

144954521.Text: 【临时】噢，那又怎样，银鬃铁卫对裂隙了如指掌。你把地址告诉我，自然有最专业的铁卫去排除危险。

144954515.Text: 【临时】嗐，公民，这些事情由技术部来操心吧。你把地址告诉我，自然有最权威的专家去分析判断。

144954514.Text: 【临时】别担心，我一定会在报告里指出你们的功劳！

144954516.Text: 【临时】这就对了，公民！你为共同体立了一大功！回去等待吧，勋章不日就会有专人送到你的家里！

144954527.Text: 【临时】…你甚至都没问我们叫什么耶。

-1421129422.Text: 【临时】啧，别敬酒不吃吃罚酒，这是你们的义务懂吗？一切物资都是共同体的财产，你无权隐瞒！无权！我随时可以逮捕你们！

-1421129423.Text: 【临时】你就嚷嚷吧，我才不信呢。

1711038492.Text: As time goes by, the rich salmon flavor dilutes and invades the Redsunset meat slice. When the ingredients and the seasoning fuse well together, there's no point to name a dish solely based on its ingredients anymore...

1711038493.Text: What am I doing, I really have to stop thinking...

1711038494.Text: 【临时】你知道哪里有咖啡馆吗？

1711038487.Text: 【临时】好家伙，你真会挑人啊。我敢说，整个贝洛伯格你找不到第二个能解答这个问题的人了。我当然知道：哪里都没有。

1711038490.Text: 【临时】哈！这来龙去脉说来话长，我就不问你们是怎么打听到的了：总之，咖啡是一种饮品，它是从一种叫咖啡豆的植物做出来的——

144954550.Text: 【临时】——别打断我。总之，据说这「咖啡」香浓美味，能提神醒脑。唉，作为美食编辑，没能亲口品尝咖啡简直是毕生憾事！但没办法，根据史载，共同体200年左右，温室的调温功能损坏，咖啡树也灭绝了。

144954551.Text: 【临时】从那以后，咖啡豆就是稀缺品，一度只有执政官召开国宴时才会动用。但约共同体300年，最后一批存货也没了。也就是说，几百年来，人们别说喝咖啡了，就连咖啡这个词都没用啦。

144954552.Text: 【临时】我在裂界里闻到了咖啡香气…

144954553.Text: 【临时】唉，裂界里面什么都有，不知多少失落的美食都藏在里面呵。要不是领导下了死命令，我几次都差点溜进去了。

144954546.Text: 【临时】据我所知，打共同体建国以来，咖啡就是限量供给品。历史上有且只有一家咖啡厅，那是很早以前的事了…

144954547.Text: 【临时】你要不要去问问拉雯妮？她做房地产经纪很久了，行政区这一片儿的商店历史没人比她更熟悉。

144954549.Text: 【临时】哎，别客气。你要是真能找到咖啡——哪怕受了污染的也行——请一定赏我一点儿，只要能尝一口传说中的咖啡，小命我豁出去了！

-1421129389.Text: You're back! A nice livable suburb property is on offer now. Interested in checking out?

-1421129388.Text: 【临时】听说你很了解行政区的商店历史？

-1421129395.Text: 【临时】当然。我敢说，行政区百分之九十的商店是在我的中介下开的，还有百分之十就是我的。所以我的投资项目，那不是谁都有资格入选的……

-1421129394.Text: 【临时】你知道行政区以前有家咖啡馆吗？

-1421129393.Text: 【临时】咖啡馆？那是什么…听上去好像挺有商机。

-1421129392.Text: 【临时】是一种卖饮料的店，饮料叫咖啡。

-1421129383.Text: 【临时】是大概五百年的热门商铺。

-1421129382.Text: 【临时】…好像有印象了，怪名字，但是我有印象…稍等，我去查下资料。

1307753964.Text: 【临时】你们简直在浪费我的时间！

1307753967.Text: 【临时】找到了，但是费了我好大的工夫！我一看到卷宗里店主的姓氏，就想这生意一定有利可图，谁想到好容易把资料翻出来，却发现咖啡早就绝种了！我花了这么多时间，就为了这？

1307753960.Text: 【临时】给你，这是当年那家咖啡厅的建筑图、合同、备用钥匙，记住，你们欠我个人情。

1307753962.Text: 【临时】不用不用，有什么好的商机，记得找我拉雯妮。

1307753972.Text: 【临时】哈，很多人都这么说，走着瞧吧。

1307753973.Text: 【临时】【获得物品：古咖啡厅的备用钥匙x1】

-614560337.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古咖啡厅的备用钥匙】时与M1-1的铁门互动。

-614560334.Text: 【临时】是咖啡豆耶，裂界里的咖啡…真的能喝吗？

-614560341.Text: 【临时】没问题，是正常的咖啡豆。

-614560340.Text: 【临时】不过，这儿几百年没见过咖啡了…{NICKNAME}，真的要把豆子给艾格尼斯吗？她只是个记者吧？是不是交给更适合的人比较好。

-614560339.Text: 【临时】考虑下咖啡豆应该给谁吧！

2114323019.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古代咖啡豆】时与艾格尼斯对话。

2114323020.Text: As time goes by, the rich salmon flavor dilutes and invades the Redsunset meat slice. When the ingredients and the seasoning fuse well together, there's no point to name a dish solely based on its ingredients anymore...

2114323021.Text: What am I doing, I really have to stop thinking...

2114323015.Text: 【临时】哇！你太厉害了！哇，是真的！真的咖啡豆！哇！！！

2114323016.Text: 【临时】对不起，失态了。我只是太高兴了，这下我一定能写出一份轰动共同体的报道！名字我都想好了：《震惊！传说美味再现贝洛伯格！》，结尾再写上赞美普拉米亚，是她让这一神迹得以再现……

2114323026.Text: 【临时】啊，我要火了，我要红了！执政官说不定会亲自接见我……

2114323027.Text: 【临时】已经无法和这个人沟通了。

548239077.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古代咖啡豆】时与拉雯妮对话。

548239078.Text: You're back! A nice livable suburb property is on offer now. Interested in checking out?

548239080.Text: 【临时】哈？你们还真弄到手了啊…

548239073.Text: 【临时】谢谢。不过嘛，只有这么点，也没什么用啊……这点咖啡豆，用完就没了，不可持续的生意，投资起来稳亏的。

548239074.Text: 【临时】算啦，你们自己留着好啦。

548239075.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古代咖啡豆】时与歌德对话。

548239085.Text: 【临时】…喔，好怀念啊，记得我祖上曾经承包过一家专卖店，卖那种叫做咖啡的东西……

548239086.Text: 【临时】也许我还能找到以前的食谱，呵呵，到时候就请你们来喝咖啡吧。

-1017844864.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古代咖啡豆】时与华劳斯对话。

-1017844863.Text: Hmm? Is there anything worth checking out?

-1017844861.Text: 【临时】这是啥，种子吗？有点意思，我自己家有个小温室，嗐，就是专门订制了一个巨大的恒温罩，没多少钱。每月花店都会给我送花，就种在那个罩子里……没什么意思，折腾呗！

-1017844868.Text: 【临时】谢谢你的礼物，我身上也没带什么，这个送你吧，不值钱。

-1017844866.Text: 【临时】玩家背包中有【古代咖啡豆】时与帕姆对话。

-1017844855.Text: 【临时】…这不就是普通的咖啡豆吗？储备间里好多的。

2067268851.Text: 【临时】难得你有兴趣，帕姆来教你做咖啡吧。

2067268852.Text: 【临时】【获得食谱：帕姆特制咖啡】

-157573394.Text: It won't open... We'll have to go to the other side.

1146388521.Text: What are you doing? The gate's not open yet.

-439898396.Text: The way out should be clear now.

-439898369.Text: She really... caught up with us...

741037376.Text: Let's search around him for any clues.

741037377.Text: Medicine, a lab... There's quite a bit of good intel here.

741037374.Text: He also has this... It seems to be a map.

741037375.Text: Who would spend any extra time in the Fragmentum drawing a map!? ...Were they not afraid?

741037372.Text: In the pursuit of riches, people will do just about anything. If he drew this map, then the place marked on the map is probably where the lab is.

741037373.Text: Who'd have thought we'd find a clue this fast? Let's go investigate, maybe we'll find something good.

741037386.Text: What a mess... Maybe we can find another clue around here.

741037387.Text: The prospectors who entered the Fragmentum looking for treasure found a strange man, who forced them to take some wonder medicine with them.

-1987845977.Text: A medicine dealer wouldn't give away medicine to other people for free, and for no apparent reason... No, that strange man is more likely to be a doctor, one who created the medicine.

-1987845976.Text: My thoughts exactly. So that means that someone actually came down here and began conducting medical research.

-1987845979.Text: No matter whether they found success or not, that bravery is commendable.

-1987845978.Text: Let me see... Yep, this is the first marker on the map.

-1987845981.Text: There's a lot of stuff scattered around here, as well as some tents... Seems like they were left here by the prospectors.

-1987845980.Text: Let's search the area for anything related to the medicine.

-1987845983.Text: 【临时】这是…什么人留下的笔记，过去看看吧。

-1987845982.Text: More about the pursuit of riches. These notes were probably written by the same person.

-1987845969.Text: 【临时】这大概就是最初流到市面上的「特效药」了吧？可惜这人高兴得太早，没有等到药物的副作用完全显现。

-1987845968.Text: Hmm... Where could the medicine lab be in this Fragmentum?

-421762036.Text: It doesn't seem to be nearby. Does the map offer any more clues?

-421762035.Text: Yeah... Although it's not very clear, I can see another spot marked on the map. But, it requires going deeper into the Fragmentum.

-421762038.Text: We've come this far, haven't we? We have to get to the bottom of this. Let's keep going.

-421762037.Text: The elevator seems to be a working. Let's get on and try it.

-421762042.Text: Huh? Behind the door... I think I see someone.

-421762041.Text: It doesn't look like we can open it from this side. We'll need to take the long way around.

-421762028.Text: The location marked on the map seems to be higher up.

-421762027.Text: Things recreated by the Fragmentum are often torn to pieces, but the environment here is quite intact.

1144321905.Text: Perhaps it is a stronghold for the prospectors.

741037283.Text: That person on the other side... They don't seem okay...

741037277.Text: Hmph... Such realistic apparitions... Hehe, so my time has come, huh...

741037276.Text: We aren't apparitions, we're very much alive.

741037279.Text: Someone alive...? Hee-hee... In this dreadful place, one second you're alive, the next you're a ghost...

741037288.Text: Open the door? N—No, I can't do that. There are monsters on that side... Hehe... If I open the door, the monsters will... hehe.

-1987846073.Text: How long have you been trapped here?

-1987846078.Text: ...How long? Oh, I don't know, hehe...

-1987846077.Text: What happened? Nothing... except that a few people disappeared... Hehe...

-1987846067.Text: Companions... Hehe... Yes, I think? Companions...

-421762133.Text: He looks like he has been through some kind of traumatic incident...

-421762134.Text: I'm a doctor, perhaps we can help. Please, open the door.

-421762131.Text: Doctor... I see, a doctor... Hehe. The door... I will open it, if you help me with something... Hehe.

-421762132.Text: What do you need us to do?

-421762137.Text: My companions... There were two. But monsters got both of them... Hehe.

-421762138.Text: They had some insignia on them. Bring the insignia... hehe... to me.

-421762135.Text: Bring them to me, and I will open the door for you... Hehe...

-421762136.Text: I got a bad feeling about this...

-421762126.Text: But there's no other path. Just see it as helping out a poor person in need.

1144321808.Text: That person was probably here with his companions looking for valuable treasure.

1144321807.Text: Then his companions suddenly got attacked by some monsters... leaving him alone, and he hid.

1144321810.Text: As time went by, he eventually lost his sanity...

1144321809.Text: You search the monster to find an insignia engraved with words you can barely make out.

1144321806.Text: Look, there are some words engraved on the back of the insignia.

1144321805.Text: Don't look back... What does that mean?

1144321816.Text: It's not clear. Let's make a note of it for now and think more on it later.

1144321815.Text: ...We've cleared out this area of monsters.

-1228331187.Text: I thought there might have been hope that his companions had survived...

-1228331188.Text: This kind of accident happens every day. Once they step foot into the Devil's Stomach, they were aware of what they were getting themselves into.

-1228331185.Text: Come on, let's go back to that prospector.

-1228331192.Text: Bring it closer, my eyes aren't too great now...

-1228331189.Text: Hehe, indeed, it's what I want... Hehe...

-1228331190.Text: As we agreed... I will open the door for you, hehe...

-1228331179.Text: Hehe... You guys want to go further in?

-1228331180.Text: In here, there is something... rather terrifying... Hehe...

337752753.Text: Hehe... But... you all seem rather strong...

337752755.Text: I—I should go now. The other prospectors are waiting for me.

337752750.Text: Y—You best take care of yourselves... Hehe...

741037313.Text: That guy gives me the creeps...

741037315.Text: Just because he's spooked doesn't mean we should let him spook us, too.

741037308.Text: This is the second location marked on the map.

741037309.Text: This mechanism was clearly not naturally created within the Fragmentum.

741037310.Text: Behind it might be the lab where that medicine is being produced...

741037311.Text: The door is locked... How do we get in?

741037320.Text: Let's look around for any clues.

-1987846043.Text: Let's search the surroundings and see if he left anything behind.

-1987846041.Text: 【临时】宝箱的后面好像有什么东西？

-1987846040.Text: Hey! It looks a lot like the insignia from those other two prospectors, and there's something engraved on the back as well...

-1987846047.Text: Riches are blinding... What does that mean?

-1987846046.Text: I remember the previous insignia: "Don't look back," "Right goes astray"... and now "Riches are blinding."

-1987846045.Text: Perhaps the clues are hidden in these words...

-1987846044.Text: Follow the clues as you advance: don't turn back and keep going left with the gate being the reference object.

-1987846035.Text: Wow! The key was hidden here! We really are a team of geniuses, huh?

-1987846034.Text: There's also a note from a prospector. Let's take a look.

-421762102.Text: It's probably a note from the same person.

-421762101.Text: He brought a group with him into the Fragmentum, planning to make a fortune off of the medicine — but he was chased off by the crazed doctor making the medicine.

-421762100.Text: The story is complete if we combine this with the first clue. He was able to steal some wonder medicine from the lab, but was attacked by monsters on his way out of the Fragmentum... and perished.

-421762099.Text: Whatever may have happened, we have the key. Let's go back to the door.

-421762106.Text: Alright, it works! The door is open!

-421762105.Text: Shhh, keep your voice down — there could be someone inside for all we know.

-421762104.Text: Who would've expected a place like this in the Fragmentum... incredible!

-421762103.Text: It must have taken quite the ambition and persistence to construct a lab on such a large scale.

-421762094.Text: Do you guys think this lab looks a lot like Natasha's clinic?

1144321839.Text: There's a lot of notes on the table. They were probably left by the doctor.

1144321840.Text: Let's investigate. Maybe we can find some useful information.

1144321841.Text: He made himself a guinea pig in his own experiment? Why...?

1144321836.Text: Perhaps he was overly confident, staking everything on this experiment.

1144321837.Text: If the doctor was also infected with crystallization sickness... he might not be alive anymore.

1144321847.Text: When he saw that the medicine was initially effective, he thought he had succeeded.

1144321848.Text: He had stayed in the Fragmentum for too long, and had been tormented by his sickness for too long.

-1228331156.Text: Hold on, the paper he used...

-1228331153.Text: No! Tale a closer look — there's the watermark of the Silvermane Guards in the bottom left corner. Only clerks of the Union would use such paper.

-1228331160.Text: Why would a doctor in the Underground have this kind of paper then...

-1228331159.Text: Maybe they just found it somewhere? After all, the Fragmentum can have just about everything.

-1228331157.Text: He wanted to use the remains of Fragmentum monsters as ingredients in the medicine?

-1228331148.Text: Was this simply a wild, vain dream? Or was it a hallucinatory madness produced by more failures than one could endure?

-1228331147.Text: Hallucinations, pain, emotional stress... It seems the doctor was having a breakdown—

741037214.Text: What's this? A nice little ornament, it doesn't seem to really fit with the atmosphere here.

741037213.Text: That's a gift I gave Vache.

741037219.Text: Is my brother... Or, was my brother. A doctor who ventured into the Devil's Stomach alone and invented the wonder medicine — I'm sure it is Vache.

741037218.Text: What happened between you and Vache?

741037217.Text: Before... I was a girl with no home, wandering the streets of the Underground. Then one day, a young doctor took me in.

741037216.Text: He took me back to the clinic and taught me about medicine. Gradually, I came to see him as family, the only family I had.

741037223.Text: But one day, suddenly... he just disappeared. It was like he evaporated into thin air.

741037222.Text: How do you know it's him?

-1987846140.Text: The handwriting on the notes and letters match that of my memory.

-1987846141.Text: As well as that ornament March 7th found.

-1987846142.Text: And most importantly... I know he would be capable of doing something like this.

-1987846143.Text: What kind of person is Vache?

-1987846136.Text: Gentle, and rather reserved. But on the topic of anything in medicine... he would completely change, becoming almost maniacal.

-1987846137.Text: If I ever made a mistake in an experiment, he would criticize me very harshly... then a while later, he would apologize and comfort me.

-1987846138.Text: Why didn't he tell you he was leaving?

-1987846139.Text: Perhaps because... I had grown up. He couldn't hide his secret from me forever.

-1987846132.Text: Vache isn't one of us. He is an Overworlder, dispatched to the Underground to keep an eye on us.

-1987846133.Text: When he took me in, it was all just to better conceal his true identity from everyone else.

-421762200.Text: In the end, I was the only one who ever felt that we were a family.

-421762201.Text: In the years since, I've constantly been trying to forget that person. Who would've thought that he would...

-421762196.Text: A monster of the Fragmentum... but unlike any of the others we have seen.

-421762197.Text: Could this be the object of Vache's experiment?

-421762198.Text: You've sacrificed everything... all for an ideal that can never be realized.

-421762191.Text: No matter what, I won't lose to you!

1144321742.Text: That guy was more difficult than any of the other monsters we've encountered in the Fragmentum. How can the work of one doctor...

1144321741.Text: People in the most dire of circumstances are often capable of unleashing unimaginable strength.

1144321740.Text: Although Vache was a bit of a weirdo... he wanted to rescue those suffering from crystallization sickness. He must have been quite a sincere guy deep down.

1144321746.Text: But his aspirations as a doctor... I never doubted.

1144321745.Text: There's still this medicine-producing equipment in the lab. What should we do with it all?

1144321743.Text: Lock the lab, but don't destroy the lab equipment.

1144321749.Text: If we don't destroy this equipment once and for all, perhaps someone else will one day use it to make a strange medicine...

-1228331254.Text: If we spread word about the monsters down here and terrible fate of all those prospectors... I don't think anyone will dare to come into the Fragmentum for any wonder medicine again.

-1228331255.Text: Destroy all of the lab equipment.

-1228331256.Text: Eliminate the danger at its source — this is the safest course of action.

-1228331250.Text: Sorry, Vache... but we have our mission to complete as well.

-1228331251.Text: So our first mission is in the books, huh?

741037244.Text: He must be a big shot, seems like he's everywhere.

741037245.Text: Miss March, it'd be best if you tempered your curiosity. The truth can be frightening, you know.

741037250.Text: Natasha's treating me like a kid...

741037251.Text: Such a stupid thing to do... all for the sake of avoiding work...

741037248.Text: Does it seem stupid to you?

741037249.Text: Maybe when you see the environment they work in, you'll change your mind.

741037181.Text: Don't look back, "Right goes astray," "Riches are blinding."

741037182.Text: Perhaps the clues are hidden in these words...

741037640.Text: I want you to see all of this clearly, successor.

741037641.Text: Every night, you will have these dreams. You will come to understand everything...

741037638.Text: I want you to see all of this clearly...

-562924569.Text: ...Come on, your toys aren't that dangerous.

-562924570.Text: Then why hasn't he woken up yet?

-562924571.Text: 【临时】因为他睡着啦，睡得特别沉，你没听见他说梦话吗？…啊，可怜的孩子，那个梦一定特别可怕。

-562924572.Text: Uh, sleep talking? I thought a virus was attacking his brain and he was fighting back.

-562924573.Text: Your bombs don't contain viruses, Sampo. The others have woken up already, right? What are you gonna do about the Overworld girl?

-562924574.Text: What am I gonna do about her? Why, wait for the opportunity and then send her back together with the rest of them of course... Wait, why are you—

-562924575.Text: I can see through you, Sampo Koski.

-562924560.Text: ...I didn't mean to bring her along! The smoke was too dense, I was in a daze! Before I knew it, I'd somehow dragged her down here too.

-562924561.Text: Hah, nothing to worry about, this matter's got nothing to do with Wildfire... right? They won't get involved?

-2129008509.Text: 【临时】这得看你怎么处理了，桑博。你知道「地火」对上层区的人会怎么做，干嘛要趟这潭浑水呢？这和你又没有关系。

-2129008510.Text: Sampo Koski always takes care of his friends.

-2129008511.Text: 【临时】呵。那你可要留点神，那小姑娘好像跑出去了。再见。

-2129008512.Text: Wait, aren't you going to give him/her a shot, or some medicine, or something?

-2129008513.Text: Medical supplies are scarce down here, Sampo. I don't have any to waste on a sleeping beauty.

-2129008515.Text: Are you feeling better? Any aches or pains?

-2129008501.Text: I've got migraines all over my body.

-1725723982.Text: That's good, you were out for a long time. I was worried sick. Eh-he.

-1725723983.Text: ...Hmm, seems like there's some brain damage after all. Unless that was a creative metaphor.

-1725723984.Text: Yeah, you were babbling something about destruction, Stellarons... Whatever it was, it sounded scary.

-1725723985.Text: Anyway! Now that you're up, let's get moving. It seems that fate had plans for us. I saved your life, so I guess it's time for you to repay the favor. I need your help in re-capturing that girl from the Overworld.

-1725723989.Text: Aha, the ol' pretend amnesia card eh? You fought her up there — you almost didn't make it. Luckily your friend Sampo here always looks out for the little guy and came to the rescue... Ring any bells?

-1725723974.Text: I accidentally brought her along with me... I thought I'd locked her away but, uhh... I guess the lock wasn't sturdy enough...

-1725723975.Text: They're already up. The young guy — miserable-looking fellow — he said he was gathering information and took off on his own. The girl just suddenly disappeared.

1003159373.Text: Not to get you down or anything brother, but you might wanna start choosing your friends more carefully. It would seem I care about you more than they do.

1003159372.Text: Great. To tell you the truth, if the Bluecoats or Wildfire were to find out that I smuggled someone from up there down here... I don't think either side would let me go.

1003159371.Text: So stop dawdling, we've got work to do!

1003159370.Text: But I've still got a few questions...

1003159369.Text: *sigh* Do you have to ask them now? Fine, ask away and I'll answer as fast as I can.

1003159368.Text: What did you use to knock us out?

1003159367.Text: That's a trade secret, a recipe I got from someone a long time ago. The materials are quite difficult to find.

1003159366.Text: I put my life on the line, y'know!

1003159381.Text: Is that good enough for you?

1003159380.Text: Did you really bring that girl down here by accident?

1762674260.Text: Of course not. I had no guarantee there weren't more of them chasing me, so I needed a hostage at the very least.

1762674259.Text: But now that the adrenaline's gone, I'm kicking myself. What a mess. Regardless, the most important thing right now is figuring out where she is.

1762674257.Text: This is the region below Belobog — the Underworld I talked about.

1762674256.Text: Let's not waste time. If you want to know more, we can talk while we're on the move.

1762674255.Text: Who was the person that just spoke to you?

1762674254.Text: Natasha, she's the doctor around here.

1762674253.Text: I'll give you a word of caution — she's the last person you want to mess with. There aren't that many doctors in the Underworld, so if you get on her bad side, the entire Underworld will be out for you.

1762674267.Text: ...Maybe I don't have any more questions.

196590319.Text: Then let's go, what are we still hanging around for!

-562924536.Text: Dear friends, welcome to humanity's last bastion against the Eternal Freeze... "Below-bog!" Ahaha... *sigh* Tough crowd.

-562924539.Text: Hard to believe, right? A whole other realm below the Union, away from sun, snow, wind...

-562924538.Text: The only thing we have down here is an endless supply of Geomarrow. It provides energy and warmth for both the Underworld and the Overworld.

-562924542.Text: Oh, you know, surveying the unfamiliar environment, collecting crucial intel...

-562924529.Text: Preparations... That way we'll be better prepared if we have to jump into the Fragmentum again.

-2129008478.Text: The whole cavern has a complex structure. It's cordoned off by rocky walls on all sides. Only the cable cars and support beams reach the surface.

-2129008477.Text: This must be the Underworld they spoke of.

-2129008482.Text: Based on past experience, we don't have to worry about her. She'll reappear eventually.

-2129008484.Text: I still think we should try and find her!

-2129008483.Text: There's a commotion around the market.

-2129008470.Text: Sounds like somewhere she'd turn up...

-2129008469.Text: Markets are usually pretty lively,  sounds like a March destination alright...

-1725723953.Text: 【临时】向丹恒讲述了自己的普拉米亚之梦。

-1725723952.Text: That doesn't sound like an ordinary dream. Do you think it mean...clues for the Stellaron are in the underground?

-1725723955.Text: And just what are you two whispering about so earnestly, hmm?

-1725723954.Text: Has someone sighted our Overworld girl?

-1725723956.Text: Alas, this truly is a nightmare. We need to find her as soon as possible...

-562924638.Text: The Geomarrow in this vein is running low, everyone's getting anxious. The taxes are due soon, what are we gonna do about the quota?

-562924639.Text: Calm down, the Geomarrow's never going to run out. That stuff is like the rats down here — never far off.

-562924636.Text: Pick another spot, dig for a while, and you'll strike another vein in no time.

-562924637.Text: You're too young and too naive... The mining teams control the veins down here, there's nothing for us.

-562924601.Text: Hey, don't get too full of yourself! This isn't over!

-562924607.Text: Hey! You're up! Quick, look who's here...

-562924604.Text: Causing trouble of course, she's like a spoilt princess. She can't open her mouth without referring to someone as a devil or calling them impertinent.

-2129008544.Text: Wait... is this March 7th calling someone else a spoilt princess?

-2129008543.Text: Well, I'm just used to being strict with others, and going easy on myself.

-2129008542.Text: Anyway, it's like this: I was taking a stroll, and suddenly I caught sight of her coming my way. I took cover so she wouldn't see me.

-2129008541.Text: She kept looking behind her, almost like someone was following her, and then she bumped into someone. Whoever it was grabbed hold of her and started arguing, but then...

-2129008547.Text: She threw him to the ground like it was nothing.

-2129008545.Text: I guess she did it without thinking — I mean, I'd probably do the same thing. It's normal to be frightened if some stranger grabs you.

-2129008536.Text: Sucks to be the stranger though — that lady means business. Not many girls her age can stand up to me and Danheng.

-1725724017.Text: I could tell from when we fought her. That lady means business. Not many girls her age can stand up to me and Danheng.

-1725724016.Text: That girl isn't the pampered princess you take her for.

-1725724015.Text: Friends, don't just stand there — do something! This could easily boil over! I can't go picking sides now, how would I make it up to the one I didn't help...

-1725724014.Text: Pretty brave of you to ask us a favor! We still haven't settled the score with you!

-1725724021.Text: We shouldn't be drawing attention to ourselves right now, but Bronya is going to cause a lot of trouble here, I don't think we can ignore this.

-1725724020.Text: Sampo's right, we can't just stand here.

-1725724018.Text: Danheng's right, we can't ignore this.

-1725724009.Text: Exactly! That's what I've been— ...The young master speaks with reason.

-1725724008.Text: 7th is right, you should sit tight and get some rest.

1003159338.Text: Hey, I was just venting, get your own opinion!

1003159339.Text: Actually, I agree with Danheng. In a sense, this girl and us are peas in the same pod...

1003159340.Text: Well said! The young lady is wise and righteous!

1003159335.Text: Uh-oh, let's get out of here!

1003159336.Text: Hmph, worthless coward. Running at the first sign of trouble.

1003159337.Text: Miss Seele! Oh, it's a good thing you showed up when you did. Many thanks~

1003159346.Text: These prospectors sure have some nerve. With Svarog behind them, they even have the audacity to attack us here.

1003159347.Text: Shut up, Sampo. The threat of Svarog will be dealt with in due time, right now Wildfire's need is more urgent.

1762674226.Text: What was your intention in bringing me here?

1762674227.Text: Hmph, listen to her. "What was your intention?" ...She still thinks she's an Overworld princess.

1762674228.Text: Ahaha... Eh-he... Let's all be friends, shall we?

1762674223.Text: What are you planning to do?

1762674233.Text: Our chief wants to see you.

-562924701.Text: ...I toil away every day, risking my life to find relics for these guys. They won't even tell me where their base is.

-562924702.Text: They've never treated me as one of their own!

-562924703.Text: What kind of organization is Wildfire?

-562924696.Text: Even if knew — which I'm not saying I do — I wouldn't want to open my mouth.

-562924697.Text: I'm sure you'll know more soon enough.

-562924698.Text: Why are we even waiting for him?

-562924699.Text: Huh, you're still thinking of escaping, aren't you?

-562924692.Text: Other than the heavily guarded cable cars, only Wildfire knows how to get from the Underworld to the Overworld.

-562924693.Text: Anyway, you should think of this as a business transaction. You need to get to know the boss before you can start negotiating.

-2129008641.Text: It's not hard to understand why they don't see you as one of their own.

-2129008642.Text: *sigh* Sampo Koski, forever doling out warm compassion, and forever getting the cold shoulder in return.

-2129008643.Text: I'm used to it by now. Such is the way of this cruel world.

-2129008644.Text: This is gonna be a looong wait...

-2129008637.Text: ...That hurt more than the spear AND the distrust.

-562924668.Text: {NICKNAME}, do you remember why we came here?

-562924670.Text: To dispel the effect of the Stellaron on the star rail.

-562924665.Text: That's right! Say, you've got a good memory!

-562924664.Text: *sigh* It's just a shame we don't have anything to go on yet...

-562924667.Text: This sure is a divided world. There's barely anyone left on the planet, yet somehow their biggest concern is separating themselves into "up theres" and "down heres."

-562924666.Text: Ugh... if we don't get things sorted quickly, Himeko and Mr. Yang are gonna laugh at us again...

-562924660.Text: No way, we've only been here a day.

-2129008610.Text: Mr. Yang holds the record for being away from the train for the longest — two months, to be exact. Five days after Danheng got back, there was still no trace of him. Even then, Himeko wasn't worried — told us it was no big deal.

-2129008609.Text: Wait, that's not right. The record-holder was the passenger who came aboard before Danheng. I heard that after he got off, we never saw him again.

-2129008612.Text: Himeko told me she had a chat with Mr. Yang, and they agreed that there was no point in looking for him.

-2129008611.Text: Maybe they're solving their own problems.

-2129008606.Text: Well, I'd rather be solving problems with them together. In fact, I'd rather be battling the Antimatter Legion than stuck down here.

-2129008605.Text: If Himeko and Mr. Yang were here we'd be a lot safer, they're super reliable.

-562924767.Text: March is in a bad mood.

-562924764.Text: Staying in one place is like torture to her.

-562924765.Text: We need to abandon her on a planet for half a year. Maybe then she'll finally learn the value of peace and quiet...

-562924762.Text: She thinks it's her fault that we're stuck here.

-562924763.Text: If you follow the logic, it was me that brought up escape. Her contribution was merely selecting a rash approach.

-562924760.Text: And if you go back even further... it would seem that we're too nosy for our own good. Accidents like this are inevitable.

-562924759.Text: I see... and you don't think you're ending your analysis a little prematurely?

-2129008707.Text: If he hadn't appeared, we might not have encountered all this trouble. Perhaps we would've successfully escaped, cleared up the misunderstandings, exonerated ourselves, and successfully trailblazed this planet.

-2129008708.Text: It was precisely his appearance and his "rescue" that pushed us into our current position.

-2129008706.Text: Actions are more telling than words.
