-2129008703.Text: In my opinion, people like Sampo are the most dangerous of all. He hasn't divulged his true intentions, and has pushed us into a corner already.

-2129008704.Text: Do you see the danger here? We're paying a heavy price for his actions, but he's still managed to convince you that he's a friend.

-2129008701.Text: My trust is a rare commodity.

-2129008702.Text: You seem to enjoy a metaphor.

-2129008699.Text: I myself don't find them very... "cool..." but everyone has their manner of speaking.

-2129008700.Text: Oh, there's one thing I'm very interested in. What did he use when he put us to sleep?

-1725724180.Text: He said it's a trade secret.

-1725724178.Text: I'm not affected by normal poisons...

-1725724176.Text: There are many things in the universe. It's not that surprising that there are toxins here that could paralyze me...

-1725724177.Text: In any case... be wary of Sampo Koski.

-562924734.Text: Bored? Your brother Sampo's always up for a chat.

-562924732.Text: Of course not. I rent this place for you! Two tins a day! I treat it as a gesture of gratitude. After all, you did help me with those Bluecoats.

-562924731.Text: Safety is paramount, you know. I would never divulge my own residence.

-562924729.Text: ...Is something I was smart enough not to reveal.

-562924728.Text: If I'd let Wildfire know that she was a high-ranking officer from the Silvermane Guards, there's no telling what they'd have done to her.

-562924727.Text: The one thing that Underworlders hate more than anything else are those Bluecoats. They take off with the ore that the people here mine and issue meager supplies based on the amount seized. Of course, it's hardly an equal exchange. We businessmen call it a monopoly.

-562924726.Text: I'm not surprised the people here hate them.

-2129008675.Text: Valued clients of mine. *sigh* I guess there's no harm in telling you... The routes I use to travel between here and the surface, including the one I used to save you... All of them are Wildfire's secret passageways.

-2129008674.Text: There's a cost, of course. Wildfire gets to purchase the relics they want at a reduced price, but still leaves me with enough to make a proft.

-2129008673.Text: 【临时】希儿是他们的干部，是首领奥列格信任的骨干。大概在她看来，我就是「地火」的一条狗吧。

-2129008672.Text: And a very contented lapdog, I should add!

-2129008671.Text: What does Wildfire want from us?

-2129008670.Text: I don't know. They're a righteous bunch though, so you won't get the short end of the stick. I'd recommend you strongly consider whatever it is they ask of you.

-2129008669.Text: After all, those passageways to the surface are all under their control.

-2129008668.Text: ...Don't look at me! I plan on living a while longer. Without Wildfire's go-ahead, my lips are sealed.

-2129008667.Text: I've got nothing else to say.

-562924305.Text: ...Don't think this is all over just because you helped me.

-562924306.Text: I'll repay my debt to you, sooner or later... But your illicit dealings with these devilscendants is unforgivable.

-562924307.Text: I already said we've got nothing to do with whatever "devils" you're talking about.

-562924308.Text: Be that as it may, Mother— ...Madam Consul told me herself: One of you is carrying a seed of ruin...

-562924309.Text: ...and I don't believe she would be mistaken.

-562924310.Text: Whatever you say, just don't slow us down.

-562924311.Text: How about joining us for a temporary truce?

-562924312.Text: So, you want to return to the surface too? Then we share a common goal. I won't hinder you, unless you are planning something that will bring harm to this world... in which case you will have to face me.

-562924313.Text: The Consul's decree is absolute... At least, it is to me. When we get to the surface, I—

-2129008245.Text: The premise being that your identity won't be revealed first...

-2129008246.Text: In any case, for the time being we're all in the same boat.

-2129008247.Text: ...I don't need you to tell me what happens if my identity is revealed.

-2129008248.Text: The devils brought about the Eternal Freeze that begot the world's end. Their spawn are imprisoned underground, to atone for their sins with labor.

-2129008249.Text: I've studied Plamya's Admonishments since I was a child. I always thought that the devilscendants trapped down here must've hated us to the core.

-2129008250.Text: The people down here don't look much like devils.

-2129008251.Text: Well, they're not going to write the word "Devil" on their foreheads are they.

-2129008252.Text: Still... having come into contact with them... I didn't feel the sense of caution, fear, or loathing that I was expecting...

-2129008253.Text: ...and no matter how much I keep my guard up, all I've seen so far are ordinary people, going about their business.

-2129008254.Text: Oh? A crisis of faith, perhaps?

-1725723720.Text: Do not ridicule me. I can form my own opinions and judgments.

-1725723722.Text: In the centuries since we settled on the surface... something must have changed.

-1725723723.Text: I need to think things over.

-562924272.Text: No can do. No one leaves before the chief gets here.

-562924274.Text: Hmph, perhaps not... but you're still gonna have to do what I say.

-562924277.Text: I'll do whatever I want, thanks.

-562924276.Text: Off you go then, I'd like to see you try.

-562924281.Text: How did you know we were from the surface?

-562924280.Text: We know everything Sampo gets up to, so we heard about his run-in with you. We have our sources, and they keep us well-informed.

-2129008214.Text: How did you know where we were?

-2129008216.Text: Besides, the girl created quite the fuss.

-2129008215.Text: Bit young for a henchwoman aren't you? How'd that happen?

-2129008220.Text: The chief will be here soon. Sit quiet.

1023362348.Text: Who are these people, Sampo? And where's my order?

1023362347.Text: About that... Bad luck I'm afraid! The Silvermane Guards spotted me on the way out...

1023362345.Text: ...seems you did manage to bring something down here, though.

1023362344.Text: They helped me escape from the Bluecoats! You know me... Sampo Koski! Staunch repayer of debts... There was nothing else I could've done back there.

1023362343.Text: But I promise — swear even — I knocked them out! Wildfire's secrets are safe and the passageways are sound! Hehe...

1023362357.Text: Pleasure to meet you. I'm Oleg.

-1705521006.Text: I'm March, this is Danheng. And that's... Danheng's sister.

-1705521008.Text: I'm March, this is Danheng. And that's... Danheng's sister.

-1705521010.Text: This is {NICKNAME}. I'm March. This is Danheng. And that's... Danheng's sister.

-1705521011.Text: We're innocent civilians! Please, just take us back.

-1705521012.Text: Oh? You mean the young lady wearing the... how shall I put it... Silvermane Guard-esque uniform?

-1705521013.Text: There's no use trying to pull the wool over my eyes. I've been around the block a few more times than you.

-1705520998.Text: Huh... this guy seems pretty savvy.

-1705520999.Text: Someone in my position has to be especially cautious. Sometimes I may come across as impolite — but that's something I cannot avoid. I hope you understand.

-2108805533.Text: I propose we just have an amiable conversation. Naturally, things will only go smoothly if we commit to being open and honest with each other. What do you say?

-2108805534.Text: If that sounds okay to you, perhaps you'd be kind enough to tell me what brings you here?

-2108805535.Text: Of course, if you'd rather stay mysterious, I'm sure Sampo will give me a full and detailed account.

-2108805537.Text: ...And you expect me to believe that?

-2108805538.Text: We're here to clear out the garbage.

-2108805539.Text: Hahaha. You should probably leave that kind of talk to scarier-looking types.

-2108805540.Text: We're hear to save this planet.

-2108805525.Text: Hahaha. Do you really think that's all I need to hear?

-542721593.Text: So... you're not from down here, and you're not from up there. Hahaha, outsiders in the truest sense of the word... Very interesting!

-542721594.Text: Seele! Sampo! Leave us be! I want to have a nice chat with our friends here.

1023362381.Text: That Oleg person seemed pretty nice.

1023362377.Text: Heh, maybe you should be more careful then.

1023362388.Text: In any case, it beats being shouted at.

1023362389.Text: If we can gain his trust, maybe we can get some clues about the Stellaron.

-1705520975.Text: Danheng and I aren't good at negotiating. We'll leave it to you.

-1705520977.Text: What do you make of him, o wise one?

-1705520976.Text: I think he's tougher and shrewder than he lets on.

-1705520978.Text: Weigh his words carefully. Treat him with caution.

-1705520981.Text: Still, my intuition tells me that he's not a difficult person to communicate with.

-1705520980.Text: The people we encountered above ground seemed to be on higher alert. Perhaps we'll have more luck establishing relations with this "chief."

-1705520967.Text: Maybe he can help us to uncover the clues we're looking for.

-1705520966.Text: Let's try and establish communication with him. Maybe he can help us uncover the clues we need.

-2108805502.Text: Wildfire... I've seen that name in my reports that I present to the Madam Consul.

-2108805501.Text: I don't recall the specifics, but I remember it was a clandestine organization with a secret agenda.

-2108805504.Text: Can you be a bit more specific?

-2108805503.Text: No. The Madam Consul rarely lets me read her reports. She said that I'll understand everything after I succeed her.

-2108805505.Text: In the report, I remember seeing the words "confidential," "out of control," and "history."

-2108805508.Text: The rest was written in cipher, which I haven't learned yet.

-2108805507.Text: There isn't organization in this world without a secret agenda.

-2108805494.Text: That's where you're mistaken, trust me. People don't need secret agendas to do bad things.

-2108805493.Text: From the materials that I've seen, the Silvermane Guards have investigated and dealt with countless unlawful organizations. I still remember one called "The Rightful Rights Society," where members would cover for each other...

-542721561.Text: ...to allow them to slip away from their spouses and children, and do things they liked to do — like building snowmen!

-542721563.Text: Let's see what this Mr. Oleg has in store for us.

1023362282.Text: Okay, okay, let's cut the pleasantries. To be honest, I'm not sure what to do with you guys. You shouldn't have come down here.

1023362285.Text: But let me ask you a simple question: Are you willing to remain here?

1023362278.Text: Haha, interesting, it would seem there's disagreement already.

1023362281.Text: That's nothing new. We have to get back to the surface.

1023362291.Text: Yeah, we have to get back to the surface. Right, Danheng?

-1705521073.Text: What do you mean "either way"? We have to get back to the surface.

-1705521070.Text: Hahaha, good! It would seem we're in agreement. I'd end up with a splitting headache if you chose to stay.

-1705521071.Text: This poor pedantic place isn't built to withstand respectable otherworldly guests, nor feisty Silvermane Guards for that matter. Seeing as all of you are keen to get back, we might as well move on to conditions.

-1705521077.Text: Straight to the point — not bad, boy. You're sharper than you look.

-1705521075.Text: Hahaha, I don't need your money. I could do with your sense of humor though.

-1705521064.Text: First I want to ask this young miss over here a question: What are undergrounders, in your eyes?

-1705521065.Text: ...You are the devil's children. Descendants of the sinful devils that brought about the Eternal Freeze that destroyed the world.

-2108805599.Text: To atone for your sins, Plamya sentenced you to labor underground, to extract Geomarrow.

-2108805597.Text: Devil's children? Sin? The Eternal Freeze? ...All of it lies! The people down here are no different from you Overworlders. There's no difference between us. We are all human!

-2108805598.Text: And yet because of those lies... we've been imprisoned underground for generations...

-2108805603.Text: Lies? Even children are familiar with this history.

-2108805604.Text: History contains some of the biggest lies of all, little girl! If you don't believe me, look at us! Look at Seele! Look at Sampo! Take a good look at the people here and the conditions they live in!

-2108805601.Text: And when you're done, put your condescending tone aside and think for a moment: Are those that live beneath you truly devils?

-2108805591.Text: The lives of the people down here are all the proof you need. If you don't believe me, stay here for a while, and if you still feel that we're irredeemable, take it up with me again.

-2108805592.Text: You said stay here for a while...

-542721658.Text: That's right. I'm not trying to create trouble for you, I want to strike a deal: If you agree to help me with some assignments, I'll arrange for people to escort you to the Overworld through the passageways.

-542721656.Text: Plotting? That's a strong word. I merely want to give you a chance to earn my trust. If I don't trust you, how can I ever agree to reveal our secrets to you?

-542721657.Text: Once we're finished and understand each other better, I'll send you on your way. I might even ask you for some Overworld favors in future.

-542721662.Text: Of course, whether you choose to be involved in that is completely up to you.

-542721663.Text: What do you want us to do?

-542721661.Text: I heard that some of you are very skilled. My requests won't be beyond your abilities.

-542721650.Text: We have some things we want to investigate.

-542721651.Text: Oh? Pray tell, what could there possibly be down here that would stoke the interests of an outsider?

-946006185.Text: A Stellaron. Have you heard that word before?

-946006186.Text: ...I can't say I have. But if you can successfully complete these assignments, every corner of the underground is yours to investigate. Down here, we're upfront with each other. Not like our Overworlder brothers and sisters.

-946006189.Text: Seele will act as your guide and help you with Wildfire's assignments.

-946006187.Text: I'm sure you'll get along just fine. A word of caution, though: try not to upset her.

-946006188.Text: Once that girl gets angry, there's nothing I can do for you, hahahaha!

-946006177.Text: So she's here to monitor us?

-946006178.Text: *sigh* I'd recommend you look to her as a guide, and not with ill intent.

620077756.Text: 【临时】希儿是来帮助你们的，别惹她不高兴。

620077758.Text: Not a chance, I have other uses for Sampo Koski... There are still a few debts I have to settle with him.

620077757.Text: And anyway, would you really feel better if he were by your side?

620077752.Text: Seele's nothing like him. She's loyal to a fault, and will do her best to help all of you.

620077751.Text: Goodbye for now. When you're ready, go and talk to Seele.

1023362315.Text: Quit stalling, you've got work to do.

1023362316.Text: You haven't told us what we need to do yet.

1023362317.Text: You've heard of Geomarrow, right? I'm sure Sampo must have mentioned it.

1023362311.Text: Good. That'll save me having to explain everything from the start.

1023362313.Text: *sigh* Has that guy told you anything useful?

1023362322.Text: Well, now you've heard of it.

1023362323.Text: The only thing we know is its name.

-1705521041.Text: Geomarrow is a crystalline ore that gives off a beautiful light — like an aurora. It's nothing like everyday crude ore.

-1705521040.Text: Wow~ Sounds like something out of a fairy tale!

-1705521039.Text: Hmph, it may sound like a fairy tale, but I'd advise you to stay well away from it when you see it.

-1705521038.Text: There's a power within it that makes people lose their mind.

-1705521045.Text: If you get close without protection, your body cannot withstand its power. Your mind will be blur if you stay even longer.

-1705521044.Text: Whoa... This took a scary left turn...

-1705521043.Text: Scary? Well, miners risk their lives on a daily basis for that stuff.

-1705521042.Text: They do it just so those ingrates on the surface can have their energy, and for whatever meager supplies they might get in exchange.

-1705521033.Text: Live for Geomarrow, die for Geomarrow. If you were born in the underground, that's your fate.

-2108805567.Text: They use it, but they may as well not bother. If you're in those mines every day, it doesn't matter how tightly you're wrapped up. It's still only a matter of time.

-2108805566.Text: Miners who breathe in Geomarrow dust over an extended period all end up catching crystallization sickness. I've heard people say it starts off in the lungs and gradually causes the body to crystallize. There's no cure.

-2108805565.Text: But recently, a bunch of profiteers came out of nowhere and started selling this so-called "wonder medicine." They claim it can cure crystallization sickness. I heard that some people did have reduced symptoms after taking it.

-2108805571.Text: Nothing's that simple down here, princess.

-2108805570.Text: Use your brain. If that medicine's so miraculous, why is it only sold on the black market?

-2108805560.Text: Does it cause crystallization sickness to get worse?

-542721626.Text: Huh, seems you have a conscience, at least.

-542721625.Text: The medicine helps with the symptoms and pain for a short while, but it can't cure the disease. Just when the patient thinks they're back from death's door, their sickness gets even worse.

-542721624.Text: Originally, the chief had assigned me to track down the source of the medicine. But since he asked you to come along, I guess I'm stuck with you guys.

-542721631.Text: Try not to do anything stupid, and don't hold me up.

1023362216.Text: The man we're looking for is called Dodge — Wildfire has been looking into him for quite some time. He often shows up around here.

1023362215.Text: This place is pretty busy, I can see why he's hard to find... It must be easy to blend in with the crowd.

1023362221.Text: These people... Are they all suffering from crystallization sickness? Is there a hospital nearby?

1023362220.Text: Hospital? What the heck is a "hospital"?

1023362219.Text: You know... a hospital. A place people go when they're sick.

1023362218.Text: Oh, I guess you mean Natasha's clinic. Yeah, we've got one.

1023362225.Text: But like I said, there's no cure for crystallization sickness. People come for medicine to dull the pain.

1023362224.Text: Recently, the numbers visiting the clinic suddenly shot up. They probably heard about the "wonder medicine" and wanted to try their luck.

-1705521139.Text: I've got descriptions from family members of Dodge's victims, and a portrait based on them. But that's about it.

-1705521140.Text: He's a sly operator, so it's gonna be hard to spot him with just this portrait. Wildfire has been staking it out here for a few days now, but we didn't find so much as a hair. I guess he caught wind of us.

-1705521141.Text: Is that why you wanted strangers like us to help investigate?

-1705521134.Text: I guess that's what the chief has in mind, yeah.

-1705521136.Text: Ho? You sound confident, I'll give you that.

-1705521137.Text: Anyway, now you know the situation. Go talk to those people and gather some clues. If anything happens, call my name, I'll be close by. Don't wander too far.

-1705521131.Text: I've been here a few times now, they'll probably recognize me.

-2108805665.Text: Aren't you worried that we'll make a break for it?

-2108805666.Text: Then I'd round you up and send you off for mining!

-2108805667.Text: Alright, that's enough. Get to work!

1023362249.Text: My neck... my arms... my legs... It hurts all over... Arghhhh...

1023362247.Text: How am I feeling? Just peachy, thanks... How do I look like I'm feeling?

1023362253.Text: I'm not here for a cup of tea, that's for sure.

1023362250.Text: The medicine... I must get that medicine... Where's Dodge gone off to?

1023362251.Text: Do you know where he is?

1023362256.Text: He told me to wait here... said he'd find me...

-1705521107.Text: Why are you asking? If you want medicine you better get in line...

-1705521109.Text: Where can I buy that medicine...?

-1705521099.Text: I heard there's someone selling a "wonder medicine" here, so I came to take a look.

-1705521098.Text: After my dad caught crystallization sickness, I tried forever to get him over to the clinic. The old man's too stubborn...

-2108805634.Text: He'd say things like: "It's nothing, I'm not going to let it affect the family."

-2108805633.Text: Then, a week ago, he collapsed... He's been delirious and talking nonsense ever since...

-2108805636.Text: I heard there's a trustworthy medicine trader here, but I've come several days in a row now and haven't caught sight of him.

-2108805630.Text: His name was... Dodge, I think?

-2108805632.Text: If you find him, can you let me know?

-2108805625.Text: I'm going to look around again. Goodbye.

-542721693.Text: I think you should give up.

-542721695.Text: I guess I'll look for him myself then.

-542721694.Text: Hehe... Brothers, sisters! Looking for medicine?

-542721688.Text: But of course! This stuff will get to the root of the sickness — guaranteed.

-542721691.Text: It's in great demand right now — you won't find it anywhere else. I'm only charging slightly higher than the market price, hehe.

-542721684.Text: This is special medicine that was no easy task to acquire! One dose and your illness will be cured.

-946006220.Text: Wait... Are you here for medicine? If you aren't here to buy anything, get lost!

-946006222.Text: Dodge, he... No... I mean, Dodge? Never heard of him!

-946006221.Text: You've heard the name, right? Your reaction gave it away.

-946006216.Text: You'd best come clean. Don't make me get the Wildfire here. I hear they are especially interested in peddlers of fake medicine recently...

-946006215.Text: Argh!? There...There's no need! My mighty awe-inspiring heroes, please have mercy!

-946006218.Text: I won't peddle anything here ever again, I promise!

-946006217.Text: Calm down, just tell us where we can find Dodge.

-946006211.Text: Where's Dodge? If you don't tell us, you're in for a world of pain!

620077721.Text: I'll tell you! I'll tell you! Please, don't hit me!

620077722.Text: On the way here, I saw him selling that mysterious medicine to a kid, not too far from here.

620077719.Text: Oh! He's w—wearing a distinctive coat, he shouldn't be hard to recognize.

620077720.Text: That's all I know, hehe. Please go about your business, and I won't bother—

620077725.Text: You're not going anywhere yet! Hand over the medicine.

620077726.Text: O—Oh that's right, the medicine. Here you go, hehe.

620077723.Text: I just saw the opportunity to make a buck, mixed some water with some powdered sugar... This concoction is harmless, really.

620077724.Text: Hehe, as long as the medicine does no harm. Frankly speaking, some people really light up with excitement when they get this, thinking they can be saved.

620077729.Text: You dare be a fraud in matters of human life. You'd best never cross paths with us again.

216793194.Text: It seems like Dodge is nearby. We should be able to catch him this time.

216793195.Text: These people, are they unaware that the medicine has side effects?

216793193.Text: Then why are they still buying it?

216793198.Text: 【临时】为啥不买？能暂时消除痛苦就够了。至于那点副作用？还不如来个痛快的。

216793199.Text: The people here place so little value on their lives... It's something I cannot understand.

216793196.Text: Tsk. You're a spoiled princess. How could you understand any of this?

1023362150.Text: So... How much money did you bring?

1023362153.Text: I went to the mines, but the foreman chased me away. He said children shouldn't be working there...

1023362152.Text: Huh? And you just gave up like that? What about your brother?

1023362155.Text: I—I want to save him! But I don't have any money left, I swear. Can I have some medicine first...?

1023362154.Text: Just a little bit, please! When I have the money, I'll buy the rest...

1023362157.Text: Are you kidding me!? I starved for two days going around in circles in that hellhole to get this medicine... Two days!

1023362156.Text: I risked my life for this stuff! You should have the decency to do the same and earn enough to save your brother. That's how it works, kid. Full price or nothing.

-1705521204.Text: Hah, why didn't you tell me you had a treasure like that lying around!? Haha, honestly...

-1705521205.Text: Age is never an excuse, kid. If they say you're too young to work in the mines, then go steal something. There's always a way out...

-1705521203.Text: Ahhh, more buyers. Let me conclude business with our little friend here first...

-1705521200.Text: You even deceive little kids! You're bad to the bone, you are!

-1705521201.Text: Tsk. You aren't here for business, but for trouble, huh?

-1705521198.Text: Huh!? Another kid! What's your name, kiddo?

-1705521199.Text: A kid-duping swindler like you doesn't deserve to even know my name... I am head of the Moles — Hook!

-1705521197.Text: I may not know who you are, but you come here looking for trouble instead of staying at home like a good little girl... Hehe, I'll just have to help your parents teach you a lesson!

-2108805731.Text: Haha, want to fight, Dodge? Well then, let me teach you a lesson, too... on the might of the Moles!

-2108805732.Text: Trying to beat up a child? Hmph, what a bully. If you dare assault her, I'll put a hole right through your palm.

-2108805729.Text: What does this have to do with you? Wait a sec... a Silvermane Guard uniform?

-2108805727.Text: We have a few questions for you, Dodge.

-2108805728.Text: Hey! Hold on, this guy was bullying us kids, so we should be the ones to punish him! You adults shouldn't interfere, this guy is mine! I—I got here first!

-2108805726.Text: We should be the ones asking who you are! You've ruined the Moles' plan, we put so much work into it!

-2108805724.Text: Of course. Stalling Dodge so that our boss, Hook, can come take care of this swindler herself.

-542721790.Text: Hehe, you didn't realize it, huh? Fooling everyone with a realistic performance is a piece of cake for I, Julian of a Thousand Faces!

-542721788.Text: Huh? New faces... No wonder I couldn't place you.

-542721789.Text: Enough talk! If you're looking for the boss, you've found her! The name's Hook, leader of The Moles! You can call me Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

-542721787.Text: That's right! We're heroes that specialize in defeating villains! An adventure squad bringing light and order to the dark Underground!

-542721784.Text: The Moles? You mean a bunch of kids that think a bit too highly of themselves?

-542721785.Text: Drat. Just a couple of meddlesome kids beat me this badly...

-542721782.Text: That's right, Dodge. Wildfire invested a great deal of energy into capturing you and could never succeed. How does it feel to have been bested by a couple of kids?

-946006318.Text: Hmph, you don't recognize The Moles as a legit adventure squad, yet you piggy-backed off of our hard work to capture Dodge. Isn't that right!?

-946006316.Text: Shame... it was the perfect plan! And of course you big ol' adults would barge in and take all the credit. If I had taken this guy to the clinic, I might have gotten out of cleaning for a week!

-946006313.Text: But you ruined it, it was all for nothing!

-946006314.Text: Which means that Pitch-Dark Hook the Great will have to go back to cleaning the clinic!

-946006311.Text: You've gone and beaten up other kids again, haven't you?

-946006312.Text: I didn't do anything wrong! Those rascals were chasing after Clara throwing rocks, of course I had to help her! Even if it meant the old witch would punish me with chores, I don't regret it!

620077623.Text: The old witch is the boss of the clinic. She trapped me and Clara there ages ago!

620077626.Text: So... why are these children looking for medicine?

620077625.Text: No idea. The old witch said she wanted some. I just wanted to help her find some to reduce my punishment!

620077628.Text: But the plan got ruined. The old witch is gonna be super angry!

620077629.Text: Ooh, eh-he... Beautiful Auntie Natasha is here!

620077631.Text: No idea. I didn't notice her at all.

216793096.Text: So this is the old witch? She's younger than I expected...

216793098.Text: What's the matter, Hook? You were taking a while, so I came to see how you were getting on.

216793101.Text: E—Everything's fine. We already caught the suspect.

216793100.Text: Oh, I was just about to say. The clinic is a complete mess, with nobody there to take out the trash or sweep up the broken glass...

216793103.Text: Auntie Natasha, I—I'm sorry. Me and the boss saw how worried you were about the fake medicine, so we thought we would help you out...

216793105.Text: Come on, don't be so strict, Natasha! Thanks to these kids, we were able to capture Dodge.

216793104.Text: What will you... do to me?

1782877038.Text: 【临时】哼，放心，我们只是想问问你手头那些号称能治愈晶化症药物的来源。

1782877037.Text: Hehe, I've heard of you clinic ladies, giving miners treatment free of charge — I know you're all a sham! You just want to steal my business, huh? ...No, I won't tell you a thing!

1782877040.Text: Mr. Dodge, you can barely stand, your leg looks to be injured quite badly! Come to our clinic so we can "fix" your leg up and put some "medicine" on it.

1782877039.Text: How calm she is makes the threat even... scarier...

1782877042.Text: Seele, do you want to come to the clinic for a chat? You guys are welcome too.

1782877041.Text: Fine. If you're also looking into fake medicine business, that's even better.

1782877044.Text: Let's go then. I'll prepare some delicious mushroom tea for you guys...

1023362183.Text: Oh, Nat, you're a medical expert — have you discovered anything about that medicine?

1023362184.Text: I've gathered samples from multiple sources, and have concluded that this medicine, let's call it "mystery tonic" for now, can indeed delay the onset of crystallization sickness.

1023362185.Text: 【临时】但是，它也有不止一种危险性副作用。

1023362187.Text: But after taking it, your liver will gradually become as hard as a rock, your heartbeat will speed up like an overheating piston, and your nerves will turn into fuses, ready to ignite at the smallest spark...

1023362188.Text: All the while, your brain will be telling you: "I'm fine, I feel great, nothing to see here."

-1705521171.Text: It's just that some of the ingredients... I don't recognize them. The production process doesn't look to be simple. Mixing this concoction should require a certain degree of training.

-1705521170.Text: Dodge used to transport items for a living. I don't understand how he suddenly learned to make medicine.

-1705521169.Text: Oh, I think he said: "I starved for two days going around in circles in that hellhole to get this medicine!"

-1705521167.Text: Hellhole, huh... I think we can guess where he's talking about.

-1705521166.Text: Could it be that he acquired this from within the Devil's Stomach?

-1705521164.Text: It's said that a long time ago, when the end times were upon us, a demon with a monstrous appetite appeared out of thin air. It swallowed many items from the Old World.

-2108805700.Text: Ever since, desperate people would venture back into that realm, looking for things to bring back.

-2108805699.Text: Huh? This... Devil's Stomach. Do you mean a Fragmentum?

-2108805698.Text: It sounds like you guys have encountered it already. "Fragmentum," huh...What a peculiar name.

-2108805697.Text: So people enter the Fragmentum to scavenge for a living?

-2108805696.Text: When we were fleeing our pursuers, we had no other choice but to enter the Fragmentum.

-2108805695.Text: Perhaps some people down here have no alternative. Is it really that hard to imagine why someone might be forced to scavenge there.

-2108805694.Text: If the medicine is hundreds of years old... wouldn't it have expired by now?

-2108805693.Text: ...I don't think this has a best before date...

-2108805692.Text: We undergrounders pay a tribute in Geomarrow to those in the Overworld, but what we get in return is barely enough to survive on.

-2108805691.Text: So some decide to brave the Devil's Stomach in search of valuable relics from the Old World.

-542721759.Text: Wantonly entering a fissure? This is a serious violation of the laws of the Union... It's simply too dangerous!

-542721758.Text: Hmph, if you Overworlders didn't banish us to the Underground, leaving nothing but pitiful scraps for the rest of us, then maybe nobody would be forced to risk their lives going into a place like that.

-542721757.Text: Hey, now's not the time to start fighting.

-542721756.Text: In other words, there's a good chance that this medicine came from the Fragmentum, right?

-542721755.Text: It's probable. I'll ask Dodge myself in a bit, and see what other details he can provide.

-542721754.Text: 【临时】唔…医生「问诊」的场面也许不太好看。还请在外面等待我的消息吧，以免吓到各位。

1023362612.Text: Ah, you wanna join the Moles?

1023362611.Text: Ah... As their boss, I will need to think about the team's future and bring in new blood.

1023362608.Text: The adventure squad! We dig up all sorts of hidden treasures and run rings around evil grownups! That's us! The! Moles!

1023362606.Text: Let me introduce you! The ruthless villain-smashing fist! The best treasure-hunting helper! The super weapon of the Moles! Dig-ger-tron!

1023362604.Text: Hah... Sounds like kids playing make-believe...

-1705520742.Text: 【临时】看，傲慢的坏蛋大人出现了！

-1705520743.Text: I might be small, but I'm also a legendary adventurer! I defeated the Iron King of the Mine and the Venomous Hex. Me! Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

-1705520746.Text: ...So, do you wanna join us or not?

-1705520748.Text: *Sigh* Whatever. You must be getting old or something.

-1705520749.Text: You probably forgot what going on an adventure feels like... just like daddy...

-1705520751.Text: Whoa! Your face went kinda scary-looking!

-2108805269.Text: Uh, wh—what I meant was... You probably can't fit into gaps and spaces as easily as us! For someone with your... build... you'd get stuck!

-2108805271.Text: Okay, great! You've got the right spirit!

-2108805272.Text: Being an adventurer is all about expressing yourself loudly!

-2108805274.Text: So you do wanna join, huh? Well sadly, this particular legendary adventure squad has age restrictions.

-2108805277.Text: Whoa! Your face went kinda scary-looking!

-2108805278.Text: Uh, wh—what I meant was... You probably can't fit into gaps and spaces as easily as us! For someone with your... build... you'd get stuck!

-542721328.Text: Uh, wh—what I meant was... You probably can't fit into gaps and spaces as easily as us! For someone with your... build... you'd get stuck!

-542721329.Text: But I really wanna go adventuring...

-542721330.Text: You shouldn't be all miserable like that when talking about adventuring.

-542721331.Text: Being an adventurer is all about expressing yourself loudly!

-542721334.Text: In that case! After much consideration from Hook and Diggertron, we came to a unanimous decision... Congratulations, this is a new member of The Moles! {NICKNAME}!

-542721335.Text: I didn't agree to join yet...

-542721337.Text: What am I actually doing right now...

-946005856.Text: Daddy! You're late today, did you only just finish?

-946005858.Text: You shouldn't run here so fast! It makes you cough.

-946005859.Text: I just wanted to see my precious daughter sooner! You didn't cause any trouble today, right?

-946005860.Text: Of course not! My friend here was keeping me company!

-946005861.Text: Well now, thank you for keeping this little troublemaker occupied.

-946005862.Text: I'm Fersman, a foreman at the mines. *sigh* Work keeps me very busy, so I don't have a lot of time to spend with my daughter.

-946005863.Text: We'll be heading back now. See you.

1023362645.Text: What's up? Come on, spit it out.

1023362642.Text: You seem to really cherish that scythe.

1023362643.Text: Of cours. It doesn't hurt to look after a good blade.

1023362640.Text: It even makes for a comfortable pillow. See, here. Just wide enough for my head.

1023362641.Text: Hm? What's with the weird look?

1023362636.Text: Natasha taught it to me. I gotta say, it feels like it was written for me! Too bad I can't sing in tune.

1023362637.Text: Have you known Natasha for a long time?

-1705520710.Text: I was an orphan. As a kid living on the streets, I spent my days hanging out with my crew.

-1705520713.Text: That was until I ran into Natasha. She taught me how to read and write.

-1705520712.Text: It turns out that I'm always better at charging forward.

-1705520717.Text: She's always been like that. But when Vache was still here... Ah, forget about what I said.

-1705520716.Text: Nothing. You should get some rest.

-1705520719.Text: Ha... I am pretty exhausted. Plus, I'll have to be up to take over the Wildfire night patrol later. Maybe you're right.

-280599569.Text: Why, I'm... on call of course! Sampo Koski is ready to go to hell and back for his best clients at the drop of a hat!

-280599572.Text: After all we've been through together, we should see each other as old friends!

-280599558.Text: Goodbye~ Huh? Argh — now wait just a minute! What's with the constant attitude?

-280599559.Text: After all we've been through together, we should see each other as old friends!

-1846683507.Text: As soon as I saw your face, I knew that I had more business coming my way.

-1846683508.Text: What can I do for you, friend?

-1846683511.Text: I have something I want to ask you.

-1846683512.Text: I like customers like you, nice and direct. What do you want to know?

-1846683513.Text: I heard that Natasha has a brother?

-1846683514.Text: It seems like Natasha's hiding something.

-1846683499.Text: Ah... you mean Natasha's forbidden word... "Vache."

882199848.Text: He was a doctor. Something like Natasha's mentor, I guess? I mean, the clinic was something he left to her. Vache was the one that opened the place up for large numbers of patients. It also became a kindergarten of sorts for the miners' children.

882199846.Text: Who knows? That fellow's been "missing" for a long time now.

882199845.Text: In Natasha's words: "He abandoned me and went back to his world!" You know how that story goes...

882199844.Text: ...or maybe not. *sigh* This is an emotional topic for me. I might need some "spiritual support" before I can carry on.

882199843.Text: Surely you're not gonna charge for this...

882199842.Text: I'm so happy we're starting to understand each other~

882199841.Text: I don't have any money... just the air that I breath.

882199856.Text: ...Steady now, no need to martyr yourself for a story.

882199855.Text: But information shouldn't be given freely, as they say.

-683884093.Text: Anyway, my payment is negotiable, hehe. For now, just keep your lips zipped and don't let Natasha know that I said anything.

-683884094.Text: Eh-he... maybe keeping a secret is also an acceptable form of payment...

-683884095.Text: Ahem, anything else you want to ask? If you've got the payment, I've got the intel~

-280599537.Text: You don't look like yourself when you're worried.

-280599539.Text: The people on this planet are so unfortunate. Every day is a challenge.

-280599538.Text: The people above ground need to hide behind the city walls, just trying to survive blizzards that can turn people into popsicles.

-280599541.Text: And the people above ground, well they barely have enough food to eat and clothes to keep warm. When they get sick, there's no hope for treatment. It's so sad...

-280599540.Text: Every day for them is a fight for survival.

-280599527.Text: Yeah, those miners with crystallization sickness... They must feel so helpless seeing their condition deteriorate.

-280599526.Text: They don't want people to pity them.

-1846683476.Text: I noticed that too. They're a tough lot, even the children.

-1846683475.Text: We must help them! No matter what...

-1846683478.Text: There's not much we can do.

-1846683477.Text: We can just do what we can.

-1846683480.Text: We haven't even dealt with our mission. We were chased off the moment we stepped into the city.

-1846683479.Text: Seele... Natasha... these people don't seem bad. Why can't they live in harmony with the people from the Overworld?

-280599633.Text: Mother, the underground is nothing like I'd imagined. What should I do...

-280599630.Text: What are you mumbling to yourself about?

-280599631.Text: How rude of you to eavesdrop.

-280599634.Text: I just want to resolve these matters quickly and get back to the Union.

-280599635.Text: Do you still think that the people down here are devils?

-280599624.Text: I... No, I must admit. The opinions that I had of those underground... weren't entirely accurate.

-280599625.Text: It seems like you've adapted well to life down here.

-1846683574.Text: One will never see the sky down here, and the air is cloudy and saturated with soot.

-1846683571.Text: And this stifling heat... it's nothing like the surface. I don't think I'd ever adapt to life down here.

-1846683572.Text: And yet... this is how they've always lived. How... have they managed to carry on all this time?

-1846683577.Text: When I return to the surface, I'll ask mother about the Underworld.

-1846683578.Text: You were quite excited when we caught Dodge.

-1846683575.Text: Those who deceive children are abominable. They shall face Plamya's judgment.

-1846683576.Text: It doesn't matter whether they're from the overground or the underground, children must be protected. No place has a future without children.

-1846683566.Text: Are you feeling better? You still look a little tired.

882199782.Text: If you don't mind, I'd like to be alone for a while. It's been a long day.

882199781.Text: I'm sorry. I just need some time to myself.

-280599598.Text: No. I was just going over what happened today in my head.

-280599605.Text: Seems you're even quieter than usual recently.

-280599604.Text: I try to keep quiet unless I know I can help. March 7th talks enough for both of us.

-280599603.Text: Both Seele and Natasha think that the medicine came from the Fragmentum. That argument has merit, but I sincerely hope they're mistaken.

-280599602.Text: Yeah, I don't fancy my chances there a second time.

-280599593.Text: I don't think the Fragmentum is that scary.

-280599592.Text: Last time, luck was on our side. The true danger of the Fragmentum lies in its unpredictability.

-1846683542.Text: It's like escaping from a labyrinth. Stumbling upon an exit is largely down to luck, and you'd be just as lost the next time you entered.

-1846683541.Text: Anyway, there's no point in dwelling on it... If those two guessed correctly, there aren't many other options available to us.

-1846683540.Text: What do you think about the crystallization sickness and the mystery tonic?

-1846683539.Text: ...Do I look like someone who's versed in medical science?

-1846683546.Text: If we were in a world with more advanced medical infrastructure, I don't think that sickness would be untreatable.

-1846683545.Text: But things are what they are down here. People can only save themselves. Besides, none of us are doctors.

-1846683544.Text: I suppose Natasha must seem like a ray of hope to them.

-1846683543.Text: We must be cautious if we're to make it out of here.

-280599700.Text: 【临时】我…我知道的，全都告诉你了。

-280599701.Text: It seems that my treatment did just the trick. Dodge gave up the location of the source of the fake medicine. Let's get ready to head out.

-280599694.Text: ...Natasha, are you coming with us as well?

-280599695.Text: Dodge isn't the only one who has been profiting off of the fissures. The prospectors are also gathering up things of value from inside. Dodge wasn't the first swindler, and likely won't be the last.

-280599696.Text: Find the source and completely cut off the supply. That's the most effective way.

-280599690.Text: Clara? Why are you here? Do you feel ill?

-280599691.Text: 【临时】娜塔莎姐姐…我是来看虎克的。

-1846683639.Text: Some kids nearby told me that she and Julian were talking about finding some strange medicine. I was worried that they were going to cause some trouble again, so...

-1846683640.Text: Fersman already took that naughty little rascal home.

-1846683641.Text: Don't worry, she was really a big help. Dodge thought children were all easy to deceive, so he let his guard down around Hook.

-1846683635.Text: Clara, since you're here, go ahead and take this medicine I prescribed for you. Remember to take it on time, and try to stay away from fissures and Geomarrow.

-1846683637.Text: The medical equipment I have here is too crude to make a proper diagnosis... but Clara came down with a bad cough recently...

-1846683631.Text: S—Sorry for making everyone worry. But, I've felt better lately, really.

-1846683632.Text: It would be great if every kid was as lucky as Hook, she is seemingly impervious to any ailments.

882199716.Text: The immune system of children is not as strong as that of adults, after all. All I can do for now is give them some medicine to try to keep Clara healthy.

882199715.Text: But when... when will be able to find a cure for crystallization sickness?

882199714.Text: You can do it, Natasha. When that day comes, you will be able to fulfill your brother's—

882199713.Text: This is my own aspiration... It has nothing to do with him.

882199720.Text: Have you been to the Fragmentum before?

882199719.Text: No, regrettably... I should be able to help if we get into a fight, but first aid is my first point of call.

882199724.Text: But we can't come to any decisions just yet. Seele hasn't given us the go-ahead.

882199723.Text: If it's fine with Natasha, it's fine with me.

-683884225.Text: But I won't be going with you guys. The chief's given me an urgent assignment.

-683884226.Text: Eh? You're not coming? I thought Mr. Oleg told you to protect us.

-683884227.Text: I just heard that Svarog's lapdogs are creating trouble again, so they need my help. Tsk, it never ends...

-683884221.Text: No-one you need to worry about. Focus on the counterfeit medicine for now!

-683884222.Text: Don't worry, you can leave this to us.

-683884223.Text: Hmph, I hope all goes smoothly so we can finally fix this awful mess. Then I can focus on completing the mission from Oleg.

-683884224.Text: Anyway, you guys better look after Natasha! If she doesn't come back in one piece, you've had it!

-683884217.Text: You can trust us. Let's go.

-683884218.Text: Well, it's just another trip into the Fragmentum. Hopefully March can have us jump to the right location this time.

2044999130.Text: Danheng, if you've got nothing nice to say, then—

2044999129.Text: Ahh, it's great to see so much enthusiasm. Sorry for the trouble.

2044999128.Text: 【临时】房门内传来一阵阵惨叫声……

2044999127.Text: 【临时】<color=#F2D79D>等待到娜塔莎的「问诊」结束。</color>

-280599666.Text: Hmph, tax period is over and peak boxing season is coming... If I can raise the price by 10%...

-280599669.Text: Hmm? What are you guys doing? You can't watch the fights without tickets. Otherwise, don't get in the way of my business.

-280599668.Text: Why there are so many people?

-280599663.Text: 【临时】你眼瞎吗？都叫「搏击俱乐部」了，那当然是来看拳的。

-280599665.Text: 【临时】你算哪根葱，敢这么跟我说话？我警告你，老子一句话就能让你消失。

-280599664.Text: Oh, wait, your strange outfit, could it be...

-280599659.Text: 【临时】哈哈，原来是「外来者」！怪我有眼无珠，多有冒犯，你们不要介意。

-280599658.Text: 【临时】自我介绍一下，我是史考特，「搏击俱乐部」的老板。我听说了，你们跟蓝甲人狠狠干了一仗，是吧？

-1846683608.Text: I heard you guys fought off the Bluecoats, right? Hehe, you're just the type of talent I need.

-1846683607.Text: 【临时】我这儿就缺这种人才。怎么样，有没有兴趣来打两场表演赛？观众保证爆满…当然，报酬肯定少不了你的。

-1846683610.Text: I'm not interested in fighting weaklings.

-1846683609.Text: 【临时】好大的口气，你该不会是怂了吧？

-1846683603.Text: 【临时】你这是什么态度？不会是想找个借口逃跑吧？

-1846683606.Text: 【临时】哼，无趣…不过我这儿还有个别的差事，说不定你会感兴趣。

-1846683605.Text: 【临时】你见过那群打拳的土鳖了吗？整天就知道埋头训练，个个搞得灰头土脸…他们根本不懂观众想看的是什么。

-1846683600.Text: 【临时】观众想要的是刺激，懂吗？刺激！感官上的冲击，视觉上的刺激，最好是拳拳到肉，那才是票房大爆的秘诀！

-1846683599.Text: 【临时】我已经受够这群小丑了，什么新花样都想不出来！没法给我带来收益的家伙，压根就是一群无耻的寄生虫。

882199747.Text: 【临时】是时候了，我得探索新的商业模式，能让所有人吓一大跳的模式。

882199748.Text: 【临时】你去过「裂隙」吧？那你肯定见过那种怪物，有着人的身形，穿着特别的盔甲……

882199745.Text: That's the boxer I'm looking for. Do you get my drift?

882199746.Text: 【临时】不懂也没关系——只要你能带回一套怪物的盔甲，我必然重金酬谢。

882199752.Text: 【临时】还要怎么考虑？反正对你们来说也是小菜一碟吧？

882199749.Text: Hmph, then I'll be waiting for you. Don't let me down...

882199755.Text: You bastard, do you know the consequences of making me angry?

882199756.Text: 【临时】哼，不懂规矩的外来人，先不跟你计较。我敢打赌，你会回来找我的。

-683884194.Text: That Scott really makes it hard for you to like him...

-683884193.Text: It seems he's quite a bully here in the Underground. It won't do us any good to get on his bad side.

-683884196.Text: However, such people tend to be generous and stick to their word.

-683884195.Text: It's beaten. Should we bring its armor back to Scott?

-683884190.Text: Look, there's something squirming on the surface of the armor. Disgusting...

-683884189.Text: Oh? Back so soon? I hope you've got something for me...

-683884192.Text: I swear by my money, if you try to cheat...

-683884191.Text: Here, the armor from the Fragmentum soldier you wanted.

-683884186.Text: Ahahaha! That's it. That's exactly what I want!

-683884185.Text: You had an annoying attitude but you still got the job done... I was right about you, ahahaha!

2044999161.Text: Here, take the reward I promised you. I stick to my words.

2044999162.Text: I'll come to you if any more business ideas come up, ahahaha!

2044999160.Text: Please don't bother me if there's nothing urgent. It's almost my turn.

2044999165.Text: Why do you dress like that?

2044999166.Text: Why? Because Mr. Scott said so.

2044999163.Text: Mr. Scott has been in bad mood because the fights haven't been selling well.

2044999164.Text: He said he'd come up with a new idea to attract the audience.

2044999170.Text: Yeah... I wonder who it is?

478915221.Text: Do you have a chance to win?

478915219.Text: Ahh... you don't know the rules, I got it.

478915224.Text: The fights are scripted, so the outcome is already settled. We're fighters, but also actors.

478915225.Text: I don't care if I win or lose, as long as I get paid.

478915228.Text: Will it really sell more tickets if the boxers dress up as a Fragmentum monster?

478915229.Text: Apparently, Scott thinks gimmicks can make more money than authentic fights.

-1087168721.Text: If that's the case, I feel sorry for the boxers who are training hard.

-1087168720.Text: That's true, but we don't have the right to change rules of this world.

-1087168723.Text: 【临时】不想把力气花在毫无意义的地方。

-1087168716.Text: 【临时】他们可是在拼命为你打工啊。

-280599762.Text: It seems to be an ancient recipe copied by the Fragmentum. It's rare to see one so intact.

-280599763.Text: Maybe someone in the city is interested in it? Let's take this.

-280599760.Text: Oh, you are Mr. Oleg's guests. Hello there.

-280599761.Text: It must be tiring running errands all day, huh? Why don't you come inside and take a break?

-280599756.Text: T-This is the "Everwinter Castle Sashimi Recipes," thought to be lost for almost a hundred years!

-280599757.Text: Where did you find it? T-Tell me, quickly!

-1846683705.Text: In the Fragm... I found it in the "Devil's Stomach"...

-1846683706.Text: What? The "Devil's Stomach"? That place contains such a treasure?

-1846683703.Text: Aren't you a bit too excited?

-1846683704.Text: You don't understand. This is... something I've been dreaming of.

-1846683701.Text: With this recipe book, I can finally accomplish what I've always wanted... the Chef's Course...

-1846683699.Text: You've never heard of it before? Chef's Course is a menu decided completely by the chef. The dishes are created using top ingredients. It provides the ultimate dining experience for the customers.

-1846683700.Text: Let me see... "Snowflake Fish Sashimi?" We don't have that fish in the Underground. I can probably replace that with Brownweave Mudfish, which should taste about the same.

-1846683697.Text: Can that be a proper substitute...

-1846683698.Text: Glacial King Crab Roe Sushi. "Glacial King Crab," oh, that's a cool name, isn't it? The roe of all crabs are more or less the same right? So Rock Crabs will have to make do.

882199649.Text: Salt Grilled Eel. Ah, another dish I've only seen in books. I've heard that eel is a kind of smooth tender fish, so it's possible to use olm instead...

882199651.Text: Outsider, can you do me a favor? I want to do a Chef's Course tasting and invite my dearest old customers.

882199654.Text: Can you help me contact them? I need to focus on the recipes.

882199653.Text: Of course, I will pay you for your troubles.

882199655.Text: Thank you, thank you so much! The tasting is scheduled for tonight. I must hurry up to get the ingredients.

882199658.Text: I've written down the names of guests I want to invite on this piece of paper. Take this. I'm counting on you!

882199657.Text: Sorry, we have other things to do...

-683884291.Text: Ah... right, of course you'd be too busy for such trivial matters!

-683884292.Text: Forget it, forget it, just go... Let me be alone for a while...

-683884289.Text: Sashimi... that means eating it raw, right?

-683884290.Text: Can those ingredients mentioned by Gertie edible? Why do I feel nauseous after hearing what she said...

-683884287.Text: For the sake of everyone's health, I strongly advise us against eating it.

-683884288.Text: You're back, outsider. Is there something you'd like to report?

-683884285.Text: Gertie invites you for the Chef's Course tasting.

-683884286.Text: Chef's Course... what is that? Did she come up with some other weird dishes?

-683884283.Text: That woman is always trying to come up with new dishes. But there're only so many fine ingredients in the Underground.

-683884284.Text: However, I don't want to hurt that innovation spirit of hers... Please tell her I'll join her tonight.

2044999064.Text: I got work to do. Please don't bother me if there's nothing urgent...

2044999063.Text: Gertie invites you for a the Chef's Course tasting.

2044999066.Text: Chef's... course? What is she up to now?

2044999065.Text: That Gertie likes to come up with bizarre creative dishes and people always complain... I don't dare let her handle food for the mining team anymore.

2044999068.Text: If you ask me, there's nothing wrong with her cooking skill, but why does she have to push herself so hard on creativity?

2044999067.Text: She may have her own pursuits.

2044999069.Text: Hmph... fine, it'll seem petty of me to complain about a free meal.

2044999072.Text: Tell her I'll be right over after I'm done here.

478915123.Text: Sorry, I was too focused on waiting for a red olm to appear...

478915122.Text: Gertie invites you for the Chef's Course tasting.

478915125.Text: ... Chef's Course? Ah! You mean that thing Master Gertie was talking about before?

478915124.Text: That means, she finally found the right recipes?

478915127.Text: We discovered a recipe book in the Fragmentum.

478915126.Text: But we'll see if they are the right ones...

478915129.Text: Ah, that's fantastic news! To tell the truth, I've always looked up to Chef Gertie. I hope to become a chef as good as her one day.

478915128.Text: She's not content with the daily simple meals, so she's always exploring new knife skills and cooking methods... There's a glow in her eyes whenever you mention cuisine...

478915131.Text: Ah, sorry, am I too excited?

478915130.Text: Please tell Gertie that I will definitely be there tonight! If she needs any help, I'm also glad to give a hand.

-1087168818.Text: Thank you! Thank you all for taking the time to help with preparations for the Chef's Course.

-1087168819.Text: Tonight's menu includes three courses. I hope everyone will... uh... give some feedback.

-1087168816.Text: The first one, tonight's appetizer, Brownweave Mudfish. Enjoy!

-1087168817.Text: As expected of Chef Gertie! The knife work is clean and skilled. This mudfish looks rich and luxuriant. Then without further ado...

-1087168811.Text: Hahaha, that's good! The freshness is what sashimi dishes are supposed to taste like!

1641714537.Text: Next up, Rock Crab Roe Sushi!

1641714536.Text: Of course, you'll need some dipping sauce when eating sushi. The sauce in front of you is my homemade soy sauce. Make sure you eat it all in one bite! It will leave a pleasant aftertaste in your mouth.

1641714539.Text: Sushi... that's a long lost cuisine from hundreds of years ago, right? It's incredible that Chef Gertie can recreate such an ancient skill! Here I go...

1641714543.Text: H-How is it? You have no idea how hard it is to get good rice in the Underground.

1641714542.Text: Uh... the crab roe... is plentiful...

75630596.Text: Sh-She threw up! Are the dishes cooked by Gertie that scary?

75630597.Text: Are you okay? Did you choke? The rice was a little hard, so I'll need to improve it next time.

75630599.Text: Ahem. Now for the final dish. You won't get disappointed. Salt Grilled Olm!

75630602.Text: Wow, the salty aroma, perfectly grilled olm meat with golden sauce. All look appetizing!

75630601.Text: Elaine, where did you learn to compliment people like that?

75630603.Text: Antonia, drink some water. I have a feeling this dish will be to your liking.

-1490453341.Text: It melts in your mouth. The gravy is very rich... Some sweetness lingers in your mouth. I've never tasted anything this delicious before!

-1490453342.Text: It's very, very fantastic! As expected of Chef Gertie!

-1490453339.Text: Do you guys really think so?

-1490453340.Text: I was wrong about you, Gertie. To cook with what's available to you, even if you don't have the best ingredients, is a testament to your ability as a chef.

-1490453337.Text: Such praise from Mr. Oleg is the motivation I need to continue innovating.

-1490453338.Text: Salt Grilled Olm... It's stunning flavors completely makes me forget about the previous two dishes.

-280599733.Text: Please, no more sashimi or sushi...

-280599732.Text: Speaking of which, it's my first time making dishes with raw and cold ingredients. Don't worry! I'll do better next time.

-280599731.Text: Outsider, I must thank you all! Thanks to the recipes you discovered, I had the opportunity to continue developing my skills at this age.

-280599730.Text: This is a little something for your troubles. Take it! If you're ever feeling tired, feel free to stop by for a meal.

-280599729.Text: Gertie is really passionate about cooking.

-280599728.Text: Undergrounders aren't too interested in the flavors of food, right? It's hard enough to fill their bellies.

-280599727.Text: Even so, it's good that there are still some people who pursuit beauty in life.

-280599303.Text: How arrogant. Don't blame me if this hurts!

-280599304.Text: You dare attack anyone on our turf.

-280599305.Text: Well? How about a couple rounds with me?

-280599309.Text: You dare attack anyone on our turf.

-280599311.Text: Well? How about a couple rounds with me?

741036259.Text: Shh, wait... it seems like there are people up ahead.

741036257.Text: Hmph, profiting from disaster... not today!

741036354.Text: Who are you? Can't you see we're busy? Go away.

741036353.Text: Wait a minute... I've seen you before. Are you from Wildfire?

741036352.Text: Stop overreacting. The boss told us this would happen.

741036351.Text: Hey, there's been a rockfall up ahead. You can't get through here!

741036350.Text: Humor me then. What are you doing here?

741036349.Text: Are you trying to save people, or trying to steal things?

741036363.Text: I'm going to knock you flat on your feet.

741036362.Text: Your boss will have to come and save you.

-1987847001.Text: Hey! Way to take the sting out of things.

-1987847002.Text: You guys are pushing your luck!

-1987847003.Text: *sigh* You asked for it. Let's see if your bite matches your bark.

741036064.Text: Pfft, amateurs. Doesn't take much to defeat them.

741036065.Text: Let's go. There will surely be more of them to take care further in!

741036158.Text: When did that kid overtake us?

741036161.Text: It seems like she's stirring up trouble.

741036162.Text: What should we do? If Hook's hurt by those things...

741036165.Text: What should we do? How about smash them to bits!

741036127.Text: Hook! What are you doing here...

741036130.Text: Ugh, yes, thank you very much...

741036131.Text: Now, go home! It's too dangerous down here!

741036128.Text: But... But daddy... They still haven't found him yet...

741036129.Text: It's Hook's birthday today... Daddy said he prepared a gift for Hook...

741036135.Text: *Sigh* If there's one problem I don't know how to solve, it's a kid bawling their eyes out in front of me...

-1987847229.Text: Oh? You've finally met your match, then.

-1987847228.Text: Hook, this is no play area, and it's no Natasha's clinic either.

-1987847231.Text: We're in a mine shaft right now. This tunnel could collapse at any moment.

-1987847230.Text: Those prospectors won't be nice to you just because you're a kid. They're not like your dad, and they certainly won't let you off with a light scolding.

-1987847225.Text: So, listen to Auntie Bronya. Be a good girl and go home, okay?

-1987847226.Text: Before going to work today, daddy said he prepared a gift for me.

-1987847221.Text: But Hook prepared a gift for daddy too... a long time ago. He doesn't know...

-1987847220.Text: Hook wants to give the gift to daddy herself.

-421763288.Text: Let's bring her with us and we can save Fersman together.

-421763287.Text: We can't leave her here by herself.

-421763290.Text: I agree. Even if we forced her to go back, a kid like her would just end up following us again.

-421763289.Text: ...If we run into trouble, it won't be easy with her here.

-421763284.Text: Oh brother, you guys are gonna drive me nuts...

-421763283.Text: Let me get one thing straight. If anything does happen, I'm taking Hook and getting the heck out of here.

-421763286.Text: Hook, you're sticking with me, got it?

-421763285.Text: O—Okay Seele! Hook will be a good girl!

-421763279.Text: We should get going! Let's keep Hook safe!

741036222.Text: We can't get through here, the path is blocked.

741036221.Text: It'll take a lot of strength to remove obstacles like these.

741036220.Text: There's not much you or I can do... Why not let Hook have a try.

741036227.Text: Hook and Diggertron to the rescue!

741036225.Text: Wow, it really worked! Hook, your amazing!

741036231.Text: Hook! Stay where we can see you!

741036230.Text: Let's try not to alert any nearby enemies.

-1987847132.Text: Ugh, why won't this elevator move?

-1987847133.Text: It won't start. It's damaged pretty badly...

-1987847134.Text: You'd think an experienced group of adventurers like us would have a handyman handy, right?

-1987847128.Text: Come with us, we won't hurt you.

-1987847129.Text: You're villains! Hook's gonna beat you up and rescue daddy!

-1987847130.Text: Hah! You wanna talk like a grown-up? Then you better fight like a grown-up!

-1987847131.Text: Scoundrels, picking a fight with a child?

-1987847125.Text: Tsk! Protect Hook! I'll deal with them!

-421763192.Text: Geez... are kids getting stronger these days?

1144320750.Text: Tsk, picking fights with children again——

1144320749.Text: Oi, cowards! If you've got the guts, fight me!

1144320748.Text: Not good! That thing's about to explode!

1144320752.Text: Do you need me to escort you from here, Fersman?

1144320751.Text: No... Ahem... I'm too tired. I want to stay here.

741036457.Text: Hmph, there are some more prospectors in our way up ahead.

741036555.Text: Look around this area for some clues. Don't go too far!

741036545.Text: They dropped a bunch of stuff too...

-1987846802.Text: Yep, machines left over from the Old World.

-1987846800.Text: Valuable... If they're still usable, you should be able to trade them for tins and supplies down here.

-1987846801.Text: It's a shame... These machines are all that remains from the destruction of the Old World. The oldest components must be more than seven hundred years old, so they're nothing more than scrap metal at this point.

-1987846806.Text: Ever since Svarog built up his army of prospectors, they've been scouring the underground for relics...

-1987846807.Text: Why would they want to collect scrap?

-1987846805.Text: People think he's trying to find the Old World weaponry...

-1987846810.Text: Others say that Svarog wants to form an army of robots to rebel against the surface...

-1987846811.Text: Everyone has their own idea about what the prospectors are doing... none of them are particularly positive.

-421762861.Text: Everyone, come here! What is this?

-562925720.Text: Fersman! Was he really working with the prospectors this whole time?

-562925727.Text: Shh... don't let Hook hear you.

-562925726.Text: It looks like he really doesn't trust Wildfire.

-562925724.Text: No wonder the old fool refused to work with us.

-562925715.Text: With respect, I don't think Oleg has told us the whole truth either.

-562925714.Text: I'm afraid we'll have to talk to Fersman himself if we want to get to the bottom of this.

-2129009664.Text: So they planned this whole thing from the start?

-2129009663.Text: It would appear that this so-called "accident" was premeditated by Fersman.

-2129009662.Text: As for why he'd go to such lengths, I'm afraid we'll have to ask him in person.

-2129009661.Text: Whatever his reasons, he's made a fool out of all of us.

-2129009668.Text: Why would he do something like this...

-2129009666.Text: I didn't know things would get this complicated...

-2129009665.Text: Feels like we've landed ourselves in a messy situation...

-2129009656.Text: It seems like wherever there are people, there'll be conflicts of interest... both the Union and the Underworld are alike in that respect.

-2129009655.Text: Ooh, nice speech. Had a cool "overworldy" leader vibe to it.

-1725725136.Text: Regardless of any conflict between the prospectors and Wildfire, our goal is unchanged...

-1725725135.Text: We must help Hook find her father. Nothing more, nothing less.

-1725725134.Text: Seele, Bronya! What are you whispering for?

-1725725141.Text: N—Never you mind! Come on, missy, let's go find your dad.

-562925631.Text: That huge mass of metal... th—that robot... that's Svarog!?

-562925628.Text: So the leader of the prospectors isn't human, but mechanical? I should've known to expect this many surprises in the Underground...

-562925618.Text: Wildfire. They should not be here.

-562925658.Text: Hook, you brat! Stop playing around! Come out, you hear me!

-562925661.Text: She's not here. One of Svarog's robots must've taken her away.

-562925660.Text: Damn it! I wasn't watching her. Why did I let my guard down...

-562925663.Text: If I'm not mistaken, Hook isn't in danger for the time being.

-562925662.Text: How can you be so sure, Danheng...

-562925649.Text: You remember the prospectors we met earlier? They seemed obsessed with capturing her.

-562925648.Text: Now Svarog has her. I'm sure there's an ulterior motive at play. We still have some time.

-2129009598.Text: So they want to use Hook as a hostage?

-2129009600.Text: Perhaps Svarog and Fersman aren't as close as we imagined.

-2129009599.Text: Is Hook valuable to them in some way?

-2129009601.Text: I'm struggling to see how. A troublemaker like her can only be bad news for Svarog...

-2129009604.Text: With any luck that girl will have sent the prospectors stir-crazy by the time we show up.

-2129009590.Text: There's no point speculating. Let's find a way forward.

-2129009589.Text: Over there! It looks like there's a path!

-1725725071.Text: No idea where it leads... Still, we won't find out if we don't try it.

-1725725070.Text: What are we waiting for? Let's go! We have to rescue Hook!

-562925562.Text: My people have taken her. Don't worry, her vital signs are normal. She has a strong constitution.

-562925563.Text: I will abide by our agreement.

-562925566.Text: Svarog, return Hook to us right now!

-562925567.Text: Seele... and you guys? Why... Why do you come... at such a bad time?

-562925552.Text: We have orders from Oleg to rescue you and all the miners trapped here.

-562925553.Text: But, it seems like Oleg was worried about nothing, you don't need any help.

-2129009502.Text: Wildfire's here. *cough*... Svarog, you should leave, n—now!

-2129009503.Text: Are you afraid of Wildfire, Fersman? I am unable to understand this kind of complicated emotion.

-2129009504.Text: Once a threat reaches a certain level in my processes, it should be eliminated. That is all.

-2129009505.Text: I can make them perish, Fersman.

-2129009507.Text: *cough* I'm... sorry! *cough* I cannot leave here, that's not part of my plan...

-2129009508.Text: That robot's about to attack! Take cover!

-1725724974.Text: I am unable to reach the same conclusion.

-1725724975.Text: People who intrude upon this place must be eliminated.

-1725724977.Text: Ugh, you fool! Look at the mess you've created!

-562925853.Text: S—Seele... You all are alright... I'm glad.

-562925852.Text: Fersman, can you still move? I'll take you to Natasha.

-562925851.Text: No... *cough* My illness... This is as far as I go.

-562925849.Text: I'm tired, I will stay here... The place I worked all my life.

-562925848.Text: I heard a miner say that if you follow these tunnels and keep going down, you will arrive at another world...

-562925847.Text: Hehe, such pain that I can't breathe... I've been waiting for this day. But the only... the only thing I'm worried about is... *cough* that child.

-2129009796.Text: So, I made a choice. I had planned to let this matter quietly disappear along with me, and I could leave this life without losing all dignity. It's a shame, you guys came... *cough*.

-2129009795.Text: I'm sorry to have concerned you all. And Oleg... I hope he can forgive me for my decision.

-2129009794.Text: That this... this diary. I wrote everything I want to say in there.

-2129009793.Text: Please, don't let the other miners find out. And don't tell... anyone else...

-2129009792.Text: Quick, leave here. Svarog and those robots... they won't rest until they achieve their objective. They will catch up soon...

-562925694.Text: Are you Adeline? We're here to rescue you.

-562925688.Text: Didn't you hear us? We're here to rescue you!

-562925689.Text: HEY! YOU WANNA GET SAVED OR WHAT!?

-562925690.Text: ...Ah, sorry... My ears aren't too good...

-562925691.Text: Are you... from Wildfire? Thank goodness... I'm saved...

-562925685.Text: Yeah. My brother's with Wildfire. But Mr. Fersman... he doesn't trust Wildfire, so I never told him.

-2129009633.Text: Do you know where Fersman is?

-2129009634.Text: When the accident happened... I saw him. He was running deeper into the mine...

-2129009635.Text: I tried to follow him for a while... but I must have taken a wrong turn...

-2129009636.Text: I hesitated, and then lost my way...

-2129009632.Text: I saw someone sneaking around. It looked like they were digging for something... I-I got scared, so I took cover.

-2129009626.Text: We've cleared most of the obstacles, so get moving!

-562925458.Text: If her eyes are any better than her ears, then old Fersman is somewhere further in...

-562925457.Text: Why would he run in the opposite direction?

-562925456.Text: So, daddy is in front of us, right?

-562925360.Text: How anyone could fall asleep in a place like this...

-562925361.Text: I don't know if he's brave or stupid...

-562925366.Text: Oi! Get up! You've got toadstools growing on your butt!

-562925367.Text: ...Wah— what? Where!? Where are the toadstools!

-562925364.Text: Huh... I can't feel anything there.

-562925365.Text: W—Wait! Who are you guys? Are you prospectors? If you're looking for your friends, they're not here.

-562925371.Text: There's been an accident in the mine, you need to get out of here!

-2129009303.Text: Rescue...? Oh, then there must have been an accident! Quick, run!

-2129009301.Text: Why didn't you run away with the others?

-2129009302.Text: Well... I guess I must have taken a nap in the hidden corner while everyone else was escaping.

-2129009308.Text: Well... it's a corner that's... pretty great for catching up on your sleep, hehe. It's hard to find — I'm the only one that knows where it is.

-2129009305.Text: Do you know where Fersman is?

-2129009306.Text: Mr. Fersman? He didn't get out?

-2129009311.Text: Come to think of it, I swear he was shouting something in my dream... Shame, if I hadn't been so tired I could've asked him.

-1725724776.Text: Prospectors? I saw some, quite a few in fact.

-1725724777.Text: I must have opened my eyes when a bunch of them were running past. The one leading the way was huge.

-1725724774.Text: If I remember correctly.... they went that way. Yeah, that's right! Deeper into the mine.

-1725724780.Text: Follow this path back out of the mine. You shouldn't run into anyone.

-1725724781.Text: Right. Just a five minute siesta and I'll be on my—

1023361195.Text: In other words, after the accident occurred, not only did Fersman choose not to escape, he chose to move deeper into the mine...

1023361196.Text: With a group of prospectors following soon after.

1023361190.Text: I'm not sure. At least we know that we're heading in the right direction.

1023361292.Text: 【临时】这道门锁住了…后面有什么东西吗？

1023361287.Text: 【临时】看上去应该是矿工的临时休息区。

1023361286.Text: 【临时】看来他们撤离的时候顺带把门锁上了。居然还挺冷静的嘛？

1023361288.Text: 【临时】走旁边的路吧，一定要找到那家伙……

1023361261.Text: That ore emitting a strange light... is that Geomarrow?

1023361258.Text: It's safe to take a closer look, right...?

1023361259.Text: If you just look at it, you'll be fine.

1023361257.Text: I had a dream about Geomarrow before.

1023361254.Text: Is that why you've got a death wish?

1023361269.Text: What happens if I do touch it?

-1705522095.Text: Your skin will start burning immediately, it'll be excruciating... and it'll leave a scar. You need protective gear and tools if you wanna touch it.

-1705522097.Text: Right, we should take it back to the Express. Maybe Himeko and Mr. Yang can find out more about it.

-1705522096.Text: At the very least, let's not shout at each other...

-1705522099.Text: Then let someone who knows what they're doing get some for you! Stay away from it.

-1705522098.Text: We can collect some samples when we leave, there's certainly no shortage of it down here.

-1705522101.Text: Let's focus on the task at hand. Time is of the essence.

1023361356.Text: Someone's over there... It seems to be a girl.

1023361355.Text: It must be a miner who didn't have time to escape. We have to save her!

1023361354.Text: Do you guys... hear someone snoring loudly?

1023361353.Text: How about we follow the sound to check it out?

1023361064.Text: Why is everyone so focused on Geomarrow?

1023361065.Text: Isn't that a question for those on the surface?

1023361066.Text: Is Geomarrow the only energy resource to mine down here?

1023361067.Text: We undergrounders have been searching for hundreds of years... We've yet to find another resource worth excavating.

1023361068.Text: The worst part is this: The supplies we receive in exchange are our only means of survival. This barren subterranean realm doesn't provide any of the basic requirements for self-sufficiency.

1023361069.Text: ...Sorry. I get a bit worked up sometimes. It's late, you should go and rest.

1023361070.Text: I'll find someone to make up our most comfortable rooms for you.

1023361158.Text: Danheng, what do you think about Oleg?

1023361160.Text: What do you think of him?

1023361163.Text: Elusive. I can't see his true intentions.

1023361162.Text: Right? Sometimes he's really nice, then other times he's really gloomy.

1023361165.Text: I feel a little uneasy helping someone like him...

1023361164.Text: For all we know, he probably trusts us even less.

1023361167.Text: What people who haven't left the underground for hundreds of years would think when a group of outsiders suddenly appear...

1023361166.Text: If you were in his position, would you be completely at ease?

-1705522196.Text: I guess you do have a point...

-1705522194.Text: We're just a bunch of courageous and kind-hearted extraterrestrials! Right?

1023361127.Text: It's nothing. I think I'll sleep standing up.

1023361132.Text: Eh? Sleep standing up? Are you trying to be Pom-Pom?

1023361130.Text: Pom-Pom's our conductor. He always sleeps standing up.

1023361131.Text: Maybe that's because of how big his ears are though. They'd probably get in his way if he tried to lie down...

1023361136.Text: It sounds like everyone's an eccentric where you guys are from.

1023361137.Text: Speaking of eccentrics, I wonder what Himeko and Mr. Yang are doing right now?

-1705522227.Text: They're probably on the train drinking tea.

-1705522226.Text: Hmph, why do they get to relax? Just thinking about that makes me annoyed!

-1705522228.Text: Someone didn't have any trouble falling asleep...

-1705522223.Text: We should rest too. We have a long day ahead of us tomorrow.

-1705522222.Text: Huh? Why's the bed so hard!?

1023361224.Text: 【临时】我听到了世界的哭喊。它在枯竭…它在受苦。

1023361223.Text: 【临时】自星空之外，异界的种子降下。

1023361222.Text: 【临时】迸放出炫目的光芒，扎根于世界的土壤。

1023361229.Text: 【临时】大地之下…生长出异样的脉髓。我视之为馈赠，救赎……

1023361459.Text: Did you guys feel that? Was it an earthquake?

1023361461.Text: Now that you mention it, I don't see her anywhere!

1023361455.Text: It sounds like something big must have happened.

1023361557.Text: Finally! I thought you guys might have slept through it.

1023361551.Text: Seele was about to give me a report.

1023361552.Text: One of our scouts just sent word that there was an incident in the mines. A monster came out of a fissure and made its way into a camp.

1023361547.Text: His tone wasn't reassuring, it sounds like a terrible situation.

-1705521801.Text: As if he didn't have enough trouble to contend with...

-1705521798.Text: I'll save you the "lunch" refrain this time. But... things can and do change without warning down here.

-1705521799.Text: How can this have happened so suddenly?

-1705521804.Text: It does give you guys an opportunity... to demonstrate Wildfire's good will, right?

-1705521802.Text: It's chaos down there, impossible to know.

-1705521808.Text: They're being evacuated, but they could use some extra hands.

-1705521809.Text: Outsiders, you agreed to help Fersman, right?

-2108806328.Text: Well, if you can defeat a few monsters along the way, all the better.

-2108806326.Text: There's no time to lose, you better hurry.

-2108806331.Text: The scout also mentioned that several prospectors were scavenging at the mine under the guise of a rescue effort.

-2108806332.Text: That'll be Svarog's men... They just don't quit.

-2108806329.Text: We've heard this "Svarog" mentioned time and again since we came here. I think it's about time you told us something about him.

-2108806330.Text: Well, to put it simply, he's the biggest danger there is below ground.

-2108806335.Text: He's gathered many prospectors under his command. They don't seem to put much value on their lives. You can be sure to run into them wherever ancient relics can be found.

-2108806336.Text: For them to try and make a profit out of a mining accident... It doesn't surprise me in the least.

-542722386.Text: What are you doing? There's no time to lose. Find Fersman and make sure he hasn't gotten into an accident!
