-280600720.Text: Mining accidents... Do they often happen?

-280600719.Text: Once every now and again, they're hardly unexpected.

-280600718.Text: Still, there hasn't been an accident at old Fersman's mine for a long time now...

-280600725.Text: 【临时】以我们看到的这种开采规模和强度，矿区里出现事故也不是不能想像。

-280600724.Text: I can't wrap my head around it. What kind of luxury do you Overworlders live in that you need so much Geomarrow?

-280600723.Text: ...I can assure you we're not doing as well as you might imagine.

-280600722.Text: Hmph, I'll be the judge of that... one day.

-280600712.Text: It can be whatever you want it to be.

-280600623.Text: ...I didn't know how serious it was.

-280600628.Text: We should hurry, let's find out what happened.

-280600626.Text: Peak? Oh no, he must still be inside!

-280600627.Text: He couldn't still be asleep, could he...?

-280600616.Text: *sigh* This is what happens when you're lazy.

-1846684566.Text: Can you give it a rest already? You've been moaning for ages — it's driving me crazy!

-1846684563.Text: What do you know, huh!? I lost my living down there!

-1846684564.Text: It was such a mad rush to escape, no wonder I left my pick behind.

-1846684569.Text: Huh. Well, you'll be in trouble if Mr. Fersman finds out, I guarantee you.

-1846684567.Text: I can't believe it. Is this all that Wildfire can muster?

-1846684568.Text: Can we even count on them?

-1846684557.Text: Wildfire is no different from the prospectors — all they care about is relics. It doesn't matter to them whether we live or die.

-1846684558.Text: There's something wrong with this world... Wildfire? Svarog? Scumbags — the lot of them!

-280600654.Text: Huh? Is that a child over there?

-280600658.Text: Hook, what are you doing here? Go home right now!

-280600661.Text: It's not home without daddy... and he's still down there. Hook's really worried.

-280600660.Text: ...Can you help rescue daddy for me?

-280600647.Text: Come on now, you're boss of The Moles, right? This is no time for tears...

-1846684596.Text: Mm! I wasn't crying. My eyes were just... sweating...

-1846684598.Text: Uh-oh... I think my eyes are sweating too.

-1846684600.Text: Hook, go home and wait for us. I'll save your dad, I promise.

-280600559.Text: What did I just say!? Bystanders should vacate the—

-280600560.Text: S—Seele! Sorry, I didn't see you there!

-280600561.Text: Joshua? What are you hanging around here for? Why aren't you down there saving people?

-280600562.Text: I—I just came from down there. Look at my clothes, they're all dirty...

-280600563.Text: Save it! Tell us what the situation is, and make it snappy.

-280600550.Text: Actually, everyone's been asking me the same question... I still don't have a clear idea myself!

-280600551.Text: I questioned some of the miners that made it out. Some told me the tunnels had collapsed, others said that monsters had appeared. I even heard that some dunderhead from the demolition team might have blown something up.

-1846684499.Text: So, to sum it up in four words: You haven't got a clue.

-1846684501.Text: How many people are trapped below?

-1846684502.Text: I have a roster here. There were twelve people total in this team.

-1846684503.Text: After the accident, people came from everywhere to get a look. Luckily, the miners are all familiar faces... otherwise I might have missed them in the crowd.

-1846684504.Text: If I'm not mistaken, there are still three people down there...

-1846684505.Text: Adeline. People say she's a bit hard of hearing, so she might have been slow to react.

-1846684506.Text: Peak. We call him Slug. He likes to goof off, so who knows which corner he might have ended up in.

882198856.Text: *sigh* Mr. Fersman got dealt a bad hand. He hadn't been on night shift for a long time, now he gets caught up in all this on his first time back.

882198855.Text: His health has been getting worse recently — a lot of us were telling him to take a break. The old man's too stubborn to listen.

882198854.Text: As far as miners go, he's reached a good age... Perhaps the mine has finally claimed him...

882198853.Text: Watch your tongue! Hook's here, you know.

882198852.Text: S—Sorry! *sigh* Mr Fersman's got it tough — a daughter to raise and everything...

882198851.Text: Have Svarog's people gone down already?

882198849.Text: I wanted to stop them, but they were armed to the teeth. Plus, we were outnumbered...

882198864.Text: We had to let them in.

882198863.Text: 【临时】情况都知道了，这就下去看看。

-683885085.Text: Th—Then I'll leave the rescuing to you...

-683885086.Text: I—I'll leave the rescuing to you.

-280600852.Text: Once we're down there, there might not be an easy way back.

-280600849.Text: Bronya, you don't look too good.

-280600847.Text: It's just... I really want to help that child.

-280600846.Text: Of course, we want to help her too.

-280600757.Text: In any case, the incident with the medicine has come to a close. You've done well, outsiders.

-280600754.Text: Now, let's talk about your next assignment.

-280600755.Text: Let's get on with it. Bronya's eager to get back to the surface.

-280600752.Text: ...Not to say that I'm not, but I never said anything to that effect.

-280600753.Text: I understand, but young folks have to learn the virtue of patience.

-280600750.Text: Can March 7th take a break first?

-280600751.Text: You're just saying that so you can take a break!

-280600748.Text: I won't be long, and when I'm done nagging you can have your break.

-280600749.Text: The next assignment has to do with Mr. Fersman — a foreman at the mines.

-1846684697.Text: Huh, Mr. Fersman? Isn't that Hook's father?

-1846684698.Text: That's right. Then I assume you met him already?

-1846684696.Text: Very good, that should help things go smoothly.

-1846684694.Text: Geez, your memory is terrible! Natasha's clinic — he's the one that picked Hook up, remember?

-1846684691.Text: If you've met him already, let me cut to the chase.

-1846684692.Text: There are dozens of mines of various sizes down here. The one you went to is the largest, and Fersman is the foreman there.

-1846684689.Text: That mine is abundant in Geomarrow. When it comes to taxes, it's got one of the best yields of all the mines. Quite a few miners have decided to settle in the vicinity.

-1846684690.Text: Of course, there's no such thing as an easy lunch down here, let alone a free one. Monsters will frequently run about causing havoc.

882198658.Text: The monsters are quite the headache for Fersman, and he's always on the lookout for someone to help him.

882198660.Text: The situation is more complicated than you think.

882198662.Text: It's not that we don't want to.

882198661.Text: Fersman is a stubborn customer — we don't exactly see eye to eye. I'm sure he knows that Wildfire could help him out, but he doesn't want us to get involved.

882198664.Text: Is there some past animosity between you?

882198663.Text: Differing views and differing goals I guess. Something for us undergrounders to figure out, and something of little concern to outsiders. The only thing you need to focus on is the assignment at hand.

882198666.Text: Are you trying to use us to improve relations with Fersman?

882198665.Text: There's no fooling you... I could do with people like you by my side, it's a pity you can't stay here forever.

-683885284.Text: Simple? Like I said, there's no easy lunch down here. Trouble creeps up on you in the most unexpected situations.

-683885281.Text: Chief, let me go with them. Maybe I can have a talk with old Fersman... as a member of Wildfire.

-683885279.Text: So, that's the assignment. Go to the mines and find Fersman. He might get you to defeat a few monsters for him... or he might get you to do something else.

-683885280.Text: Regardless, try to win him over for me.

-683885277.Text: Anyway, it's getting late. Go and get some rest.

-280600788.Text: Wow, I didn't expect you to be so considerate.

-280600783.Text: I'm stronger than I look, it takes a lot to knock me down for the count.

-280600782.Text: It felt like the incident in the Fragmentum hit you pretty hard.

-280600779.Text: Well, I don't think there's much to regret.

-280600778.Text: He's never deviated from the path of being a healer.

-1846684728.Text: I knew him. He wouldn't have regretted choosing that path.

-1846684727.Text: Do you want to come with us for the next assignment?

-1846684729.Text: *sigh* It's getting late I'm afraid, I have to go back to the clinic. The children will need looking after tomorrow.

-1846684724.Text: I wish you all the best of luck. We will meet again.

-280600691.Text: Ah, outsiders, you have returned. And you've brought Natasha with you.

-280600692.Text: It looks like you've been through a lot.

-280600693.Text: I trust you have been well since we last met, Oleg?

-280600686.Text: Very much so, thank you, Miss Natasha.

-280600688.Text: Come, tell me about what you found.

-280600689.Text: 【临时】一行人将寻找药物来源的过程转述给了奥列格。

-280600682.Text: So you're saying that you found a lab in the Fragmentum, and where able to cut off the medicine at its source?

-280600683.Text: We won't be seeing that medicine back on the market.

-1846684634.Text: As far as I could tell, despite the side effects, it was a great help to many of the miners.

-1846684627.Text: One should never rely on that kind of false comfort.

-1846684628.Text: One day, I'll find a way to cure crystallization sickness.

-1846684629.Text: But the only way to completely prevent the terrible illness brought on my Geomarrow is to leave the Underground.

-1846684630.Text: I can't wait to hear of your success, Natasha.

-1846684401.Text: 【临时】喂！现在可不是开小差的时候，快点去找费斯曼大叔吧！

-1846684400.Text: 【临时】希儿姐姐，你们刚才跑哪儿去了！

-280600361.Text: {NICKNAME}, Danheng, come and see what I found!

-280600358.Text: The color of this rock is so special, it's totally different from other geomarrow ores!

-280600359.Text: Indeed, I don't feel burnt in my hands either. It feels like... a normal gem.

-280600364.Text: This place is so badly contaminated, miners seem to have all moved away. No wonder no one ever found it before.

-280600365.Text: Want to authenticate it? What if it's valuable, hehe!

-280600362.Text: ... Hmph? What do you want from me?

-280600363.Text: I stopped authenticating relics long ago, just leave me alone...

-280600368.Text: We want you to authenticate this rock.

-280600369.Text: Are you deaf or stupid... Hmm? Not an ancient relic? Alright, give me that thing.

-1846684301.Text: Hmph... It's not an ordinary rock, where did you find it?

-1846684302.Text: Inside a huge mine. It was scattered in a very contaminated place.

-1846684299.Text: Are you saying that you've never seen it before?

-1846684300.Text: Ah-hem, are you kidding me! There's nothing underground that Balaway hasn't seen before!

-1846684305.Text: Right, I've looked at it, it's just a normal rock with some dirty stomach stuff attached.

-1846684306.Text: Nothing valuable. I'll take it for 80. How about that?

-1846684303.Text: 80? No way, I won't even sell you for 800!

-1846684304.Text: You seem to be sure that this rock is valuable...

-1846684309.Text: You tell me, just look at it! It's crystal clear and has this royal blue glow... It must be a rare rock!

-1846684310.Text: Hmph, don't trust me huh? Go ask someone else then, no one would pay for this crap.

882199054.Text: Pfft, sure we'll do! {NICKNAME}, Danheng, let's go.

882199053.Text: Hmph, tax period is over and peak boxing season is coming... If I can raise the price by 10%...

882199056.Text: Hmm? What are you guys doing? You can't watch the fights without tickets. Otherwise, don't get in the way of my business.

882199055.Text: Oh, it's you again, outsiders. What's up? Want to watch a game? Or want to try it out yourself?

882199050.Text: We want you to authenticate this rock.

882199049.Text: What? Authenticate? That's something the Miner's Lamp Balaway does!

882199052.Text: Oh ya, but I heard that old fella stopped doing it for no reason.

882199051.Text: That's why you came to me? I bet you heard some good stuff about my eye on valuable things! Hahaha!

882199046.Text: Give it to me, let's have a look.

-683884888.Text: Done, it's worth nothing. You can have it.

-683884885.Text: No-nothing!? No way, take a closer look!

-683884886.Text: I'll tell you the same thing no matter what. It's worth nothing. Plus, the underground has nothing but millions of shiny rocks, this crap is nothing special.

-683884891.Text: But it's not a geomarrow, look how pretty it is!

-683884892.Text: Girl, beauty is worth nothing when it comes to rocks. Stop dreaming now, you're not getting any money from the undergrounders without something with authentic value.

-683884889.Text: Hmph, forget it! {NICKNAME}, Danheng, let's find someone else.

-683884890.Text: I got work to do. Please don't bother me if there's nothing urgent...

-683884896.Text: A rock? Alright, bring it over.

2044998468.Text: We found it deep inside the mine. It's pretty, isn't it?

2044998467.Text: Strange, I've been working in the tunnels for hundreds of tax periods, never seen rocks like this before.

2044998470.Text: It does have pretty colors... What else does it do?

2044998469.Text: What... else? Isn't being pretty good enough?

2044998464.Text: It's just a rock no matter how pretty it is. Does it heat up stuff? Is it hard enough? These are the things you look at when you authenticate the value of a rock.

2044998463.Text: But in other worlds, there're rocks that sell at a good price for being pretty...

2044998466.Text: Other worlds? I have no idea what you're talking about. But here in the underground, this crap does nothing.

2044998465.Text: No way! I can't believe it! There must be someone who sees its true value.

2044998460.Text: I've never seen you being this persistent before...

2044998459.Text: Of course! I saw it right at first sight from a pile of gravel.

478914527.Text: Something that catches our eyes must be real treasures! I can't be wrong!

478914529.Text: You seem irritated... Am I wrong?

478914523.Text: Ah right, I've got an idea! I'll let Yang authenticate when we're back to the express.

478914522.Text: Yang is knowledgeable, he must know how valuable this rock is!

478914525.Text: I can't believe this snowy world used to have all sorts of civilizations.

478914524.Text: What happened exactly during the past millennium?

478914519.Text: Ah, {NICKNAME}, you're back! Come tell me about your story in Jarilo-VI?

478914518.Text: March 7th wants to authenticate a rock.

-1087169414.Text: Oh? Gem authentication is not quite my strength, but it does no harm to take a look.

-1087169415.Text: Don't worry, I trust Yang's taste!

-1087169418.Text: I can't tell its specific constituents from the look of it, but one thing I'm sure of is that it's a real gem, not something replicated by the Fragmentum Energy.

-1087169419.Text: You might need a careful laboratory test to estimate its value.

-1087169416.Text: Ah! That thing in Yang's hands, what's that!

-1087169422.Text: Oh? Pom-Pom likes shiny round stuff, just like a cat!

1641713941.Text: That-this... yes, it must be it!

1641713943.Text: The legendary Cesium Blue Pillar Stone!

1641713942.Text: Cesium Blue Pillar Stone, is it valuable?

1641713937.Text: March 7th only cares about money! So superficial pom!

1641713936.Text: Generally speaking, gems that contain cesium element is usually green. There're pillars of other colors as well, but usually not as perfect.

1641713939.Text: The one you have in here... hmm..mhm...

1641713938.Text: It's the best of the best Cesium Blue Pillar Stone pom!

1641713933.Text: Such precious item deserves to be preserved away from March 7th. Let's keep it here on the express!

1641713932.Text: Are you trying to enrich your little vault with it, Pom-Pom...

75630000.Text: Non-nonsense pom! Pom-Pom doesn't do stuff like that!

75629999.Text: Oh? I don't see your sincerity though?

75630002.Text: Da-damn it! Here, take it back!

75630001.Text: Hehe, that's the way to go!

75629996.Text: Hehe! I knew it's valuable! These undergrounders knew nothing about rocks!

75629995.Text: Rather say, "someone's garbage could be a treasure for someone else."

741035201.Text: I heard there was an incident in the mine. I hurried over to see if I could help.

741035202.Text: Everyone has already evacuated. Are you guys hurt? Any wounds that need tending to?

741035196.Text: What is it? Oh, where's Mr. Fersman...?

741035197.Text: Fersman... didn't want to leave. He insisted on remaining down there.

741035199.Text: So, this entire ordeal was all Mr. Fersman's careful planning, from start to finish.

741035208.Text: He knew he didn't have long to live...

741035209.Text: So he wanted to entrust Svarog with everything...

741035234.Text: How can you leave Hook behind like this...

741035233.Text: How's that kid gonna survive by herself down here!

741035231.Text: Hook shouldn't be in any immediate danger, and I think I know where she might be...

741035230.Text: You should report back to Oleg first. He is surely concerned about your status.

741035102.Text: I can't! No matter how hard I try, I just can't figure it out!

741035100.Text: Why Fersman would put his trust in that tin can over us.

741035105.Text: Do you really need to ask? Improve the welfare of all undergrounders.

741035104.Text: Maintain order, repel prospectors, repair and recycle relics... What else would be doing?

741035110.Text: And what have we accomplished? We couldn't save Fersman, and Hook was captured.

-1987848252.Text: Other than smashing in some scrap metal, we didn't achieve anything...

-1987848253.Text: Maybe Svarog's not that bad after all?

-1987848254.Text: I was just wondering that myself...

-1987848255.Text: But he really tried to kill us back in the mine.

-1987848248.Text: If we can't reason with him, then we're gonna come to blows again sooner or later.

-1987848251.Text: I just can't figure it out...

741035132.Text: Natasha said she should be safe.

741035133.Text: I heard her... But if I can't confirm that with my own two eyes...

741035138.Text: You seem to care a lot about her.

741035136.Text: The Union children — at least, the ones I met... they all seem... spoiled.

741035137.Text: But Hook's different. She reminds me of... when I was young.

741035142.Text: Were you also born with monstrous strength?

-1987848221.Text: No, I wasn't referring to that.

-1987848220.Text: When I was young... I was also quite impulsive. I also relied on my mother a lot.

-1987848223.Text: If anything were to happen to Madam Cocolia, I wouldn't hesitate to act.

-1987848222.Text: Mm... talking about this is a little embarrassing.

-1987848217.Text: We've got one more assignment left. After that, you can go home.

-1987848219.Text: I'll try my best to convince Madam Cocolia about you. I promise.

-1987848218.Text: Maybe her judgment was wrong... but... just this one time.

741035435.Text: Did you know Mr. Fersman well?

741035434.Text: He... had visited the clinic previously. Even then, his prognosis wasn't optimistic.

741035429.Text: Maybe he didn't want Hook to worry. He also wanted to sustain his family, so he continued to work.

741035428.Text: Do you know where Hook is right now?

741035431.Text: I have a good idea where she might be...

741035430.Text: Don't worry, leave it to me.

741035425.Text: How should I report all this to Oleg?

741035424.Text: Just because you're protecting a secret, doesn't mean you can't talk about facts.

-1987847922.Text: I suggest you tell Oleg the truth about how Svarog entered the mine and attacked you.

-1987847923.Text: But as to whether you tell Oleg everything about Mr. Fersman's plan... I'll leave that to you to decide.

-1987847920.Text: If you guys decide to respect his last request of us, to not tell others about his plan, then I will remain silent as well.

-1987847926.Text: You should get going. Oleg will be waiting for you at the same spot.

-1987847927.Text: It was a blessing that no one was hurt.

741035466.Text: You should pull yourself together as soon as possible.

741035460.Text: Naturally. It's only natural to feel sad.

741035463.Text: But one should remain rational in these situations.

741035456.Text: Even as we speak, farewells are happening throughout the universe.

741035457.Text: The grief that we share is real, but there's nothing special about it.

-1987847891.Text: Remember that — it might help you to focus on the here and now.

-1987847889.Text: Always think of what is relevant to you, and what your most pressing goal happens to be.

-1987847888.Text: Learn to observe the world around you, rather than getting caught up in it.

-1987847895.Text: Those are good things to start with.

-562926747.Text: I didn't think things would turn out like this...

-562926750.Text: Good. We've gotta hang in there.

-562926748.Text: Yeah... It's hard to come to terms with everything that's happened over the last few days.

-562926749.Text: Me, Himeko, and Mr. Yang... We've been to many worlds before.

-562926738.Text: But we were mostly just passersby.

-562926739.Text: This is the first time I've delved so deep into a world... and gotten to know the people living there.

-2129010687.Text: It seems that there really isn't a person out there without a sorrow or two of their own...

-2129010688.Text: I hope we can get back to the Astral Express soon...

-562926713.Text: Before we find Oleg... shouldn't we make sure we're on the same page?

-562926712.Text: Let's just be honest with him. There's nothing to hide.

-562926719.Text: But didn't Natasha tell us to keep Mr. Fersman's secret?

-562926718.Text: Let's respect Mr. Fersman's will and keep his secret.

-562926717.Text: Yeah, I think that's for the best.

-562926716.Text: What about you, Seele? What do you think?

-562926707.Text: Are you asking me to hide things from the chief? I've never done that before.

-562926706.Text: But that is what old Fersman wanted... even Natasha requested it...

-2129010656.Text: I'll let you guys lead the way.

-562926681.Text: I understand. Thank you for saving the people down there and figuring out the truth. This mission is complete.

-562926682.Text: That headstrong fool. He lived and died in those mines.

-562926683.Text: Were you and Mr. Fersman friends before?

-562926684.Text: A long, long time ago. Water under the bridge.

-562926685.Text: But in short, the mining accident was caused by Svarog, correct?

-562926672.Text: Under the command of Svarog, a large group of prospectors broke into the mines under the guise of a rescue effort, and proceeded to cause havoc.

-562926673.Text: This was an attack — an attack on you as you commenced your mission for Wildfire, as well as an attack on the miners as they gathered Geomarrow to survive.

-2129010621.Text: I solemnly request all of you to assist Wildfire in defeating Svarog.

-2129010622.Text: The missions don't end, do they?

-2129010623.Text: Hey, did you forget your promise to help us investigate the Stellaron?

-2129010624.Text: I remember. I would never go back on a promise.

-2129010625.Text: We don't actually work for you, you know?

-2129010626.Text: Don't misunderstand. These aren't orders or anything.

-2129010627.Text: We are still in the Underground to look for the Stellaron...

-2129010628.Text: You guys really are determined when it comes to this "Stellaron" business. How important is this thing exactly?

-2129010613.Text: Of course. You didn't travel all this way just to help us undergrounders. We shouldn't press for more help than you have already given us.

-2129010614.Text: But I believe helping Wildfire defeat Svarog can bring you benefit as well.

-1725726095.Text: How will you persuade us this time?

-1725726096.Text: You have seen the Geomarrow in the mine, correct?

-1725726097.Text: No-one knows how Geomarrow was discovered, how it became the source of energy to support life in the city, or why fissures are scattered across the Underground...

-1725726098.Text: At least, no-one still alive today can remember the answers to these ancient questions. But robots are different.

-1725726100.Text: There are hundreds of years of information recorded in ancient relics. We've been gathering these items to try to piece together the truth of our planet's history, and discover knowledge lost to time.

-1725726101.Text: For this reason, as you have probably noticed, Wildfire has developed mutually beneficial relationships with most of the mines.

-1725726086.Text: We help the miners eradicate monsters and maintain order. In return, they hand any relics they come across over to us.

-1725726087.Text: Out of all the ancient relics we have come across, the most intriguing is...

1003157259.Text: The Union has mechanical units that are able to recognize and execute commands, but none of them are able to fluently communicate with humans like Svarog.

1003157258.Text: When I first saw Svarog, I thought it was a person clad in armor.

1003157256.Text: No-one knows. He just suddenly appeared in the Underworld, perhaps unearthed by a prospector.

1003157255.Text: The prospectors see Svarog as having dominion over all the ancient machines in the underground, as well as the ability to repair those relics... like some kind of mechanical god.

1003157254.Text: And so, Svarog also became their god. The prospectors formed a group with him as their leader, obeying his every command.

1003157269.Text: Some of them even believe that Svarog will be able to bring a better life to the people of the Underworld, just as Plamya has done in the Overworld.

1003157268.Text: You dare compare Plamya to a machine?

1762672148.Text: Even praying to Plamya can't make broken machines work again, right?

1762672147.Text: So the prospectors began stealing your business.

1762672146.Text: Business? Wildfire doesn't gather ancient relics for profit.

1762672145.Text: But those prThe prospectors have no principles. They make deals with the Silvermane Guards, dismantling precious ancient machinery and selling it for scrap metal...

1762672144.Text: Even if Svarog's arrival managed to unite them, their nature will never change.

1762672143.Text: They disrupt order, and we strive to maintain it. It's that simple.

1762672142.Text: Why was Mr. Fersman unwilling to cooperate?

1762672141.Text: Fersman was exhausted by life down here. He just wanted to keep his head down and make a steady living.

1762672156.Text: He felt all along that Wildfire was brewing something behind the scenes, and saw us as a threat to everyone's livelihood.

1762672155.Text: I don't know where the conspiracies come from. We are open and honest. Everything we do is for the sake of maintaining peace and order.

196588207.Text: What does any of this have to do with the Stellaron?

196588206.Text: Svarog guards the secrets of the past. Defeat him, and we will be able to recover the truth of history. Help Wildfire one more time, and I promise you will benefit immensely.

196588205.Text: Danheng, March, what do you think?

196588204.Text: Hmm... The vanishing of the Old World, the onset of the Eternal Freeze. It's difficult to discount the arrival of a Stellaron.

196588203.Text: Fascinating... All this forgotten history and ancient technology.

196588202.Text: But... does Svarog have to be destroyed?

196588201.Text: We're not warmongers. We only desire knowledge.

196588200.Text: 【临时】史瓦罗不愿与任何人分享知识。想要获得那些历史真相，唯有击败他这一条路。

196588215.Text: Also, his invasion of the mines and the damage he caused — his actions have already shown that he does not value human life.

196588214.Text: It seems that this won't be resolved peacefully then...

599872734.Text: We met Svarog face to face, he's no pushover. It won't be easy for us to deal with him.

599872733.Text: Won't be easy is an understatement. We'd be putting our lives in serious danger.

599872732.Text: What will Wildfire be doing while we're in the field?

599872731.Text: We won't be on the sidelines.

599872730.Text: A guide will take you to their hideout, but you'll have to find a way to infiltrate.

599872729.Text: After confirming Svarog's position, I'll send reinforcements to assist you.

599872728.Text: But remember, do everything you can to avoid escalation. I don't want the underground to get dragged into war.

599872727.Text: Alright, let's get to it then.

599872742.Text: That's the spirit! You guys are making me feel old.

599872741.Text: We could do with a break first.

-966211208.Text: When you're ready, go to this location. I'll tell our guide to wait for you there.

-966211211.Text: I'll need to trouble you again. Please accompany our outsider friends.

-966211213.Text: Alright. I'll be waiting for the good news.

-562926650.Text: Do we really want to help Wildfire deal with Svarog?

-562926653.Text: After reading the diary Mr. Fersman left behind, Svarog doesn't seem all that bad.

-562926652.Text: You're only thinking that way because you found out about his deal with Fersman.

-562926655.Text: Try to see it from another angle. If we hadn't entered the mine... if we hadn't rescued those trapped miners.

-562926654.Text: As a bystander in that situation, how would you see Svarog?

-562926641.Text: I'd... I'd probably see him as a villain.

-562926640.Text: I never thought of it that way... You're pretty smart sometimes, Danheng!

-562926879.Text: Sampo? What are you doing here? Wait a minute... There's no way you're the—

-562926876.Text: Aha! Long time no see, valued clients.

-562926877.Text: It didn't occur to you that the guide would be yours truly? *sigh* How saddening...

-562926874.Text: Who else down here — or up there for that matter — is more well-informed, resourceful, and loyal to Wildfire than I, Sampo Kos—

-562926872.Text: Svarog's hideout. Just take us there already.

-562926873.Text: Hehe, my dear Seele, hasty as ever.

-562926870.Text: Svarog's usual haunt is close at hand, I'll take you guys over at once.

-562926871.Text: How come you know his whereabouts?

-2129010819.Text: Well... that's a secret of course. How could a qualified intelligence dealer reveal their sources?

-2129010820.Text: This is base camp for the prospectors. After they dig up relics from the Fragmentum or the mines, they bring them all here.

-2129010817.Text: My powers of deduction tell me that their boss must be nearby~

-2129010818.Text: So we might be seeing action up ahead, right?

-2129010815.Text: Hey, easy soldier! The chief told you to keep a low profile, right?

-2129010816.Text: Let's find a way to infiltrate first, then we can make a few inquiries with our prospector friends.

-2129010813.Text: I'm firmly opposed to solving conflict with violence.

-2129010812.Text: Otherwise, I'd be run off my feet right now... But, here I am passing the time with you guys.

-1725726292.Text: Speaking of which, I'm planning on starting up a new line of business called "Handshakes with Aliens."

-1725726293.Text: Interested? I'm sure we could work out an equitable profit-sharing agreement~

-1725726291.Text: Stop wasting your breath and start leading the way.

-1725726288.Text: *sigh* Very well! Stay close to me then.

-562926846.Text: Wildfire wants to get rid of Svarog, but is forcing us to do the deed for them.

-562926845.Text: No matter how you look at it, there are underhanded tactics at play here.

-562926844.Text: Undergrounders don't care about underhanded tactics, missy. The priority is getting things done.

-562926843.Text: Put it this way. With Wildfire's existing manpower, they don't have a chance in hell against Svarog.

-562926842.Text: The problem is you guys are too capable, so old Oleg has seen a glimmer of hope.

-562926813.Text: Oleg told us to avoid escalation, or we could start a war...

-562926808.Text: You can't imagine how loyal the prospectors are to Svarog.

-562926809.Text: If you do slip up, the Underworld may well sink into civil war.

-562926810.Text: Huh... When I put it like that, it sounds kinda exciting, don't you think?

-562926783.Text: The entrance is a lot more impressive than I'd imagined!

-562926782.Text: I thought it'd be a bunch of ruins or something.

-562926777.Text: Those prospectors make a pretty penny, they'd never allow their god to stay in a shack.

-562926776.Text: But if you ask me, I don't think Svarog could care less.

-562926779.Text: Alright, let's quit loitering and get in there.

-562926481.Text: It seems like Svarog's lapdogs have made this place their home.

-562926486.Text: Well, infiltration was easier than expected, hehe...

-562926484.Text: There's a big door up ahead.

-562926485.Text: Let's go and have a look.

-562926490.Text: 【临时】（机器人身上有个显眼的按钮…）

-2129010423.Text: 【临时】喂！别瞎碰淘金者的东西，万一自爆了怎么办？

-562926450.Text: So, how have your takings been recently?

-562926449.Text: Terrible. Wildfire's got a tight grip on the mines these days — it's nearly impossible to sneak in.

-562926448.Text: *sigh* Same here. If things go on like this, we might have to try our luck in the Devil's Stomach.

-562926455.Text: Be my guest. I'd never venture into that godforsaken place...

1023360170.Text: I heard that Anton met the boss a few days ago?

1023360173.Text: Yeah, that fella dug up a whole heap of relics from who-knows-where. Makes me sick...

1023360172.Text: He's only been at this half a year, how did he manage it?

1023360167.Text: Damn it, I wanna meet the boss already!

1023360204.Text: You mean the mine incident? I heard bits and pieces.

1023360205.Text: It sounds like things got out of hand. I heard the boss brought a kid back with him.

1023360198.Text: What? A kid? Who was it?

1023360199.Text: Not a clue. The word is that he sent her away again after.

1023360236.Text: Ugh, this is a heavy door... Oh, I see how this works.

1023360234.Text: Is that... a spell or something?

1023360233.Text: It might be, but we've yet to find a planet where it actually works.

1023360232.Text: Sampo, you must have been here before?

1023360230.Text: Ever since I sold a few of my ancient relics to the Marev brothers, I'm not very willing to hang around on prospectors' turf.

1023360245.Text: Do you know how to open this door?

1023360244.Text: Not a clue. I'm afraid that due to some... personal misunderstandings, I'm not very welcome on prospector home turf.

-1705523118.Text: A fat lot of good you're turning out to be.

-1705523119.Text: Tsk, tsk, so much for our well-informed, resourceful—

-1705523121.Text: Ahem, it's like this... I've played cards with some of the more loudmouthed prospectors before. I remember them boasting about how fancy Svarog's lair was.

-1705523122.Text: Let me think... What was it they said again...?

-1705523123.Text: Oh! That's right. They said that only prospectors who are certified and have made contributions can enter Svarog's domain.

-1705523124.Text: Those invited are questioned by robots to filter out any outsiders that might have made it in.

-1705523125.Text: I'll bet the mechanism for this door has something to do with the questions they were talking about~

-1705523110.Text: Sounds logical enough. But where do we get the answers from?

-1705523111.Text: Let's mingle with the crowd... I'm sure we'll uncover some clues.

-2108807645.Text: You can't rush an investigation you know~

-2108807646.Text: Why are you still standing there? Answer the question and I'll let you pass~

-2108807647.Text: Walk around and see what the prospectors say.

1023360269.Text: Do you know? I heard the tax rate will be increased by <color=#FFFF00>ten percent</color>.

1023360266.Text: What? Another increase? They can go to hell...

1023360267.Text: As if relics weren't already impossible to find... How do they expect us to make a living?

1023360264.Text: In this tax period, <color=#FFFF00>I only got 20 and I have to hand over 12 of them</color>.

1023360265.Text: Even if I get a decent price for the remainder, I'm still barely making enough to feed myself.

1023360262.Text: The way things are going, we'd probably be better off back in the mines...

1023360038.Text: Found it. This must be what we're looking for.

1023360040.Text: How do we start this piece of junk?

1023360046.Text: Certification result: Failure. Subject has no access permissions.

-1705523316.Text: What the heck is it talking about...

-1705523317.Text: I've got a bad feeling about this.

-1705523310.Text: What is the relic tax rate for the next tax period?

-2108807843.Text: Certification result: Failure. Subject is judged to be a threat.

-2108807841.Text: W—Wait a minute! What does that mean!?

-2108807839.Text: Tsk, looks like we're gonna have to solve this problem with our fists after all!

-2108807836.Text: Where can we find answers to questions like this...

-542723902.Text: There were a few prospectors talking on the way in.

-542723903.Text: Perhaps we can get some information from them.

1023360072.Text: I'm guessing this is our "open sesame..."

1023360073.Text: It seems like my deductions were on the money~

1023360074.Text: In other words, we only need to repeat the process twice more, right?

1023360075.Text: So what are we waiting for? Let's find the next one.

1023360103.Text: But the robot's broken. What should we do now...

1023360109.Text: I'm guessing this is our "open sesame..."

1023360108.Text: I don't think one is going to cut it, though.

1023360107.Text: Let's go and look for the others somewhere else~

1023360137.Text: Yo, let me show you the real old school. It's the dark horse reppin' clan prospector comin' straight from the underground, I'm out to get ya.

1023360134.Text: 【临时】老东西的韵脚全部都弱爆，让你知道谁才是冠军材料！

1023360135.Text: 【临时】你的节奏和旋律都蠢得不行，只会吹牛的废柴怎么会赢？

1023360140.Text: 【临时】没见过世面的容我提个醒，连冠的说唱歌手叫<color=#f2d79d>「黑豆饼」</color>！

1023360434.Text: Found it! The second certification robot!

-1705522920.Text: Who is the reigning champion of the Prospector Clan Rap Battle?

-1705522919.Text: Certification result: Failure. Subject is judged to be a threat.

-1705522925.Text: I think I should start answering these.

-1705522923.Text: I guess we'll have to turn you into scrap metal!

-2108807446.Text: Certification result: Failure. Subject is judged to be a threat.

-2108807452.Text: I think I should start answering these.

-2108807450.Text: I guess we'll have to turn you into scrap metal!

-2108807455.Text: Give me some time, I'll be back soon.

-2108807456.Text: Let's go around and gather some more intel.

1023360467.Text: There's only one left! We're acing this!

1023360468.Text: Hehe, as expected of my valued clients, I only deal with the best.

-280601745.Text: We managed to collect two passes.

-280601712.Text: No, no, no. You can't insert the microcrystalline unit into the heat sink substrate like that.

-280601711.Text: How do you know? Balaway from Miner's Lamp said that you need to pay attention to the interfaces when assembling relics.

-280601710.Text: To make this thing move again, we need to connect the microcrystalline unit to the operating brake module. Otherwise, it's just a pile of scrap metal.

-280601717.Text: If you believe a word that senile idiot Balaway says, you're done for. The last time he acted on his own advice, he electrocuted himself.

-280601716.Text: You should be asking people with real smarts. No one is better at fixing machines than Marev.

-280601715.Text: I saw him connecting a microcrystalline conductor unit to a logic control hub to get a vibrating drill to work.

-280601714.Text: Marev? Him and his brother wouldn't know a real relic if it him them in the face. Last I heard, he got scammed by someone selling counterfeit junk.

-280601679.Text: This must be the last one.

-1846685619.Text: A microcrystalline unit should be connected to which component?

-1846685623.Text: Certification result: Failure. Subject is judged to be a threat.

-1846685625.Text: Doesn't sound good. Get ready to fight!

-1846685611.Text: Just try and stand in our way, you'll be scrap metal in no time!

882197734.Text: Give me some time, I'll be back soon.

882197733.Text: Let's go around and gather some more intel.

-280601649.Text: Well, well, seems we make quite the team! Svarog's certification system was a breeze.

-280601648.Text: Let's get back to that door and take another look.

-280601874.Text: Well, well, seems we make quite the team! Svarog's certification system was a breeze.

-280601875.Text: I—I abhor such violent methods, of course... but one can't argue with results, can they?

-280601872.Text: Let's go back to the door and see what we can do.

-280601842.Text: Ahh, the fruits of impeccable intel! Not to mention my—

-280601841.Text: Yeah, yeah, thank you very much. Geez.

-280601840.Text: We must keep our guard up from here on out.

-280601839.Text: You remember what the chief said, right? When we find Svarog, we need to avoid an escalation.

-280601837.Text: Good. We'll have to wait until reinforcements arrive.

-280601836.Text: Someone else may be itching for a fight...

-1846685786.Text: Hmm? Are you trying to imply something?

-1846685785.Text: Alright, alright, there's plenty of time for tension yet.

-280601811.Text: Why is it so dark in here?

-280601812.Text: It's too quiet... not a prospector in sight.

-280601781.Text: Hey! Why's there another door here!?

-280601780.Text: Its structure seems more complex than that of the outer ring door...

-280601775.Text: Hey! What's all the commotion? Where did you come from?

-280601774.Text: Hold on, you guys look kind of familiar...

-280601776.Text: You're the one we beat up back in the mines, the one that ran away with their tail between their legs.

-280601771.Text: H—Hey! Don't be so mean! ...Anyway, what are you guys doing here?

-280601770.Text: Wait... did you decide to join the prospectors?

-1846685720.Text: What!? We'd have to be insane to—

-1846685719.Text: T—To refuse! Haha, yes brother, we are here to seek refuge with his magnanimity, the great Svarog.

-1846685722.Text: We have gotten our certifications in order, but now this large door has blocked our path.

-1846685721.Text: Brother, do you happen to know how we might gain an audience with the great one, hmm?

-1846685716.Text: Hmph. I guess you must have realized that those amateurs at Wildfire have no future, right?

-1846685715.Text: If you want to meet the boss, you'll need more than certification. To can't pass through this door because have not yet made enough contributions.

-1846685718.Text: This area is just where those average oafs outside place the ancient relics they collect. If you want to qualify to see the boss, you have to have made outstanding contributions first.

-1846685717.Text: For example, turning over one hundred relics for three consecutive tax periods.

-1846685711.Text: That's right! To become a Senior Prospector and receive direct instruction from Svarog, you have to be as hardworking as me!

882197635.Text: One hundred relics per tax period... Is this guy delirious?

882197636.Text: Where would we find that many relics?

882197634.Text: Working one's way up to Senior Prospector, like myself, is dependent on one's own capability.

882197639.Text: 1% genius and 99% sweat and tears. You gotta have both, you understand?

882197637.Text: Please, just because you're not up to the task doesn't mean others aren't.

882197638.Text: You need to put in real elbow grease... and avoid any funny business!

882197643.Text: Three tax periods... Can Himeko and Yang wait that long?

882197644.Text: Eh? Are you seriously considering it?

-683886305.Text: We don't have the time or the intention to do anything of the sort.

-683886308.Text: Then it serves you right, no wonder you got turned away.

-683886307.Text: Hmph, people like myself put in the hard work. What's stopping you from achieving the same?

-683886302.Text: What kind of terrible pep talk is this guy trying to give us?

-683886301.Text: Enough with this nonsense. Looks like I'll just have to beat you up again!

-683886304.Text: Wait, stop! Ahem... Well, there is another way, but it all depends on whether you've got the guts.

-683886303.Text: *yawn* Perhaps we should just drag our Senior Prospector here over to the door?

-683886298.Text: N—No, don't! Besides, I may have slacked off a little in the last tax period... I don't have authorization for this door anymore...

-683886297.Text: You can slam my head against the door if you like, it still won't open.

2044997050.Text: Well, if you've made up your mind...

2044997047.Text: Have you heard the name "Bloody Rabbit" before?

2044997053.Text: So you have? I see you're better informed than the others.

2044997054.Text: A ghostly prospector, a demon of ill omen with a cursed left hand...

2044997051.Text: Or something like that? I think it came up in a card game once.

2044997052.Text: That's the one. We call him the "Bloody Rabbit" around here.

2044997057.Text: When it comes to finding relics, he's better than three of me put together.

2044997058.Text: He's a short figure wrapped in blood-red clothes. No one dares look at his face. They say that those who gaze upon will be wracked with terrible nightmares, even during the day...

478913108.Text: ...and anyone who accidentally touches him, however lightly, will suffer from bad luck, or even worse, go missing completely... There are no exceptions.

478913109.Text: Each time he appears, everyone scatters. No one dares approach him.

478913106.Text: So, if you have the guts, see if you can try and take a piece of his pie...

478913107.Text: Where can we find the "Bloody Bunny?"

478913110.Text: Tell us. Where can we find him?

478913116.Text: That's where he gets his relics, never the mines.

478913117.Text: Are you really going? Hehe... Don't say I didn't warn you.

-1087170833.Text: There isn't a monster down here that I haven't seen before. Bring it on!

-1087170832.Text: So it's back into the Fragmentum... to catch a rabbit.

-1087170835.Text: We'd have better luck pulling one out of a hat.

-280601481.Text: It's not working... It says "No Access Permissions."

-280601478.Text: Why do you need permission to do everything here!

-280601448.Text: Come on then, what intel do you have about this rabbit?

-280601447.Text: Nothing concrete, I'm afraid... mostly urban legends.

-280601446.Text: Undergrounders are a superstitious bunch, and rumours spread quickly down here.

-280601453.Text: Bloody Rabbit? Nothing more than a cover.

-280601452.Text: Whoever it is, he's disguised himself as something supernatural... and now others don't dare to approach him. Sounds like a strategy for a monopoly over all the relics in the Fragmentum, wouldn't you say?

-280601451.Text: Hehe, seems we could all learn a thing or two from our rabbit. He appears to be a successful businessman.

176385164.Text: Sampo, what kind of people want to become prospectors?

176385167.Text: Well, they come in all shapes and sizes: slackers, gamblers, fortune-seekers, and...

176385161.Text: People sick of working themselves to the bone for the sake of Overworlders, hehe.

176385198.Text: Red clothes... short... could that be...?

176385192.Text: A Guardbot? Why is it here in the Fragmentum?

176385228.Text: Look out! Find somewhere to hide!

176385039.Text: You... We met at the clinic...

1742468976.Text: Huh! So the Bloody Rabbit was Clara!?

1742468975.Text: I—If it's okay with you, please don't call me that. I don't like it...

1742468978.Text: Ah, sorry! I was a bit overwhelmed, that's all.

1742468977.Text: What are you doing here, Clara?

1742468979.Text: ...components that can only be found here. So...

1742468982.Text: That's way too dangerous! Does Natasha know?

-986414381.Text: I told her that... I was out for a walk...

-986414377.Text: There are lots of monsters in the stomach, I need someone to accompany me.

-986414378.Text: So my friend sent Perkins to help.

-986414375.Text: But before I knew what was happening, everyone was already fighting.

-986414376.Text: It'll take a long time to fix Perkins...

-986414373.Text: So it was all one big misunderstanding...

-986414374.Text: What's your relationship with the prospectors?

579669560.Text: The prospectors... seem to be afraid of me.

579669559.Text: They gave me lots of nicknames.

579669562.Text: I don't mind though. After all, I'm... sick.

579669561.Text: I just want everyone to look after themselves... so nobody comes to harm.

579669563.Text: You said the robot was sent by your friend.

579669566.Text: Would this "friend" happen to be Svarog?

579669568.Text: Oh, uh, e—everyone! Can you keep that a secret? Please?

579669567.Text: I know that many people are afraid of Mr. Svarog.

2145753501.Text: But if possible, I want everyone to get along peacefully...

2145753502.Text: That's... a lot to take in.

2145753505.Text: Can you take us to Svarog?

2145753504.Text: Do you have permission to enter Svarog's base?

2145753506.Text: But... I'm sorry! I... I can't take you there.

2145753509.Text: Mr. Svarog is very nice to me.

2145753508.Text: But he doesn't like other people... Especially people from Wildfire.

-583129854.Text: If I bring everyone to him, it might get dangerous...

-583129855.Text: Ahh, well that wouldn't be any good now, would it?

-583129852.Text: The young lady makes a very reasonable point!

-583129853.Text: And yet, you guys still have your reasons for wanting to see Svarog.

-583129850.Text: My, what a pickle we're in! Hehe...

-583129851.Text: *sigh* Could you at least try and sound less shifty?

-583129848.Text: Look, it's too dangerous here. This is no place for children, Clara.

-583129849.Text: But... I still have components to collect. I can't leave yet.

-583129846.Text: I don't want to cause any trouble for anyone... I should be okay by myself...

982954088.Text: If something happened to Clara, Natasha would never forgive any of us.

982954091.Text: Clara, we'll help you find those components.

982954090.Text: I—I'm sorry I can't help everyone... and sorry for taking up your time.

982954093.Text: Come on then, let's make it quick.

982954092.Text: Perhaps Clara has some useful information.

176385065.Text: What should we do...? I can't believe Clara and Svarog know each other...

176385066.Text: But if we don't complete our assignment, then we won't be able to go back to the surface.

176385067.Text: Ideally, we need to come up with a peaceful solution.

176385068.Text: If we don't, one side will suffer heavy losses.

176385097.Text: I think this unit can be used...

176385103.Text: Judging by the shape, it looks like some kind of... fan?

176385101.Text: The cooling system of the base's generator is broken... With the generator not working, the gate can't be opened and the defense system is offline.

176385100.Text: There's lots of abandoned machinery down here... I like to find them and see if they can still be used for anything...

176385107.Text: I think if we move this bit here... and then, pry it open like this...

176385106.Text: Okay, I think I did it right.

1742469039.Text: {NICKNAME}, remember what I said in the mine? The adventurers' team should always have a handyman.

1742469038.Text: Hook usually breaks things... so I help her fix them.

1742469037.Text: Gradually... I just got good at it.

1742469044.Text: Is that why you get along with robots so well?

1742469043.Text: Can you repair more complex stuff too?

1742469042.Text: Actually... this is where I first met Mr. Svarog.

1742469041.Text: I remember his body was covered in dust... and he had a lot of dents and scratches.

1742469048.Text: Back then, he had a problem with his arm. It was shaking, like an old man's.

1742469047.Text: I felt sorry for him, so I helped him look for components.

-986414315.Text: Mr. Svarog taught me how to fix all kinds of things. Eventually, I even fixed all his problems.

-986414317.Text: I can't believe what I'm hearing.

-986414318.Text: The children down here are remarkable.

-986414311.Text: Doesn't look like there's any more useful things here.

-986414312.Text: We need to go further in... is that okay?

176385134.Text: Umm... I don't know how to explain it...

176385135.Text: 【临时】应该是某种「轴承」部件吧，用来支撑机械内部的旋转结构。

176385132.Text: 【临时】以前培育太空植物时使用的微型发电机里面也有类似的零件。眼前的这个只是大了好几号。

176385133.Text: Huh? You used to grow plants?

176385138.Text: Bearing component... So that's what it's called. Thanks Danheng, I'll try and remember that.

176385139.Text: Will you disassemble this as well?

1742469071.Text: It's the first one I've seen, but it doesn't look too tricky.

1742469069.Text: Mr. Svarog's base has two big generators. They each have different components that malfunctioned...

1742469070.Text: I did a bit of basic maintenance and discovered that the issue with one is that the bearing component is rusty.

1742469073.Text: I think that should be fine.

1742469074.Text: Yikes, you're a natural kid! A wasted talent, I should add.

1742469079.Text: Young lady, would you like to go into the relic business with me? There are a lot of people up there willing to pay a handsome price, y'know.

-986414284.Text: Thank you, everyone... for helping me find these components.

-986414283.Text: It's... time for me to go back now.

-986414286.Text: Are you going back to Natasha's clinic?

-986414285.Text: No, I only visit there once a week...

-986414280.Text: I... need to go to Mr. Svarog first.

-986414279.Text: Are you going back to Svarog?

-986414276.Text: You don't need to accompany me...

-986414275.Text: It's too dangerous here. We're staying with you.

176385428.Text: Did Seele want us to come up with a plan or something just now?

176385431.Text: What should we do? We don't have a plan, right?

176385430.Text: Unlike you, March, this little girl is quite perceptive. She knows we're here on behalf of Wildfire, and has probably guessed our intentions.

176385425.Text: Perhaps if we're open and honest with her, we might just convince her of a few things.

176385427.Text: Wait a minute! What do you mean unlike me!

176385462.Text: We still have something to talk about.

176385456.Text: You still want to see Mr. Svarog, right?

176385457.Text: But... I don't want to see everyone arguing...

176385458.Text: We just want to talk to him.

176385459.Text: I know you don't want to cause trouble...

176385452.Text: But... I still don't think it'll work out.

176385453.Text: You just need to let us see him.

1742469401.Text: But if things don't turn out well...

1742469402.Text: Mr. Svarog... He's very rational. Sometimes, I feel he's too... rational...

1742469403.Text: He's not evil, but once he decides something... people could get hurt for sure.

1742469404.Text: I don't want to see anyone get hurt... and I don't want Mr. Svarog to...

1742469397.Text: I'm sure you're aware of the relationship between Wildfire and Svarog?

1742469399.Text: I'm sorry, but I must state my position.

1742469400.Text: If we let current circumstances develop, Wildfire and the prospectors will descend into war.

1742469393.Text: Rather than waiting for that to happen, why not let us meet Svarog and try to strike a peace deal.

-986413954.Text: Is it... really possible? For everyone to coexist?

-986413953.Text: You said that Svarog is very rational.

-986413951.Text: A rational person can be persuaded by facts and reasoning. Just like Danheng here.

-986413957.Text: As long as you help us.

-986413955.Text: Seele, surely Wildfire feels the same way, right?

-986413962.Text: Well, I know Chief Oleg pretty well...

-986413961.Text: ...and if this problem can be solved without violence, then I... don't think he would object.

579669987.Text: Whoa, whoa, are you sure about this folks?

579669988.Text: Don't forget, Oleg's assignment was for you to eliminate—

579669983.Text: We know that Svarog struck a deal with Fersman.

579669984.Text: Even though we didn't exactly get along back in the mines... it would seem that he is capable of cooperating with others.

579669986.Text: ...I understand. If that's what everyone thinks...

579669979.Text: I'll take you to Mr. Svarog.

-1127576748.Text: If — and I mean if — Svarog doesn't want to talk to us... what do we do?

-1127576749.Text: We'll have to play it by ear.

-1127576754.Text: And if we do end up coming to blows, at least we tried our best.

-1127576755.Text: No matter what, let's remain calm.

-1127576752.Text: We can't let Clara's efforts go to waste.

-1127576718.Text: So Clara, they say you're pretty good at finding relics... is that true?

-1127576717.Text: I collected 327 in the last tax period.

-1127576722.Text: It's all thanks to Mr. Svarog sending out robots to help.

-1127576685.Text: We've waltzed through the main gate twice now... How strange.

-1127576686.Text: They do seem a little on edge.

-1127576688.Text: I'm used to it by now.

-1127576689.Text: They're afraid that something bad will happen to them if they get too close to me.

-1127576690.Text: Ignore them. People are instinctively hostile to that which they don't understand.

-1127576691.Text: What's the layout of this base?

-1127576677.Text: The outer ring... is where everyone rests and chats.

1601306671.Text: The middle ring is where we store all the relics... Only certified prospectors can enter.

1601306670.Text: And the inner circle is where Mr. Svarog stores the most important things.

1601306669.Text: Only a few people have been there, including me.

1601306668.Text: Don't you get stopped by the robots?

1601306665.Text: After all, I repaired many of them myself.

1601306678.Text: Actually, you don't have to worry too much about me.

2004591198.Text: As long as Mr. Svarog is watching over me, I'm safe.

2004591197.Text: Besides Svarog, is there anything else in the inner circle?

2004591196.Text: There are some very old machines, huge ones. I can't really explain what it is they do exactly... But I see Mr. Svarog by the machines a lot, fiddling with all the installations.

2004591195.Text: Recently, some installations malfunctioned and monsters broke in.

2004591194.Text: Nothing that a quick clear out won't fix.

-1127576652.Text: Svarog's beard! The Bloody Rabbit is here...

-1127576655.Text: That thing's an ill omen, I tell you. Stay away from it if you know what's good for you.

-1127576654.Text: Who are they? Don't they know the Bloody Rabbit brings bad luck to people around?

-1127576657.Text: It's the rabbit again... How come the boss relies on him so much?

-1127576656.Text: That's it, I'm getting outta here. I suggest you all do the same.

-1127576659.Text: I kinda wanna ask for scavenging techniques...

-1127576658.Text: Are you crazy? Did you get bored of living or something?

-1127576881.Text: Huh, sounds like the elevator and Clara are best friends.

-1127576878.Text: It's called "little Timmy." Luckily it has its own reserve power supply.

-1127576879.Text: Wow, even the elevators here have names?

-1127576876.Text: The gate won't open because Mr. Machine who supplies the power has malfunctioned...

-1127576877.Text: Everyone, please help Clara. Let's get them working again.

-1127576850.Text: This is one of the generators.

-1127576849.Text: This generator's cooling system has overheated. I'll need some time to replace the fan...

-1127576846.Text: ...As long as we're helping not hindering.

-1127576844.Text: How could a cooling system overheat?

-1127576843.Text: I heard that some careless people placed lots of machinery here while it was still running. It nearly caused a fire...

-1127576842.Text: Luckily, this generator is really strong. It managed to boost its cooling function and reduce the temperature until someone found out.

1601306504.Text: But it got really tired. That's how it ended up like this.

1601306505.Text: Why didn't Svarog fix it himself?

1601306507.Text: If he found out, the people responsible would get into big trouble.

1601306508.Text: Aww, you're such a sweet girl, Clara.

1601306510.Text: It's nearly done... Danheng, can you hold down this button?

1601306513.Text: There's one more generator to go... then we can see Mr. Svarog.

-1127576818.Text: Its components are really old... Let's install some new bearings...

-1127576812.Text: Looks pretty complicated... You've got a good head on your shoulders, Clara!

-1127576814.Text: You all say really nice things about me. A—Actually, it makes me feel a bit... shy...

-1127576815.Text: I learned most of this from Mr. Svarog.

1601306539.Text: 【临时】记得有一次，克拉拉在找遗物的时候，不小心踩进了被遗弃的矿坑里。

1601306538.Text: I must have hit my head really hard. I was unconscious...

1601306537.Text: When I woke up, Mr. Svarog was holding me. He'd taken me back to the base.

1601306536.Text: In the end I found out that he'd been worried for my safety, so he followed me the whole way.

1601306543.Text: Who would've thought a big robot like him could have such a delicate side...

1601306542.Text: Is there anyone else that's allowed to get close to Svarog?

1601306541.Text: Any member of the family who works hard collecting relics is allowed to see him...

1601306540.Text: Even the prospectors who've only seen him once get really excited about it.

1601306547.Text: It sounds like Svarog has become an object of worship for them.

1601306546.Text: The same way that Overworlders worship the Consul.

2004591064.Text: If we remove this screw... and then replace this one here...

2004591063.Text: I think that should do it.

2004591070.Text: That's both generators fixed... the certification system for the door should have reset now!

2004591069.Text: Great work, Clara! Let's go and talk to Svarog.

-1127576781.Text: I know I asked before, but... do you really think we can co-exist?

-1127576485.Text: I fixed the malfunctioning generator! The inner circle is back to normal.

-1127576490.Text: Repairs are my duty, Clara. But thank you.

-1127576491.Text: Actually... I brought some friends here.

-1127576494.Text: They want to... talk to you.

1601306869.Text: Mr. Svarog! They aren't bad people.

1601306871.Text: Stay calm, we're just here to talk.

1601306870.Text: We don't want to fight you.

1601306865.Text: Clara, return to the shelter and rest.

2004591396.Text: Phew... I can finally let my hair down.

2004591395.Text: Oi! Big guy! We're here for peace talks.

2004591392.Text: Evaluation result: Rejected. Subject has no leverage.

2004591391.Text: You know about my deal with Mr. Fersman, and you are cooperating with Wildfire. You are enemies.

2004591394.Text: What is it that Wildfire has done?

2004591393.Text: Wildfire possesses assets belonging to the Geomarrow Development Group.

2004591387.Text: Relics form part of the subterranean system. Ownership is non-transferable.

438507455.Text: This Geomarrow Development Group... isn't that something from hundreds of years ago?

438507454.Text: What's the point of guarding something that no longer exists?

438507457.Text: The humans of the development group may have perished, but the group's mission remains.

438507456.Text: I was rebooted. I will repair and rebuild.

438507451.Text: The subterranean system must return to full operational capacity.

438507450.Text: Wouldn't cooperating with Wildfire be a win-win situation?

438507453.Text: The objectives of Wildfire are incompatible with our own.

438507452.Text: Quoting speech from Departed Friend Fersman...

438507447.Text: What Wildfire wants is war, and war could destroy everything.

438507446.Text: That's ridiculous! We aren't looking for a fight with the prospectors.

841791982.Text: The enemy of Wildfire is not below ground.

841791981.Text: Huh? What does that mean? Say something that I can actually understand! Hello?

841791984.Text: If we go to war, Clara will suffer.

841791978.Text: Nevertheless, the actions of Wildfire will eventually endanger the lives of Clara and Hook.

841791977.Text: Tsk, this is all based on conspiracy theories!

841791980.Text: Did old Fersman tell you this?

841791979.Text: Eliminating Wildfire is the optimal course of action.

841791974.Text: Short-term sacrifices will be exchanged for long-term stability.

841791973.Text: Our conversation has ended. Evaluation result remains unchanged.

-724291959.Text: Subterranean system mainframe control unit Svarog, initiating peacekeeping mode. Human safety protocols temporarily disengaged.

-724291960.Text: This guy's about to get serious!

-724291957.Text: We tried our best to make peace, now we gotta try our best to crush this guy! This is no ordinary robot.

-724291958.Text: {NICKNAME}, Seele, keep him busy at the front.

-724291963.Text: As if I need you to tell me!

-724291961.Text: Got it! I'll do my best.

-1127576454.Text: This sucks! I thought robots were supposed to follow those three laws or something?

-1127576453.Text: Like, aren't robots supposed to obey orders given to them by human beings? This guy didn't think very long before attacking us!

-1127576452.Text: You mean the laws from those science fiction novels...?

-1127576459.Text: Argh! I thought that was real!

458710169.Text: Your chance of survival is minimal.

458710265.Text: I am a guardian of the subterranean system. You are the greatest threat to the system.

458710259.Text: My strength... it's coming back to me!

458710037.Text: Where's a doctor when you need one?

458710040.Text: Hey, can we figure the technicalities out a little later?

458710043.Text: Lucky for all of us, Sampo Koski always comes prepared!

2024793976.Text: Base breached, armed Wildfire personnel detected. Calculating probability...

2024793975.Text: All subjects are high-risk individuals! Commencing — complete — annihilation!

458710108.Text: The child... he took her and ran...

458710133.Text: Protecting Clara was the robot's highest priority...

458710131.Text: Don't worry about me, I was just talking out loud.

458710136.Text: We were in serious danger there. Thank you.

458710134.Text: It's a doctor's duty to heal.

458710140.Text: 【临时】得亏桑博够机灵，他按约定的方式留了信息。

458710141.Text: Wildfire had made a number of preparations.

2024794073.Text: So, you were waiting to attack all along?

2024794071.Text: If I told you that Wildfire wanted your peace talks to succeed, would you believe me?

2024794072.Text: Peace talks, combat... we were prepared for any eventuality.

2024794077.Text: We didn't find any trace of Hook...

2024794078.Text: A prospector brought her to the clinic. She'd collapsed.

2024794075.Text: But don't worry. The kid was in an unfamiliar environment, so she refused to talk to anyone, or even eat.

2024794076.Text: It seems like Svarog's intention really was to look after her.

2024794081.Text: Yes, he observed the promise he'd made to Fersman.

2024794082.Text: You know, there's a saying among the miners here: "machines don't go back on their word."

1621509546.Text: Still, looking after a child must have exceeded even his capabilities.

1621509547.Text: Refusing to eat... that child really is stubborn.

1621509544.Text: She may seem fearless, but away from her father, and surrounded by all those prospectors and machines... I'm sure any child would have struggled.

1621509545.Text: After they sent her over, I fed her some soup. She's probably still sleeping.

1621509550.Text: As for the matter of Fersman... I still haven't figure out how to tell her.

1621509551.Text: Svarog is no longer a threat.

1621509549.Text: After hearing Sampo's initial report, I really did think that the peace talks had a chance...

1621509554.Text: I didn't think the situation would escalate to such an extent before our arrival.

-1107373809.Text: We attacked her family, after all...

-1107373808.Text: If you can, I'd like you to go and look for her.

-1107373811.Text: See it as a small request.

-1107373810.Text: Thanks to you, we've brought this matter to a close. Thank you.

458710430.Text: Well, that takes care of that.

458710433.Text: I guess you can head back to the surface now.

458710427.Text: That robot didn't seem as heartless as we thought.

458710426.Text: But in the end, combat was unavoidable.

458710429.Text: Chin up, Seele. This isn't like you.

458710428.Text: Huh, my heartfelt apologies. I have my off days too, y'know.

458710423.Text: I thought we wouldn't have to create a chaos this time. But in the end, a combat was unavoidable.

458710422.Text: Don't get me wrong, I'd never blindly pursue peace just for the sake of it, and I'm no stranger to solving problems with violence, but... I suddenly feel tired...

2024794372.Text: Fersman and Svarog said that Wildfire has a secret agenda.

2024794371.Text: All this time we've been striving to bring everyone trapped underground back to the surface — to defeat the Consul that sealed us away and give everyone a better life.

2024794374.Text: Why would they assert that Wildfire wants to start a war and bring misery to the people?

2024794373.Text: It might be best to ask Oleg that question.

2024794368.Text: Oleg doesn't always treat me as an adult. He says that I'll understand everything in time, and that I should spare no effort.

2024794367.Text: Even if I asked him right now, I think that's what he'd say to me.

2024794364.Text: Down here, we're all victims of a shared hardship. To leave this place, and use our strength to create a fair society... Isn't that the obvious goal?

2024794363.Text: Yet from the very beginning, I've seen people with fear in their eyes. Afraid of Wildfire... of the future we're taking them to.

1621509845.Text: I... I'm starting to doubt the meaning of everything we're doing.

1621509844.Text: Stopping everything won't dispel your doubts.

1621509847.Text: If you do nothing, nothing will change.

1621509846.Text: Hmph... Y'know, you're actually pretty good at inspiring people.

1621509841.Text: I'm sorry, {NICKNAME}, I shouldn't be bothering you with any of this.

1621509840.Text: After all, you guys are only passing through. You'll be on your way once you've found what you're looking for, right? Bronya will return to the surface and go back to her life of luxury... lucky her.

1621509843.Text: As for me... I'll be keeping up the struggle.

1621509842.Text: Argh, this sucks! How can I put up my best fight with all these doubts in my head?

458710465.Text: It doesn't feel like you're very happy about it.

458710458.Text: I don't know why... but it feels like there's something I've left unfinished.

458710459.Text: Take it easy, it's about time you took a breath or two.

458710460.Text: At daybreak, the Consul is a glittering star, illuminating the people's path.

458710461.Text: At nightfall, the Consul is a flickering flame, comforting the people's soul.

458710455.Text: But for the Underworlders... where is their guiding light?

2024794403.Text: Have you decided what you want to do when you get back?

2024794404.Text: I will lodge a representation with Madam Cocolia, urging her to cease her pursuit of you.

2024794405.Text: And then... I wish to talk to her about the relationship between the Union and the underground.

2024794406.Text: Exactly what kind of world should the Consul be striving to protect...

2024794399.Text: On this matter, I wish to hear her answer again.

2024794400.Text: You and Seele really seemed to compliment each other just now.

2024794402.Text: ...but on the battlefield, you can put your life in her hands.

2024794395.Text: If she'd been born in the Union, she would have become an officer of great standing, like Gepard.

1621509876.Text: ... Perhaps that's true for {NICKNAME} and companions. You are visitors from outside.

1621509878.Text: Perhaps I can do something in my power to help them... to help the underground.

1621509879.Text: Oh, the thing you've been searching for this whole time... it's called a Stellaron, right?

1621509872.Text: When we get to the surface, I will inquire with Madam Cocolia. Perhaps she can help you.

1621509873.Text: What kind of world should the Consul strive to protect...

-845251749.Text: {NICKNAME}, how do you feel? Thrilled to emerge victorious or just happy to be alive?

-845251746.Text: ...Not a trick question, of course. The important thing is finding the joy in all life's twists and turns!

-845251747.Text: Ahem, ruminations aside... You guys have helped Wildfire take care of a longstanding headache. In return, they'll have you back on the surface in no time. Heck, they might even help you with your own cause.

-845251752.Text: *sigh* It pains me to see a valued client go, but you have my heartfelt congratulations.

-845251753.Text: I thought I might've gotten carried away bringing you down here to the Underground. But it turned out to be a very correct decision, haha.

-845251750.Text: Oh? Is there something else you want to ask me?

-845251751.Text: When did you call in for reinforcements?

-845251740.Text: Hehe, I have my secret way of making contact under unique circumstances...

-845251741.Text: I can share with you if you want to know, but such a secret won't come free.

1883631607.Text: 【临时】不过话说回来，你们的神经也太大条了。我的人生信条是：随时都得有个备用计划…还有备用的备用计划。

1883631609.Text: What did Svarog mean when he said about stopping Wildfire?

1883631608.Text: Did he say that? I didn't hear.

1883631603.Text: Honestly, it's not that complicated to find out.

1883631602.Text: You can ask the leader of "Wildfire" directly.

1883631605.Text: You seem to take pleasure in causing trouble.

1883631604.Text: Oh friend, whatever could have given you that impression?

1883631615.Text: It's more that I take pleasure in watching other people fight.

1883631614.Text: Fierce words, fists flying, what's not to like!

317547666.Text: When can I get back to the ground?

317547668.Text: If it was my call, I would take you back at any time.

317547667.Text: But I need to follow orders, no?

317547661.Text: Hey, hey! Where's this sentimental feeling coming from?

317547664.Text: Despite our friendship, I won't be giving you a discount.

317547663.Text: Just a joke! I shall always be happy to assist you.

-1248536275.Text: You should have thrown in the towel!

-845251715.Text: But I can't shake off this feeling of regret...

-845251714.Text: You want to spend more time in the Underground?

-845251721.Text: No way! I want to go back to the express! Back to the express!

-845251720.Text: I'm talking about Clara and Svarog.

-845251719.Text: You mean what happened to Clara and Svarog?

-845251709.Text: Clara promised to help us out of good intentions.

-845251708.Text: But her family got hurt by this...

1883631639.Text: There's no need to feel bad. We were out of good intentions too.

1883631640.Text: That's true, but I still feel like we should apologize to her...

1883631634.Text: Before we leave, let's apologize to Clara.

1883631636.Text: But where would Svarog take her?

1883631637.Text: Ah! Do you remember? Clara mentioned that the first time she met Svarog was in the Fragmentum.

1883631646.Text: Could that be where they headed?

-845251683.Text: Hopefully, we'll find some clues about Stellaron at Svarog's base. That way, it'll allow us to contact the express to push our searches further.

-845251684.Text: What if we can't find anything?

-845251685.Text: Then we'll follow Bronya back to the surface and talk to the Consul who's after us.

-845251686.Text: Compared to that, it sounds pretty good staying in the Underground.

-845251687.Text: Just thinking about it makes me feel excited...

-845251689.Text: I've grown tired of the tedious and depressing atmosphere here.

-845251674.Text: It's a dying world, like a candle almost extinguished. It is a torture for all the people who live in this world.

-845251675.Text: I hope to see it revived next time we come back.

1883631673.Text: I wonder what it will look like the next time we come back.

1883631672.Text: ... There are many worlds like this in the universe. Your journey has just begun.

1883631671.Text: When you've explored more worlds, this place will become a distant memory.

1883631670.Text: But if you still remember this place, feel free to come back.

1883631669.Text: I hope we'll find some clues about the Stellaron at Svarog's base...

-845251653.Text: It's what the Wildfire has been searching for. It's buried deep in the Underground, a past forgotten by the people.

-845251652.Text: Let's take a closer look together, {NICKNAME}. I wonder if there's anything like this where you're from?

-845251655.Text: The appearance suggests it's a completely different technology from the express.

-845251881.Text: This is what Svarog mentioned... the thing that the "Geomarrow Development Group" left behind.

-845251878.Text: A long time ago, people had already explored the Underground and built the sophisticated underground mining system.

-845251879.Text: All the "ancient relics" that we've been using, including Svarog, are all fragments of the technology left behind from that era.

-845251876.Text: I don't know what happened to the undergrounds, but they gradually abandoned the technology and their memories of the past.

-845251877.Text: Miss Natasha, you know quite a bit...

-845251874.Text: The doctor for "Wildfire" knows her history...

-845251875.Text: Sorry for keeping from you guys for so long. It's about time to re-introduce myself...

-845251872.Text: I am Natasha, the true chief of the "Wildfire" organization.

1883631475.Text: Oleg is in charge of appearing in public while I'm in charge of keeping the organization running from the shadows. I'm also responsible for its more important goals.

1883631474.Text: Why did you hide your identity?

1883631477.Text: I'm sorry, but it was for safety reasons. After all, the goal of "Wildfire" isn't something welcomed by many people.

1883631476.Text: 【临时】这难道就是桑博说的：凡事都要有个备用计划吗？连组织的首领都有个备份。

1883631479.Text: Yes... This is a secret known only to a few.

1883631478.Text: But now, I don't feel a need to hide my identity anymore, especially with you guys.

1883631482.Text: R-Really? When? Why? Am I the only one who didn't know?

317547533.Text: My identity will not interfere with our cooperation.

317547535.Text: Svarog said the enemy of "Wildfire" is not underground. But Fersman seems to believe you guys only want war...

317547538.Text: So for you guys, eliminating Svarog was just a step towards your goal? What "Wildfire" seeks is a war with the surface, right?

317547537.Text: Fersman and Svarog seem to have some misunderstandings about "Wildfire."

317547540.Text: The only way to completely prevent the terrible illness brought on by Geomarrow is to leave the Underground.

317547539.Text: From the start, the goal of "Wildfire" is to free Geomarrow from the Jailer.

317547542.Text: That's the meaning behind the name of the organization. The "fire," never to be extinguished, will burn everything that suppresses it.

317547541.Text: 【临时】这和奥列格说的不一样，他说「地火」的目标是找到失落的历史真相。

-1248536407.Text: All you did was use us to remove the obstacles in your way.

-1248536408.Text: No matter how much we resist, we must understand history for what it is and why we are trapped underground.

-1248536405.Text: Fersman and Svarog might believe that "Wildfire" is looking for a reason to start a war with the Union... but they don't understand those that want to start a war don't need a reason.

-1248536406.Text: Wildfire isn't like what they've imagined. We don't want to remain oblivious to the truth, nor do we want to stay in the Underground forever.

-1248536403.Text: We need wisdom and knowledge to fight better. We have no chance of winning if we rely on brute force alone.

-1248536401.Text: What information is hidden away here?

-1248536402.Text: It's been in Svarog's hands ever since we've known about it.

-1248536399.Text: It could be historical files, machine design drawings, or even manuals... No matter what it is, it's invaluable to us because we don't know anything.
